- ---
- 
- ## 🚀 Features
- 
- - **⚡ Blazing Fast**: Sub-50ms search results across millions of documents
- - **📂 Filename Search**: Instant filename-only search mode for ultra-fast navigation
- - **💾 Minimal Footprint**: <30MB RAM usage at idle (vs 200MB+ for Electron apps)
- - **📄 Universal Format Support**: PDF, DOCX, XLSX, EPUB, EML, MSG, ZIP, Markdown, Code files, ODT, ODP, ODS
- - **🔍 Full-Text Search**: BM25 scoring, boolean queries, exact phrase matching
- - **📊 Advanced Filters**: Filter by size (`size:>1MB`), extension (`ext:rs`), or path (`path:src`)
- - **🔄 Live Indexing**: Automatic file watching and incremental updates
- - **🎯 Smart Filtering**: .gitignore support, custom exclude patterns
- - **🌙 Native UI**: Beautiful dark/light themes using Iced's high-performance renderer.
- 
- ## 📥 Installation
- 
- ### Prerequisites
- 
- - **Windows**: Windows 10/11
- - **macOS**: macOS 10.15+
- - **Linux**: Vulkan-compatible drivers & development tools (pkg-config, libfontconfig1-dev)
- 
- ### Download
- 
- Download the latest release for your platform from the [Releases](https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll/releases) page.
- 
- ### Build from Source
- 
- ```bash
- # Clone the repository
- git clone https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll.git
- cd FindAll
- 
- # Build the application
- cargo build --release
- 
- # Run in development mode
- cargo run
- ```
- 
- ## 🎮 Usage
- 
- ### First Launch
- 
- 1. **Initial Setup**: Select folders to index on first launch
- 2. **Indexing**: The app will scan and index your files (this may take a few minutes for large directories)
- 3. **Search**: Press `Alt+Space` (or your custom hotkey) to open the search bar from anywhere
- 
- ### Search Syntax
- 
- | Query | Description |
- |-------|-------------|
- | `rust tutorial` | Find documents containing both words |
- | `"exact phrase"` | Find exact phrase matches |
- | `rust OR python` | Boolean OR operator |
- | `code -python` | Exclude documents with "python" |
- | `title:api` | Search only in document titles |
- | `ext:pdf` | Filter by file extension |
- | `path:docs` | Filter by folder path |
- | `size:>5MB` | Filter by file size (KB, MB, GB) |
- 
- ### Keyboard Shortcuts
- 
- | Shortcut | Action |
- |----------|--------|
- | `Alt+Space` | Toggle search window (Global Hotkey, configurable in Settings) |
- | `Ctrl+Enter` | Open selected file |
- | `Ctrl+C` | Copy file path |
- | `Esc` | Close search window |
- | `↑/↓` | Navigate results |
- 
- ## 🏗️ Tech Stack
- 
- ### Core Architecture
- 
- | Component | Technology | Purpose |
- |-----------|------------|---------|
- | **Language** | Rust | Zero-overhead, memory-safe core |
- | **GUI** | Iced | High-performance, cross-platform UI framework (<30MB RAM) |
- | **Search Engine** | Tantivy | Full-text indexing with BM25 scoring |
- | **Metadata DB** | redb | Pure Rust key-value storage |
- | **Concurrency** | Rayon + Tokio | Parallel processing + async I/O |
- 
- ### Supported File Formats
- 
- | Format | Parser | Status |
- |--------|--------|--------|
- | PDF | `pdf-extract` / `lopdf` | ✅ Supported |
- | DOCX | `zip` + `quick-xml` | ✅ Supported |
- | XLSX, XLS, XLSB | `calamine` | ✅ Supported |
- | EPUB, EML, MSG | Native + `zip` | ✅ Supported |
- | ZIP, 7z, RAR | `zip` / `sevenz` | ✅ Supported (ZIP) |
- | TXT, MD, Code | Native Rust | ✅ Supported |
- | ODT, ODP, ODS | `litchi` | ✅ Supported |
- | Images (OCR) | `ocrs` / Tesseract | 🚧 Planned |
- 
- ## 📊 Performance
- 
- Benchmarks on AMD Ryzen 7 5800X with NVMe SSD:
- 
- | Metric | Value |
- |--------|-------|
- | **Index 10,000 PDFs** | ~45 seconds |
- | **Index 100,000 TXT files** | ~12 seconds |
- | **Search latency (p50)** | 12ms |
- | **Search latency (p99)** | 45ms |
- | **Idle RAM usage** | ~25MB |
- | **Peak RAM (indexing)** | ~180MB |
- 
- ### Comparison
- 
- | Feature | Flash Search | AnyTXT | Windows Search | Recoll |
- |---------|-------------|---------|----------------|---------|
- | Startup Time | Instant | ~2s | System | ~3s |
- | RAM Usage | 25MB | 80MB | 150MB+ | 120MB |
- | PDF Search | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ | ✅ |
- | Live Updates | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
- | Cross-Platform | ✅ | ⚠️ | ❌ | ✅ |
- 
- ## 🗺️ Roadmap
- 
- ### Phase 1: Core (Completed ✅)
- - [x] Project setup with Iced
- - [x] Basic file parsers (PDF, DOCX, TXT)
- - [x] Tantivy integration
- - [x] Parallel file scanning
- 
- ### Phase 2: Polish (Completed ✅)

- - [x] Search result preview panel
- - [x] Export search results (CSV, JSON)
- - [x] Fast filename-only search & indexing
- - [x] Enhanced indexing telemetry
- 
- ### Phase 3: Advanced Features (In Progress 🚧)

- - [ ] Natural language queries
- - [ ] Plugin system for custom parsers
- - [ ] OCR support for images and scanned PDFs
- - [ ] Cloud sync for index
- 
- ### Phase 4: Enterprise (Future 🔮)
- - [ ] Network drive support
- - [ ] Multi-user indexing
- - [ ] Web interface
- - [ ] API for integrations
- 
- ## 🤝 Contributing
- 
- We welcome contributions! Please see our [Contributing Guide](CONTRIBUTING.md) for details.
- 
- ### Development Setup
- 
- ```bash
- # Install Rust
- curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
- 
- # Clone and setup
- git clone https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll.git
- cd FindAll
- 
- # Run development server
- cargo run
- ```
-

+ 
+ <h2 align="center">🚀 Features</h2>
+ 
+ <div align="center">
+ 
+ - **⚡ Blazing Fast**: Sub-50ms search results across millions of documents
+ - **📂 Filename Search**: Instant filename-only search mode for ultra-fast navigation
+ - **💾 Minimal Footprint**: <30MB RAM usage at idle (vs 200MB+ for Electron apps)
+ - **📄 Universal Format Support**: PDF, DOCX, XLSX, EPUB, EML, MSG, ZIP, Markdown, Code files, ODT, ODP, ODS
+ - **🔍 Full-Text Search**: BM25 scoring, boolean queries, exact phrase matching
+ - **📊 Advanced Filters**: Filter by size (`size:>1MB`), extension (`ext:rs`), or path (`path:src`)
+ - **🔄 Live Indexing**: Automatic file watching and incremental updates
+ - **🎯 Smart Filtering**: .gitignore support, custom exclude patterns
+ - **🌙 Native UI**: Beautiful dark/light themes using Iced's high-performance renderer.
+ 
+ </div>
+ 
+ <h2 align="center">📥 Installation</h2>
+ 
+ <h3 align="center">Prerequisites</h3>
+ 
+ <div align="center">
+ 
+ - **Windows**: Windows 10/11
+ - **macOS**: macOS 10.15+
+ - **Linux**: Vulkan-compatible drivers & development tools (pkg-config, libfontconfig1-dev)
+ 
+ </div>
+ 
+ <h3 align="center">Download</h3>
+ 
+ <div align="center">
+ 
+ Download the latest release for your platform from the [Releases](https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll/releases) page.
+ 
+ </div>
+ 
+ <h3 align="center">Build from Source</h3>
+ 
+ ```bash
+ # Clone the repository
+ git clone https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll.git
+ cd FindAll
+ 
+ # Build the application
+ cargo build --release
+ 
+ # Run in development mode
+ cargo run
+ ```
+ 
+ <h2 align="center">🎮 Usage</h2>
+ 
+ <h3 align="center">First Launch</h3>
+ 
+ <div align="center">
+ 
+ 1. **Initial Setup**: Select folders to index on first launch
+ 2. **Indexing**: The app will scan and index your files (this may take a few minutes for large directories)
+ 3. **Search**: Press `Alt+Space` (or your custom hotkey) to open the search bar from anywhere
+ 
+ </div>
+ 
+ <h3 align="center">Search Syntax</h3>
+ 
+ <div align="center">
+ 
+ | Query | Description |
+ |-------|-------------|
+ | `rust tutorial` | Find documents containing both words |
+ | `"exact phrase"` | Find exact phrase matches |
+ | `rust OR python` | Boolean OR operator |
+ | `code -python` | Exclude documents with "python" |
+ | `title:api` | Search only in document titles |
+ | `ext:pdf` | Filter by file extension |
+ | `path:docs` | Filter by folder path |
+ | `size:>5MB` | Filter by file size (KB, MB, GB) |
+ 
+ </div>
+ 
+ <h3 align="center">Keyboard Shortcuts</h3>
+ 
+ <div align="center">
+ 
+ | Shortcut | Action |
+ |----------|--------|
+ | `Alt+Space` | Toggle search window (Global Hotkey, configurable in Settings) |
+ | `Ctrl+Enter` | Open selected file |
+ | `Ctrl+C` | Copy file path |
+ | `Esc` | Close search window |
+ | `↑/↓` | Navigate results |
+ 
+ </div>
+ 
+ <h2 align="center">🏗️ Tech Stack</h2>
+ 
+ <h3 align="center">Core Architecture</h3>
+ 
+ <div align="center">
+ 
+ | Component | Technology | Purpose |
+ |-----------|------------|---------|
+ | **Language** | Rust | Zero-overhead, memory-safe core |
+ | **GUI** | Iced | High-performance, cross-platform UI framework (<30MB RAM) |
+ | **Search Engine** | Tantivy | Full-text indexing with BM25 scoring |
+ | **Metadata DB** | redb | Pure Rust key-value storage |
+ | **Concurrency** | Rayon + Tokio | Parallel processing + async I/O |
+ 
+ </div>
+ 
+ <h3 align="center">Supported File Formats</h3>
+ 
+ <div align="center">
+ 
+ | Format | Parser | Status |
+ |--------|--------|--------|
+ | PDF | `pdf-extract` / `lopdf` | ✅ Supported |
+ | DOCX | `zip` + `quick-xml` | ✅ Supported |
+ | XLSX, XLS, XLSB | `calamine` | ✅ Supported |
+ | EPUB, EML, MSG | Native + `zip` | ✅ Supported |
+ | ZIP, 7z, RAR | `zip` / `sevenz` | ✅ Supported (ZIP) |
+ | TXT, MD, Code | Native Rust | ✅ Supported |
+ | ODT, ODP, ODS | `litchi` | ✅ Supported |
+ | Images (OCR) | `ocrs` / Tesseract | 🚧 Planned |
+ 
+ </div>
+ 
+ <h2 align="center">📊 Performance</h2>
+ 
+ <div align="center">
+ 
+ Benchmarks on AMD Ryzen 7 5800X with NVMe SSD:
+ 
+ </div>
+ 
+ <div align="center">
+ 
+ | Metric | Value |
+ |--------|-------|
+ | **Index 10,000 PDFs** | ~45 seconds |
+ | **Index 100,000 TXT files** | ~12 seconds |
+ | **Search latency (p50)** | 12ms |
+ | **Search latency (p99)** | 45ms |
+ | **Idle RAM usage** | ~25MB |
+ | **Peak RAM (indexing)** | ~180MB |
+ 
+ </div>
+ 
+ <h3 align="center">Comparison</h3>
+ 
+ <div align="center">
+ 
+ | Feature | Flash Search | AnyTXT | Windows Search | Recoll |
+ |---------|-------------|---------|----------------|---------|
+ | Startup Time | Instant | ~2s | System | ~3s |
+ | RAM Usage | 25MB | 80MB | 150MB+ | 120MB |
+ | PDF Search | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ | ✅ |
+ | Live Updates | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
+ | Cross-Platform | ✅ | ⚠️ | ❌ | ✅ |
+ 
+ </div>

### File: `CHANGELOG.md`

- Size: 897 bytes
- Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134168536866482981 }

```markdown
   1 | # Changelog
   2 | 
   3 | All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
   4 | 
   5 | ## [0.2.0] - 2024-03-01
   6 | 
   7 | ### Added
   8 | - Structured logging with tracing and log file rotation
   9 | - CLI `--version` flag
  10 | - Proper error handling instead of panics on startup
  11 | - CI test and clippy checks before build
  12 | 
  13 | ### Fixed
  14 | - Replaced all `expect()`/`unwrap()` with proper error handling
  15 | - Standardized logging with tracing (replaced eprintln!/println!)
  16 | - Pinned litchi git dependency for reproducible builds
  17 | - NSIS uninstall hook now properly removes PATH entry
  18 | 
  19 | ### Changed
  20 | - Updated version from 0.1.0 to 0.2.0
  21 | - Added tracing-appender for log rotation
  22 | 
  23 | ## [0.1.0] - 2024-01-01
  24 | 
  25 | ### Added
  26 | - Initial release
  27 | - Full-text search with Tantivy
  28 | - File metadata database with redb
  29 | - Iced-based UI
  30 | - Multiple file format parsers (PDF, DOCX, EPUB, etc.)
  31 | - File system watcher for live indexing
```

### File: `CONTRIBUTING.md`

```markdown
   1 | # Contributing to Flash Search
   2 | 
   3 | Thank you for your interest in contributing to Flash Search!
   4 | 
   5 | ## Development Setup
   6 | 
   7 | ```bash
   8 | # Install Rust
   9 | curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
  10 | 
  11 | # Clone and setup
  12 | git clone https://github.com/yourusername/flash-search.git
  13 | cd flash-search
  14 | 
  15 | # Run development server
  16 | cargo run
  17 | ```
  18 | 
  19 | ## Building
  20 | 
  21 | ```bash
  22 | # Debug build
  23 | cargo build
  24 | 
  25 | # Release build
  26 | cargo build --release
  27 | 
  28 | # Run tests
  29 | cargo test
  30 | ```
  31 | 
  32 | ## Code Style
  33 | 
  34 | - Use `cargo fmt` before committing
  35 | - Use `cargo clippy` to catch common mistakes
  36 | - Follow existing code conventions
  37 | 
  38 | ## Pull Request Process
  39 | 
  40 | 1. Fork the repository
  41 | 2. Create a feature branch
  42 | 3. Make your changes
  43 | 4. Run tests and formatting
  44 | 5. Submit a pull request
  45 | 
  46 | ## Reporting Issues
  47 | 
  48 | Please include:
  49 | - Steps to reproduce
  50 | - Expected vs actual behavior
  51 | - System information
  52 | 
  53 | ## License
  54 | 
  55 | By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under GPLv3.
```

### File: `README.md`

- Size: 7429 bytes
- Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134175100624650067 }

```markdown
   1 | <h1 align="center">Flash Search</h1>
   2 | 
   3 | <p align="center">
   4 |   <img src="assets/logo.png" alt="Flash Search Logo" width="120">
   5 | </p>
   6 | 
   7 | <p align="center">
   8 |   <b>Ultrafast local full-text search with minimal resource footprint</b>
   9 | </p>
  10 | 
  11 | <p align="center">
  12 |   <a href="#features">Features</a> •
  13 |   <a href="#installation">Installation</a> •
  14 |   <a href="#usage">Usage</a> •
  15 |   <a href="#tech-stack">Tech Stack</a> •
  16 |   <a href="#performance">Performance</a> •
  17 |   <a href="#roadmap">Roadmap</a>
  18 | </p>
  19 | 
  20 | <p align="center">
  21 |   <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Rust-000000?style=flat&logo=rust&logoColor=white" alt="Rust">
  22 |   <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Iced-0.14-blue?style=flat&logo=rust&logoColor=white" alt="Iced">
  23 |   <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/License-GPL--3.0-blue.svg" alt="License">
  24 | </p>
  25 | 
  26 | ---
  27 | 
  28 | <h2 align="center">🚀 Features</h2>
  29 | 
  30 | <div align="center">
  31 | 
  32 | - **⚡ Blazing Fast**: Sub-50ms search results across millions of documents
  33 | - **📂 Filename Search**: Instant filename-only search mode for ultra-fast navigation
  34 | - **💾 Minimal Footprint**: <30MB RAM usage at idle (vs 200MB+ for Electron apps)
  35 | - **📄 Universal Format Support**: PDF, DOCX, XLSX, EPUB, EML, MSG, ZIP, Markdown, Code files, ODT, ODP, ODS
  36 | - **🔍 Full-Text Search**: BM25 scoring, boolean queries, exact phrase matching
  37 | - **📊 Advanced Filters**: Filter by size (`size:>1MB`), extension (`ext:rs`), or path (`path:src`)
  38 | - **🔄 Live Indexing**: Automatic file watching and incremental updates
  39 | - **🎯 Smart Filtering**: .gitignore support, custom exclude patterns
  40 | - **🌙 Native UI**: Beautiful dark/light themes using Iced's high-performance renderer.
  41 | 
  42 | </div>
  43 | 
  44 | <h2 align="center">📥 Installation</h2>
  45 | 
  46 | <h3 align="center">Prerequisites</h3>
  47 | 
  48 | <div align="center">
  49 | 
  50 | - **Windows**: Windows 10/11
  51 | - **macOS**: macOS 10.15+
  52 | - **Linux**: Vulkan-compatible drivers & development tools (pkg-config, libfontconfig1-dev)
  53 | 
  54 | </div>
  55 | 
  56 | <h3 align="center">Download</h3>
  57 | 
  58 | <div align="center">
  59 | 
  60 | Download the latest release for your platform from the [Releases](https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll/releases) page.
  61 | 
  62 | </div>
  63 | 
  64 | <h3 align="center">Build from Source</h3>
  65 | 
  66 | ```bash
  67 | # Clone the repository
  68 | git clone https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll.git
  69 | cd FindAll
  70 | 
  71 | # Build the application
  72 | cargo build --release
  73 | 
  74 | # Run in development mode
  75 | cargo run
  76 | ```
  77 | 
  78 | <h2 align="center">🎮 Usage</h2>
  79 | 
  80 | <h3 align="center">First Launch</h3>
  81 | 
  82 | <div align="center">
  83 | 
  84 | 1. **Initial Setup**: Select folders to index on first launch
  85 | 2. **Indexing**: The app will scan and index your files (this may take a few minutes for large directories)
  86 | 3. **Search**: Press `Alt+Space` (or your custom hotkey) to open the search bar from anywhere
  87 | 
  88 | </div>
  89 | 
  90 | <h3 align="center">Search Syntax</h3>
  91 | 
  92 | <div align="center">
  93 | 
  94 | | Query | Description |
  95 | |-------|-------------|
  96 | | `rust tutorial` | Find documents containing both words |
  97 | | `"exact phrase"` | Find exact phrase matches |
  98 | | `rust OR python` | Boolean OR operator |
  99 | | `code -python` | Exclude documents with "python" |
 100 | | `title:api` | Search only in document titles |
 101 | | `ext:pdf` | Filter by file extension |
 102 | | `path:docs` | Filter by folder path |
 103 | | `size:>5MB` | Filter by file size (KB, MB, GB) |
 104 | 
 105 | </div>
 106 | 
 107 | <h3 align="center">Keyboard Shortcuts</h3>
 108 | 
 109 | <div align="center">
 110 | 
 111 | | Shortcut | Action |
 112 | |----------|--------|
 113 | | `Alt+Space` | Toggle search window (Global Hotkey, configurable in Settings) |
 114 | | `Ctrl+Enter` | Open selected file |
 115 | | `Ctrl+C` | Copy file path |
 116 | | `Esc` | Close search window |
 117 | | `↑/↓` | Navigate results |
 118 | 
 119 | </div>
 120 | 
 121 | <h2 align="center">🏗️ Tech Stack</h2>
 122 | 
 123 | <h3 align="center">Core Architecture</h3>
 124 | 
 125 | <div align="center">
 126 | 
 127 | | Component | Technology | Purpose |
 128 | |-----------|------------|---------|
 129 | | **Language** | Rust | Zero-overhead, memory-safe core |
 130 | | **GUI** | Iced | High-performance, cross-platform UI framework (<30MB RAM) |
 131 | | **Search Engine** | Tantivy | Full-text indexing with BM25 scoring |
 132 | | **Metadata DB** | redb | Pure Rust key-value storage |
 133 | | **Concurrency** | Rayon + Tokio | Parallel processing + async I/O |
 134 | 
 135 | </div>
 136 | 
 137 | <h3 align="center">Supported File Formats</h3>
 138 | 
 139 | <div align="center">
 140 | 
 141 | | Format | Parser | Status |
 142 | |--------|--------|--------|
 143 | | PDF | `pdf-extract` / `lopdf` | ✅ Supported |
 144 | | DOCX | `zip` + `quick-xml` | ✅ Supported |
 145 | | XLSX, XLS, XLSB | `calamine` | ✅ Supported |
 146 | | EPUB, EML, MSG | Native + `zip` | ✅ Supported |
 147 | | ZIP, 7z, RAR | `zip` / `sevenz` | ✅ Supported (ZIP) |
 148 | | TXT, MD, Code | Native Rust | ✅ Supported |
 149 | | ODT, ODP, ODS | `litchi` | ✅ Supported |
 150 | | Images (OCR) | `ocrs` / Tesseract | 🚧 Planned |
 151 | 
 152 | </div>
 153 | 
 154 | <h2 align="center">📊 Performance</h2>
 155 | 
 156 | <div align="center">
 157 | 
 158 | Benchmarks on AMD Ryzen 7 5800X with NVMe SSD:
 159 | 
 160 | </div>
 161 | 
 162 | <div align="center">
 163 | 
 164 | | Metric | Value |
 165 | |--------|-------|
 166 | | **Index 10,000 PDFs** | ~45 seconds |
 167 | | **Index 100,000 TXT files** | ~12 seconds |
 168 | | **Search latency (p50)** | 12ms |
 169 | | **Search latency (p99)** | 45ms |
 170 | | **Idle RAM usage** | ~25MB |
 171 | | **Peak RAM (indexing)** | ~180MB |
 172 | 
 173 | </div>
 174 | 
 175 | <h3 align="center">Comparison</h3>
 176 | 
 177 | <div align="center">
 178 | 
 179 | | Feature | Flash Search | AnyTXT | Windows Search | Recoll |
 180 | |---------|-------------|---------|----------------|---------|
 181 | | Startup Time | Instant | ~2s | System | ~3s |
 182 | | RAM Usage | 25MB | 80MB | 150MB+ | 120MB |
 183 | | PDF Search | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ | ✅ |
 184 | | Live Updates | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
 185 | | Cross-Platform | ✅ | ⚠️ | ❌ | ✅ |
 186 | 
 187 | </div>
 188 | 
 189 | <h2 align="center">🗺️ Roadmap</h2>
 190 | 
 191 | <h3 align="center">Phase 1: Core (Completed ✅)</h3>
 192 | <div align="center">
 193 | 
 194 | - [x] Project setup with Iced
 195 | - [x] Basic file parsers (PDF, DOCX, TXT)
 196 | - [x] Tantivy integration
 197 | - [x] Parallel file scanning
 198 | 
 199 | </div>
 200 | 
 201 | <h3 align="center">Phase 2: Polish (Completed ✅)</h3>
 202 | <div align="center">
 203 | 
 204 | - [x] Advanced search filters (date, size, type)
 205 | - [x] Search result preview panel
 206 | - [x] Export search results (CSV, JSON)
 207 | - [x] Fast filename-only search & indexing
 208 | - [x] Enhanced indexing telemetry
 209 | 
 210 | </div>
 211 | 
 212 | <h3 align="center">Phase 3: Advanced Features (In Progress 🚧)</h3>
 213 | <div align="center">
 214 | 
 215 | - [x] Search history and favorites (Pinned files)
 216 | - [ ] Natural language queries
 217 | - [ ] Plugin system for custom parsers
 218 | - [ ] OCR support for images and scanned PDFs
 219 | - [ ] Cloud sync for index
 220 | 
 221 | </div>
 222 | 
 223 | <h3 align="center">Phase 4: Enterprise (Future 🔮)</h3>
 224 | <div align="center">
 225 | 
 226 | - [ ] Network drive support
 227 | - [ ] Multi-user indexing
 228 | - [ ] Web interface
 229 | - [ ] API for integrations
 230 | 
 231 | </div>
 232 | 
 233 | <h2 align="center">🤝 Contributing</h2>
 234 | 
 235 | <div align="center">
 236 | 
 237 | We welcome contributions! Please see our [Contributing Guide](CONTRIBUTING.md) for details.
 238 | 
 239 | </div>
 240 | 
 241 | <h3 align="center">Development Setup</h3>
 242 | 
 243 | ```bash
 244 | # Install Rust
 245 | curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
 246 | 
 247 | # Clone and setup
 248 | git clone https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll.git
 249 | cd FindAll
 250 | 
 251 | # Run development server
 252 | cargo run
 253 | ```
 254 | 
 255 | <h2 align="center">📄 License</h2>
 256 | 
 257 | <div align="center">
 258 | 
 259 | This project is licensed under the GNU General Public License v3 - see the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.
 260 | 
 261 | </div>
 262 | 
 263 | <h2 align="center">🙏 Acknowledgments</h2>
 264 | 
 265 | <div align="center">
 266 | 
 267 | - [Tantivy](https://github.com/quickwit-oss/tantivy) - The blazing-fast search engine library
 268 | - [Iced](https://iced.rs/) - The cross-platform UI framework for Rust

  30 | use crate::settings::{AppSettings, SettingsManager};
  31 | use crate::watcher::WatcherManager;
  32 | use std::sync::{Arc, RwLock};
  33 | use tokio::sync::mpsc;
  34 | 
  35 | pub struct AppState {
  36 |     pub indexer: Arc<IndexManager>,
  37 |     pub metadata_db: Arc<MetadataDb>,
  38 |     pub settings_manager: Arc<SettingsManager>,
  39 |     pub settings_cache: Arc<RwLock<AppSettings>>,
  40 |     pub watcher: std::sync::Mutex<WatcherManager>,
  41 |     pub filename_index: Option<Arc<FilenameIndex>>,
  42 |     pub progress_tx: mpsc::Sender<crate::scanner::ProgressEvent>,
  43 |     pub scanner: Arc<crate::scanner::Scanner>,
  44 | }
  45 | 
  46 | impl AppState {
  47 |     pub fn new(
  48 |         indexer: Arc<IndexManager>,
  49 |         metadata_db: Arc<MetadataDb>,
  50 |         settings_manager: SettingsManager,
  51 |         watcher: WatcherManager,
  52 |         filename_index: Option<Arc<FilenameIndex>>,
  53 |         progress_tx: mpsc::Sender<crate::scanner::ProgressEvent>,
  54 |         scanner: Arc<crate::scanner::Scanner>,
  55 |     ) -> Self {
  56 |         let cache = settings_manager.load().unwrap_or_default();
  57 |         let mut watcher = watcher;
  58 |         let _ = watcher.update_watch_list(cache.index_dirs.clone());
  59 |         Self {
  60 |             indexer,
  61 |             metadata_db,
  62 |             settings_manager: Arc::new(settings_manager),
  63 |             settings_cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(cache)),
  64 |             watcher: std::sync::Mutex::new(watcher),
  65 |             filename_index,
  66 |             progress_tx,
  67 |             scanner,
  68 |         }
  69 |     }
  70 | }
  71 | 
  72 | #[cfg(test)]
  73 | mod tests {
  74 |     #[test]
  75 |     fn test_export_csv() {
  76 |         // Basic test placeholder
  77 |     }
  78 | }
```

   9 | use iced::{Element, Subscription, Task};
  10 | use std::path::PathBuf;
  11 | use std::sync::Arc;
  12 | use tokio::sync::mpsc;
  13 | 
  14 | pub mod search;
  15 | pub mod settings;

  17 | 
  18 | #[derive(Clone, Debug)]
  19 | pub struct FileItem {
  20 |     pub title: String,
  21 |     pub path: String,
  22 |     pub score: f32,
  23 |     pub extension: Option<String>,
  24 | }
  25 | 
  26 | impl From<SearchResult> for FileItem {
  27 |     fn from(r: SearchResult) -> Self {
  28 |         let ext = r.file_path.split('.').next_back().map(String::from);
  29 |         FileItem {
  30 |             title: r
  31 |                 .file_path
  32 |                 .split(['\\', '/'])
  33 |                 .next_back()
  34 |                 .unwrap_or("Unknown")
  35 |                 .to_string(),
  36 |             path: r.file_path,
  37 |             score: r.score,
  38 |             extension: ext,
  39 |         }
  40 |     }
  41 | }
  42 | 
  43 | impl From<FilenameSearchResult> for FileItem {
  44 |     fn from(r: FilenameSearchResult) -> Self {
  45 |         let ext = r.file_name.split('.').next_back().map(String::from);
  46 |         FileItem {
  47 |             title: r.file_name,
  48 |             path: r.file_path,
  49 |             score: 1.0,
  50 |             extension: ext,
  51 |         }
  52 |     }
  53 | }
  54 | 
  55 | #[derive(Clone, Debug)]
  56 | pub enum Tab {

  58 |     Settings,
  59 | }
  60 | 
  61 | #[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]

  66 |

  76 |     TabChanged(Tab),
  77 |     RebuildIndex,
  78 |     IndexRebuilt,

  80 |     AddFolder,

  82 |     RemoveFolder(usize),

 105 | 
 106 | pub struct App {
 107 |     state: Option<Arc<AppState>>,
 108 |     error: Option<String>,
 109 |     search_error: Option<String>,
 110 |     search_query: String,
 111 |     results: Vec<FileItem>,
 112 |     selected_index: Option<usize>,
 113 |     is_searching: bool,
 114 |     settings: AppSettings,
 115 |     active_tab: Tab,
 116 |     files_indexed: i32,
 117 |     index_size: String,
 118 |     is_dark: bool,
 119 |     search_mode: SearchMode,
 120 |     filter_extension: String,
 121 |     filter_size: String,
 122 |     preview_content: Option<String>,
 123 |     is_loading_preview: bool,
 124 |     rebuild_progress: Option<f32>,
 125 |     rebuild_status: Option<String>,
 126 |     progress_rx: Option<Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<mpsc::Receiver<ProgressEvent>>>>,
 127 |     tray_icon: Option<tray_icon::TrayIcon>,
 128 |     hotkey_manager: Option<global_hotkey::GlobalHotKeyManager>,
 129 | }
 130 | 
 131 | impl App {
 132 |     fn new(state: Result<Arc<AppState>, String>) -> Self {
 133 |         match state {
 134 |             Ok(state) => {
 135 |                 let settings = state.settings_manager.load().unwrap_or_default();
 136 |                 let stats = state.indexer.get_statistics().unwrap_or_default();
 137 |                 let index_size = format!("{:.1} MB", (stats.total_size_bytes as f64) / 1_048_576.0);
 138 |                 let is_dark = matches!(settings.theme, crate::settings::Theme::Dark);
 139 | 
 140 |                 let mut app = App {
 141 |                     state: Some(state),
 142 |                     error: None,
 143 |                     search_error: None,
 144 |                     search_query: String::new(),
 145 |                     results: Vec::new(),
 146 |                     selected_index: None,
 147 |                     is_searching: false,
 148 |                     settings: settings.clone(),
 149 |                     active_tab: Tab::Search,
 150 |                     files_indexed: stats.total_documents as i32,
 151 |                     index_size,
 152 |                     is_dark,
 153 |                     search_mode: SearchMode::FullText,
 154 |                     filter_extension: String::new(),
 155 |                     filter_size: String::new(),
 156 |                     preview_content: None,
 157 |                     is_loading_preview: false,
 158 |                     rebuild_progress: None,
 159 |                     rebuild_status: None,
 160 |                     progress_rx: None,
 161 |                     tray_icon: crate::system::tray::create_tray_icon().ok(),
 162 |                     hotkey_manager: None,
 163 |                 };
 164 | 
 165 |                 if let Ok(manager) = global_hotkey::GlobalHotKeyManager::new() {
 166 |                     if let Ok(hotkey) = settings
 167 |                         .global_hotkey
 168 |                         .parse::<global_hotkey::hotkey::HotKey>()
 169 |                     {
 170 |                         if manager.register(hotkey).is_err() {
 171 |                             app.error = Some(format!(
 172 |                                 "Hotkey conflict: '{}' is already registered by another application. Please choose an alternative in Settings.",
 173 |                                 settings.global_hotkey
 174 |                             ));
 175 |                         }
 176 |                     } else if !settings.global_hotkey.is_empty() {
 177 |                         app.error = Some(format!(
 178 |                             "Invalid hotkey format: '{}'",
 179 |                             settings.global_hotkey
 180 |                         ));
 181 |                     }
 182 |                     app.hotkey_manager = Some(manager);
 183 |                 }
 184 | 
 185 |                 app
 186 |             }
 187 |             Err(err_msg) => App {
 188 |                 state: None,
 189 |                 error: Some(err_msg),
 190 |                 search_error: None,
 191 |                 search_query: String::new(),
 192 |                 results: Vec::new(),
 193 |                 selected_index: None,
 194 |                 is_searching: false,
 195 |                 settings: AppSettings::default(),
 196 |                 active_tab: Tab::Search,
 197 |                 files_indexed: 0,
 198 |                 index_size: "0 MB".to_string(),
 199 |                 is_dark: false,
 200 |                 search_mode: SearchMode::FullText,
 201 |                 filter_extension: String::new(),
 202 |                 filter_size: String::new(),
 203 |                 preview_content: None,
 204 |                 is_loading_preview: false,
 205 |                 rebuild_progress: None,
 206 |                 rebuild_status: None,
 207 |                 progress_rx: None,
 208 |                 tray_icon: crate::system::tray::create_tray_icon().ok(),
 209 |                 hotkey_manager: None,
 210 |             },
 211 |         }
 212 |     }
 213 | 
 214 |     fn parse_size_filter(size_str: &str) -> (Option<u64>, Option<u64>) {
 215 |         let size_str = size_str.trim();
 216 |         if size_str.is_empty() {
 217 |             return (None, None);
 218 |         }
 219 | 
 220 |         let (op, num_str) = if let Some(stripped) = size_str.strip_prefix(">=") {
 221 |             (">=", stripped)
 222 |         } else if let Some(stripped) = size_str.strip_prefix("<=") {
 223 |             ("<=", stripped)
 224 |         } else if let Some(stripped) = size_str.strip_prefix(">") {
 225 |             (">", stripped)
 226 |         } else if let Some(stripped) = size_str.strip_prefix("<") {
 227 |             ("<", stripped)
 228 |         } else {
 229 |             (">=", size_str)
 230 |         };
 231 | 
 232 |         let num_str = num_str.trim();
 233 |         let multiplier: u64 = if num_str.ends_with("KB") {
 234 |             1024
 235 |         } else if num_str.ends_with("MB") {
 236 |             1024 * 1024
 237 |         } else if num_str.ends_with("GB") {
 238 |             1024 * 1024 * 1024
 239 |         } else {
 240 |             1
 241 |         };
 242 | 
 243 |         let num: u64 = num_str
 244 |             .trim_end_matches(|c: char| c.is_alphabetic())
 245 |             .parse()
 246 |             .unwrap_or(0);
 247 |         let bytes = num * multiplier;
 248 | 
 249 |         match op {
 250 |             ">" => (Some(bytes + 1), None),
 251 |             "<" => (None, Some(bytes.saturating_sub(1))),
 252 |             ">=" => (Some(bytes), None),
 253 |             "<=" => (None, Some(bytes)),
 254 |             _ => (Some(bytes), None),
 255 |         }
 256 |     }
 257 | 
 258 |     fn perform_search(&mut self) -> Task<Message> {
 259 |         let state = match &self.state {
 260 |             Some(s) => s.clone(),
 261 |             None => return Task::none(),
 262 |         };
 263 | 
 264 |         let query = self.search_query.clone();
 265 |         let max_results = self.settings.max_results;
 266 |         let mode = self.search_mode.clone();
 267 |         let extension = if self.filter_extension.is_empty() {
 268 |             None
 269 |         } else {
 270 |             Some(vec![self.filter_extension.clone()])
 271 |         };
 272 |         let (min_size, max_size) = Self::parse_size_filter(&self.filter_size);
 273 | 
 274 |         self.is_searching = true;
 275 |         self.results.clear();
 276 |         self.preview_content = None;
 277 | 
 278 |         Task::future(async move {
 279 |             let result = match mode {
 280 |                 SearchMode::Filename => {
 281 |                     match search_filenames_internal(query, max_results, &state).await {
 282 |                         Ok(results) => Message::SearchResultsReceived(
 283 |                             results.into_iter().map(FileItem::from).collect(),
 284 |                         ),
 285 |                         Err(e) => Message::SearchError(e.to_string()),
 286 |                     }
 287 |                 }
 288 |                 SearchMode::FullText => {
 289 |                     match search_query_internal(
 290 |                         query,
 291 |                         max_results,
 292 |                         &state,
 293 |                         min_size,
 294 |                         max_size,
 295 |                         extension,
 296 |                     )
 297 |                     .await
 298 |                     {
 299 |                         Ok(results) => Message::SearchResultsReceived(
 300 |                             results.into_iter().map(FileItem::from).collect(),
 301 |                         ),
 302 |                         Err(e) => Message::SearchError(e.to_string()),
 303 |                     }
 304 |                 }
 305 |             };
 306 |             result
 307 |         })
 308 |     }
 309 | 
 310 |     fn load_preview(&mut self) -> Task<Message> {
 311 |         let idx = match self.selected_index {
 312 |             Some(i) => i,
 313 |             None => return Task::none(),
 314 |         };
 315 | 
 316 |         let item = match self.results.get(idx) {
 317 |             Some(i) => i.clone(),
 318 |             None => return Task::none(),
 319 |         };
 320 | 
 321 |         let path = item.path.clone();
 322 |         let query = self.search_query.clone();
 323 |         self.is_loading_preview = true;
 324 | 
 325 |         Task::future(async move {
 326 |             let preview = match get_file_preview_highlighted_internal(path, query).await {
 327 |                 Ok(result) => Some(result.content),
 328 |                 Err(_) => None,
 329 |             };
 330 |             Message::PreviewLoaded(preview)
 331 |         })
 332 |     }
 333 | 
 334 |     fn save_settings(&self) {
 335 |         if let Some(state) = &self.state {
 336 |             let _ = state.settings_manager.save(&self.settings);
 337 |         }
 338 |     }
 339 | }
 340 | 
 341 | fn update(app: &mut App, message: Message) -> Task<Message> {
 342 |     if app.error.is_some() {
 343 |         match message {
 344 |             Message::DismissError => {
 345 |                 app.error = None;
 346 |                 Task::none()
 347 |             }
 348 |             Message::Quit => {
 349 |                 std::process::exit(0);
 350 |             }
 351 |             _ => Task::none(),
 352 |         }
 353 |     } else {
 354 |         match message {
 355 |             Message::SearchQueryChanged(q) => {
 356 |                 app.search_query = q;
 357 |                 if !app.search_query.is_empty() {
 358 |                     app.perform_search()
 359 |                 } else {
 360 |                     Task::none()
 361 |                 }
 362 |             }
 363 |             Message::SearchSubmitted => app.perform_search(),
 364 |             Message::SearchResultsReceived(results) => {
 365 |                 app.results = results;
 366 |                 app.is_searching = false;
 367 |                 app.search_error = None;
 368 |                 if !app.results.is_empty() {
 369 |                     app.selected_index = Some(0);
 370 |                 }
 371 |                 app.load_preview()
 372 |             }
 373 |             Message::SearchError(err) => {
 374 |                 app.search_error = Some(err);
 375 |                 app.is_searching = false;
 376 |                 app.results.clear();
 377 |                 Task::none()
 378 |             }
 379 |             Message::ResultSelected(idx) => {
 380 |                 app.selected_index = Some(idx);
 381 |                 app.load_preview()
 382 |             }
 383 |             Message::OpenFile(path) => {
 384 |                 let p = PathBuf::from(&path);
 385 |                 if p.is_absolute() && p.exists() {
 386 |                     let _ = opener::open(p);
 387 |                 } else {
 388 |                     tracing::warn!("Blocked attempt to open invalid or relative path: {}", path);
 389 |                 }
 390 |                 Task::none()
 391 |             }
 392 |             Message::CopyPath(path) => {
 393 |                 if let Ok(mut clipboard) = arboard::Clipboard::new() {
 394 |                     let _ = clipboard.set_text(&path);
 395 |                 }
 396 |                 Task::none()
 397 |             }
 398 |             Message::TabChanged(tab) => {
 399 |                 app.active_tab = tab;
 400 |                 Task::none()
 401 |             }
 402 |             Message::RebuildIndex => {
 403 |                 let state = match &app.state {
 404 |                     Some(s) => s.clone(),
 405 |                     None => return Task::none(),
 406 |                 };
 407 |                 let settings = app.settings.clone();
 408 |                 app.rebuild_progress = Some(0.0);
 409 |                 app.rebuild_status = Some("Starting rebuild...".to_string());
 410 |                 if let Some(tray) = &app.tray_icon {
 411 |                     let _ = tray.set_tooltip(Some("Flash Search - Rebuilding Index..."));
 412 |                 }
 413 |                 app.files_indexed = 0;
 414 |                 let rx = app.progress_rx.clone();
 415 |                 Task::batch(vec![
 416 |                     Task::future(async move {
 417 |                         let _ = state.indexer.clear();
 418 |                         let _ = state.indexer.commit();
 419 |                         let _ = state.metadata_db.clear();
 420 |                         for dir in settings.index_dirs {
 421 |                             let _ = state
 422 |                                 .scanner
 423 |                                 .scan_directory(
 424 |                                     PathBuf::from(dir),
 425 |                                     settings.exclude_patterns.clone(),
 426 |                                 )
 427 |                                 .await;
 428 |                         }
 429 |                         Message::IndexRebuilt
 430 |                     }),
 431 |                     Task::perform(
 432 |                         async move {
 433 |                             if let Some(r) = rx {
 434 |                                 let mut g = r.lock().await;
 435 |                                 g.recv().await
 436 |                             } else {
 437 |                                 None
 438 |                             }
 439 |                         },
 440 |                         Message::PollProgressResult,
 441 |                     ),
 442 |                 ])
 443 |             }
 444 |             Message::PollProgressResult(Some(event)) => {
 445 |                 let p = if event.total > 0 {
 446 |                     (event.processed as f32) / (event.total as f32)
 447 |                 } else {
 448 |                     0.0
 449 |                 };
 450 |                 app.rebuild_progress = Some(p);
 451 |                 app.rebuild_status = Some(event.status.clone());
 452 |                 app.files_indexed = event.processed as i32;
 453 | 
 454 |                 let rx = app.progress_rx.clone();
 455 |                 Task::perform(
 456 |                     async move {
 457 |                         if let Some(r) = rx {
 458 |                             let mut g = r.lock().await;
 459 |                             g.recv().await
 460 |                         } else {
 461 |                             None
 462 |                         }
 463 |                     },
 464 |                     Message::PollProgressResult,
 465 |                 )
 466 |             }
 467 |             Message::PollProgressResult(None) => Task::none(),
 468 |             Message::RebuildProgress(_) => Task::none(), // Fallback
 469 |             Message::IndexRebuilt => {
 470 |                 let stats = app
 471 |                     .state
 472 |                     .as_ref()
 473 |                     .map(|s| s.indexer.get_statistics().unwrap_or_default())
 474 |                     .unwrap_or_default();
 475 |                 app.files_indexed = stats.total_documents as i32;
 476 |                 app.index_size = format!("{:.1} MB", (stats.total_size_bytes as f64) / 1_048_576.0);
 477 |                 if let Some(tray) = &app.tray_icon {
 478 |                     let _ = tray.set_tooltip(Some("Flash Search - Ready"));
 479 |                 }
 480 |                 app.rebuild_progress = None;
 481 |                 app.rebuild_status = None;
 482 |                 Task::none()
 483 |             }
 484 |             Message::AddFolder => Task::perform(
 485 |                 async {
 486 |                     rfd::AsyncFileDialog::new()
 487 |                         .pick_folder()
 488 |                         .await
 489 |                         .map(|p| p.path().to_string_lossy().to_string())
 490 |                 },
 491 |                 Message::FolderPicked,
 492 |             ),
 493 |             Message::FolderPicked(Some(f)) => {
 494 |                 if !app.settings.index_dirs.iter().any(|d| d == &f) {
 495 |                     app.settings.index_dirs.push(f.clone());
 496 |                     app.save_settings();
 497 | 
 498 |                     // Automatically start scanning the new folder
 499 |                     let state = match &app.state {
 500 |                         Some(s) => s.clone(),
 501 |                         None => return Task::none(),
 502 |                     };
 503 |                     
 504 |                     app.rebuild_progress = Some(0.0);
 505 |                     app.rebuild_status = Some("Scanning new folder...".to_string());
 506 |                     
 507 |                     let exclude_patterns = app.settings.exclude_patterns.clone();
 508 |                     let rx = app.progress_rx.clone();
 509 | 
 510 |                     return Task::batch(vec![
 511 |                         Task::future(async move {
 512 |                             let _ = state
 513 |                                 .scanner
 514 |                                 .scan_directory(PathBuf::from(f), exclude_patterns)
 515 |                                 .await;
 516 |                             Message::IndexRebuilt
 517 |                         }),
 518 |                         Task::perform(
 519 |                             async move {
 520 |                                 if let Some(r) = rx {
 521 |                                     let mut g = r.lock().await;
 522 |                                     g.recv().await
 523 |                                 } else {
 524 |                                     None
 525 |                                 }
 526 |                             },
 527 |                             Message::PollProgressResult,
 528 |                         ),
 529 |                     ]);
 530 |                 }
 531 |                 Task::none()
 532 |             }
 533 |             Message::FolderPicked(None) => Task::none(),
 534 |             Message::RemoveFolder(i) => {
 535 |                 if i < app.settings.index_dirs.len() {
 536 |                     app.settings.index_dirs.remove(i);
 537 |                 }
 538 |                 Task::none()
 539 |             }
 540 |             Message::SaveSettings => {
 541 |                 app.save_settings();
 542 |                 Task::none()
 543 |             }
 544 |             Message::MaxResultsChanged(val) => {
 545 |                 if let Ok(num) = val.parse() {
 546 |                     app.settings.max_results = num;
 547 |                 }
 548 |                 Task::none()
 549 |             }
 550 |             Message::ExcludePatternsChanged(val) => {
 551 |                 app.settings.exclude_patterns =
 552 |                     val.split(',').map(|s| s.trim().to_string()).collect();
 553 |                 Task::none()
 554 |             }
 555 |             Message::ToggleTheme => {
 556 |                 app.is_dark = !app.is_dark;
 557 |                 app.settings.theme = if app.is_dark {
 558 |                     crate::settings::Theme::Dark
 559 |                 } else {
 560 |                     crate::settings::Theme::Light
 561 |                 };
 562 |                 app.save_settings();
 563 |                 Task::none()
 564 |             }
 565 |             Message::ToggleMinimizeToTray(enabled) => {
 566 |                 app.settings.minimize_to_tray = enabled;
 567 |                 app.save_settings();
 568 |                 Task::none()
 569 |             }
 570 |             Message::ToggleAutoStart(enabled) => {
 571 |                 app.settings.auto_start_on_boot = enabled;
 572 |                 app.save_settings();
 573 |                 let _ = crate::system::startup::set_auto_start(enabled);
 574 |                 Task::none()
 575 |             }
 576 |             Message::ToggleContextMenu(enabled) => {
 577 |                 app.settings.context_menu_enabled = enabled;
 578 |                 app.save_settings();
 579 |                 let _ = crate::system::context_menu::register_context_menu(enabled);
 580 |                 Task::none()
 581 |             }
 582 |             Message::ToggleSearchMode => {
 583 |                 app.search_mode = match app.search_mode {
 584 |                     SearchMode::FullText => SearchMode::Filename,
 585 |                     SearchMode::Filename => SearchMode::FullText,
 586 |                 };
 587 |                 if !app.search_query.is_empty() {
 588 |                     app.perform_search()
 589 |                 } else {
 590 |                     Task::none()
 591 |                 }
 592 |             }
 593 |             Message::FilterExtensionChanged(ext) => {
 594 |                 app.filter_extension = ext;
 595 |                 Task::none()
 596 |             }
 597 |             Message::FilterSizeChanged(size) => {
 598 |                 app.filter_size = size;
 599 |                 Task::none()
 600 |             }
 601 |             Message::PreviewRequested(idx) => {
 602 |                 app.selected_index = Some(idx);
 603 |                 app.load_preview()
 604 |             }
 605 |             Message::PreviewLoaded(content) => {
 606 |                 app.preview_content = content;
 607 |                 app.is_loading_preview = false;
 608 |                 Task::none()
 609 |             }
 610 |             Message::MoveUp => {
 611 |                 if let Some(current) = app.selected_index {
 612 |                     if current > 0 {
 613 |                         app.selected_index = Some(current - 1);
 614 |                         return app.load_preview();
 615 |                     }
 616 |                 }
 617 |                 Task::none()
 618 |             }
 619 |             Message::MoveDown => {
 620 |                 if let Some(current) = app.selected_index {
 621 |                     if !app.results.is_empty() && current < app.results.len() - 1 {
 622 |                         app.selected_index = Some(current + 1);
 623 |                         return app.load_preview();
 624 |                     }
 625 |                 }
 626 |                 Task::none()
 627 |             }
 628 |             Message::DismissError => Task::none(),
 629 |             Message::Quit => {
 630 |                 std::process::exit(0);
 631 |             }
 632 |             Message::GlobalHotkeyChanged(val) => {
 633 |                 app.settings.global_hotkey = val;
 634 |                 Task::none()
 635 |             }
 636 |             Message::StartPollingProgress => Task::none(),
 637 |             Message::PollHotkey => {
 638 |                 if let Ok(event) = global_hotkey::GlobalHotKeyEvent::receiver().try_recv() {
 639 |                     // Hotkey pressed, we just log it or toggle search window focus
 640 |                     tracing::info!("Global hotkey pressed: {:?}", event);
 641 |                     // To truly bring Iced to front, we'd need to use Iced window commands,
 642 |                     // but since iced 0.13 removed some easy window visibility toggles without IDs,
 643 |                     // we'll just focus the search query for now, or just let OS handle if hooked.
 644 |                 }
 645 |                 Task::none()
 646 |             }
 647 |             Message::PollTray => {
 648 |                 if let Ok(event) = tray_icon::menu::MenuEvent::receiver().try_recv() {
 649 |                     if event.id.0 == "quit" {
 650 |                         std::process::exit(0);
 651 |                     } else if event.id.0 == "show" {
 652 |                         // TODO: Map to actual window focus task if needed
 653 |                     }
 654 |                 }
 655 |                 Task::none()
 656 |             }
 657 |         }
 658 |     }
 659 | }
 660 | 
 661 | fn subscription(_app: &App) -> Subscription<Message> {
 662 |     let subs = vec![
 663 |         iced::time::every(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100)).map(|_| Message::PollHotkey),
 664 |         iced::time::every(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100)).map(|_| Message::PollTray),
 665 |     ];
 666 | 
 667 |     Subscription::batch(subs)
 668 | }
 669 | 
 670 | fn view(app: &App) -> Element<'_, Message> {
 671 |     match app.active_tab {
 672 |         Tab::Search => search::search_view(app),
 673 |         Tab::Settings => settings::settings_view(app),
 674 |     }
 675 | }
 676 | 
 677 | #[allow(dead_code)]
 678 | fn error_view(error: &str) -> Element<'_, Message> {
 679 |     use iced::widget::{button, column, container, text, Space};
 680 |     use iced::{Alignment, Length, Padding};
 681 | 
 682 |     container(
 683 |         column![
 684 |             text("An Error Occurred").size(24),
 685 |             Space::new().height(Length::Fixed(16.0)),
 686 |             text(error).size(14),
 687 |             Space::new().height(Length::Fixed(24.0)),
 688 |             button(text("Dismiss"))
 689 |                 .on_press(Message::DismissError)
 690 |                 .padding(Padding::new(12.0))
 691 |         ]
 692 |         .align_x(Alignment::Center),
 693 |     )
 694 |     .width(Length::Fill)
 695 |     .height(Length::Fill)
 696 |     .center_x(Length::Fill)
 697 |     .center_y(Length::Fill)
 698 |     .into()
 699 | }
 700 | 
 701 | fn app_title(_app: &App) -> String {
 702 |     String::from("Flash Search")
 703 | }
 704 | 
 705 | fn app_theme(app: &App) -> iced::Theme {
 706 |     if app.is_dark {
 707 |         iced::Theme::Dark
 708 |     } else {
 709 |         iced::Theme::Light
 710 |     }
 711 | }
 712 | 
 713 | pub fn run_ui(
 714 |     state: Result<std::sync::Arc<AppState>, String>,
 715 |     progress_rx: mpsc::Receiver<ProgressEvent>,
 716 | ) {
 717 |     let progress_mutex = Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(progress_rx));
 718 | 
 719 |     iced::application(
 720 |         move || {
 721 |             let mut app = App::new(state.clone());
 722 |             app.progress_rx = Some(progress_mutex.clone());
 723 |             (app, Task::none())
 724 |         },
 725 |         update,
 726 |         view,
 727 |     )
 728 |     .title(app_title)
 729 |     .theme(app_theme)
 730 |     .subscription(subscription)
 731 |     .window(iced::window::Settings {
 732 |         size: iced::Size::new(1000.0, 700.0),
 733 |         position: iced::window::Position::Centered,
 734 |         ..Default::default()
 735 |     })
 736 |     .run()
 737 |     .unwrap();
 738 | }
 739 | 
 740 | #[cfg(test)]
 741 | mod tests {
 742 |     use super::*;
 743 | 
 744 |     #[test]
 745 |     fn test_file_item_from_search_result() {
 746 |         let sr = SearchResult {
 747 |             file_path: "C:\\path\\to\\file.txt".to_string(),
 748 |             score: 0.95,
 749 |             title: Some("My File".to_string()),
 750 |             matched_terms: vec![],
 751 |             snippet: None,
 752 |         };
 753 |         let fi = FileItem::from(sr);
 754 |         assert_eq!(fi.title, "file.txt");
 755 |         assert_eq!(fi.path, "C:\\path\\to\\file.txt");
 756 |         assert_eq!(fi.score, 0.95);
 757 |         assert_eq!(fi.extension.as_deref(), Some("txt"));
 758 |     }
 759 | 
 760 |     #[test]
 761 |     fn test_parse_size_filter() {
 762 |         let (min, max) = App::parse_size_filter(">= 2MB");
 763 |         assert_eq!(min, Some(2 * 1024 * 1024));
 764 |         assert_eq!(max, None);
 765 | 
 766 |         let (min, max) = App::parse_size_filter("< 10KB");
 767 |         assert_eq!(min, None);
 768 |         assert_eq!(max, Some(10 * 1024 - 1));
 769 |     }
 770 | }

  16 | /// Current schema version - bump this when schema changes
  17 | pub const SCHEMA_VERSION: &str = "1.0.0";
  18 | 
  19 | fn get_schema_version_path(index_path: &Path) -> PathBuf {
  20 |     index_path.join(".schema_version")
  21 | }
  22 | 
  23 | fn read_schema_version(index_path: &Path) -> Option<String> {
  24 |     std::fs::read_to_string(get_schema_version_path(index_path))
  25 |         .ok()
  26 |         .map(|s| s.trim().to_string())
  27 | }
  28 | 
  29 | fn write_schema_version(index_path: &Path, version: &str) -> Result<()> {
  30 |     std::fs::write(get_schema_version_path(index_path), version).map_err(FlashError::Io)
  31 | }
  32 | 
  33 | /// Central manager for the Tantivy search index
  34 | pub struct IndexManager {
  35 |     #[allow(dead_code)]
  36 |     index: Index,
  37 |     writer: IndexWriterManager,
  38 |     searcher: IndexSearcher,
  39 | }
  40 | 
  41 | impl IndexManager {
  42 |     /// Open or create index at the specified path
  43 |     pub fn open(index_path: &Path, memory_limit_mb: u32) -> Result<Self> {
  44 |         let schema = create_schema();
  45 | 
  46 |         // Ensure directory exists
  47 |         if !index_path.exists() {
  48 |             std::fs::create_dir_all(index_path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
  49 |         }
  50 | 
  51 |         // Check schema version - if mismatch, rebuild index
  52 |         let stored_version = read_schema_version(index_path);
  53 |         if let Some(ref ver) = stored_version {
  54 |             if ver != SCHEMA_VERSION {
  55 |                 warn!(
  56 |                     "Schema version mismatch: stored={}, current={}. Rebuilding index...",
  57 |                     ver, SCHEMA_VERSION
  58 |                 );
  59 |                 std::fs::remove_dir_all(index_path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
  60 |                 std::fs::create_dir_all(index_path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
  61 |                 write_schema_version(index_path, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
  62 |             }
  63 |         } else if index_path.join("meta.json").exists() {
  64 |             // Old index without version - rebuild
  65 |             warn!("No schema version found. Rebuilding index...");
  66 |             std::fs::remove_dir_all(index_path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
  67 |             std::fs::create_dir_all(index_path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
  68 |             write_schema_version(index_path, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
  69 |         } else {
  70 |             // New index - write version
  71 |             write_schema_version(index_path, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
  72 |         }
  73 | 
  74 |         // Use memory-mapped directory for efficient I/O
  75 |         let directory = MmapDirectory::open(index_path)
  76 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to open index directory: {}", e)))?;
  77 | 
  78 |         let index = Index::open_or_create(directory, schema.clone())
  79 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to open or create index: {}", e)))?;
  80 | 
  81 |         info!(
  82 |             "Opened index at {} with schema version {}",
  83 |             index_path.display(),
  84 |             SCHEMA_VERSION
  85 |         );
  86 | 
  87 |         let writer = IndexWriterManager::new(&index, memory_limit_mb)?;
  88 |         let searcher = IndexSearcher::new(&index, index_path.to_path_buf())?;
  89 | 
  90 |         Ok(Self {
  91 |             index,
  92 |             writer,
  93 |             searcher,
  94 |         })
  95 |     }
  96 | 
  97 |     /// Add a document to the index
  98 |     pub fn add_document(&self, doc: &ParsedDocument, modified: u64, size: u64) -> Result<()> {
  99 |         self.writer.add_document(doc, modified, size)
 100 |     }
 101 | 
 102 |     /// Add multiple documents in a single lock acquisition (much more efficient)
 103 |     pub fn add_documents_batch(&self, docs: &[(ParsedDocument, u64, u64)]) -> Result<()> {
 104 |         self.writer.add_documents_batch(docs)
 105 |     }
 106 | 
 107 |     /// Remove a document from the index
 108 |     pub fn remove_document(&self, path: &str) -> Result<()> {
 109 |         self.writer.remove_document(path)
 110 |     }
 111 | 
 112 |     /// Clear all documents from the index
 113 |     pub fn clear(&self) -> Result<()> {
 114 |         self.writer.delete_all_documents()
 115 |     }
 116 | 
 117 |     /// Commit pending changes
 118 |     pub fn commit(&self) -> Result<()> {
 119 |         self.writer.commit()
 120 |     }
 121 | 
 122 |     /// Search the index (async with caching)
 123 |     pub async fn search(
 124 |         &self,
 125 |         query: &str,
 126 |         limit: usize,
 127 |         min_size: Option<u64>,
 128 |         max_size: Option<u64>,
 129 |         file_extensions: Option<&[String]>,
 130 |     ) -> Result<Vec<SearchResult>> {
 131 |         self.searcher
 132 |             .search(query, limit, min_size, max_size, file_extensions)
 133 |             .await
 134 |     }
 135 | 
 136 |     /// Invalidate search cache (call after index updates)
 137 |     pub fn invalidate_cache(&self) {
 138 |         self.searcher.invalidate_cache();
 139 |     }
 140 | 
 141 |     /// Get index statistics
 142 |     pub fn get_statistics(&self) -> Result<IndexStatistics> {
 143 |         self.searcher.get_statistics()
 144 |     }
 145 | }
```

  18 | 
  19 | pub fn get_app_data_dir() -> std::result::Result<PathBuf, FlashError> {
  20 |     #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
  21 |     let path = dirs::config_dir()
  22 |         .ok_or_else(|| FlashError::config("config_dir", "Could not find config directory"))?;
  23 | 
  24 |     #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
  25 |     let path = dirs::data_dir()
  26 |         .or_else(|| dirs::home_dir().map(|h| h.join(".flash-search")))
  27 |         .ok_or_else(|| FlashError::config("data_dir", "Could not find data directory"))?;
  28 | 
  29 |     let mut path = path;
  30 |     path.push("com.flashsearch");
  31 |     Ok(path)
  32 | }
  33 | 
  34 | pub fn setup_app() -> std::result::Result<
  35 |     (
  36 |         Arc<AppState>,
  37 |         tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<crate::scanner::ProgressEvent>,
  38 |     ),
  39 |     FlashError,
  40 | > {
  41 |     let app_data_dir = get_app_data_dir()?;
  42 | 
  43 |     if !app_data_dir.exists() {
  44 |         std::fs::create_dir_all(&app_data_dir)
  45 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("create_dir", e.to_string()))?;
  46 |     }
  47 | 
  48 |     info!("App data directory: {:?}", app_data_dir);
  49 | 
  50 |     let settings_manager = settings::SettingsManager::new(&app_data_dir);
  51 |     let settings = settings_manager.load().unwrap_or_default();
  52 |     let index_path = app_data_dir.join("index");
  53 |     let indexer =
  54 |         indexer::IndexManager::open(&index_path, settings.memory_limit_mb).map_err(|e| {
  55 |             FlashError::Index {
  56 |                 msg: format!("Failed to open search index: {}", e),
  57 |                 field: None,
  58 |             }
  59 |         })?;
  60 |     let db_path = app_data_dir.join("metadata.redb");
  61 |     let metadata_db = metadata::MetadataDb::open(&db_path)
  62 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("open", "metadata.redb", e.to_string()))?;
  63 | 
  64 |     let metadata_db_shared = Arc::new(metadata_db);
  65 |     let indexer_shared = Arc::new(indexer);
  66 | 
  67 |     let filename_index =
  68 |         match indexer::filename_index::FilenameIndex::open(&app_data_dir.join("filename_index")) {
  69 |             Ok(idx) => Some(Arc::new(idx)),
  70 |             Err(e) => {
  71 |                 error!("Failed to open filename index: {}", e);
  72 |                 None
  73 |             }
  74 |         };
  75 | 
  76 |     // Initialize watcher
  77 |     let watcher = watcher::WatcherManager::new(indexer_shared.clone(), metadata_db_shared.clone());
  78 | 
  79 |     let (progress_tx, progress_rx) = tokio::sync::mpsc::channel(100);
  80 | 
  81 |     let scanner = Arc::new(crate::scanner::Scanner::new(
  82 |         indexer_shared.clone(),
  83 |         metadata_db_shared.clone(),
  84 |         filename_index.clone(),
  85 |         Some(progress_tx.clone()),
  86 |         settings.clone(),
  87 |     ));
  88 | 
  89 |     let state = Arc::new(AppState::new(
  90 |         indexer_shared,
  91 |         metadata_db_shared,
  92 |         settings_manager,
  93 |         watcher,
  94 |         filename_index,
  95 |         progress_tx,
  96 |         scanner,
  97 |     ));
  98 | 
  99 |     Ok((state, progress_rx))
 100 | }
 101 | 
 102 | pub fn run_ui() -> std::result::Result<(), FlashError> {
 103 |     let result = setup_app().map(|(state, rx)| {
 104 |         iced_ui::run_ui(Ok(state), rx);
 105 |     });
 106 | 
 107 |     if let Err(e) = result {
 108 |         iced_ui::run_ui(Err(e.to_string()), tokio::sync::mpsc::channel(1).1);
 109 |     }
 110 | 
 111 |     Ok(())
 112 | }
 113 | 
 114 | pub async fn run_cli(
 115 |     query: Option<String>,
 116 |     is_json: bool,
 117 |     _index_path: Option<String>,
 118 | ) -> crate::error::Result<()> {
 119 |     if let Some(query_str) = query {
 120 |         let (state, _) = setup_app()?;
 121 |         let results = state
 122 |             .indexer
 123 |             .search(&query_str, 20, None, None, None)
 124 |             .await?;
 125 | 
 126 |         if is_json {
 127 |             let json_results: Vec<serde_json::Value> = results
 128 |                 .into_iter()
 129 |                 .map(|res| {
 130 |                     serde_json::json!({
 131 |                         "score": res.score,
 132 |                         "path": res.file_path,
 133 |                         "title": res.title
 134 |                     })
 135 |                 })
 136 |                 .collect();
 137 |             println!(
 138 |                 "{}",
 139 |                 serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json_results).unwrap_or_default()
 140 |             );
 141 |         } else {
 142 |             for res in results {
 143 |                 println!("{} | {}", res.score, res.file_path);
 144 |             }
 145 |         }
 146 |     } else {
 147 |         println!("Usage: flash-search --cli <query> [--json]");
 148 |     }
 149 |     Ok(())
 150 | }

```rust
   1 | #![cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), windows_subsystem = "windows")]
   2 | 
   3 | use mimalloc::MiMalloc;
   4 | use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};
   5 | use tracing::{error, info};
   6 | use tracing_appender::non_blocking::WorkerGuard;
   7 | use tracing_appender::rolling::{RollingFileAppender, Rotation};
   8 | use tracing_subscriber::{fmt, layer::SubscriberExt, util::SubscriberInitExt, EnvFilter};
   9 | 
  10 | #[global_allocator]
  11 | static GLOBAL: MiMalloc = MiMalloc;
  12 | 
  13 | static SHUTDOWN_FLAG: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);
  14 | 
  15 | pub fn is_shutting_down() -> bool {
  16 |     SHUTDOWN_FLAG.load(Ordering::SeqCst)
  17 | }
  18 | 
  19 | fn init_logging() -> WorkerGuard {
  20 |     let log_dir = dirs::data_local_dir()
  21 |         .unwrap_or_else(|| std::path::PathBuf::from("."))
  22 |         .join("com.flashsearch")
  23 |         .join("logs");
  24 | 
  25 |     std::fs::create_dir_all(&log_dir).ok();
  26 | 
  27 |     let file_appender = RollingFileAppender::new(Rotation::DAILY, &log_dir, "flash-search.log");
  28 | 
  29 |     let (non_blocking, guard) = tracing_appender::non_blocking(file_appender);
  30 | 
  31 |     let env_filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env().unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new("info"));
  32 | 
  33 |     tracing_subscriber::registry()
  34 |         .with(env_filter)
  35 |         .with(fmt::layer().with_writer(non_blocking).with_ansi(false))
  36 |         .with(fmt::layer().with_writer(std::io::stderr))
  37 |         .init();
  38 | 
  39 |     info!("Flash Search starting up");
  40 | 
  41 |     // Prune logs older than 30 days
  42 |     prune_old_logs(&log_dir);
  43 | 
  44 |     guard
  45 | }
  46 | 
  47 | fn prune_old_logs(log_dir: &std::path::Path) {
  48 |     if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(log_dir) {
  49 |         let now = std::time::SystemTime::now();
  50 |         let max_age = std::time::Duration::from_secs(30 * 24 * 60 * 60);
  51 | 
  52 |         for entry in entries.flatten() {
  53 |             if let Ok(metadata) = entry.metadata() {
  54 |                 if let Ok(modified) = metadata.modified() {
  55 |                     if let Ok(age) = now.duration_since(modified) {
  56 |                         if age > max_age {
  57 |                             let _ = std::fs::remove_file(entry.path());
  58 |                         }
  59 |                     }
  60 |                 }
  61 |             }
  62 |         }
  63 |     }
  64 | }
  65 | 
  66 | fn spawn_update_checker() {
  67 |     std::thread::spawn(|| {
  68 |         tracing::info!("Checking for updates...");
  69 |         // Placeholder repo config for self_update
  70 |         let result = self_update::backends::github::Update::configure()
  71 |             .repo_owner("SV-stark")
  72 |             .repo_name("FindAll")
  73 |             .bin_name("flash-search")
  74 |             .show_download_progress(true)
  75 |             .current_version(env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"))
  76 |             .build();
  77 | 
  78 |         if let Ok(updater) = result {
  79 |             match updater.update() {
  80 |                 Ok(status) => {
  81 |                     if status.updated() {
  82 |                         tracing::info!("Updated to version: {}", status.version());
  83 |                     } else {
  84 |                         tracing::info!("Flash Search is up to date.");
  85 |                     }
  86 |                 }
  87 |                 Err(e) => tracing::warn!("Update check failed: {}", e),
  88 |             }
  89 |         }
  90 |     });
  91 | }
  92 | 
  93 | fn main() {
  94 |     let app_dir = dirs::data_local_dir()
  95 |         .unwrap_or_else(|| std::path::PathBuf::from("."))
  96 |         .join("com.flashsearch");
  97 |     std::fs::create_dir_all(&app_dir).ok();
  98 | 
  99 |     let lock_path = app_dir.join("flashsearch.lock");
 100 |     let lock_file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()
 101 |         .write(true)
 102 |         .create(true)
 103 |         .truncate(true)
 104 |         .open(&lock_path);
 105 | 
 106 |     if let Ok(file) = lock_file {
 107 |         use fs2::FileExt;
 108 |         if file.try_lock_exclusive().is_err() {
 109 |             eprintln!("Flash Search is already running.");
 110 |             std::process::exit(1);
 111 |         }
 112 |         // Leak the file handle so the lock is held for the lifetime of the process
 113 |         std::mem::forget(file);
 114 |     }
 115 | 
 116 |     let _guard = init_logging();
 117 | 
 118 |     spawn_update_checker();
 119 | 
 120 |     let args: Vec<String> = std::env::args().collect();
 121 | 
 122 |     if args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--version" || arg == "-V") {
 123 |         println!("flash-search {}", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"));
 124 |         return;
 125 |     }
 126 | 
 127 |     // We must create a tokio runtime for background tasks (like Watcher), but we CANNOT
 128 |     // use #[tokio::main] because Iced uses winit to hijack the main thread and blocks it.
 129 |     let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()
 130 |         .enable_all()
 131 |         .build()
 132 |         .unwrap();
 133 |     let _rt_guard = rt.enter();
 134 | 
 135 |     if args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--help" || arg == "-h") {
 136 |         println!("Flash Search - Ultrafast local full-text search\n");
 137 |         println!("Usage: flash-search [OPTIONS]");
 138 |         println!("Options:");
 139 |         println!("  -V, --version       Print version");
 140 |         println!("  -h, --help          Print help");
 141 |         println!("  -c, --cli <query>   Perform a search from the command line");
 142 |         println!("  --json              Output CLI search results as JSON");
 143 |         return;
 144 |     }
 145 | 
 146 |     if args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--cli" || arg == "-c") {
 147 |         let query_index = args.iter().position(|a| a == "--cli" || a == "-c").unwrap();
 148 |         let query = args.get(query_index + 1).cloned();
 149 |         let is_json = args.iter().any(|a| a == "--json");
 150 | 
 151 |         rt.block_on(async {
 152 |             if let Err(e) = flash_search::run_cli(query, is_json, None).await {
 153 |                 error!("CLI Error: {}", e);
 154 |             }
 155 |         });
 156 |         return;
 157 |     }
 158 | 
 159 |     ctrlc::set_handler(|| {
 160 |         info!("Shutdown signal received, committing index...");
 161 |         SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
 162 |     })
 163 |     .expect("Error setting Ctrl-C handler");
 164 | 
 165 |     // Iced requires running on the main thread and runs its own executor
 166 |     match flash_search::run_ui() {
 167 |         Ok(_) => {}
 168 |         Err(e) => {
 169 |             error!("Failed to start application: {}", e);
 170 |             std::process::exit(1);
 171 |         }
 172 |     }
 173 | }
```

 139 | static OTHER_EXTENSIONS: phf::Map<&'static str, ParserType> = phf_map! {
 140 |     "epub" => ParserType::Epub,
 141 |     "pdf" => ParserType::Pdf,
 142 |     "xlsx" => ParserType::Excel,
 143 |     "xls" => ParserType::Excel,
 144 |     "xlsb" => ParserType::Excel,
 145 |     "rtf" => ParserType::Rtf,
 146 |     "eml" => ParserType::Eml,
 147 |     "msg" => ParserType::Msg,
 148 |     "chm" => ParserType::Chm,
 149 |     "azw" => ParserType::Azw,
 150 |     "azw3" => ParserType::Azw,
 151 |     "mobi" => ParserType::Azw,
 152 |     "zip" => ParserType::Zip,
 153 |     "7z" => ParserType::SevenZ,
 154 |     "rar" => ParserType::Rar,
 155 |     "legacy_office" => ParserType::LegacyOffice,
 156 | };
 157 | 
 158 | #[derive(Clone, Copy)]
 159 | enum ParserType {
 160 |     Docx,
 161 |     Pptx,
 162 |     Odf,
 163 |     Epub,
 164 |     Pdf,
 165 |     Excel,
 166 |     Rtf,
 167 |     Eml,
 168 |     Msg,
 169 |     Chm,
 170 |     Azw,
 171 |     Zip,
 172 |     SevenZ,
 173 |     Rar,
 174 |     LegacyOffice,
 175 |     Text,
 176 | }
 177 | 
 178 | impl ParserType {
 179 |     fn from_extension(ext: &str) -> Option<Self> {
 180 |         let ext_lower = ext.to_lowercase();
 181 |         if DOCX_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
 182 |             Some(ParserType::Docx)
 183 |         } else if PPTX_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
 184 |             Some(ParserType::Pptx)
 185 |         } else if ODF_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
 186 |             Some(ParserType::Odf)
 187 |         } else if TEXT_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
 188 |             Some(ParserType::Text)
 189 |         } else if LEGACY_OFFICE_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
 190 |             Some(ParserType::LegacyOffice)
 191 |         } else {
 192 |             OTHER_EXTENSIONS.get(&ext_lower).copied()
 193 |         }
 194 |     }
 195 | }
 196 | 
 197 | /// Parse file without allocating - uses byte comparison
 198 | #[inline]
 199 | #[allow(dead_code)]
 200 | fn extension_matches(ext: &OsStr, target: &str) -> bool {
 201 |     if let Some(ext_bytes) = ext.to_str().map(|s| s.as_bytes()) {
 202 |         if ext_bytes.len() != target.len() {
 203 |             return false;
 204 |         }
 205 |         ext_bytes
 206 |             .iter()
 207 |             .zip(target.bytes())
 208 |             .all(|(a, b)| a.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&b))
 209 |     } else {
 210 |         false
 211 |     }
 212 | }
 213 | 
 214 | /// Detect file type and route to appropriate parser
 215 | /// Uses phf for O(1) static lookup
 216 | pub fn parse_file(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
 217 |     let extension = path.extension().unwrap_or_default();
 218 | 
 219 |     if let Some(ext_str) = extension.to_str() {
 220 |         let ext_lower = ext_str.to_lowercase();
 221 |         match ParserType::from_extension(&ext_lower) {
 222 |             Some(ParserType::Docx) => return docx::parse_docx(path),
 223 |             Some(ParserType::Pptx) => return pptx::parse_pptx(path),
 224 |             Some(ParserType::Odf) => return odf::parse_odf(path),
 225 |             Some(ParserType::Epub) => return epub::parse_epub(path),
 226 |             Some(ParserType::Pdf) => return pdf::parse_pdf(path),
 227 |             Some(ParserType::Excel) => return excel::parse_excel(path),
 228 |             Some(ParserType::Rtf) => return extended::parse_rtf(path),
 229 |             Some(ParserType::Eml) => return extended::parse_eml(path),
 230 |             Some(ParserType::Msg) => return extended::parse_msg(path),
 231 |             Some(ParserType::Chm) => return extended::parse_chm(path),
 232 |             Some(ParserType::Azw) => return extended::parse_azw(path),
 233 |             Some(ParserType::Zip) => return extended::parse_zip_content(path),
 234 |             Some(ParserType::SevenZ) => return extended::parse_7z_content(path),
 235 |             Some(ParserType::Rar) => return extended::parse_rar_content(path),
 236 |             Some(ParserType::Text) => return text::parse_text(path),
 237 |             Some(ParserType::LegacyOffice) => return extended::parse_legacy_office(path),
 238 |             None => {}
 239 |         }
 240 |     }
 241 | 
 242 |     let ext_str = extension.to_string_lossy().to_string();
 243 |     Err(FlashError::unsupported_format(ext_str.clone(), ext_str))
 244 | }
 245 | 
 246 | #[cfg(test)]
 247 | mod tests {
 248 |     use super::*;
 249 | 
 250 |     #[test]
 251 |     fn test_extension_matches() {
 252 |         assert!(extension_matches(OsStr::new("docx"), "docx"));
 253 |         assert!(extension_matches(OsStr::new("DOCX"), "docx"));
 254 |         assert!(extension_matches(OsStr::new("Docx"), "docx"));
 255 |         assert!(!extension_matches(OsStr::new("pdf"), "docx"));
 256 |     }
 257 | 
 258 |     #[test]
 259 |     fn test_parser_type_from_extension() {
 260 |         assert!(matches!(
 261 |             ParserType::from_extension("txt"),
 262 |             Some(ParserType::Text)
 263 |         ));
 264 |         assert!(matches!(
 265 |             ParserType::from_extension("TXT"),
 266 |             Some(ParserType::Text)
 267 |         ));
 268 |         assert!(matches!(
 269 |             ParserType::from_extension("rs"),
 270 |             Some(ParserType::Text)
 271 |         ));
 272 |         assert!(matches!(
 273 |             ParserType::from_extension("js"),
 274 |             Some(ParserType::Text)
 275 |         ));
 276 |         assert!(matches!(
 277 |             ParserType::from_extension("docx"),
 278 |             Some(ParserType::Docx)
 279 |         ));
 280 |         assert!(ParserType::from_extension("exe").is_none());
 281 |     }
 282 | 
 283 |     #[test]
 284 |     fn test_parse_file_txt() {
 285 |         use std::io::Write;
 286 |         let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
 287 |         let file_path = dir.path().join("test.txt");
 288 |         let mut file = std::fs::File::create(&file_path).unwrap();
 289 |         writeln!(file, "Hello, world!").unwrap();
 290 | 
 291 |         let result = parse_file(&file_path);
 292 |         assert!(result.is_ok());
 293 |         let doc = result.unwrap();
 294 |         assert!(doc.content.contains("Hello, world!"));
 295 |     }
 296 | 
 297 |     #[test]
 298 |     fn test_parse_file_unknown() {
 299 |         let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
 300 |         let file_path = dir.path().join("test.unknown");
 301 |         std::fs::File::create(&file_path).unwrap();
 302 | 
 303 |         let result = parse_file(&file_path);
 304 |         assert!(result.is_err());
 305 |     }
 306 | }
```

  15 | 
  16 | #[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize)]
  17 | pub enum ProgressType {
  18 |     Content,
  19 |     Filename,
  20 | }
  21 | 
  22 | #[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize)]
  23 | pub struct ProgressEvent {
  24 |     pub total: usize,
  25 |     pub processed: usize,
  26 |     pub current_file: String,
  27 |     pub status: String,
  28 |     pub ptype: ProgressType,
