```rust
  30 |     pub eta_seconds: u64,
  31 |     pub current_folder: String,
  32 | }
  33 | 
  34 | const BATCH_SIZE: usize = 50;
  35 | 
  36 | #[derive(Debug)]
  37 | struct IndexTask {
  38 |     doc: ParsedDocument,
  39 |     modified: u64,
  40 |     size: u64,
  41 |     content_hash: [u8; 32],
  42 | }
  43 | 
  44 | pub struct Scanner {
  45 |     indexer: Arc<IndexManager>,
  46 |     metadata_db: Arc<MetadataDb>,
  47 |     filename_index: Option<Arc<crate::indexer::filename_index::FilenameIndex>>,
  48 |     progress_tx: Option<mpsc::Sender<ProgressEvent>>,
  49 |     settings: crate::settings::AppSettings,
  50 | }
  51 | 
  52 | impl Scanner {
  53 |     pub fn new(
  54 |         indexer: Arc<IndexManager>,
  55 |         metadata_db: Arc<MetadataDb>,
  56 |         filename_index: Option<Arc<crate::indexer::filename_index::FilenameIndex>>,
  57 |         progress_tx: Option<mpsc::Sender<ProgressEvent>>,
  58 |         settings: crate::settings::AppSettings,
  59 |     ) -> Self {
  60 |         Self {
  61 |             indexer,
  62 |             metadata_db,
  63 |             filename_index,
  64 |             progress_tx,
  65 |             settings,
  66 |         }
  67 |     }
  68 | 
  69 |     #[instrument(skip(self, exclude_patterns), fields(root = %root.display()))]
  70 |     pub async fn scan_directory(&self, root: PathBuf, exclude_patterns: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {
  71 |         info!("Starting directory scan for {}", root.display());
  72 | 
  73 |         // P3/P4: Run blocking WalkBuilder in a separate thread to avoid blocking Tokio runtime
  74 |         // and allow pipelined consumption.
  75 |         let (path_tx, path_rx): (
  76 |             std::sync::mpsc::Sender<PathBuf>,
  77 |             std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<PathBuf>,
  78 |         ) = std::sync::mpsc::channel();
  79 |         let total = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
  80 |         let root_clone = root.clone();
  81 |         let tx_clone = self.progress_tx.clone();
  82 |         let total_clone = total.clone();
  83 | 
  84 |         let walker_handle = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
  85 |             let mut builder = WalkBuilder::new(&root_clone);
  86 |             // ... (keep logic same) ...
  87 | 
  88 |             // Add overrides
  89 |             let mut override_builder = ignore::overrides::OverrideBuilder::new(&root_clone);
  90 |             for pattern in &exclude_patterns {
  91 |                 let ignore_pattern = format!("!{}", pattern);
  92 |                 if let Err(e) = override_builder.add(&ignore_pattern) {
  93 |                     warn!("Invalid exclude pattern '{}': {}", pattern, e);
  94 |                 }
  95 |             }
  96 |             if let Ok(overrides) = override_builder.build() {
  97 |                 builder.overrides(overrides);
  98 |             }
  99 | 
 100 |             builder.follow_links(true).standard_filters(false);
 101 | 
 102 |             // --- Stage 1: Filename Indexing ---
 103 |             info!("Stage 1: Filename Indexing");
 104 |             let builder = builder.build_parallel();
 105 |             builder.run(|| {
 106 |                 let path_tx = path_tx.clone();
 107 |                 let tx = tx_clone.clone();
 108 |                 let total = total_clone.clone();
 109 |                 Box::new(move |entry| {
 110 |                     if let Ok(entry) = entry {
 111 |                         if entry.file_type().map(|ft| ft.is_file()).unwrap_or(false) {
 112 |                             let path = entry.path().to_path_buf();
 113 |                             let _ = path_tx.send(path);
 114 |                             let count = total.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
 115 | 
 116 |                             // Send progress update periodically
 117 |                             if count % 100 == 0 {
 118 |                                 if let Some(tx) = &tx {
 119 |                                     let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
 120 |                                         ptype: ProgressType::Filename,
 121 |                                         current_file: entry
 122 |                                             .file_name()
 123 |                                             .to_string_lossy()
 124 |                                             .to_string(),
 125 |                                         current_folder: "".to_string(),
 126 |                                         processed: count,
 127 |                                         total: 0, // Unknown
 128 |                                         status: "Scanning filenames...".to_string(),
 129 |                                         eta_seconds: 0,
 130 |                                         files_per_second: 0.0,
 131 |                                     });
 132 |                                 }
 133 |                             }
 134 |                         }
 135 |                     }
 136 |                     ignore::WalkState::Continue
 137 |                 })
 138 |             });
 139 | 
 140 |             // Final update for stage 1
 141 |             let final_count = total_clone.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
 142 |             if let Some(tx) = &tx_clone {
 143 |                 let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
 144 |                     ptype: ProgressType::Filename,
 145 |                     current_file: "".to_string(),
 146 |                     current_folder: "".to_string(),
 147 |                     processed: final_count,
 148 |                     total: final_count,
 149 |                     status: "Filename scan complete".to_string(),
 150 |                     eta_seconds: 0,
 151 |                     files_per_second: 0.0,
 152 |                 });
 153 |             }
 154 |         });
 155 | 
 156 |         // --- Stage 2: Content Indexing (Batched) ---
 157 |         // Parse files in parallel via Rayon, collect results via mpsc,
 158 |         // then batch-write to the index and metadata DB.
 159 |         let (task_tx, task_rx) = std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel::<IndexTask>(BATCH_SIZE * 8);
 160 |         let metadata_db_for_parser = self.metadata_db.clone();
 161 |         let metadata_db_for_writer = self.metadata_db.clone();
 162 |         let indexer_clone = self.indexer.clone();
 163 |         let filename_index_clone = self.filename_index.clone();
 164 |         let progress_tx_clone = self.progress_tx.clone();
 165 |         let total_files = total.clone();
 166 | 
 167 |         // Configure Rayon thread pool based on settings limit
 168 |         let threads = self.settings.indexing_threads as usize;
 169 |         let pool = rayon::ThreadPoolBuilder::new()
 170 |             .num_threads(threads)
 171 |             .build()
 172 |             .unwrap_or_else(|_| rayon::ThreadPoolBuilder::new().build().unwrap());
 173 |         let file_size_limit_mb = self.settings.index_file_size_limit_mb;
 174 | 
 175 |         // --- Stage 2a: Parallel parsing (Rayon) → sends IndexTask into channel ---
 176 |         let parser_handle = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
 177 |             info!("Stage 2a: Parallel Parsing ({} threads)", threads);
 178 | 
 179 |             pool.install(|| {
 180 |                 let mut chunk = Vec::with_capacity(200);
 181 |                 for path in path_rx.into_iter() {
 182 |                     chunk.push(path);
 183 |                     if chunk.len() >= 200 {
 184 |                         Scanner::process_chunk(
 185 |                             &chunk,
 186 |                             &metadata_db_for_parser,
 187 |                             file_size_limit_mb,
 188 |                             &task_tx,
 189 |                         );
 190 |                         chunk.clear();
 191 |                     }
 192 |                 }
 193 |                 if !chunk.is_empty() {
 194 |                     Scanner::process_chunk(
 195 |                         &chunk,
 196 |                         &metadata_db_for_parser,
 197 |                         file_size_limit_mb,
 198 |                         &task_tx,
 199 |                     );
 200 |                 }
 201 |             });
 202 |             // task_tx is dropped here, closing the channel
 203 |         });
 204 | 
 205 |         // --- Stage 2b: Sequential batch writer (single thread) ---
 206 |         let writer_handle = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
 207 |             info!("Stage 2b: Batch Writing");
 208 |             let start = Instant::now();
 209 |             let mut doc_batch: Vec<(crate::parsers::ParsedDocument, u64, u64)> =
 210 |                 Vec::with_capacity(BATCH_SIZE);
 211 |             let mut meta_batch: Vec<(String, u64, u64, [u8; 32])> = Vec::with_capacity(BATCH_SIZE);
 212 |             let mut processed: usize = 0;
 213 | 
 214 |             for task in task_rx.iter() {
 215 |                 // Add to filename index
 216 |                 if let Some(f_index) = &filename_index_clone {
 217 |                     let path = std::path::Path::new(&task.doc.path);
 218 |                     if let Some(name) = path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {
 219 |                         let _ = f_index.add_file(&task.doc.path, name);
 220 |                     }
 221 |                 }
 222 | 
 223 |                 // Clone path before moving doc
 224 |                 let doc_path = task.doc.path.clone();
 225 |                 doc_batch.push((task.doc, task.modified, task.size));
 226 |                 meta_batch.push((doc_path, task.modified, task.size, task.content_hash));
 227 |                 processed += 1;
 228 | 
 229 |                 // Flush batch when full
 230 |                 if doc_batch.len() >= BATCH_SIZE {
 231 |                     let _ = indexer_clone.add_documents_batch(&doc_batch);
 232 |                     let _ = indexer_clone.commit();
 233 |                     indexer_clone.invalidate_cache();
 234 |                     let _ = metadata_db_for_writer.batch_update_metadata(&meta_batch);
 235 |                     doc_batch.clear();
 236 |                     meta_batch.clear();
 237 |                 }
 238 | 
 239 |                 // Progress update
 240 |                 if processed % 10 == 0 {
 241 |                     let current_total = total_files.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
 242 |                     let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
 243 |                     let rate = if elapsed > 0.0 {
 244 |                         processed as f64 / elapsed
 245 |                     } else {
 246 |                         0.0
 247 |                     };
 248 |                     if let Some(tx) = &progress_tx_clone {
 249 |                         let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
 250 |                             ptype: ProgressType::Content,
 251 |                             current_file: "".to_string(),
 252 |                             current_folder: "".to_string(),
 253 |                             processed,
 254 |                             total: current_total,
 255 |                             status: "Indexing contents...".to_string(),
 256 |                             eta_seconds: if rate > 0.0 {
 257 |                                 (current_total.saturating_sub(processed) as f64 / rate) as u64
 258 |                             } else {
 259 |                                 0
 260 |                             },
 261 |                             files_per_second: rate,
 262 |                         });
 263 |                     }
 264 |                 }
 265 |             }
 266 | 
 267 |             // Flush remaining items (B1: always commit at end)
 268 |             if !doc_batch.is_empty() {
 269 |                 let _ = indexer_clone.add_documents_batch(&doc_batch);
 270 |                 let _ = indexer_clone.commit();
 271 |                 indexer_clone.invalidate_cache();
 272 |                 let _ = metadata_db_for_writer.batch_update_metadata(&meta_batch);
 273 |             }
 274 | 
 275 |             // Final progress
 276 |             if let Some(tx) = &progress_tx_clone {
 277 |                 let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
 278 |                     ptype: ProgressType::Content,
 279 |                     current_file: "".to_string(),
 280 |                     current_folder: "".to_string(),
 281 |                     processed,
 282 |                     total: processed,
 283 |                     status: "All files indexed".to_string(),
 284 |                     eta_seconds: 0,
 285 |                     files_per_second: 0.0,
 286 |                 });
 287 |             }
 288 | 
 289 |             info!(
 290 |                 "Indexed {} files in {:.2}s",
 291 |                 processed,
 292 |                 start.elapsed().as_secs_f64()
 293 |             );
 294 |         });
 295 | 
 296 |         // Wait for all stages to complete
 297 |         walker_handle
 298 |             .await
 299 |             .map_err(|e| crate::error::FlashError::index(format!("Walk task failed: {}", e)))?;
 300 |         parser_handle
 301 |             .await
 302 |             .map_err(|e| crate::error::FlashError::index(format!("Parse task failed: {}", e)))?;
 303 |         writer_handle
 304 |             .await
 305 |             .map_err(|e| crate::error::FlashError::index(format!("Write task failed: {}", e)))?;
 306 | 
 307 |         // Commit filename index to disk
 308 |         if let Some(f_index) = &self.filename_index {
 309 |             let _ = f_index.commit();
 310 |         }
 311 | 
 312 |         Ok(())
 313 |     }
 314 | 
 315 |     fn process_chunk(
 316 |         chunk: &[PathBuf],
 317 |         metadata_db: &Arc<MetadataDb>,
 318 |         file_size_limit_mb: u32,
 319 |         task_tx: &std::sync::mpsc::SyncSender<IndexTask>,
 320 |     ) {
 321 |         let limit_bytes = (file_size_limit_mb as u64) * 1024 * 1024;
 322 |         let mut batch_entries = Vec::with_capacity(chunk.len());
 323 |         let mut valid_paths = Vec::with_capacity(chunk.len());
 324 | 
 325 |         for path in chunk {
 326 |             let metadata = match std::fs::metadata(path) {
 327 |                 Ok(m) => m,
 328 |                 Err(_) => continue,
 329 |             };
 330 | 
 331 |             let size = metadata.len();
 332 |             if size > limit_bytes {
 333 |                 warn!(
 334 |                     "Skipping large file: {} ({} bytes > limit of {} bytes)",
 335 |                     path.display(),
 336 |                     size,
 337 |                     limit_bytes
 338 |                 );
 339 |                 continue;
 340 |             }
 341 | 
 342 |             let modified = metadata
 343 |                 .modified()
 344 |                 .unwrap_or(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
 345 |                 .duration_since(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
 346 |                 .unwrap_or_default()
 347 |                 .as_secs();
 348 | 
 349 |             batch_entries.push((path.to_string_lossy().to_string(), modified, size));
 350 |             valid_paths.push((path.clone(), modified, size));
 351 |         }
 352 | 
 353 |         if batch_entries.is_empty() {
 354 |             return;
 355 |         }
 356 | 
 357 |         let needs_reindex = metadata_db
 358 |             .batch_needs_reindex(&batch_entries)
 359 |             .unwrap_or_else(|_| vec![true; batch_entries.len()]);
 360 | 
 361 |         let paths_to_parse: Vec<_> = valid_paths
 362 |             .into_iter()
 363 |             .enumerate()
 364 |             .filter(|(i, _)| needs_reindex[*i])
 365 |             .map(|(_, data)| data)
 366 |             .collect();
 367 | 
 368 |         paths_to_parse
 369 |             .into_par_iter()
 370 |             .for_each(|(path, modified, size)| {
 371 |                 if let Ok(parsed) = parse_file(&path) {
 372 |                     let content_hash = blake3::hash(parsed.content.as_bytes()).into();
 373 |                     let _ = task_tx.send(IndexTask {
 374 |                         doc: parsed,
 375 |                         modified,
 376 |                         size,
 377 |                         content_hash,
 378 |                     });
 379 |                 } else {
 380 |                     warn!("Failed to parse file {:?}", path);
 381 |                 }
 382 |             });
 383 |     }
 384 | }
 385 | 
 386 | #[cfg(test)]
 387 | mod tests {
 388 |     use super::*;
 389 |     use crate::settings::AppSettings;
 390 |     use crate::indexer::IndexManager;
 391 |     use crate::metadata::MetadataDb;
 392 |     use std::sync::Arc;
 393 |     use tempfile::tempdir;
 394 | 
 395 |     #[test]
 396 |     fn test_scanner_new() {
 397 |         let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
 398 |         let index_path = dir.path().join("index");
 399 |         let db_path = dir.path().join("metadata.redb");
 400 |         
 401 |         let settings = AppSettings::default();
 402 |         let indexer = Arc::new(IndexManager::open(&index_path, 100).unwrap());
 403 |         let metadata_db = Arc::new(MetadataDb::open(&db_path).unwrap());
 404 |         
 405 |         let scanner = Scanner::new(
 406 |             indexer,
 407 |             metadata_db,
 408 |             None,
 409 |             None,
 410 |             settings,
 411 |         );
 412 |         
 413 |         assert!(scanner.filename_index.is_none());
 414 |     }
 415 | 
 416 |     #[test]
 417 |     fn test_progress_event_serialization() {
 418 |         let event = ProgressEvent {
 419 |             total: 100,
 420 |             processed: 50,
 421 |             current_file: "test.txt".to_string(),
 422 |             status: "Indexing...".to_string(),
 423 |             ptype: ProgressType::Content,
 424 |             files_per_second: 10.5,
 425 |             eta_seconds: 5,
 426 |             current_folder: "/home/user".to_string(),
 427 |         };
 428 |         let json = serde_json::to_string(&event).unwrap();
 429 |         assert!(json.contains("test.txt"));
 430 |         assert!(json.contains("Content"));
 431 |     }
 432 | }
 433 |
```

```python
   1 | import os
   2 | import subprocess
   3 | 
   4 | def find_iced_signature():
   5 |     cargo_home = os.environ.get('CARGO_HOME', os.path.expanduser('~/.cargo'))
   6 |     registry_src = os.path.join(cargo_home, 'registry', 'src')
   7 |     
   8 |     for root, dirs, files in os.walk(registry_src):
   9 |         if 'iced-0.14' in root or 'iced-0.13' in root:
  10 |             if 'application.rs' in files:
  11 |                 filepath = os.path.join(root, 'application.rs')
  12 |                 with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
  13 |                     content = f.read()
  14 |                     print(f"Found {filepath}")
  15 |                     lines = content.split('\n')
  16 |                     for i, l in enumerate(lines):
  17 |                         if 'pub fn application<' in l or 'pub fn application(' in l:
  18 |                             print("\n".join(lines[i-2:i+15]))
  19 |                             return
  20 |     print("Could not find iced application source.")
  21 | 
  22 | find_iced_signature()
```

```python
   1 | import os
   2 | import subprocess
   3 | 
   4 | def find_iced_signature():
   5 |     # Use rustc to output the docs or we can just search local crates cache
   6 |     registry_path = os.path.expanduser('~/.cargo/registry/src')
   7 |     # Windows cargo registry
   8 |     cargo_home = os.environ.get('CARGO_HOME', os.path.expanduser('~/.cargo'))
   9 |     registry_src = os.path.join(cargo_home, 'registry', 'src')
  10 |     
  11 |     for root, dirs, files in os.walk(registry_src):
  12 |         if 'iced-0.14' in root or 'iced-0.13' in root:
  13 |             if 'application.rs' in files:
  14 |                 filepath = os.path.join(root, 'application.rs')
  15 |                 with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
  16 |                     content = f.read()
  17 |                     print(f"Found {filepath}")
  18 |                     lines = content.split('\n')
  19 |                     for i, l in enumerate(lines):
  20 |                         if 'pub fn application' in l:
  21 |                             print("\n".join(lines[i:i+10]))
  22 |                             return
  23 |     print("Could not find iced application source.")
  24 | 
  25 | find_iced_signature()
```

### File: `reorder.py`

- Size: 1672 bytes
- Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134171135530775359 }

```python
   1 | import sys
   2 | import re
   3 | 
   4 | def main():
   5 |     if len(sys.argv) < 2:
   6 |         return
   7 |     
   8 |     filepath = sys.argv[1]
   9 |     with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
  10 |         content = f.read()
  11 | 
  12 |     parts = content.split('## File Contents\n\n')
  13 |     if len(parts) < 2:
  14 |         print("Could not find '## File Contents'")
  15 |         with open('context.md', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
  16 |             f.write(content)
  17 |         return
  18 | 
  19 |     header = parts[0] + '## File Contents\n\n'
  20 |     body = parts[1]
  21 | 
  22 |     blocks = re.split(r'(### File: `[^`]+`)', body)
  23 | 
  24 |     if len(blocks) < 3:
  25 |         with open('context.md', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
  26 |             f.write(content)
  27 |         return
  28 | 
  29 |     parsed_blocks = []
  30 |     for i in range(1, len(blocks), 2):
  31 |         heading = blocks[i]
  32 |         content_block = blocks[i+1]
  33 |         
  34 |         m = re.search(r'### File: `([^`]+)`', heading)
  35 |         fname = m.group(1).replace('\\', '/') if m else ""
  36 |         
  37 |         parsed_blocks.append((fname, heading + content_block))
  38 | 
  39 |     def get_priority(fname):
  40 |         if fname == 'src/lib.rs': return 0
  41 |         if fname == 'src/main.rs': return 1
  42 |         if fname == 'Cargo.toml': return 2
  43 |         if fname.startswith('tests/'): return 3
  44 |         if fname.startswith('benches/'): return 4
  45 |         if fname.startswith('examples/'): return 5
  46 |         return 6
  47 | 
  48 |     parsed_blocks.sort(key=lambda x: (get_priority(x[0]), x[0]))
  49 | 
  50 |     final_content = header + "".join([b[1] for b in parsed_blocks])
  51 |     with open('context.md', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
  52 |         f.write(final_content)
  53 |     print("Reorganized successfully")
  54 | 
  55 | if __name__ == "__main__":
  56 |     main()
```

```rust
   1 | #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
   2 | pub fn set_auto_start(enable: bool, app_name: &str, exe_path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
   3 |     use winreg::enums::{HKEY_CURRENT_USER, KEY_ALL_ACCESS};
   4 |     use winreg::RegKey;
   5 | 
   6 |     let hkcu = RegKey::predef(HKEY_CURRENT_USER);
   7 |     let run_key = hkcu
   8 |         .open_subkey_with_flags(
   9 |             r"Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run",
  10 |             KEY_ALL_ACCESS,
  11 |         )
  12 |         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  13 | 
  14 |     if enable {
  15 |         run_key
  16 |             .set_value(app_name, &exe_path)
  17 |             .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  18 |     } else {
  19 |         let _ = run_key.delete_value(app_name);
  20 |     }
  21 |     Ok(())
  22 | }
  23 | 
  24 | #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
  25 | pub fn is_auto_start_enabled(app_name: &str) -> Result<bool, String> {
  26 |     use winreg::enums::HKEY_CURRENT_USER;
  27 |     use winreg::RegKey;
  28 | 
  29 |     let hkcu = RegKey::predef(HKEY_CURRENT_USER);
  30 |     let run_key = hkcu
  31 |         .open_subkey(r"Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run")
  32 |         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  33 | 
  34 |     match run_key.get_value::<String, _>(app_name) {
  35 |         Ok(_) => Ok(true),
  36 |         Err(_) => Ok(false),
  37 |     }
  38 | }
  39 | 
  40 | #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
  41 | pub fn set_auto_start(_enable: bool, _app_name: &str, _exe_path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
  42 |     Err("Auto-start not implemented for this platform".to_string())
  43 | }
  44 | 
  45 | #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
  46 | pub fn is_auto_start_enabled(_app_name: &str) -> Result<bool, String> {
  47 |     Ok(false)
  48 | }
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::indexer::searcher::SearchResult;
   2 | use std::fs::File;
   3 | use std::io::Write;
   4 | 
   5 | pub fn export_results_csv(results: &[SearchResult], path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
   6 |     let mut file = File::create(path).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
   7 |     writeln!(file, "Score,File Path,Title").map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
   8 |     for r in results {
   9 |         let title = r.title.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
  10 |         writeln!(file, "{},{},{}", r.score, r.file_path, title).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  11 |     }
  12 |     Ok(())
  13 | }
  14 | 
  15 | pub fn export_results_json(results: &[SearchResult], path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
  16 |     let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(results).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  17 |     std::fs::write(path, json).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  18 |     Ok(())
  19 | }
```

```rust
  26 |             state.filename_index.clone(),
  27 |             Some(progress_tx),
  28 |             settings,

  30 |         if let Err(e) = scanner.scan_directory(path, exclude_patterns).await {

  36 | }
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::commands::AppState;

   5 | use std::sync::Arc;
   6 | 
   7 | pub async fn search_query_internal(
   8 |     query: String,
   9 |     limit: usize,
  10 |     state: &Arc<AppState>,
  11 |     min_size: Option<u64>,
  12 |     max_size: Option<u64>,
  13 |     file_extensions: Option<Vec<String>>,
  14 | ) -> Result<Vec<SearchResult>, String> {
  15 |     state
  16 |         .indexer
  17 |         .search(
  18 |             &query,
  19 |             limit,
  20 |             min_size,
  21 |             max_size,
  22 |             file_extensions.as_deref(),
  23 |         )
  24 |         .await
  25 |         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())
  26 | }
  27 | 
  28 | pub async fn get_file_preview_internal(path: String) -> Result<String, String> {
  29 |     let path_buf = std::path::PathBuf::from(path);
  30 |     let result = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || parse_file(&path_buf))
  31 |         .await
  32 |         .map_err(|e| format!("Preview task failed: {}", e))?;
  33 | 
  34 |     match result {
  35 |         Ok(doc) => Ok(doc.content[..std::cmp::min(doc.content.len(), 10000)].to_string()),
  36 |         Err(e) => Err(e.to_string()),
  37 |     }
  38 | }
  39 | 
  40 | pub async fn get_file_preview_highlighted_internal(
  41 |     path: String,
  42 |     query: String,
  43 | ) -> Result<PreviewResult, String> {
  44 |     use crate::indexer::query_parser::extract_highlight_terms;
  45 |     let path_buf = std::path::PathBuf::from(path);
  46 |     let matched_terms = extract_highlight_terms(&query);
  47 |     let result = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || parse_file(&path_buf))
  48 |         .await
  49 |         .map_err(|e| format!("Preview task failed: {}", e))?;
  50 | 
  51 |     match result {
  52 |         Ok(doc) => Ok(PreviewResult {
  53 |             content: doc.content[..std::cmp::min(doc.content.len(), 10000)].to_string(),
  54 |             matched_terms,
  55 |         }),
  56 |         Err(e) => Err(e.to_string()),
  57 |     }
  58 | }
  59 | 
  60 | pub async fn search_filenames_internal(
  61 |     query: String,
  62 |     limit: usize,
  63 |     state: &Arc<AppState>,
  64 | ) -> Result<Vec<FilenameSearchResult>, String> {
  65 |     if let Some(ref filename_index) = state.filename_index {
  66 |         filename_index
  67 |             .search(&query, limit)
  68 |             .map(|results| {
  69 |                 results
  70 |                     .into_iter()
  71 |                     .map(|r| FilenameSearchResult {
  72 |                         file_path: r.file_path,
  73 |                         file_name: r.file_name,
  74 |                     })
  75 |                     .collect()
  76 |             })
  77 |             .map_err(|e| e.to_string())
  78 |     } else {
  79 |         Err("Filename index not initialized".to_string())
  80 |     }
  81 | }
  82 | 
  83 | pub async fn get_filename_index_stats_internal(
  84 |     state: &Arc<AppState>,
  85 | ) -> Result<FilenameIndexStats, String> {
  86 |     if let Some(ref filename_index) = state.filename_index {
  87 |         let stats = filename_index.get_stats().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  88 |         Ok(FilenameIndexStats {
  89 |             total_files: stats.total_files,
  90 |             index_size_bytes: stats.index_size_bytes,
  91 |         })
  92 |     } else {
  93 |         Err("Filename index not initialized".to_string())
  94 |     }
  95 | }
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::commands::AppState;

   6 | pub fn get_settings_internal(state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<AppSettings, String> {

   8 | }
   9 | 
  10 | pub fn save_settings_internal(settings: AppSettings, state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<(), String> {
  11 |     *state.settings_cache.write().unwrap() = settings.clone();
  12 |     state
  13 |         .settings_manager
  14 |         .save(&settings)
  15 |         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  16 | 
  17 |     let mut watcher = state.watcher.lock().unwrap();
  18 |     watcher
  19 |         .update_watch_list(settings.index_dirs)
  20 |         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  21 | 
  22 |     Ok(())
  23 | }
  24 | 
  25 | pub fn get_recent_searches_internal(state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<Vec<String>, String> {
  26 |     Ok(state
  27 |         .settings_cache
  28 |         .read()
  29 |         .unwrap()
  30 |         .recent_searches
  31 |         .clone()
  32 |         .unwrap_or_default())
  33 | }
  34 | 
  35 | pub fn add_recent_search_internal(query: String, state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<(), String> {
  36 |     let mut cache = state.settings_cache.write().unwrap();
  37 | 
  38 |     let mut recent = cache.recent_searches.clone().unwrap_or_default();
  39 |     recent.retain(|q| q != &query);
  40 |     recent.insert(0, query);
  41 |     recent.truncate(10);
  42 | 
  43 |     cache.recent_searches = Some(recent);
  44 |     state
  45 |         .settings_manager
  46 |         .save(&cache)
  47 |         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  48 | 
  49 |     Ok(())
  50 | }
  51 | 
  52 | pub fn clear_recent_searches_internal(state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<(), String> {
  53 |     let mut cache = state.settings_cache.write().unwrap();
  54 |     cache.recent_searches = Some(vec![]);
  55 |     state
  56 |         .settings_manager
  57 |         .save(&cache)
  58 |         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  59 |     Ok(())
  60 | }
  61 | 
  62 | pub fn add_search_history_internal(query: String, state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<(), String> {
  63 |     let mut cache = state.settings_cache.write().unwrap();
  64 |     let mut history = cache.search_history.clone().unwrap_or_default();
  65 | 
  66 |     let mut found = false;
  67 |     for item in &mut history {
  68 |         if item.query == query {
  69 |             item.frequency += 1;
  70 |             item.last_used = std::time::SystemTime::now()
  71 |                 .duration_since(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
  72 |                 .unwrap()
  73 |                 .as_secs();
  74 |             found = true;
  75 |             break;
  76 |         }
  77 |     }
  78 | 
  79 |     if !found {
  80 |         history.insert(
  81 |             0,
  82 |             crate::settings::SearchHistoryItem {
  83 |                 query,
  84 |                 frequency: 1,
  85 |                 last_used: std::time::SystemTime::now()
  86 |                     .duration_since(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
  87 |                     .unwrap()
  88 |                     .as_secs(),
  89 |             },
  90 |         );
  91 |     }
  92 | 
  93 |     history.sort_by(|a, b| b.frequency.cmp(&a.frequency));
  94 |     history.truncate(50);
  95 | 
  96 |     cache.search_history = Some(history);
  97 |     state
  98 |         .settings_manager
  99 |         .save(&cache)
 100 |         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
 101 | 
 102 |     Ok(())
 103 | }
 104 | 
 105 | pub fn get_search_history_internal(
 106 |     limit: usize,
 107 |     state: &Arc<AppState>,
 108 | ) -> Result<Vec<SearchHistoryItem>, String> {
 109 |     let mut history = state
 110 |         .settings_cache
 111 |         .read()
 112 |         .unwrap()
 113 |         .search_history
 114 |         .clone()
 115 |         .unwrap_or_default();
 116 |     history.truncate(limit);
 117 | 
 118 |     Ok(history)
 119 | }
 120 | 
 121 | pub fn pin_file_internal(path: String, state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<(), String> {
 122 |     let mut cache = state.settings_cache.write().unwrap();
 123 |     if !cache.pinned_files.contains(&path) {
 124 |         cache.pinned_files.push(path);
 125 |         state
 126 |             .settings_manager
 127 |             .save(&cache)
 128 |             .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
 129 |     }
 130 |     Ok(())
 131 | }
 132 | 
 133 | pub fn unpin_file_internal(path: String, state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<(), String> {
 134 |     let mut cache = state.settings_cache.write().unwrap();
 135 |     cache.pinned_files.retain(|p| p != &path);
 136 |     state
 137 |         .settings_manager
 138 |         .save(&cache)
 139 |         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
 140 |     Ok(())
 141 | }
 142 | 
 143 | pub fn get_pinned_files_internal(state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<Vec<String>, String> {
 144 |     Ok(state.settings_cache.read().unwrap().pinned_files.clone())
 145 | }
```

```rust
  13 |         Command::new("explorer")
  14 |             .arg("/select,")

  29 |

  41 |     clipboard.set_text(text).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
  42 |     Ok(())
  43 | }
  44 | 
  45 | pub async fn export_results_internal(
  46 |     results: Vec<SearchResult>,
  47 |     format: String,
  48 | ) -> Result<(), String> {
  49 |     let mut dialog = rfd::AsyncFileDialog::new()
  50 |         .set_title("Export Search Results")
  51 |         .set_file_name(format!("flash_search_results.{}", format));
  52 | 
  53 |     if format == "csv" {
  54 |         dialog = dialog.add_filter("CSV File", &["csv"]);
  55 |     } else if format == "json" {
  56 |         dialog = dialog.add_filter("JSON File", &["json"]);
  57 |     }
  58 | 
  59 |     if let Some(handle) = dialog.save_file().await {
  60 |         let path = handle.path().to_string_lossy().to_string();
  61 |         if format == "csv" {
  62 |             crate::commands::export_results_csv(&results, &path)?;
  63 |         } else if format == "json" {
  64 |             crate::commands::export_results_json(&results, &path)?;
  65 |         }
  66 |     }
  67 | 
  68 |     Ok(())
  69 | }
```

```rust
   6 | #[derive(Error, Debug)]

  11 |     #[error("Parse error in {path}: {cause}")]

  17 |     #[error("Database error: {operation} on {key}: {cause}")]
  18 |     Database {
  19 |         operation: String,
  20 |         key: String,
  21 |         cause: String,
  22 |     },
  23 | 
  24 |     #[error("Unsupported file format: {format}")]
  25 |     UnsupportedFormat { format: String, extension: String },

  27 |     #[error("File corrupted or encrypted: {path}")]
  28 |     CorruptedFile { path: PathBuf, operation: String },

  30 |     #[error("Search error: query '{query}' failed: {cause}")]
  31 |     Search { query: String, cause: String },
  32 | 
  33 |     #[error("Config error: {key}: {cause}")]
  34 |     Config { key: String, cause: String },

  37 |     Archive {

  51 |     #[error("{context}: {source}")]
  52 |     WithContext {
  53 |         context: String,
  54 |         source: Box<FlashError>,
  55 |     },
  56 | }
  57 | 
  58 | pub trait Contextualize<T> {
  59 |     fn context(self, msg: &str) -> std::result::Result<T, FlashError>;
  60 | }
  61 | 
  62 | impl<T, E: Into<FlashError>> Contextualize<T> for std::result::Result<T, E> {
  63 |     fn context(self, msg: &str) -> std::result::Result<T, FlashError> {
  64 |         self.map_err(|e| FlashError::WithContext {
  65 |             context: msg.to_string(),
  66 |             source: Box::new(e.into()),
  67 |         })
  68 |     }
  69 | }
  70 | 
  71 | pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, FlashError>;
  72 | 
  73 | // Helper constructors for common error patterns
  74 | impl FlashError {
  75 |     pub fn parse<P: Into<PathBuf>, S: Into<String>>(path: P, cause: S) -> Self {
  76 |         Self::Parse {
  77 |             path: path.into(),
  78 |             cause: cause.into(),
  79 |         }
  80 |     }
  81 | 
  82 |     pub fn index<S: Into<String>>(msg: S) -> Self {
  83 |         Self::Index {
  84 |             msg: msg.into(),
  85 |             field: None,
  86 |         }
  87 |     }
  88 | 
  89 |     pub fn index_field<S: Into<String>>(field: S, msg: S) -> Self {
  90 |         Self::Index {
  91 |             msg: msg.into(),
  92 |             field: Some(field.into()),
  93 |         }
  94 |     }
  95 | 
  96 |     pub fn database<S: Into<String>, S2: Into<String>>(operation: S, key: S, cause: S2) -> Self {
  97 |         Self::Database {
  98 |             operation: operation.into(),
  99 |             key: key.into(),
 100 |             cause: cause.into(),
 101 |         }
 102 |     }
 103 | 
 104 |     pub fn unsupported_format<S: Into<String>>(format: S, extension: S) -> Self {
 105 |         Self::UnsupportedFormat {
 106 |             format: format.into(),
 107 |             extension: extension.into(),
 108 |         }
 109 |     }
 110 | 
 111 |     pub fn corrupted_file<P: Into<PathBuf>, S: Into<String>>(path: P, operation: S) -> Self {
 112 |         Self::CorruptedFile {
 113 |             path: path.into(),
 114 |             operation: operation.into(),
 115 |         }
 116 |     }
 117 | 
 118 |     pub fn search<S1: Into<String>, S2: Into<String>>(query: S1, cause: S2) -> Self {
 119 |         Self::Search {
 120 |             query: query.into(),
 121 |             cause: cause.into(),
 122 |         }
 123 |     }
 124 | 
 125 |     pub fn config<S1: Into<String>, S2: Into<String>>(key: S1, cause: S2) -> Self {
 126 |         Self::Config {
 127 |             key: key.into(),
 128 |             cause: cause.into(),
 129 |         }
 130 |     }
 131 | 
 132 |     pub fn archive<S1: Into<String>, S2: Into<String>, S3: Into<String>>(
 133 |         archive_type: S1,
 134 |         operation: S2,
 135 |         cause: S3,
 136 |     ) -> Self {
 137 |         Self::Archive {
 138 |             archive_type: archive_type.into(),
 139 |             operation: operation.into(),
 140 |             cause: cause.into(),
 141 |         }
 142 |     }
 143 | 
 144 |     pub fn poisoned_lock<S: Into<String>>(lock_name: S) -> Self {
 145 |         Self::PoisonedLock {
 146 |             lock_name: lock_name.into(),
 147 |         }
 148 |     }
 149 | 
 150 |     pub fn not_found<S: Into<String>>(resource: S, identifier: S) -> Self {
 151 |         Self::NotFound {
 152 |             resource: resource.into(),
 153 |             identifier: identifier.into(),
 154 |         }
 155 |     }
 156 | }
 157 | 
 158 | impl From<tantivy::TantivyError> for FlashError {
 159 |     fn from(e: tantivy::TantivyError) -> Self {
 160 |         FlashError::Index {
 161 |             msg: e.to_string(),
 162 |             field: None,
 163 |         }
 164 |     }
 165 | }
 166 | 
 167 | #[cfg(test)]
 168 | mod tests {
 169 |     use super::*;
 170 | 
 171 |     #[test]
 172 |     fn test_error_display() {
 173 |         let err = FlashError::parse("/test/file.pdf", "Invalid PDF structure");
 174 |         assert_eq!(
 175 |             err.to_string(),
 176 |             "Parse error in /test/file.pdf: Invalid PDF structure"
 177 |         );
 178 |     }
 179 | 
 180 |     #[test]
 181 |     fn test_index_error() {
 182 |         let err = FlashError::index_field("content", "Field not found in schema");
 183 |         assert_eq!(err.to_string(), "Index error: Field not found in schema");
 184 |     }
 185 | 
 186 |     #[test]
 187 |     fn test_database_error() {
 188 |         let err = FlashError::database("get_metadata", "/test/file.txt", "Key not found");
 189 |         assert_eq!(
 190 |             err.to_string(),
 191 |             "Database error: get_metadata on /test/file.txt: Key not found"
 192 |         );
 193 |     }
 194 | 
 195 |     #[test]
 196 |     fn test_helper_constructors() {
 197 |         let err = FlashError::unsupported_format("PDF", "pdf");
 198 |         matches!(err, FlashError::UnsupportedFormat { .. });
 199 |     }
 200 | 
 201 |     #[test]
 202 |     fn test_io_conversion() {
 203 |         let io_err = std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound, "File not found");
 204 |         let flash_err: FlashError = io_err.into();
 205 |         matches!(flash_err, FlashError::Io(_));
 206 |     }
 207 | }
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::indexer::searcher::SearchResult;
   2 | use std::path::PathBuf;
   3 | use tempfile::TempDir;
   4 | 
   5 | #[test]
   6 | fn test_export_results_csv() {
   7 |     let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();
   8 |     let csv_path = temp_dir.path().join("results.csv");
   9 | 
  10 |     let results = vec![
  11 |         SearchResult {
  12 |             file_path: "/path/to/file1.txt".to_string(),
  13 |             title: Some("File 1".to_string()),
  14 |             score: 1.5,
  15 |             matched_terms: vec!["test".to_string()],
  16 |             snippet: Some("content...".to_string()),
  17 |         },
  18 |         SearchResult {
  19 |             file_path: "/path/to/file2.txt".to_string(),
  20 |             title: Some("File 2".to_string()),
  21 |             score: 1.2,
  22 |             matched_terms: vec![],
  23 |             snippet: None,
  24 |         },
  25 |     ];
  26 | 
  27 |     crate::commands::export_results_csv(&results, csv_path.to_str().unwrap()).unwrap();
  28 | 
  29 |     let content = std::fs::read_to_string(csv_path).unwrap();
  30 |     assert!(content.contains("Score,File Path,Title"));
  31 |     assert!(content.contains("1.5,/path/to/file1.txt,File 1"));
  32 |     assert!(content.contains("1.2,/path/to/file2.txt,File 2"));
  33 | }
  34 | 
  35 | #[test]
  36 | fn test_export_results_json() {
  37 |     let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();
  38 |     let json_path = temp_dir.path().join("results.json");
  39 | 
  40 |     let results = vec![SearchResult {
  41 |         file_path: "/path/to/file1.txt".to_string(),
  42 |         title: Some("File 1".to_string()),
  43 |         score: 1.5,
  44 |         matched_terms: vec!["test".to_string()],
  45 |         snippet: Some("content...".to_string()),
  46 |     }];
  47 | 
  48 |     crate::commands::export_results_json(&results, json_path.to_str().unwrap()).unwrap();
  49 | 
  50 |     let content = std::fs::read_to_string(json_path).unwrap();
  51 |     assert!(content.contains("file_path"));
  52 |     assert!(content.contains("1.5"));
  53 | }
```

```rust
  19 | fn load_icon(icon_name: &str) -> iced::widget::Svg<'_> {
  20 |     let svg_data = match icon_name {
  21 |         "search" => SEARCH_ICON_SVG,
  22 |         "file" => FILE_ICON_SVG,
  23 |         "settings" => SETTINGS_ICON_SVG,
  24 |         "refresh" => REFRESH_ICON_SVG,
  25 |         "sun" => SUN_ICON_SVG,
  26 |         "moon" => MOON_ICON_SVG,
  27 |         _ => SEARCH_ICON_SVG,
  28 |     };
  29 |     svg::Svg::new(svg::Handle::from_memory(svg_data.as_bytes()))
  30 | }
  31 | 
  32 | pub fn search_view(app: &App) -> Element<'_, Message> {
  33 |     let mode_text = match app.search_mode {
  34 |         SearchMode::FullText => "Full Text",
  35 |         SearchMode::Filename => "Filename",
  36 |     };
  37 | 
  38 |     let search_icon = container(load_icon("search")).padding(Padding::new(12.0));
  39 | 
  40 |     let input = TextInput::new("Search files...", &app.search_query)
  41 |         .on_input(Message::SearchQueryChanged)
  42 |         .on_submit(Message::SearchSubmitted)
  43 |         .padding(Padding::new(12.0))
  44 |         .size(16)
  45 |         .style(theme::search_input())
  46 |         .width(Length::Fill);
  47 | 
  48 |     let search_btn = button(container(load_icon("search")).padding(Padding::new(8.0)))
  49 |         .on_press(Message::SearchSubmitted)
  50 |         .style(theme::primary_button())
  51 |         .padding(Padding::new(12.0));
  52 | 
  53 |     let search_bar = row![search_icon, input, search_btn]
  54 |         .spacing(0)
  55 |         .align_y(Alignment::Center)
  56 |         .width(Length::Fill);
  57 | 
  58 |     let search_bar_container = container(search_bar)
  59 |         .style(theme::input_container)
  60 |         .width(Length::Fill);
  61 | 
  62 |     let error_display: Element<Message> = if let Some(ref err) = app.search_error {
  63 |         container(
  64 |             row![
  65 |                 container(load_icon("settings")).style(|_theme| theme::error_container_style()),
  66 |                 text(err).size(13).style(|_theme| theme::error_text_style()),
  67 |                 Space::new().width(Length::Fill),
  68 |                 button("Dismiss")
  69 |                     .on_press(Message::DismissError)
  70 |                     .padding(Padding::new(4.0))
  71 |                     .style(theme::secondary_button())
  72 |             ]
  73 |             .spacing(8)
  74 |             .align_y(Alignment::Center),
  75 |         )
  76 |         .padding(Padding::new(12.0))
  77 |         .style(theme::error_container)
  78 |         .width(Length::Fill)
  79 |         .into()
  80 |     } else {
  81 |         Space::new().height(Length::Fixed(0.0)).into()
  82 |     };
  83 | 
  84 |     let filter_ext = TextInput::new("ext:pdf", &app.filter_extension)
  85 |         .on_input(Message::FilterExtensionChanged)
  86 |         .on_submit(Message::SearchSubmitted)
  87 |         .padding(Padding::new(8.0))
  88 |         .size(13)
  89 |         .width(Length::Fixed(100.0))
  90 |         .style(theme::small_input());
  91 | 
  92 |     let filter_size = TextInput::new("size:>1MB", &app.filter_size)
  93 |         .on_input(Message::FilterSizeChanged)
  94 |         .on_submit(Message::SearchSubmitted)
  95 |         .padding(Padding::new(8.0))
  96 |         .size(13)
  97 |         .width(Length::Fixed(100.0))
  98 |         .style(theme::small_input());
  99 | 
 100 |     let mode_btn = button(text(mode_text).size(13))
 101 |         .on_press(Message::ToggleSearchMode)
 102 |         .padding(Padding::new(12.0))
 103 |         .style(theme::secondary_button());
 104 | 
 105 |     let theme_btn = button(if app.is_dark {
 106 |         container(load_icon("sun"))
 107 |     } else {
 108 |         container(load_icon("moon"))
 109 |     })
 110 |     .on_press(Message::ToggleTheme)
 111 |     .padding(Padding::new(10.0))
 112 |     .style(theme::icon_button());
 113 | 
 114 |     let sep1 = container(
 115 |         Space::new()
 116 |             .width(Length::Fixed(1.0))
 117 |             .height(Length::Fixed(20.0)),
 118 |     );
 119 | 
 120 |     let rebuild_display: Element<_> = if let Some(progress) = app.rebuild_progress {
 121 |         let status = app.rebuild_status.as_deref().unwrap_or("Rebuilding...");
 122 |         column![text(status).size(12), progress_bar(0.0..=1.0, progress),]
 123 |             .spacing(4)
 124 |             .align_x(Alignment::Center)
 125 |             .width(Length::Fixed(120.0))
 126 |             .into()
 127 |     } else {
 128 |         button(row![container(load_icon("refresh")), text("Rebuild").size(13)].spacing(6))
 129 |             .on_press(Message::RebuildIndex)
 130 |             .padding(Padding::new(10.0))
 131 |             .style(theme::secondary_button())
 132 |             .into()
 133 |     };
 134 | 
 135 |     let settings_btn = button(container(load_icon("settings")).padding(Padding::new(4.0)))
 136 |         .on_press(Message::TabChanged(Tab::Settings))
 137 |         .padding(Padding::new(10.0))
 138 |         .style(theme::icon_button());
 139 | 
 140 |     let stats = text(format!("{} files | {}", app.files_indexed, app.index_size)).size(13);
 141 | 
 142 |     let filter_group = row![mode_btn, sep1, filter_ext, filter_size,]
 143 |         .spacing(8)
 144 |         .align_y(Alignment::Center);
 145 | 
 146 |     let toolbar_spacer = Space::new().width(Length::Fixed(16.0));
 147 | 
 148 |     let filter_row = row![
 149 |         filter_group,
 150 |         Space::new().width(Length::Fill),
 151 |         stats,
 152 |         toolbar_spacer,
 153 |         theme_btn,
 154 |         rebuild_display,
 155 |         settings_btn
 156 |     ]
 157 |     .spacing(8)
 158 |     .align_y(Alignment::Center)
 159 |     .width(Length::Fill);
 160 | 
 161 |     let top_section = container(
 162 |         column![search_bar_container, error_display, filter_row]
 163 |             .spacing(12)
 164 |             .align_x(Alignment::Center),
 165 |     )
 166 |     .style(theme::top_bar_container)
 167 |     .padding(Padding::new(16.0))
 168 |     .width(Length::Fill);
 169 | 
 170 |     let results_panel: Element<Message> = if app.settings.index_dirs.is_empty() {
 171 |         container(
 172 |             column![
 173 |                 container(load_icon("settings")),
 174 |                 Space::new().height(Length::Fixed(16.0)),
 175 |                 text("Add a folder to get started").size(24),
 176 |                 Space::new().height(Length::Fixed(8.0)),
 177 |                 text("Configure your indexed directories in Settings.").size(14),
 178 |                 Space::new().height(Length::Fixed(24.0)),
 179 |                 button(text("Go to Settings").size(16))
 180 |                     .on_press(Message::TabChanged(Tab::Settings))
 181 |                     .padding(Padding::new(14.0))
 182 |                     .style(theme::primary_button()),
 183 |             ]
 184 |             .align_x(Alignment::Center),
 185 |         )
 186 |         .width(Length::Fill)
 187 |         .height(Length::Fill)
 188 |         .center_x(Length::Fill)
 189 |         .center_y(Length::Fill)
 190 |         .into()
 191 |     } else if app.is_searching {
 192 |         container(column![text("Searching...").size(14),].align_x(Alignment::Center))
 193 |             .width(Length::Fill)
 194 |             .height(Length::Fill)
 195 |             .center_x(Length::Fill)
 196 |             .center_y(Length::Fill)
 197 |             .into()
 198 |     } else if app.results.is_empty() && !app.search_query.is_empty() {
 199 |         container(
 200 |             column![
 201 |                 text("No results found").size(16),
 202 |                 Space::new().height(Length::Fixed(8.0)),
 203 |                 text("Try adjusting your search terms").size(13),
 204 |             ]
 205 |             .align_x(Alignment::Center),
 206 |         )
 207 |         .width(Length::Fill)
 208 |         .height(Length::Fill)
 209 |         .center_x(Length::Fill)
 210 |         .center_y(Length::Fill)
 211 |         .into()
 212 |     } else if app.results.is_empty() {
 213 |         container(
 214 |             column![
 215 |                 container(load_icon("search")),
 216 |                 Space::new().height(Length::Fixed(12.0)),
 217 |                 text("Start searching").size(16),
 218 |                 text("Enter keywords to find files").size(13),
 219 |             ]
 220 |             .align_x(Alignment::Center),
 221 |         )
 222 |         .width(Length::Fill)
 223 |         .height(Length::Fill)
 224 |         .center_x(Length::Fill)
 225 |         .center_y(Length::Fill)
 226 |         .into()
 227 |     } else {
 228 |         let items: Vec<_> = app
 229 |             .results
 230 |             .iter()
 231 |             .enumerate()
 232 |             .map(|(i, item)| {
 233 |                 let ext_str = item.extension.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
 234 |                 let is_selected = app.selected_index == Some(i);
 235 | 
 236 |                 let dir = std::path::Path::new(&item.path)
 237 |                     .parent()
 238 |                     .map(|p| p.to_string_lossy().to_string())
 239 |                     .unwrap_or_default();
 240 | 
 241 |                 let file_icon = container(load_icon("file")).padding(Padding::new(8.0));
 242 | 
 243 |                 let ext_badge =
 244 |                     container(text(ext_str.to_uppercase()).size(10)).padding(Padding::new(4.0));
 245 | 
 246 |                 let score_text = format!("{:.1}", item.score * 100.0);
 247 |                 let score_badge =
 248 |                     container(text(format!("{}%", score_text)).size(10)).padding(Padding::new(4.0));
 249 | 
 250 |                 let header = row![
 251 |                     file_icon,
 252 |                     text(&item.title).size(15),
 253 |                     Space::new().width(Length::Fill),
 254 |                     score_badge,
 255 |                     ext_badge,
 256 |                 ]
 257 |                 .align_y(Alignment::Center);
 258 | 
 259 |                 let body = text(dir).size(12);
 260 | 
 261 |                 let card_content = column![header, body].spacing(4).align_x(Alignment::Start);
 262 | 
 263 |                 let card = container(card_content)
 264 |                     .padding(Padding::new(12.0))
 265 |                     .width(Length::Fill);
 266 | 
 267 |                 let card_container = if is_selected {
 268 |                     container(card).style(theme::result_card_hover)
 269 |                 } else {
 270 |                     container(card).style(theme::result_card_normal)
 271 |                 };
 272 | 
 273 |                 let button_style = theme::result_button(is_selected);
 274 | 
 275 |                 button(card_container)
 276 |                     .on_press(Message::ResultSelected(i))
 277 |                     .width(Length::Fill)
 278 |                     .padding(Padding::new(0.0))
 279 |                     .style(button_style)
 280 |                     .into()
 281 |             })
 282 |             .collect();
 283 |         let list = Scrollable::new(column(items).spacing(8)).height(Length::Fill);
 284 |         container(list)
 285 |             .padding(Padding::new(12.0))
 286 |             .width(Length::Fill)
 287 |             .height(Length::Fill)
 288 |             .into()
 289 |     };
 290 | 
 291 |     let preview_panel: Element<Message> = if app.is_loading_preview {
 292 |         container(column![text("Loading preview...").size(14),].align_x(Alignment::Center))
 293 |             .width(Length::Fill)
 294 |             .height(Length::Fill)
 295 |             .center_x(Length::Fill)
 296 |             .center_y(Length::Fill)
 297 |             .into()
 298 |     } else if let Some(ref preview) = app.preview_content {
 299 |         let scroll = Scrollable::new(
 300 |             container(text(preview).size(14))
 301 |                 .padding(Padding::new(20.0))
 302 |                 .width(Length::Fill),
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::error::Result;
   2 | use fst::automaton::Subsequence;
   3 | use fst::{IntoStreamer, Streamer};
   4 | use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
   5 | use std::path::Path;
   6 | use std::sync::Arc;
   7 | use std::sync::RwLock;
   8 | 
   9 | #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone)]
  10 | pub struct FilenameEntry {
  11 |     pub path: String,
  12 |     pub name: String,
  13 | }
  14 | 
  15 | #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone)]
  16 | pub struct FilenameSearchResult {
  17 |     pub file_path: String,
  18 |     pub file_name: String,
  19 | }
  20 | 
  21 | #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone)]
  22 | pub struct FilenameIndexStats {
  23 |     pub total_files: usize,
  24 |     pub index_size_bytes: u64,
  25 | }
  26 | 
  27 | /// File extension for the binary index file
  28 | const INDEX_FILENAME: &str = "filenames.bin";
  29 | /// Legacy JSON filename for migration
  30 | const LEGACY_INDEX_FILENAME: &str = "filenames.json";
  31 | 
  32 | pub struct FilenameIndex {
  33 |     entries: Arc<RwLock<Vec<FilenameEntry>>>,
  34 |     data_path: std::path::PathBuf,
  35 |     fst_map: Arc<RwLock<Vec<u8>>>,
  36 | }
  37 | 
  38 | impl FilenameIndex {
  39 |     pub fn open(data_path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
  40 |         let data_path = data_path.to_path_buf();
  41 | 
  42 |         let entries = if data_path.exists() {
  43 |             // Try bincode first, then fall back to legacy JSON
  44 |             let bin_path = data_path.join(INDEX_FILENAME);
  45 |             let json_path = data_path.join(LEGACY_INDEX_FILENAME);
  46 | 
  47 |             if bin_path.exists() {
  48 |                 match std::fs::read(&bin_path) {
  49 |                     Ok(bytes) => {
  50 |                         match bincode::serde::decode_from_slice(&bytes, bincode::config::standard())
  51 |                         {
  52 |                             Ok((entries, _)) => {
  53 |                                 let entries: Vec<FilenameEntry> = entries;
  54 |                                 tracing::info!(
  55 |                                     "Loaded {} filenames from bincode index",
  56 |                                     entries.len()
  57 |                                 );
  58 |                                 entries
  59 |                             }
  60 |                             Err(e) => {
  61 |                                 tracing::warn!("Failed to parse bincode filename index: {}", e);
  62 |                                 Vec::new()
  63 |                             }
  64 |                         }
  65 |                     }
  66 |                     Err(_) => Vec::new(),
  67 |                 }
  68 |             } else if json_path.exists() {
  69 |                 // Migrate from legacy JSON
  70 |                 match std::fs::read_to_string(&json_path) {
  71 |                     Ok(content) => match serde_json::from_str::<Vec<FilenameEntry>>(&content) {
  72 |                         Ok(entries) => {
  73 |                             tracing::info!(
  74 |                                 "Migrated {} filenames from legacy JSON index",
  75 |                                 entries.len()
  76 |                             );
  77 |                             // Save as bincode immediately and remove JSON
  78 |                             Self::save_to_disk_sync(&entries, &data_path);
  79 |                             let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&json_path);
  80 |                             entries
  81 |                         }
  82 |                         Err(e) => {
  83 |                             tracing::warn!("Failed to parse legacy JSON filename index: {}", e);
  84 |                             Vec::new()
  85 |                         }
  86 |                     },
  87 |                     Err(_) => Vec::new(),
  88 |                 }
  89 |             } else {
  90 |                 Vec::new()
  91 |             }
  92 |         } else {
  93 |             Vec::new()
  94 |         };
  95 | 
  96 |         let fst_map = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Self::build_fst(&entries)));
  97 | 
  98 |         Ok(Self {
  99 |             entries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(entries)),
 100 |             data_path,
 101 |             fst_map,
 102 |         })
 103 |     }
 104 | 
 105 |     pub fn add_file(&self, path: &str, name: &str) -> Result<()> {
 106 |         let entry = FilenameEntry {
 107 |             path: path.to_string(),
 108 |             name: name.to_string(),
 109 |         };
 110 | 
 111 |         let entries = self.entries.clone();
 112 | 
 113 |         if let Ok(mut guard) = entries.write() {
 114 |             guard.push(entry);
 115 | 
 116 |             if guard.len() % 1000 == 0 {
 117 |                 let data = guard.clone();
 118 |                 let data_path = self.data_path.clone();
 119 |                 std::thread::spawn(move || {
 120 |                     Self::save_to_disk_sync(&data, &data_path);
 121 |                 });
 122 |             }
 123 |         }
 124 | 
 125 |         Ok(())
 126 |     }
 127 | 
 128 |     pub fn commit(&self) -> Result<()> {
 129 |         let entries = self.entries.clone();
 130 | 
 131 |         if let Ok(guard) = entries.read() {
 132 |             let data_path = self.data_path.clone();
 133 |             let data: Vec<_> = guard
 134 |                 .iter()
 135 |                 .map(|e| FilenameEntry {
 136 |                     path: e.path.clone(),
 137 |                     name: e.name.clone(),
 138 |                 })
 139 |                 .collect();
 140 | 
 141 |             if let Ok(mut fst_guard) = self.fst_map.write() {
 142 |                 *fst_guard = Self::build_fst(&data);
 143 |             }
 144 | 
 145 |             std::thread::spawn(move || {
 146 |                 Self::save_to_disk_sync(&data, &data_path);
 147 |             });
 148 |         }
 149 | 
 150 |         Ok(())
 151 |     }
 152 | 
 153 |     fn build_fst(entries: &[FilenameEntry]) -> Vec<u8> {
 154 |         let mut items: Vec<(String, u64)> = entries
 155 |             .iter()
 156 |             .enumerate()
 157 |             .map(|(i, e)| (format!("{}\0{}", e.name.to_lowercase(), i), i as u64))
 158 |             .collect();
 159 |         items.sort_by(|a, b| a.0.cmp(&b.0));
 160 | 
 161 |         let mut build = fst::MapBuilder::memory();
 162 |         for (k, v) in items {
 163 |             let _ = build.insert(k, v);
 164 |         }
 165 |         build.into_inner().unwrap_or_default()
 166 |     }
 167 | 
 168 |     /// Save entries to disk using bincode (P3: replaces JSON for ~10x smaller + faster)
 169 |     fn save_to_disk_sync(entries: &[FilenameEntry], data_path: &std::path::Path) {
 170 |         match bincode::serde::encode_to_vec(entries, bincode::config::standard()) {
 171 |             Ok(bytes) => {
 172 |                 let _ = std::fs::write(data_path.join(INDEX_FILENAME), bytes);
 173 |             }
 174 |             Err(e) => {
 175 |                 tracing::warn!("Failed to serialize filename index: {}", e);
 176 |             }
 177 |         }
 178 |     }
 179 | 
 180 |     pub fn search(&self, query: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<FilenameSearchResult>> {
 181 |         let fst_bytes = self.fst_map.read().unwrap();
 182 |         if fst_bytes.is_empty() {
 183 |             return Ok(Vec::new());
 184 |         }
 185 | 
 186 |         // FST Map
 187 |         let map = match fst::Map::new(fst_bytes.clone()) {
 188 |             Ok(m) => m,
 189 |             Err(_) => return Ok(Vec::new()),
 190 |         };
 191 | 
 192 |         // Fuzzy / Subsequence matching
 193 |         let query_lower = query.to_lowercase();
 194 |         let aut = Subsequence::new(&query_lower);
 195 | 
 196 |         let mut stream = map.search(aut).into_stream();
 197 | 
 198 |         let entries_lock = match self.entries.read() {
 199 |             Ok(g) => g,
 200 |             Err(_) => return Ok(Vec::new()),
 201 |         };
 202 | 
 203 |         let mut results = Vec::new();
 204 |         while let Some((_, v)) = stream.next() {
 205 |             if results.len() >= limit {
 206 |                 break;
 207 |             }
 208 |             if let Some(entry) = entries_lock.get(v as usize) {
 209 |                 results.push(FilenameSearchResult {
 210 |                     file_path: entry.path.clone(),
 211 |                     file_name: entry.name.clone(),
 212 |                 });
 213 |             }
 214 |         }
 215 | 
 216 |         Ok(results)
 217 |     }
 218 | 
 219 |     pub fn clear(&self) -> Result<()> {
 220 |         let entries = self.entries.clone();
 221 | 
 222 |         if let Ok(mut guard) = entries.write() {
 223 |             guard.clear();
 224 |             let data_path = self.data_path.clone();
 225 |             std::thread::spawn(move || {
 226 |                 let _ = std::fs::remove_file(data_path.join(INDEX_FILENAME));
 227 |                 let _ = std::fs::remove_file(data_path.join(LEGACY_INDEX_FILENAME));
 228 |             });
 229 |         }
 230 | 
 231 |         Ok(())
 232 |     }
 233 | 
 234 |     pub fn get_stats(&self) -> Result<FilenameIndexStats> {
 235 |         let entries = self.entries.clone();
 236 | 
 237 |         let entries = match entries.read() {
 238 |             Ok(guard) => guard,
 239 |             Err(_) => {
 240 |                 return Ok(FilenameIndexStats {
 241 |                     total_files: 0,
 242 |                     index_size_bytes: 0,
 243 |                 })
 244 |             }
 245 |         };
 246 | 
 247 |         let size: u64 = entries
 248 |             .iter()
 249 |             .map(|e| e.path.len() as u64 + e.name.len() as u64 + 32)
 250 |             .sum();
 251 | 
 252 |         Ok(FilenameIndexStats {
 253 |             total_files: entries.len(),
 254 |             index_size_bytes: size,
 255 |         })
 256 |     }
 257 | 
 258 |     pub fn rebuild_index(&self, paths: Vec<(String, String)>) -> Result<()> {
 259 |         let entries = self.entries.clone();
 260 |         let data_path = self.data_path.clone();
 261 | 
 262 |         if let Ok(mut guard) = entries.write() {
 263 |             *guard = paths
 264 |                 .into_iter()
 265 |                 .map(|(path, name)| FilenameEntry { path, name })
 266 |                 .collect();
 267 | 
 268 |             let data: Vec<_> = guard
 269 |                 .iter()
 270 |                 .map(|e| FilenameEntry {
 271 |                     path: e.path.clone(),
 272 |                     name: e.name.clone(),
 273 |                 })
 274 |                 .collect();
 275 | 
 276 |             if let Ok(mut fst_guard) = self.fst_map.write() {
 277 |                 *fst_guard = Self::build_fst(&data);
 278 |             }
 279 | 
 280 |             std::thread::spawn(move || {
 281 |                 Self::save_to_disk_sync(&data, &data_path);
 282 |             });
 283 |         }
 284 | 
 285 |         Ok(())
 286 |     }
 287 | }
```

```rust
   1 | use regex::Regex;
   2 | use std::sync::OnceLock;
   3 | 
   4 | static OPERATOR_REGEX: OnceLock<Regex> = OnceLock::new();
   5 | 
   6 | /// Parsed query with operators and search terms
   7 | #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   8 | pub struct ParsedQuery {
   9 |     /// The original text query (for Tantivy)
  10 |     pub text_query: String,
  11 |     /// Extension filter (e.g., "pdf", "docx")
  12 |     pub extension: Option<String>,
  13 |     /// Path filter (search in specific path)
  14 |     pub path_filter: Option<String>,
  15 |     /// Title filter
  16 |     pub title_filter: Option<String>,
  17 |     /// Size filters
  18 |     pub min_size: Option<u64>,
  19 |     pub max_size: Option<u64>,
  20 |     /// Whether fuzzy matching is enabled
  21 |     pub fuzzy: bool,
  22 | }
  23 | 
  24 | impl ParsedQuery {
  25 |     pub fn new(query: &str) -> Self {
  26 |         Self::parse(query)
  27 |     }
  28 | 
  29 |     fn parse(input: &str) -> Self {
  30 |         let mut extension = None;
  31 |         let mut path_filter = None;
  32 |         let mut title_filter = None;
  33 |         let mut min_size = None;
  34 |         let mut max_size = None;
  35 |         let fuzzy = true;
  36 | 
  37 |         // Parse operators
  38 |         // ext:pdf, path:docs, title:report, size:>1MB, size:<10MB, exact:"phrase"
  39 |         let operator_regex = OPERATOR_REGEX.get_or_init(|| {
  40 |             Regex::new(
  41 |                 r#"(?i)(ext|path|title|size):(?:([<>]?)(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)(MB|KB|GB|B)?|"([^"]*)"|(\S+))"#,
  42 |             )
  43 |             .unwrap()
  44 |         });
  45 | 
  46 |         let mut remaining = input.to_string();
  47 | 
  48 |         // Process all operators
  49 |         for cap in operator_regex.captures_iter(input) {
  50 |             let operator = cap
  51 |                 .get(1)
  52 |                 .map(|m| m.as_str().to_lowercase())
  53 |                 .unwrap_or_default();
  54 |             let value = cap
  55 |                 .get(5)
  56 |                 .map(|m| m.as_str().to_string()) // Quoted value
  57 |                 .or_else(|| cap.get(6).map(|m| m.as_str().to_string())) // Unquoted value
  58 |                 .unwrap_or_default();
  59 | 
  60 |             match operator.as_str() {
  61 |                 "ext" => {
  62 |                     extension = Some(value.trim_start_matches('.').to_lowercase());
  63 |                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
  64 |                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
  65 |                     }
  66 |                 }
  67 |                 "path" => {
  68 |                     path_filter = Some(value.to_lowercase());
  69 |                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
  70 |                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
  71 |                     }
  72 |                 }
  73 |                 "title" => {
  74 |                     title_filter = Some(value.to_lowercase());
  75 |                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
  76 |                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
  77 |                     }
  78 |                 }
  79 |                 "size" => {
  80 |                     // Handle size operators
  81 |                     if let Some(op) = cap.get(2) {
  82 |                         let op = op.as_str();
  83 |                         if let Some(num_str) = cap.get(3) {
  84 |                             if let Ok(num) = num_str.as_str().parse::<f64>() {
  85 |                                 let multiplier = cap
  86 |                                     .get(4)
  87 |                                     .map(|m| match m.as_str().to_uppercase().as_str() {
  88 |                                         "GB" => 1024 * 1024 * 1024,
  89 |                                         "MB" => 1024 * 1024,
  90 |                                         "KB" => 1024,
  91 |                                         _ => 1,
  92 |                                     })
  93 |                                     .unwrap_or(1);
  94 | 
  95 |                                 let bytes = (num * multiplier as f64) as u64;
  96 |                                 match op {
  97 |                                     ">" => min_size = Some(bytes),
  98 |                                     "<" => max_size = Some(bytes),
  99 |                                     _ => {}
 100 |                                 }
 101 |                             }
 102 |                         }
 103 |                     } else if let Ok(size_val) = value.parse::<u64>() {
 104 |                         // Exact size match (treat as minimum for practical purposes)
 105 |                         min_size = Some(size_val);
 106 |                         max_size = Some(size_val + 1);
 107 |                     }
 108 |                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
 109 |                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
 110 |                     }
 111 |                 }
 112 |                 _ => {}
 113 |             }
 114 |         }
 115 | 
 116 |         // Clean up remaining text for full-text search
 117 |         let text_query = remaining
 118 |             .split_whitespace()
 119 |             .collect::<Vec<_>>()
 120 |             .join(" ")
 121 |             .trim()
 122 |             .to_string();
 123 | 
 124 |         Self {
 125 |             text_query: if text_query.is_empty() {
 126 |                 "*".to_string()
 127 |             } else {
 128 |                 text_query
 129 |             },
 130 |             extension,
 131 |             path_filter,
 132 |             title_filter,
 133 |             min_size,
 134 |             max_size,
 135 |             fuzzy,
 136 |         }
 137 |     }
 138 | 
 139 |     /// Check if a path matches the extension filter
 140 |     pub fn matches_extension(&self, path: &str) -> bool {
 141 |         if let Some(ref ext) = self.extension {
 142 |             let path_lower = path.to_lowercase();
 143 |             path_lower.ends_with(&format!(".{}", ext))
 144 |         } else {
 145 |             true
 146 |         }
 147 |     }
 148 | 
 149 |     /// Check if a path matches the path filter
 150 |     pub fn matches_path(&self, path: &str) -> bool {
 151 |         if let Some(ref filter) = self.path_filter {
 152 |             path.to_lowercase().contains(filter)
 153 |         } else {
 154 |             true
 155 |         }
 156 |     }
 157 | 
 158 |     /// Check if a title matches the title filter
 159 |     pub fn matches_title(&self, title: Option<&str>) -> bool {
 160 |         if let Some(ref filter) = self.title_filter {
 161 |             if let Some(t) = title {
 162 |                 t.to_lowercase().contains(filter)
 163 |             } else {
 164 |                 false
 165 |             }
 166 |         } else {
 167 |             true
 168 |         }
 169 |     }
 170 | }
 171 | 
 172 | /// Extract search terms for highlighting from a query
 173 | pub fn extract_highlight_terms(query: &str) -> Vec<String> {
 174 |     let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
 175 | 
 176 |     let mut terms: Vec<String> = parsed
 177 |         .text_query
 178 |         .split_whitespace()
 179 |         .filter(|t| *t != "*")
 180 |         .map(|t| t.to_lowercase())
 181 |         .collect();
 182 | 
 183 |     if terms.is_empty() && parsed.text_query == "*" {
 184 |         terms.push("*".to_string());
 185 |     }
 186 | 
 187 |     // Also add title filter terms
 188 |     if let Some(title) = parsed.title_filter {
 189 |         terms.push(title);
 190 |     }
 191 | 
 192 |     terms
 193 | }
 194 | 
 195 | #[cfg(test)]
 196 | mod tests {
 197 |     use super::*;
 198 | 
 199 |     #[test]
 200 |     fn test_parse_ext_operator() {
 201 |         let query = "ext:pdf report";
 202 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
 203 |         assert_eq!(parsed.extension, Some("pdf".to_string()));
 204 |         assert_eq!(parsed.text_query, "report");
 205 |     }
 206 | 
 207 |     #[test]
 208 |     fn test_parse_path_operator() {
 209 |         let query = "path:documents important";
 210 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
 211 |         assert_eq!(parsed.path_filter, Some("documents".to_string()));
 212 |         assert_eq!(parsed.text_query, "important");
 213 |     }
 214 | 
 215 |     #[test]
 216 |     fn test_parse_size_operators() {
 217 |         let query = "size:>1MB document";
 218 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
 219 |         assert_eq!(parsed.min_size, Some(1048576));
 220 |         assert_eq!(parsed.text_query, "document");
 221 |     }
 222 | 
 223 |     #[test]
 224 |     fn test_multiple_operators() {
 225 |         let query = "ext:pdf path:reports annual size:<10MB";
 226 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
 227 |         assert_eq!(parsed.extension, Some("pdf".to_string()));
 228 |         assert_eq!(parsed.path_filter, Some("reports".to_string()));
 229 |         assert_eq!(parsed.max_size, Some(10485760));
 230 |         assert_eq!(parsed.text_query, "annual");
 231 |     }
 232 | 
 233 |     #[test]
 234 |     fn test_matches_extension() {
 235 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new("ext:pdf");
 236 |         assert!(parsed.matches_extension("file.pdf"));
 237 |         assert!(parsed.matches_extension("FILE.PDF"));
 238 |         assert!(!parsed.matches_extension("file.txt"));
 239 |     }
 240 | 
 241 |     #[test]
 242 |     fn test_matches_path() {
 243 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new("path:reports");
 244 |         assert!(parsed.matches_path("/home/user/reports/annual.pdf"));
 245 |         assert!(!parsed.matches_path("/home/user/documents/annual.pdf"));
 246 |     }
 247 | 
 248 |     #[test]
 249 |     fn test_matches_title() {
 250 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new("title:annual");
 251 |         assert!(parsed.matches_title(Some("Annual Report")));
 252 |         assert!(!parsed.matches_title(Some("Monthly Report")));
 253 |         assert!(!parsed.matches_title(None));
 254 |     }
 255 | 
 256 |     #[test]
 257 |     fn test_extract_highlight_terms() {
 258 |         let terms = extract_highlight_terms("ext:pdf report title:annual");
 259 |         assert!(terms.contains(&"report".to_string()));
 260 |         assert!(terms.contains(&"annual".to_string()));
 261 |     }
 262 | }
```

### File: `src\indexer\schema.rs`

```rust
   1 | use tantivy::schema::*;
   2 | 
   3 | /// Create Tantivy schema optimized for file search
   4 | pub fn create_schema() -> Schema {
   5 |     let mut schema_builder = Schema::builder();
   6 | 
   7 |     // File path - stored for retrieval, indexed for exact matches
   8 |     schema_builder.add_text_field("file_path", STRING | STORED);
   9 | 
  10 |     // Content - indexed for search but NOT stored (to save RAM)
  11 |     // We retrieve content from disk on demand
  12 |     let text_options = TextOptions::default().set_indexing_options(
  13 |         TextFieldIndexing::default()
  14 |             .set_tokenizer("default")
  15 |             .set_index_option(IndexRecordOption::WithFreqsAndPositions),
  16 |     );
  17 |     schema_builder.add_text_field("content", text_options);
  18 | 
  19 |     // Title - stored for display, indexed for search
  20 |     schema_builder.add_text_field("title", TEXT | STORED);
  21 | 
  22 |     // Modified timestamp - indexed for sorting
  23 |     schema_builder.add_date_field("modified", FAST | INDEXED);
  24 | 
  25 |     // File size - indexed for range queries
  26 |     schema_builder.add_u64_field("size", FAST | INDEXED);
  27 | 
  28 |     // File extension - indexed as keyword for fast filtering
  29 |     schema_builder.add_text_field("extension", STRING | STORED);
  30 | 
  31 |     schema_builder.build()
  32 | }
```

```rust
  14 | /// Maximum number of cached query results

  16 | /// Cache TTL in seconds
  17 | const CACHE_TTL_SECS: u64 = 30;
  18 | 
  19 | #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone)]

  40 | struct CachedResult {

  42 |     #[allow(dead_code)]
  43 |     timestamp: Instant,
  44 | }
  45 | 
  46 | /// Cache key for search queries
  47 | #[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq)]
  48 | pub(crate) struct CacheKey {
  49 |     query: String,
  50 |     limit: usize,
  51 |     min_size: Option<u64>,
  52 |     max_size: Option<u64>,
  53 |     extensions: Option<Vec<String>>,
  54 | }
  55 | 
  56 | /// LRU-style query result cache using moka + ahash
  57 | #[derive(Clone)]
  58 | pub struct QueryCache {
  59 |     cache: Cache<CacheKey, CachedResult>,
  60 | }
  61 | 
  62 | impl QueryCache {
  63 |     pub fn new() -> Self {
  64 |         Self {
  65 |             cache: Cache::builder()
  66 |                 .max_capacity(MAX_CACHE_SIZE as u64)
  67 |                 .time_to_live(Duration::from_secs(CACHE_TTL_SECS))
  68 |                 .build(),
  69 |         }
  70 |     }
  71 | 
  72 |     pub(crate) fn get(&self, key: &CacheKey) -> Option<Vec<SearchResult>> {
  73 |         self.cache.get(key).map(|cached| cached.results)
  74 |     }
  75 | 
  76 |     pub(crate) fn insert(&self, key: CacheKey, results: Vec<SearchResult>) {

  78 |             key,
```
