```rust
  80 |                 results,
  81 |                 timestamp: Instant::now(),
  82 |             },
  83 |         );
  84 |     }
  85 |

  87 |         self.cache.invalidate_all();
  88 |     }
  89 | }
  90 | 
  91 | impl Default for QueryCache {

  93 |         Self::new()
  94 |     }
  95 | }
  96 | 
  97 | /// Manages searching the Tantivy index
  98 | #[derive(Clone)]
  99 | pub struct IndexSearcher {
 100 |     reader: IndexReader,
 101 |     query_parser: QueryParser,
 102 |     // schema: Schema,
 103 |     path_field: Field,
 104 |     title_field: Field,
 105 |     size_field: Field,
 106 |     content_field: Field,
 107 |     extension_field: Field,
 108 |     cache: QueryCache,
 109 |     index_path: std::path::PathBuf,
 110 | }
 111 | 
 112 | impl IndexSearcher {
 113 |     pub fn new(index: &Index, index_path: std::path::PathBuf) -> Result<Self> {
 114 |         let schema = index.schema();
 115 | 
 116 |         // Pre-warm the reader by loading index into memory on startup
 117 |         // This ensures first search is fast (no initial load latency)
 118 |         let reader = index
 119 |             .reader_builder()
 120 |             .reload_policy(ReloadPolicy::OnCommitWithDelay)
 121 |             .try_into()
 122 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::search("create_index_reader", e.to_string()))?;
 123 | 
 124 |         // Pre-warm: load the index reader
 125 |         reader.reload().ok();
 126 | 
 127 |         // Get field references once to avoid repeated lookups
 128 |         let path_field = schema
 129 |             .get_field("file_path")
 130 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("file_path", "Field not found in schema"))?;
 131 |         let title_field = schema
 132 |             .get_field("title")
 133 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("title", "Field not found in schema"))?;
 134 |         let size_field = schema
 135 |             .get_field("size")
 136 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("size", "Field not found in schema"))?;
 137 |         let content_field = schema
 138 |             .get_field("content")
 139 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("content", "Field not found in schema"))?;
 140 |         let extension_field = schema
 141 |             .get_field("extension")
 142 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("extension", "Field not found in schema"))?;
 143 | 
 144 |         // Search across content, title, and file_path fields
 145 |         let default_fields: Vec<Field> = ["content", "title", "file_path"]
 146 |             .iter()
 147 |             .filter_map(|field_name| schema.get_field(field_name).ok())
 148 |             .collect();
 149 | 
 150 |         let query_parser = QueryParser::for_index(index, default_fields);
 151 | 
 152 |         Ok(Self {
 153 |             reader,
 154 |             query_parser,
 155 |             path_field,
 156 |             title_field,
 157 |             size_field,
 158 |             content_field,
 159 |             extension_field,
 160 |             cache: QueryCache::new(),
 161 |             index_path,
 162 |         })
 163 |     }
 164 | 
 165 |     /// Search the index and return top results with optional filters
 166 |     pub async fn search(
 167 |         &self,
 168 |         query: &str,
 169 |         limit: usize,
 170 |         min_size: Option<u64>,
 171 |         max_size: Option<u64>,
 172 |         file_extensions: Option<&[String]>,
 173 |     ) -> Result<Vec<SearchResult>> {
 174 |         let query_owned = query.to_string();
 175 |         let extensions_owned = file_extensions.map(|e| e.to_vec());
 176 |         let this = self.clone();
 177 | 
 178 |         tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
 179 |             this.search_sync(
 180 |                 &query_owned,
 181 |                 limit,
 182 |                 min_size,
 183 |                 max_size,
 184 |                 extensions_owned.as_deref(),
 185 |             )
 186 |         })
 187 |         .await
 188 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::search(query, format!("Search task failed: {}", e)))?
 189 |     }
 190 | 
 191 |     /// Synchronous search implementation
 192 |     pub fn search_sync(
 193 |         &self,
 194 |         query: &str,
 195 |         limit: usize,
 196 |         min_size: Option<u64>,
 197 |         max_size: Option<u64>,
 198 |         file_extensions: Option<&[String]>,
 199 |     ) -> Result<Vec<SearchResult>> {
 200 |         use super::query_parser::{extract_highlight_terms, ParsedQuery};
 201 | 
 202 |         // Create cache key
 203 |         let cache_key = CacheKey {
 204 |             query: query.to_string(),
 205 |             limit,
 206 |             min_size,
 207 |             max_size,
 208 |             extensions: file_extensions.map(|e| e.to_vec()),
 209 |         };
 210 | 
 211 |         // Check cache first
 212 |         if let Some(cached) = self.cache.get(&cache_key) {
 213 |             return Ok(cached);
 214 |         }
 215 | 
 216 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
 217 |         let highlight_terms = extract_highlight_terms(query);
 218 | 
 219 |         let searcher = self.reader.searcher();
 220 | 
 221 |         // Build the main query - use fuzzy search for better typo tolerance
 222 |         let text_query = self.build_fuzzy_query(&parsed.text_query)?;
 223 | 
 224 |         // Build query with optional filters
 225 |         let mut combine: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>)> =
 226 |             vec![(Occur::Must, text_query)];
 227 | 
 228 |         // Add size filters
 229 |         if min_size.is_some() || max_size.is_some() {
 230 |             if let Some(min_val) = min_size {
 231 |                 let lower = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, min_val);
 232 |                 let upper = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, u64::MAX);
 233 |                 let range = RangeQuery::new(Bound::Included(lower), Bound::Included(upper));
 234 |                 combine.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(range)));
 235 |             }
 236 | 
 237 |             if let Some(max_val) = max_size {
 238 |                 let lower = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, 0);
 239 |                 let upper = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, max_val);
 240 |                 let range = RangeQuery::new(Bound::Included(lower), Bound::Included(upper));
 241 |                 combine.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(range)));
 242 |             }
 243 |         }
 244 | 
 245 |         // Build file extension filter as a boolean query clause
 246 |         if let Some(extensions) = file_extensions {
 247 |             if !extensions.is_empty() {
 248 |                 let extension_queries: Vec<_> = extensions
 249 |                     .iter()
 250 |                     .map(|ext| {
 251 |                         let ext_lower = ext.to_lowercase();
 252 |                         // Use TermQuery for fast extension matching (much faster than RegexQuery)
 253 |                         let term = tantivy::Term::from_field_text(self.extension_field, &ext_lower);
 254 |                         tantivy::query::TermQuery::new(term, IndexRecordOption::Basic)
 255 |                     })
 256 |                     .collect();
 257 | 
 258 |                 if !extension_queries.is_empty() {
 259 |                     let extension_bool_query = tantivy::query::BooleanQuery::new(
 260 |                         extension_queries
 261 |                             .into_iter()
 262 |                             .map(|q| (Occur::Should, Box::new(q) as Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>))
 263 |                             .collect(),
 264 |                     );
 265 |                     combine.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(extension_bool_query)));
 266 |                 }
 267 |             }
 268 |         }
 269 | 
 270 |         let final_query: Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query> = if combine.len() == 1 {
 271 |             combine.remove(0).1
 272 |         } else {
 273 |             Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(combine))
 274 |         };
 275 | 
 276 |         let top_docs = searcher
 277 |             .search(&*final_query, &TopDocs::with_limit(limit))
 278 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::search(query, e.to_string()))?;
 279 | 
 280 |         let mut results = Vec::with_capacity(top_docs.len().min(limit));
 281 | 
 282 |         // Create snippet generator once outside the loop
 283 |         let snippet_generator =
 284 |             SnippetGenerator::create(&searcher, &*final_query, self.content_field)?;
 285 | 
 286 |         for (score, doc_address) in top_docs {
 287 |             let retrieved_doc: TantivyDocument = searcher.doc(doc_address).map_err(|e| {
 288 |                 FlashError::search(query, format!("Failed to retrieve document: {}", e))
 289 |             })?;
 290 | 
 291 |             let file_path = retrieved_doc
 292 |                 .get_first(self.path_field)
 293 |                 .and_then(|f| f.as_str())
 294 |                 .map(|s: &str| s.to_string())
 295 |                 .unwrap_or_default();
 296 | 
 297 |             let title = retrieved_doc
 298 |                 .get_first(self.title_field)
 299 |                 .and_then(|f| f.as_str())
 300 |                 .map(|s: &str| s.to_string());
 301 | 
 302 |             // Generate snippet using the pre-created generator
 303 |             let snippet = snippet_generator.snippet_from_doc(&retrieved_doc);
 304 |             // Get snippet as HTML then strip tags for plain text display
 305 |             let snippet_html = snippet.to_html();
 306 |             let snippet_text = snippet_html
 307 |                 .replace("<b>", "")
 308 |                 .replace("</b>", "")
 309 |                 .replace("&lt;", "<")
 310 |                 .replace("&gt;", ">")
 311 |                 .replace("&amp;", "&");
 312 | 
 313 |             results.push(SearchResult {
 314 |                 file_path,
 315 |                 title,
 316 |                 score,
 317 |                 matched_terms: highlight_terms.clone(),
 318 |                 snippet: Some(snippet_text),
 319 |             });
 320 | 
 321 |             if results.len() >= limit {
 322 |                 break;
 323 |             }
 324 |         }
 325 | 
 326 |         // Cache the results
 327 |         self.cache.insert(cache_key, results.clone());
 328 | 
 329 |         Ok(results)
 330 |     }
 331 | 
 332 |     /// Build a fuzzy query for better typo tolerance
 333 |     fn build_fuzzy_query(&self, text_query: &str) -> Result<Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>> {
 334 |         // Check if it's a phrase query (contains quoted strings)
 335 |         static PHRASE_REGEX: OnceLock<regex::Regex> = OnceLock::new();
 336 |         let phrase_regex = PHRASE_REGEX.get_or_init(|| regex::Regex::new(r#""([^"]+)""#).unwrap());
 337 | 
 338 |         if phrase_regex.is_match(text_query) {
 339 |             // For phrase queries, use the query parser with phrase support
 340 |             return Ok(Box::new(
 341 |                 self.query_parser
 342 |                     .parse_query(text_query)
 343 |                     .map_err(|e| FlashError::search(text_query, e.to_string()))?,
 344 |             ));
 345 |         }
 346 | 
 347 |         // For regular queries, build a fuzzy query with OR for each term
 348 |         let terms: Vec<&str> = text_query.split_whitespace().collect();
 349 | 
 350 |         if terms.is_empty() || (terms.len() == 1 && terms[0] == "*") {
 351 |             // Match all
 352 |             return Ok(Box::new(tantivy::query::AllQuery));
 353 |         }
 354 | 
 355 |         if terms.len() == 1 {
 356 |             // Single term - try exact first, then fuzzy
 357 |             let term_text = terms[0];
 358 |             let term = Term::from_field_text(self.content_field, term_text);
 359 | 
 360 |             // Try exact match first (higher priority)
 361 |             let exact =
 362 |                 tantivy::query::TermQuery::new(term, tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic);
 363 | 
 364 |             // Add fuzzy variant with edit distance of 2
 365 |             let fuzzy_term = Term::from_field_text(self.content_field, term_text);
 366 |             let fuzzy = FuzzyTermQuery::new(fuzzy_term, 2, true);
 367 | 
 368 |             // Combine with OR (exact first)
 369 |             let combined = BooleanQuery::new(vec![
 370 |                 (Occur::Should, Box::new(exact)),
 371 |                 (Occur::Should, Box::new(fuzzy)),
 372 |             ]);
 373 | 
 374 |             Ok(Box::new(combined))
 375 |         } else {
 376 |             // Multiple terms - build fuzzy query for each with AND logic
 377 |             let mut subqueries: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>)> = Vec::new();
 378 | 
 379 |             for term_text in terms {
 380 |                 let term = Term::from_field_text(self.content_field, term_text);
 381 | 
 382 |                 // Exact term query
 383 |                 let exact = tantivy::query::TermQuery::new(
 384 |                     term.clone(),
 385 |                     tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,
 386 |                 );
 387 | 
 388 |                 // Fuzzy variant
 389 |                 let fuzzy = FuzzyTermQuery::new(term, 2, true);
 390 | 
 391 |                 // Combine exact and fuzzy for this term
 392 |                 let term_query = BooleanQuery::new(vec![
 393 |                     (Occur::Should, Box::new(exact)),
 394 |                     (Occur::Should, Box::new(fuzzy)),
 395 |                 ]);
 396 | 
 397 |                 subqueries.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(term_query)));
 398 |             }
 399 | 
 400 |             Ok(Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(subqueries)))
 401 |         }
 402 |     }
 403 | 
 404 |     /// Invalidate the search cache (call after index updates)
 405 |     pub fn invalidate_cache(&self) {
 406 |         self.cache.invalidate();
 407 |     }
 408 | 
 409 |     /// Get statistics about the index
 410 |     pub fn get_statistics(&self) -> Result<IndexStatistics> {
 411 |         let searcher = self.reader.searcher();
 412 |         let total_docs = searcher.num_docs() as usize;
 413 | 
 414 |         let mut total_size = 0;
 415 |         if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(&self.index_path) {
 416 |             for entry in entries.flatten() {
 417 |                 if let Ok(metadata) = entry.metadata() {
 418 |                     if metadata.is_file() {
 419 |                         total_size += metadata.len();
 420 |                     }
 421 |                 }
 422 |             }
 423 |         }
 424 | 
 425 |         Ok(IndexStatistics {
 426 |             total_documents: total_docs,
 427 |             total_size_bytes: total_size,
 428 |             last_updated: Some(chrono::Local::now().format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S").to_string()),
 429 |         })
 430 |     }
 431 | }
 432 | 
 433 | #[cfg(test)]
 434 | mod tests {
 435 |     use super::*;
 436 | 
 437 |     #[test]
 438 |     fn test_cache_key_equality() {
 439 |         let key1 = CacheKey {
 440 |             query: "test".to_string(),
 441 |             limit: 10,
 442 |             min_size: None,
 443 |             max_size: None,
 444 |             extensions: None,
 445 |         };
 446 |         let key2 = CacheKey {
 447 |             query: "test".to_string(),
 448 |             limit: 10,
 449 |             min_size: None,
 450 |             max_size: None,
 451 |             extensions: None,
 452 |         };
 453 |         assert_eq!(key1, key2);
 454 |         
 455 |         let key3 = CacheKey {
 456 |             query: "diff".to_string(),
 457 |             ..key1.clone()
 458 |         };
 459 |         assert_ne!(key1, key3);
 460 |     }
 461 | 
 462 |     #[test]
 463 |     fn test_query_cache_insert_get() {
 464 |         let cache = QueryCache::new();
 465 |         let key = CacheKey {
 466 |             query: "test".to_string(),
 467 |             limit: 10,
 468 |             min_size: None,
 469 |             max_size: None,
 470 |             extensions: None,
 471 |         };
 472 |         let results = vec![SearchResult {
 473 |             file_path: "path".to_string(),
 474 |             title: None,
 475 |             score: 1.0,
 476 |             matched_terms: vec!["test".to_string()],
 477 |             snippet: Some("snippet".to_string()),
 478 |         }];
 479 |         cache.insert(key.clone(), results.clone());
 480 |         let cached = cache.get(&key);
 481 |         assert!(cached.is_some());
 482 |         assert_eq!(cached.unwrap().len(), 1);
 483 |     }
 484 | 
 485 |     #[test]
 486 |     fn test_query_cache_invalidate() {
 487 |         let cache = QueryCache::new();
 488 |         let key = CacheKey {
 489 |             query: "test".to_string(),
 490 |             limit: 10,
 491 |             min_size: None,
 492 |             max_size: None,
 493 |             extensions: None,
 494 |         };
 495 |         cache.insert(key.clone(), vec![]);
 496 |         cache.invalidate();
 497 |         assert!(cache.get(&key).is_none());
 498 |     }
 499 | }
 500 |
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
   2 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
   3 | use std::sync::Mutex;
   4 | use tantivy::schema::{Field, Schema};
   5 | use tantivy::{Index, IndexWriter, TantivyDocument};
   6 | use tracing::info;
   7 | 
   8 | /// Manages writing documents to the Tantivy index with batch support
   9 | pub struct IndexWriterManager {
  10 |     writer: Mutex<IndexWriter>,
  11 |     #[allow(dead_code)]
  12 |     schema: Schema,
  13 |     path_field: Field,
  14 |     content_field: Field,
  15 |     title_field: Field,
  16 |     modified_field: Field,
  17 |     size_field: Field,
  18 |     extension_field: Field,
  19 | }
  20 | 
  21 | impl IndexWriterManager {
  22 |     /// Calculate optimal heap size based on system resources
  23 |     /// Returns heap size in bytes (min 32MB, max 256MB)
  24 |     fn calculate_heap_size() -> usize {
  25 |         // Get system memory info (using sysinfo crate would be better, but this is lightweight)
  26 |         let available_memory = std::env::var("FLASH_SEARCH_MEMORY_MB")
  27 |             .ok()
  28 |             .and_then(|s| s.parse::<usize>().ok())
  29 |             .map(|mb| mb * 1_000_000)
  30 |             .unwrap_or_else(|| {
  31 |                 // Default calculation: use 5% of system memory, capped at 256MB
  32 |                 // This is a heuristic - in production, use sysinfo to get actual memory
  33 |                 let mut sys = sysinfo::System::new_all();
  34 |                 sys.refresh_memory();
  35 |                 let system_memory = sys.total_memory() as usize;
  36 | 
  37 |                 (system_memory / 20).clamp(32_000_000, 256_000_000)
  38 |             });
  39 | 
  40 |         available_memory.clamp(32_000_000, 256_000_000)
  41 |     }
  42 | 
  43 |     pub fn new(index: &Index, _memory_limit_mb: u32) -> Result<Self> {
  44 |         let schema = index.schema();
  45 | 
  46 |         // Configure with adaptive heap size based on system resources
  47 |         let heap_size = Self::calculate_heap_size();
  48 |         info!(
  49 |             "Tantivy index writer heap size: {} MB",
  50 |             heap_size / 1_000_000
  51 |         );
  52 | 
  53 |         let writer = index
  54 |             .writer(heap_size)
  55 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to create index writer: {}", e)))?;
  56 | 
  57 |         let path_field = schema
  58 |             .get_field("file_path")
  59 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("file_path", "Field not found in schema"))?;
  60 |         let content_field = schema
  61 |             .get_field("content")
  62 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("content", "Field not found in schema"))?;
  63 |         let title_field = schema
  64 |             .get_field("title")
  65 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("title", "Field not found in schema"))?;
  66 |         let modified_field = schema
  67 |             .get_field("modified")
  68 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("modified", "Field not found in schema"))?;
  69 |         let size_field = schema
  70 |             .get_field("size")
  71 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("size", "Field not found in schema"))?;
  72 |         let extension_field = schema
  73 |             .get_field("extension")
  74 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("extension", "Field not found in schema"))?;
  75 | 
  76 |         Ok(Self {
  77 |             writer: Mutex::new(writer),
  78 |             schema,
  79 |             path_field,
  80 |             content_field,
  81 |             title_field,
  82 |             modified_field,
  83 |             size_field,
  84 |             extension_field,
  85 |         })
  86 |     }
  87 | 
  88 |     /// Add a single document to the index
  89 |     /// Note: For better performance, use add_documents_batch for multiple docs
  90 |     pub fn add_document(&self, doc: &ParsedDocument, modified: u64, size: u64) -> Result<()> {
  91 |         let tantivy_doc = self.create_tantivy_document(doc, modified, size);
  92 | 
  93 |         let writer = self
  94 |             .writer
  95 |             .lock()
  96 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
  97 | 
  98 |         writer
  99 |             .add_document(tantivy_doc)
 100 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to add document: {}", e)))?;
 101 | 
 102 |         Ok(())
 103 |     }
 104 | 
 105 |     /// Add multiple documents in a single lock acquisition (much more efficient)
 106 |     pub fn add_documents_batch(&self, docs: &[(ParsedDocument, u64, u64)]) -> Result<()> {
 107 |         if docs.is_empty() {
 108 |             return Ok(());
 109 |         }
 110 | 
 111 |         let writer = self
 112 |             .writer
 113 |             .lock()
 114 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
 115 | 
 116 |         for (doc, modified, size) in docs {
 117 |             let tantivy_doc = self.create_tantivy_document(doc, *modified, *size);
 118 |             writer
 119 |                 .add_document(tantivy_doc)
 120 |                 .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to add document: {}", e)))?;
 121 |         }
 122 | 
 123 |         Ok(())
 124 |     }
 125 | 
 126 |     /// Create a Tantivy document from ParsedDocument
 127 |     #[inline]
 128 |     fn create_tantivy_document(
 129 |         &self,
 130 |         doc: &ParsedDocument,
 131 |         modified: u64,
 132 |         size: u64,
 133 |     ) -> TantivyDocument {
 134 |         let mut document = TantivyDocument::default();
 135 | 
 136 |         document.add_text(self.path_field, &doc.path);
 137 |         document.add_text(self.content_field, &doc.content);
 138 | 
 139 |         if let Some(ref title) = doc.title {
 140 |             document.add_text(self.title_field, title);
 141 |         }
 142 | 
 143 |         let modified_date = tantivy::DateTime::from_timestamp_secs(modified as i64);
 144 |         document.add_date(self.modified_field, modified_date);
 145 |         document.add_u64(self.size_field, size);
 146 | 
 147 |         // Index file extension for fast filtering
 148 |         if let Some(ext) = std::path::Path::new(&doc.path)
 149 |             .extension()
 150 |             .and_then(|e| e.to_str())
 151 |         {
 152 |             document.add_text(self.extension_field, ext.to_lowercase());
 153 |         }
 154 | 
 155 |         document
 156 |     }
 157 | 
 158 |     /// Remove a document from the index
 159 |     pub fn remove_document(&self, path: &str) -> Result<()> {
 160 |         let writer = self
 161 |             .writer
 162 |             .lock()
 163 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
 164 | 
 165 |         let term = tantivy::Term::from_field_text(self.path_field, path);
 166 |         writer.delete_term(term);
 167 | 
 168 |         Ok(())
 169 |     }
 170 | 
 171 |     /// Delete all documents from the index
 172 |     pub fn delete_all_documents(&self) -> Result<()> {
 173 |         let writer = self
 174 |             .writer
 175 |             .lock()
 176 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
 177 | 
 178 |         writer
 179 |             .delete_all_documents()
 180 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to delete all documents: {}", e)))?;
 181 | 
 182 |         Ok(())
 183 |     }
 184 | 
 185 |     /// Commit pending changes to disk
 186 |     pub fn commit(&self) -> Result<()> {
 187 |         let mut writer = self
 188 |             .writer
 189 |             .lock()
 190 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
 191 | 
 192 |         writer
 193 |             .commit()
 194 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to commit index: {}", e)))?;
 195 | 
 196 |         Ok(())
 197 |     }
 198 | }
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};

  10 | 
  11 | #[derive(Debug, Clone, rkyv::Archive, rkyv::Serialize, rkyv::Deserialize)]
  12 | pub struct FileMetadata {
  13 |     pub path: String,
  14 |     pub modified: u64,          // Unix timestamp
  15 |     pub size: u64,              // File size in bytes
  16 |     pub content_hash: [u8; 32], // Blake3 hash for content deduplication
  17 |     pub indexed_at: u64,        // When this file was last indexed
  18 | }
  19 | 
  20 | pub type RecentFileEntry = (String, Option<String>, u64, u64);
  21 | 
  22 | /// Connection metrics for monitoring
  23 | #[derive(Debug)]
  24 | pub struct ConnectionMetrics {
  25 |     pub read_operations: AtomicU64,
  26 |     pub write_operations: AtomicU64,
  27 |     pub bytes_read: AtomicU64,
  28 |     pub bytes_written: AtomicU64,
  29 | }
  30 | 
  31 | impl Default for ConnectionMetrics {
  32 |     fn default() -> Self {
  33 |         Self {
  34 |             read_operations: AtomicU64::new(0),
  35 |             write_operations: AtomicU64::new(0),
  36 |             bytes_read: AtomicU64::new(0),
  37 |             bytes_written: AtomicU64::new(0),
  38 |         }
  39 |     }
  40 | }
  41 | 
  42 | /// Snapshot of metrics for reporting
  43 | #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
  44 | pub struct ConnectionMetricsSnapshot {
  45 |     pub read_operations: u64,
  46 |     pub write_operations: u64,
  47 |     pub bytes_read: u64,
  48 |     pub bytes_written: u64,
  49 | }
  50 | 
  51 | /// Manages file metadata database using redb
  52 | /// Implements connection pooling pattern for redb (even though it's embedded)
  53 | /// to ensure proper resource management and monitoring
  54 | pub struct MetadataDb {
  55 |     db: Arc<Database>,
  56 |     metrics: Arc<ConnectionMetrics>,
  57 | }
  58 | 
  59 | impl MetadataDb {
  60 |     /// Open or create the metadata database
  61 |     pub fn open(db_path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
  62 |         let db = Arc::new(Database::create(db_path).map_err(|e| {
  63 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
  64 |         })?);
  65 | 
  66 |         // Create table if it doesn't exist
  67 |         let txn = db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
  68 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
  69 |         })?;
  70 |         {
  71 |             let _table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
  72 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
  73 |             })?;
  74 |         }
  75 |         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
  76 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
  77 |         })?;
  78 | 
  79 |         Ok(Self {
  80 |             db,
  81 |             metrics: Arc::new(ConnectionMetrics::default()),
  82 |         })
  83 |     }
  84 | 
  85 |     /// Clone with shared state (for multi-threaded access)
  86 |     pub fn clone_for_thread(&self) -> Self {
  87 |         Self {
  88 |             db: Arc::clone(&self.db),
  89 |             metrics: Arc::clone(&self.metrics),
  90 |         }
  91 |     }
  92 | 
  93 |     /// Get current metrics snapshot
  94 |     pub fn get_metrics(&self) -> ConnectionMetricsSnapshot {
  95 |         ConnectionMetricsSnapshot {
  96 |             read_operations: self.metrics.read_operations.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
  97 |             write_operations: self.metrics.write_operations.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
  98 |             bytes_read: self.metrics.bytes_read.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
  99 |             bytes_written: self.metrics.bytes_written.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
 100 |         }
 101 |     }
 102 | 
 103 |     /// Check if file needs reindexing based on modification time and hash
 104 |     pub fn needs_reindex(&self, path: &Path, modified: u64, size: u64) -> Result<bool> {
 105 |         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
 106 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 107 |         })?;
 108 | 
 109 |         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
 110 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 111 |         })?;
 112 | 
 113 |         let path_str = path.to_str().unwrap_or("");
 114 | 
 115 |         let result = match table
 116 |             .get(path_str)
 117 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string()))?
 118 |         {
 119 |             Some(metadata) => {
 120 |                 let bytes = metadata.value();
 121 |                 self.metrics.read_operations.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
 122 |                 self.metrics
 123 |                     .bytes_read
 124 |                     .fetch_add(bytes.len() as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
 125 |                 // Zero-copy read and validate
 126 |                 if let Ok(meta) =
 127 |                     rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
 128 |                 {
 129 |                     meta.modified != modified || meta.size != size
 130 |                 } else {
 131 |                     true // Reindex if validation fails
 132 |                 }
 133 |             }
 134 |             None => true, // File not indexed yet
 135 |         };
 136 | 
 137 |         Ok(result)
 138 |     }
 139 | 
 140 |     /// Update file metadata after indexing
 141 |     pub fn update_metadata(
 142 |         &self,
 143 |         path: &Path,
 144 |         modified: u64,
 145 |         size: u64,
 146 |         content_hash: [u8; 32],
 147 |     ) -> Result<()> {
 148 |         let txn = self.db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
 149 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 150 |         })?;
 151 | 
 152 |         {
 153 |             let mut table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
 154 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 155 |             })?;
 156 | 
 157 |             let metadata = FileMetadata {
 158 |                 path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
 159 |                 modified,
 160 |                 size,
 161 |                 content_hash,
 162 |                 indexed_at: SystemTime::now()
 163 |                     .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
 164 |                     .unwrap_or_default()
 165 |                     .as_secs(),
 166 |             };
 167 | 
 168 |             let bytes = rkyv::to_bytes::<rkyv::rancor::Error>(&metadata).map_err(|e| {
 169 |                 FlashError::database(
 170 |                     "database_operation",
 171 |                     "files_table",
 172 |                     format!("Serialization error: {}", e),
 173 |                 )
 174 |             })?;
 175 | 
 176 |             table
 177 |                 .insert(path.to_str().unwrap_or(""), bytes.as_slice())
 178 |                 .map_err(|e| {
 179 |                     FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 180 |                 })?;
 181 |         }
 182 | 
 183 |         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
 184 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 185 |         })?;
 186 | 
 187 |         Ok(())
 188 |     }
 189 | 
 190 |     /// Remove a file from the metadata database
 191 |     pub fn remove_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<bool> {
 192 |         let txn = self.db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
 193 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 194 |         })?;
 195 | 
 196 |         let existed = {
 197 |             let mut table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
 198 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 199 |             })?;
 200 | 
 201 |             let path_str = path.to_str().unwrap_or("");
 202 |             let removed = table.remove(path_str).map_err(|e| {
 203 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 204 |             })?;
 205 |             removed.is_some()
 206 |         };
 207 | 
 208 |         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
 209 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 210 |         })?;
 211 | 
 212 |         Ok(existed)
 213 |     }
 214 | 
 215 |     /// Clear all metadata (nuke the table)
 216 |     pub fn clear(&self) -> Result<()> {
 217 |         let txn = self.db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
 218 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 219 |         })?;
 220 | 
 221 |         {
 222 |             // Deleting the table is the fastest way to clear it
 223 |             txn.delete_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
 224 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 225 |             })?;
 226 |             // We must recreate it in the same transaction or next open?
 227 |             // Actually, open_table in next usage will recreate it if we create it here?
 228 |             // Safer to just open it again to ensure it exists empty.
 229 |             let _ = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
 230 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 231 |             })?;
 232 |         }
 233 | 
 234 |         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
 235 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 236 |         })?;
 237 | 
 238 |         Ok(())
 239 |     }
 240 | 
 241 |     /// Get metadata for a specific file
 242 |     pub fn get_metadata(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<Option<FileMetadata>> {
 243 |         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
 244 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 245 |         })?;
 246 | 
 247 |         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
 248 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 249 |         })?;
 250 | 
 251 |         let result = match table
 252 |             .get(path.to_str().unwrap_or(""))
 253 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string()))?
 254 |         {
 255 |             Some(metadata) => {
 256 |                 let bytes = metadata.value();
 257 |                 if let Ok(meta) =
 258 |                     rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
 259 |                 {
 260 |                     rkyv::deserialize::<FileMetadata, rkyv::rancor::Error>(meta).ok()
 261 |                 } else {
 262 |                     None
 263 |                 }
 264 |             }
 265 |             None => None,
 266 |         };
 267 | 
 268 |         Ok(result)
 269 |     }
 270 | 
 271 |     /// Batch update metadata for multiple files (much more efficient)
 272 |     /// Updates all files in a single transaction to minimize I/O overhead
 273 |     pub fn batch_update_metadata(
 274 |         &self,
 275 |         entries: &[(String, u64, u64, [u8; 32])], // (path, modified, size, hash)
 276 |     ) -> Result<usize> {
 277 |         if entries.is_empty() {
 278 |             return Ok(0);
 279 |         }
 280 | 
 281 |         let txn = self.db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
 282 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 283 |         })?;
 284 | 
 285 |         let indexed_at = SystemTime::now()
 286 |             .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
 287 |             .unwrap_or_default()
 288 |             .as_secs();
 289 | 
 290 |         let mut total_bytes_written = 0u64;
 291 | 
 292 |         {
 293 |             let mut table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
 294 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 295 |             })?;
 296 | 
 297 |             for (path, modified, size, content_hash) in entries {
 298 |                 let metadata = FileMetadata {
 299 |                     path: path.clone(),
 300 |                     modified: *modified,
 301 |                     size: *size,
 302 |                     content_hash: *content_hash,
 303 |                     indexed_at,
 304 |                 };
 305 | 
 306 |                 let bytes = rkyv::to_bytes::<rkyv::rancor::Error>(&metadata).map_err(|e| {
 307 |                     FlashError::database(
 308 |                         "database_operation",
 309 |                         "files_table",
 310 |                         format!("Serialization error: {}", e),
 311 |                     )
 312 |                 })?;
 313 | 
 314 |                 total_bytes_written += bytes.len() as u64;
 315 |                 total_bytes_written += path.len() as u64;
 316 | 
 317 |                 table.insert(path.as_str(), bytes.as_slice()).map_err(|e| {
 318 |                     FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 319 |                 })?;
 320 |             }
 321 |         }
 322 | 
 323 |         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
 324 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 325 |         })?;
 326 | 
 327 |         // Update metrics
 328 |         self.metrics
 329 |             .write_operations
 330 |             .fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
 331 |         self.metrics
 332 |             .bytes_written
 333 |             .fetch_add(total_bytes_written, Ordering::Relaxed);
 334 | 
 335 |         Ok(entries.len())
 336 |     }
 337 | 
 338 |     /// Batch check which files need reindexing
 339 |     /// Returns a vector of booleans indicating if each file needs reindexing
 340 |     pub fn batch_needs_reindex(
 341 |         &self,
 342 |         entries: &[(String, u64, u64)], // (path, modified, size)
 343 |     ) -> Result<Vec<bool>> {
 344 |         if entries.is_empty() {
 345 |             return Ok(vec![]);
 346 |         }
 347 | 
 348 |         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
 349 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 350 |         })?;
 351 | 
 352 |         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
 353 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 354 |         })?;
 355 | 
 356 |         let results: Vec<bool> = entries
 357 |             .iter()
 358 |             .map(|(path, modified, size)| {
 359 |                 match table.get(path.as_str()) {
 360 |                     Ok(Some(metadata)) => {
 361 |                         let bytes = metadata.value();
 362 |                         if let Ok(meta) =
 363 |                             rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
 364 |                         {
 365 |                             meta.modified != *modified || meta.size != *size
 366 |                         } else {
 367 |                             true
 368 |                         }
 369 |                     }
 370 |                     Ok(None) => true, // File not indexed yet
 371 |                     Err(e) => {
 372 |                         // Log error but assume safe to reindex rather than silently reindexing
 373 |                         tracing::warn!("Error reading metadata for '{}': {}", path, e);
 374 |                         true
 375 |                     }
 376 |                 }
 377 |             })
 378 |             .collect();
 379 | 
 380 |         Ok(results)
 381 |     }
 382 | 
 383 |     /// Get recently modified files sorted by modification time
 384 |     /// Note: This loads all files into memory. For large datasets, consider using a separate index.
 385 |     pub fn get_recent_files(&self, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<RecentFileEntry>> {
 386 |         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
 387 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 388 |         })?;
 389 | 
 390 |         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
 391 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
 392 |         })?;
 393 | 
 394 |         let mut files: Vec<(String, u64, u64)> = table
 395 |             .iter()
 396 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string()))?
 397 |             .filter_map(|entry| {
 398 |                 entry.ok().map(|(k, v)| {
 399 |                     let bytes = v.value();
 400 |                     if let Ok(meta) =
 401 |                         rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
 402 |                     {
 403 |                         (
 404 |                             k.value().to_string(),
 405 |                             meta.modified.to_native(),
 406 |                             meta.size.to_native(),
 407 |                         )
 408 |                     } else {
 409 |                         (k.value().to_string(), 0, 0)
 410 |                     }
 411 |                 })
 412 |             })
 413 |             .collect();
 414 | 
 415 |         // Use select_nth_unstable for O(n) partial sort instead of O(n log n) full sort
 416 |         if files.len() > limit {
 417 |             files.select_nth_unstable_by(limit, |a, b| b.1.cmp(&a.1)); // Reverse order for max-heap behavior
 418 |             files.truncate(limit);
 419 |         } else {
 420 |             files.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.cmp(&a.1));
 421 |         }
 422 | 
 423 |         // Convert to the expected format (without titles for now, can be enhanced)
 424 |         Ok(files
 425 |             .into_iter()
 426 |             .map(|(path, modified, size)| (path, None, modified, size))
 427 |             .collect())
 428 |     }
 429 | }
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
   2 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
   3 | use litchi::Document;
   4 | use std::path::Path;
   5 | 
   6 | const MAX_TEXT_LENGTH: usize = 10 * 1024 * 1024; // 10MB limit - reasonable for memory
   7 | 
   8 | pub fn parse_docx(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
   9 |     let doc = Document::open(path)
  10 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open document: {}", e)))?;
  11 | 
  12 |     let mut text = doc
  13 |         .text()
  14 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to extract text: {}", e)))?;
  15 | 
  16 |     // Truncate immediately to avoid holding full content in memory
  17 |     if text.len() > MAX_TEXT_LENGTH {
  18 |         text.truncate(MAX_TEXT_LENGTH);
  19 |     }
  20 | 
  21 |     let title = path.file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string());
  22 | 
  23 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
  24 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
  25 |         content: text,
  26 |         title,
  27 |     })
  28 | }
  29 | 
  30 | #[cfg(test)]
  31 | mod tests {
  32 |     // use super::*;
  33 | 
  34 |     #[test]
  35 |     fn test_docx_parsing_placeholder() {}
  36 | }
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
   2 | use crate::parsers::memory_map;
   3 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
   4 | use quick_xml::events::Event;
   5 | use quick_xml::Reader;
   6 | use std::io::Read;
   7 | use std::path::Path;
   8 | use tracing::warn;
   9 | use zip::ZipArchive;
  10 | 
  11 | const MAX_HTML_SIZE: usize = 50 * 1024 * 1024;
  12 | const MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_SIZE: usize = 200 * 1024 * 1024;
  13 | 
  14 | pub fn parse_epub(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
  15 |     let bytes = memory_map::read_file(path)?;
  16 | 
  17 |     let cursor = std::io::Cursor::new(bytes);
  18 |     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(cursor)
  19 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to read EPUB archive: {}", e)))?;
  20 | 
  21 |     let mut combined_text = String::with_capacity(1024 * 1024);
  22 |     let mut total_extracted_size: usize = 0;
  23 | 
  24 |     let file_names: Vec<String> = archive.file_names().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect();
  25 | 
  26 |     for name in file_names {
  27 |         if name.ends_with(".xhtml") || name.ends_with(".html") {
  28 |             match archive.by_name(&name) {
  29 |                 Ok(mut inner_file) => {
  30 |                     if inner_file.size() > MAX_HTML_SIZE as u64 {
  31 |                         warn!(
  32 |                             "Skipping large HTML file {} in EPUB ({} bytes)",
  33 |                             name,
  34 |                             inner_file.size()
  35 |                         );
  36 |                         continue;
  37 |                     }
  38 | 
  39 |                     let mut content = String::with_capacity(inner_file.size() as usize);
  40 | 
  41 |                     match inner_file.read_to_string(&mut content) {
  42 |                         Ok(_) => {
  43 |                             if total_extracted_size + content.len() > MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_SIZE {
  44 |                                 warn!(
  45 |                                     "EPUB {} has exceeded maximum text size limit",
  46 |                                     path.display()
  47 |                                 );
  48 |                                 break;
  49 |                             }
  50 | 
  51 |                             extract_text_from_html(&content, &mut combined_text);
  52 |                             total_extracted_size += content.len();
  53 |                         }
  54 |                         Err(e) => {
  55 |                             warn!("Failed to read {} from EPUB: {}", name, e);
  56 |                         }
  57 |                     }
  58 |                 }
  59 |                 Err(e) => {
  60 |                     warn!("Failed to access {} in EPUB: {}", name, e);
  61 |                 }
  62 |             }
  63 |         }
  64 |     }
  65 | 
  66 |     drop(archive);
  67 | 
  68 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
  69 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
  70 |         content: combined_text.trim().to_string(),
  71 |         title: extract_epub_title(path).ok(),
  72 |     })
  73 | }
  74 | 
  75 | fn extract_text_from_html(html: &str, output: &mut String) {
  76 |     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(html);
  77 |     reader.config_mut().trim_text(true);
  78 | 
  79 |     let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(1024);
  80 | 
  81 |     loop {
  82 |         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
  83 |             Ok(Event::Text(e)) => {
  84 |                 if let Ok(decoded) = e.decode() {
  85 |                     let text = match quick_xml::escape::unescape(&decoded) {
  86 |                         Ok(unescaped) => unescaped.into_owned(),
  87 |                         Err(_) => decoded.into_owned(),
  88 |                     };
  89 |                     output.push_str(&text);
  90 |                     output.push(' ');
  91 |                 }
  92 |             }
  93 |             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
  94 |             Err(e) => {
  95 |                 warn!("HTML parsing error: {}", e);
  96 |                 break;
  97 |             }
  98 |             _ => {}
  99 |         }
 100 |         buf.clear();
 101 |     }
 102 | }
 103 | 
 104 | fn extract_epub_title(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
 105 |     let bytes = memory_map::read_file(path)?;
 106 |     let cursor = std::io::Cursor::new(bytes);
 107 |     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(cursor)
 108 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to read EPUB: {}", e)))?;
 109 | 
 110 |     let op_path = if let Ok(mut container_xml) = archive.by_name("META-INF/container.xml") {
 111 |         let mut content = String::new();
 112 |         if container_xml.read_to_string(&mut content).is_ok() {
 113 |             extract_opf_path(&content)
 114 |         } else {
 115 |             None
 116 |         }
 117 |     } else {
 118 |         None
 119 |     };
 120 | 
 121 |     if let Some(opf_path) = op_path {
 122 |         if let Ok(mut opf_file) = archive.by_name(&opf_path) {
 123 |             let mut opf_content = String::new();
 124 |             if opf_file.read_to_string(&mut opf_content).is_ok() {
 125 |                 if let Some(title) = extract_title_from_opf(&opf_content) {
 126 |                     return Ok(title);
 127 |                 }
 128 |             }
 129 |         }
 130 |     }
 131 | 
 132 |     path.file_stem()
 133 |         .map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string())
 134 |         .ok_or_else(|| FlashError::parse(path, "Could not extract title"))
 135 | }
 136 | 
 137 | fn extract_opf_path(container_xml: &str) -> Option<String> {
 138 |     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(container_xml);
 139 |     let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(512);
 140 | 
 141 |     loop {
 142 |         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
 143 |             Ok(Event::Empty(e)) | Ok(Event::Start(e)) => {
 144 |                 if e.name().as_ref() == b"rootfile" {
 145 |                     for attr in e.attributes().flatten() {
 146 |                         if attr.key.as_ref() == b"full-path" {
 147 |                             if let Ok(path) = std::str::from_utf8(&attr.value) {
 148 |                                 return Some(path.to_string());
 149 |                             }
 150 |                         }
 151 |                     }
 152 |                 }
 153 |             }
 154 |             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
 155 |             _ => {}
 156 |         }
 157 |         buf.clear();
 158 |     }
 159 | 
 160 |     Some("OEBPS/content.opf".to_string())
 161 | }
 162 | 
 163 | fn extract_title_from_opf(opf_content: &str) -> Option<String> {
 164 |     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(opf_content);
 165 |     let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(1024);
 166 |     let mut in_title = false;
 167 | 
 168 |     loop {
 169 |         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
 170 |             Ok(Event::Start(e)) => {
 171 |                 if e.name().as_ref() == b"dc:title" {
 172 |                     in_title = true;
 173 |                 }
 174 |             }
 175 |             Ok(Event::Text(e)) => {
 176 |                 if in_title {
 177 |                     if let Ok(decoded) = e.decode() {
 178 |                         let text = match quick_xml::escape::unescape(&decoded) {
 179 |                             Ok(unescaped) => unescaped.into_owned(),
 180 |                             Err(_) => decoded.into_owned(),
 181 |                         };
 182 |                         let title = text;
 183 |                         if !title.trim().is_empty() {
 184 |                             return Some(title);
 185 |                         }
 186 |                     }
 187 |                 }
 188 |             }
 189 |             Ok(Event::End(e)) => {
 190 |                 if e.name().as_ref() == b"dc:title" {
 191 |                     in_title = false;
 192 |                 }
 193 |             }
 194 |             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
 195 |             _ => {}
 196 |         }
 197 |         buf.clear();
 198 |     }
 199 | 
 200 |     None
 201 | }
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
   2 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
   3 | use calamine::{open_workbook, Reader, Xls, Xlsb, Xlsx};
   4 | use std::path::Path;
   5 | 
   6 | const MAX_CELLS_PER_SHEET: usize = 1_000_000;
   7 | const MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_LENGTH: usize = 50_000_000;
   8 | 
   9 | pub fn parse_excel(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
  10 |     if let Ok(result) = parse_xlsx(path) {
  11 |         return Ok(result);
  12 |     }
  13 | 
  14 |     if let Ok(result) = parse_xlsb(path) {
  15 |         return Ok(result);
  16 |     }
  17 | 
  18 |     if let Ok(result) = parse_xls(path) {
  19 |         return Ok(result);
  20 |     }
  21 | 
  22 |     Err(FlashError::parse(
  23 |         path,
  24 |         format!("Failed to parse Excel file: {}", path.display()),
  25 |     ))
  26 | }
  27 | 
  28 | fn parse_xlsx(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
  29 |     let mut workbook: Xlsx<_> = open_workbook(path)
  30 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open XLSX: {}", e)))?;
  31 | 
  32 |     extract_excel_content(path, &mut workbook)
  33 | }
  34 | 
  35 | fn parse_xlsb(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
  36 |     let mut workbook: Xlsb<_> = open_workbook(path)
  37 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open XLSB: {}", e)))?;
  38 | 
  39 |     extract_excel_content(path, &mut workbook)
  40 | }
  41 | 
  42 | fn parse_xls(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
  43 |     let mut workbook: Xls<_> = open_workbook(path)
  44 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open XLS: {}", e)))?;
  45 | 
  46 |     extract_excel_content(path, &mut workbook)
  47 | }
  48 | 
  49 | fn extract_excel_content<P, RS>(path: &Path, workbook: &mut P) -> Result<ParsedDocument>
  50 | where
  51 |     RS: std::io::Read + std::io::Seek,
  52 |     P: Reader<RS>,
  53 | {
  54 |     let mut combined_text = String::with_capacity(1024 * 1024);
  55 |     let mut total_cells = 0usize;
  56 |     let sheet_names = workbook.sheet_names().to_vec();
  57 | 
  58 |     for sheet_name in &sheet_names {
  59 |         combined_text.push_str("Sheet: ");
  60 |         combined_text.push_str(sheet_name);
  61 |         combined_text.push('\n');
  62 | 
  63 |         if let Ok(range) = workbook.worksheet_range(sheet_name) {
  64 |             for row in range.rows() {
  65 |                 for cell in row {
  66 |                     total_cells += 1;
  67 | 
  68 |                     if total_cells > MAX_CELLS_PER_SHEET {
  69 |                         break;
  70 |                     }
  71 | 
  72 |                     let cell_text = format_cell_value(cell);
  73 |                     if !cell_text.is_empty() {
  74 |                         combined_text.push_str(&cell_text);
  75 |                         combined_text.push(' ');
  76 |                     }
  77 |                 }
  78 |                 combined_text.push('\n');
  79 | 
  80 |                 if combined_text.len() > MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_LENGTH {
  81 |                     break;
  82 |                 }
  83 |             }
  84 |         }
  85 |         combined_text.push('\n');
  86 |     }
  87 | 
  88 |     let title = path.file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string());
  89 | 
  90 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
  91 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
  92 |         content: combined_text.trim().to_string(),
  93 |         title,
  94 |     })
  95 | }
  96 | 
  97 | fn format_cell_value(cell: &calamine::Data) -> String {
  98 |     match cell {
  99 |         calamine::Data::Empty => String::new(),
 100 |         calamine::Data::String(s) => s.clone(),
 101 |         calamine::Data::Float(f) => f.to_string(),
 102 |         calamine::Data::Int(i) => i.to_string(),
 103 |         calamine::Data::Bool(b) => b.to_string(),
 104 |         calamine::Data::DateTime(dt) => dt.to_string(),
 105 |         calamine::Data::Error(e) => format!("#ERROR: {:?}", e),
 106 |         _ => String::new(),
 107 |     }
 108 | }
 109 | 
 110 | #[cfg(test)]
 111 | mod tests {
 112 |     use super::*;
 113 | 
 114 |     #[test]
 115 |     fn test_format_cell_value() {
 116 |         assert_eq!(format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Empty), "");
 117 |         assert_eq!(
 118 |             format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::String("test".to_string())),
 119 |             "test"
 120 |         );
 121 |         assert_eq!(
 122 |             format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Float(std::f64::consts::PI)),
 123 |             "3.141592653589793"
 124 |         );
 125 |         assert_eq!(format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Int(42)), "42");
 126 |         assert_eq!(format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Bool(true)), "true");
 127 |     }
 128 | 
 129 |     #[test]
 130 |     fn test_parse_excel_corrupted() {
 131 |         use std::fs;
 132 |         use tempfile::tempdir;
 133 | 
 134 |         let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
 135 |         let path = temp.path().join("corrupted.xlsx");
 136 |         fs::write(&path, b"not a valid excel file").unwrap();
 137 | 
 138 |         let result = parse_excel(&path);
 139 |         assert!(result.is_err());
 140 |     }
 141 | }
```

```rust
  34 | pub fn parse_eml(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
  35 |     let content = memory_map::read_file_as_string(path)?;
  36 | 
  37 |     let mut title = String::new();
  38 |     let mut text = String::new();
  39 |     let mut in_body = false;
  40 | 
  41 |     for line in content.lines() {
  42 |         let line_lower = line.to_lowercase();
  43 | 
  44 |         if line_lower.starts_with("subject:") {
  45 |             title = line[8..].trim().to_string();
  46 |         } else if line_lower.starts_with("from:")
  47 |             || line_lower.starts_with("to:")
  48 |             || line_lower.starts_with("date:")
  49 |         {
  50 |             text.push_str(line);
  51 |             text.push(' ');
  52 |         } else if line_lower.is_empty() {
  53 |             in_body = true;
  54 |         } else if in_body {
  55 |             text.push_str(line);
  56 |             text.push(' ');
  57 |         }
  58 |     }
  59 | 
  60 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
  61 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
  62 |         content: text.trim().to_string(),
  63 |         title: if title.is_empty() { None } else { Some(title) },

  67 | pub fn parse_msg(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
  68 |     let content = memory_map::read_file(path)?;

  70 |     let mut text = String::new();

  74 |     for byte in content {
  75 |         if byte.is_ascii_graphic() || byte == b' ' || byte == b'\n' {
  76 |             current.push(byte as char);
  77 |             in_string = true;
  78 |         } else if in_string && current.len() > 3 {
  79 |             text.push_str(&current);

  82 |             in_string = false;

  97 |     })

 100 | pub fn parse_chm(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {

 102 | 
 103 |     let mut text = String::new();

 133 | pub fn parse_azw(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
 134 |     let content = memory_map::read_file(path)?;
 135 | 
 136 |     let mut text = String::new();
 137 |     let mut current = Vec::new();
 138 | 
 139 |     if content.len() > 68 && (&content[0..4] == b"TPZ" || &content[60..68] == b"BOOKMOBI") {
 140 |         // Kindle format detected
 141 |     }
 142 | 
 143 |     for byte in content {
 144 |         if byte.is_ascii_graphic() || byte == b' ' || byte == b'\n' {
 145 |             current.push(byte);
 146 |         } else if current.len() > 5 {
 147 |             if let Ok(s) = String::from_utf8(current.clone()) {
 148 |                 if s.len() > 5
 149 |                     && s.chars()
 150 |                         .all(|c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c.is_whitespace() || c == '.' || c == ',')
 151 |                 {
 152 |                     text.push_str(&s);
 153 |                     text.push(' ');
 154 |                 }
 155 |             }
 156 |             current.clear();
 157 |         } else {
 158 |             current.clear();
 159 |         }
 160 |     }
 161 | 
 162 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
 163 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
 164 |         content: text
 165 |             .split_whitespace()
 166 |             .take(10000)
 167 |             .collect::<Vec<_>>()

 170 |     })
 171 | }

 173 | pub fn parse_zip_content(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
 174 |     use zip::ZipArchive;
 175 | 
 176 |     // Use File::open instead of reading entire file into memory (P5)
 177 |     let file = std::fs::File::open(path)
 178 |         .map_err(|e: std::io::Error| FlashError::parse("open_zip", e.to_string()))?;
 179 |     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(std::io::BufReader::new(file))
 180 |         .map_err(|e: zip::result::ZipError| FlashError::parse("open_archive", e.to_string()))?;
 181 | 
 182 |     let mut all_text = String::new();
 183 |     let max_size = 10 * 1024 * 1024; // 10MB limit
 184 | 
 185 |     for i in 0..archive.len() {
 186 |         if let Ok(file) = archive.by_index(i) {
 187 |             if !file.is_dir() {
 188 |                 let name = file.name().to_lowercase();
 189 | 
 190 |                 if let Some(ext) = name.rsplit('.').next() {
 191 |                     if TEXT_EXTENSIONS.contains(ext) {
 192 |                         // Limit read size per file and total
 193 |                         let mut content = String::new();
 194 |                         // Put a cap on individual file read to avoid safe-bomb/DoS
 195 |                         let mut take = file.take(1024 * 1024); // 1MB per file
 196 |                         if take.read_to_string(&mut content).is_ok() {
 197 |                             all_text.push_str(&content);
 198 |                             all_text.push_str("\n\n");
 199 |                         }
 200 |                     }
 201 |                 }
 202 |             }
 203 |         }
 204 |         if all_text.len() >= max_size {
 205 |             break;
 206 |         }
 207 |     }
 208 | 
 209 |     if all_text.is_empty() {
 210 |         // Just return metadata if no text extracted? Or error?
 211 |         // Original returned error, but maybe return empty doc is better?
 212 |         // Let's stick to original behavior or just return empty content.
 213 |         // Returning error might be filtered out as failure.
 214 |         // Let's return empty content if valid zip but no text.
 215 |         // But if it was empty because no text files found, that's fine.
 216 |         // The original error "unsupported_format" is a bit misleading if it IS a zip.
 217 |         // Let's return valid doc with empty content.
 218 |     }
 219 | 
 220 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
 221 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
 222 |         content: all_text,
 223 |         title: None,
 224 |     })
 225 | }
 226 | 
 227 | pub fn parse_7z_content(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
 228 |     let metadata = memory_map::get_file_size(path)?;
 229 | 
 230 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
 231 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
 232 |         content: format!("7z archive: {} bytes", metadata),
 233 |         title: None,
 234 |     })
 235 | }

 237 | pub fn parse_rar_content(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {

 243 |         title: None,
 244 |     })
 245 | }

 247 | pub fn parse_legacy_office(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
 248 |     // Litchi integration is currently disabled due to crate API incompatibility
 249 |     /*
 250 |     match litchi::extract(path) {

 253 |             content: text,
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
   2 | use memmap2::Mmap;
   3 | use std::fs::File;
   4 | use std::io::Read;
   5 | use std::path::Path;
   6 | 
   7 | const MMAP_THRESHOLD: u64 = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB
   8 | const MAX_FILE_SIZE: u64 = 100 * 1024 * 1024; // 100MB
   9 | 
  10 | pub fn read_file(path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
  11 |     let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
  12 | 
  13 |     let file_size = metadata.len();
  14 | 
  15 |     if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE {
  16 |         return Err(FlashError::parse(
  17 |             path,
  18 |             format!(
  19 |                 "File too large: {} bytes (max: {})",
  20 |                 file_size, MAX_FILE_SIZE
  21 |             ),
  22 |         ));
  23 |     }
  24 | 
  25 |     if file_size > MMAP_THRESHOLD {
  26 |         read_with_mmap(path)
  27 |     } else {
  28 |         read_with_buffer(path)
  29 |     }
  30 | }
  31 | 
  32 | pub fn read_file_as_string(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
  33 |     let bytes = read_file(path)?;
  34 |     String::from_utf8(bytes).map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Invalid UTF-8: {}", e)))
  35 | }
  36 | 
  37 | fn read_with_buffer(path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
  38 |     let mut file = File::open(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
  39 |     let mut bytes = Vec::new();
  40 |     file.read_to_end(&mut bytes).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
  41 |     Ok(bytes)
  42 | }
  43 | 
  44 | fn read_with_mmap(path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
  45 |     let file = File::open(path)
  46 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open file: {}", e)))?;
  47 | 
  48 |     // SAFETY: Memory mapping a file is unsafe because another process could modify it
  49 |     // while we are reading, potentially violating Rust's memory safety guarantees.
  50 |     // For a local desktop search engine, this risk is acceptable and typically results
  51 |     // in a process crash rather than an exploitable vulnerability.
  52 |     let mmap = unsafe { Mmap::map(&file).map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))? };
  53 | 
  54 |     Ok(mmap.to_vec())
  55 | }
  56 | 
  57 | pub fn is_mmap_applicable(path: &Path) -> bool {
  58 |     std::fs::metadata(path)
  59 |         .map(|m| m.len() > MMAP_THRESHOLD)
  60 |         .unwrap_or(false)
  61 | }
  62 | 
  63 | pub fn get_file_size(path: &Path) -> Result<u64> {
  64 |     std::fs::metadata(path)
  65 |         .map(|m| m.len())
  66 |         .map_err(FlashError::Io)
  67 | }
```

```rust
   8 | pub fn parse_odf(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {

  10 |         FlashError::parse(

  12 |             format!("Failed to open ODF document {}: {}", path.display(), e),
  13 |         )

  18 |             path,

  20 |         )

  25 |     } else {
  26 |         text
  27 |     };

  33 |         content,
  34 |         title,
  35 |     })
  36 | }
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
   2 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
   3 | use pdfium_render::prelude::*;
   4 | use std::path::Path;
   5 | use tracing::warn;
   6 | 
   7 | /// Maximum PDF content size to prevent memory issues
   8 | const MAX_PDF_SIZE: u64 = 500 * 1024 * 1024; // 500MB limit
   9 | 
  10 | /// Parse PDF file using pdf-extract crate
  11 | /// Implements proper resource management and error handling
  12 | pub fn parse_pdf(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
  13 |     // Check file size first to prevent OOM
  14 |     let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path)
  15 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to read PDF metadata: {}", e)))?;
  16 | 
  17 |     if metadata.len() > MAX_PDF_SIZE {
  18 |         return Err(FlashError::parse(
  19 |             path,
  20 |             format!(
  21 |                 "PDF file too large: {} bytes (max: {})",
  22 |                 metadata.len(),
  23 |                 MAX_PDF_SIZE
  24 |             ),
  25 |         ));
  26 |     }
  27 | 
  28 |     let content = match std::panic::catch_unwind(|| -> std::result::Result<String, String> {
  29 |         let bind = Pdfium::bind_to_library(Pdfium::pdfium_platform_library_name_at_path("./"))
  30 |             .or_else(|_| Pdfium::bind_to_system_library());
  31 | 
  32 |         let pdfium = match bind {
  33 |             Ok(b) => Pdfium::new(b),
  34 |             Err(e) => return Err(format!("Failed to bind: {}", e)),
  35 |         };
  36 | 
  37 |         let document = match pdfium.load_pdf_from_file(path, None) {
  38 |             Ok(doc) => doc,
  39 |             Err(e) => return Err(format!("Failed to load: {}", e)),
  40 |         };
  41 | 
  42 |         let mut text = String::new();
  43 |         for page in document.pages().iter() {
  44 |             if let Ok(page_text) = page.text() {
  45 |                 text.push_str(&page_text.all());
  46 |                 text.push('\n');
  47 |             }
  48 |         }
  49 |         Ok(text)
  50 |     }) {
  51 |         Ok(Ok(text)) => text,
  52 |         Ok(Err(e)) => {
  53 |             warn!("Failed to extract PDF text from {}: {}", path.display(), e);
  54 |             String::new()
  55 |         }
  56 |         Err(_) => {
  57 |             warn!(
  58 |                 "PDF extraction panicked for {}. File may be corrupted or encrypted.",
  59 |                 path.display()
  60 |             );
  61 |             String::new()
  62 |         }
  63 |     };
  64 | 
  65 |     // Try to extract title from first line or filename
  66 |     let title = extract_title_from_content(&content)
  67 |         .or_else(|| path.file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string()));
  68 | 
  69 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
  70 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
  71 |         content,
  72 |         title,
  73 |     })
  74 | }
  75 | 
  76 | /// Extract title from first non-empty line of content
  77 | /// Filters out lines that are too long (likely not titles)
  78 | fn extract_title_from_content(content: &str) -> Option<String> {
  79 |     content
  80 |         .lines()
  81 |         .find(|line| {
  82 |             let trimmed = line.trim();
  83 |             !trimmed.is_empty() && trimmed.len() < 200
  84 |         })
  85 |         .map(|line| {
  86 |             let trimmed = line.trim();
  87 |             // Clean up common PDF artifacts
  88 |             trimmed
  89 |                 .replace("\u{0000}", "") // Remove null bytes
  90 |                 .replace("\r", "") // Remove carriage returns
  91 |         })
  92 | }
  93 | 
  94 | #[cfg(test)]
  95 | mod tests {
  96 |     use super::*;
  97 | 
  98 |     #[test]
  99 |     fn test_extract_title_from_content() {
 100 |         let content = "\n\n  Document Title  \n\nBody content here\n";
 101 |         assert_eq!(
 102 |             extract_title_from_content(content),
 103 |             Some("Document Title".to_string())
 104 |         );
 105 |     }
 106 | 
 107 |     #[test]
 108 |     fn test_extract_title_empty() {
 109 |         let content = "   \n\n   \n";
 110 |         assert_eq!(extract_title_from_content(content), None);
 111 |     }
 112 | 
 113 |     #[test]
 114 |     fn test_extract_title_too_long() {
 115 |         let content = "a".repeat(300);
 116 |         assert_eq!(extract_title_from_content(&content), None);
 117 |     }
 118 | }
```

```rust
   1 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
   2 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
   3 | use litchi::Presentation;
   4 | use std::path::Path;
   5 | 
   6 | const MAX_TEXT_LENGTH: usize = 50_000_000;
   7 | 
   8 | pub fn parse_pptx(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
   9 |     let pres = Presentation::open(path).map_err(|e| {
  10 |         FlashError::parse(
  11 |             path,
  12 |             format!("Failed to open presentation {}: {}", path.display(), e),
  13 |         )
  14 |     })?;
  15 | 
  16 |     let text = pres.text().map_err(|e| {
  17 |         FlashError::parse(
  18 |             path,
  19 |             format!("Failed to extract text from {}: {}", path.display(), e),
  20 |         )
  21 |     })?;
  22 | 
  23 |     let content = if text.len() > MAX_TEXT_LENGTH {
  24 |         text[..MAX_TEXT_LENGTH].to_string()
  25 |     } else {
  26 |         text
  27 |     };
  28 | 
  29 |     let slide_count = pres.slide_count().unwrap_or(0);
  30 |     let title = path
  31 |         .file_stem()
  32 |         .map(|s| format!("{} ({} slides)", s.to_string_lossy(), slide_count));
  33 | 
  34 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
  35 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
  36 |         content,
  37 |         title,
  38 |     })
  39 | }
  40 | 
  41 | #[cfg(test)]
  42 | mod tests {
  43 |     use super::*;
  44 |     use std::fs;
  45 |     use tempfile::tempdir;
  46 | 
  47 |     #[test]
  48 |     fn test_pptx_parsing_corrupted() {
  49 |         let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
  50 |         let path = temp.path().join("corrupted.pptx");
  51 |         fs::write(&path, b"not a valid pptx").unwrap();
  52 | 
  53 |         let result = parse_pptx(&path);
  54 |         assert!(result.is_err());
  55 |     }
  56 | }
```

- Size: 4276 bytes
- Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134168914525225653 }

```rust
   1 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
   2 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
   3 | use memmap2::Mmap;
   4 | use std::fs::File;
   5 | use std::io::{BufReader, Read};
   6 | use std::path::Path;
   7 | 
   8 | /// Threshold for using memory mapping (files larger than this will be memory-mapped)
   9 | const MMAP_THRESHOLD: u64 = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB
  10 | /// Maximum file size to parse (prevent DOS)
  11 | const MAX_FILE_SIZE: u64 = 100 * 1024 * 1024; // 100MB
  12 | 
  13 | /// Parse plain text files (TXT, MD, code files)
  14 | /// Uses memory mapping for large files to reduce memory usage
  15 | pub fn parse_text(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
  16 |     let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
  17 | 
  18 |     let file_size = metadata.len();
  19 | 
  20 |     // Security: skip extremely large files
  21 |     if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE {
  22 |         return Err(FlashError::parse(
  23 |             path,
  24 |             format!(
  25 |                 "File too large: {} bytes (max: {})",
  26 |                 file_size, MAX_FILE_SIZE
  27 |             ),
  28 |         ));
  29 |     }
  30 | 
  31 |     // Choose parsing strategy based on file size
  32 |     let content = if file_size > MMAP_THRESHOLD {
  33 |         parse_with_mmap(path)?
  34 |     } else {
  35 |         parse_with_buffer(path)?
  36 |     };
  37 | 
  38 |     // Extract title from first non-empty line
  39 |     let title = extract_title(&content);
  40 | 
  41 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
  42 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
  43 |         content,
  44 |         title,
  45 |     })
  46 | }
  47 | 
  48 | /// Parse small files using buffered reader (faster for small files)
  49 | fn parse_with_buffer(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
  50 |     let file = File::open(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
  51 | 
  52 |     let mut content = String::new();
  53 |     BufReader::new(file)
  54 |         .read_to_string(&mut content)
  55 |         .map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
  56 | 
  57 |     Ok(content)
  58 | }
  59 | 
  60 | /// Parse large files using memory mapping (zero-copy, better for large files)
  61 | fn parse_with_mmap(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
  62 |     let file = File::open(path)
  63 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open file: {}", e)))?;
  64 | 
  65 |     // SAFETY: Memory mapping a file is unsafe because another process could modify it
  66 |     // while we are reading, potentially violating Rust's memory safety guarantees.
  67 |     // We only read the data and accept this risk for performance reasons.
  68 |     let mmap = unsafe { Mmap::map(&file).map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))? };
  69 | 
  70 |     // Use from_utf8_lossy to handle potential invalid UTF-8 sequences without erroring out
  71 |     // and to avoid an explicit intermediate Vec allocation (mmap.to_vec()).
  72 |     // This returns Cow<str>, into_owned() converts to String.
  73 |     Ok(String::from_utf8_lossy(&mmap).into_owned())
  74 | }
  75 | 
  76 | /// Extract title from first non-empty line
  77 | fn extract_title(content: &str) -> Option<String> {
  78 |     content
  79 |         .lines()
  80 |         .find(|line| !line.trim().is_empty())
  81 |         .map(|line| {
  82 |             // Remove markdown heading markers if present
  83 |             let trimmed = line.trim();
  84 |             if let Some(stripped) = trimmed.strip_prefix("# ") {
  85 |                 stripped.to_string()
  86 |             } else if let Some(stripped) = trimmed.strip_prefix("## ") {
  87 |                 stripped.to_string()
  88 |             } else if let Some(stripped) = trimmed.strip_prefix("### ") {
  89 |                 stripped.to_string()
  90 |             } else {
  91 |                 trimmed.to_string()
  92 |             }
  93 |         })
  94 |         .filter(|line| line.len() < 200) // Sanity check: don't use entire paragraphs
  95 | }
  96 | 
  97 | #[cfg(test)]
  98 | mod tests {
  99 |     use super::*;
 100 |     use std::io::Write;
 101 |     use tempfile::NamedTempFile;
 102 | 
 103 |     #[test]
 104 |     fn test_parse_text_file() {
 105 |         let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();
 106 |         writeln!(temp_file, "# My Title").unwrap();
 107 |         writeln!(temp_file, "Some content here").unwrap();
 108 | 
 109 |         let result = parse_text(temp_file.path()).unwrap();
 110 | 
 111 |         assert!(result.content.contains("My Title"));
 112 |         assert!(result.content.contains("Some content"));
 113 |         assert_eq!(result.title, Some("My Title".to_string()));
 114 |     }
 115 | 
 116 |     #[test]
 117 |     fn test_parse_text_no_heading() {
 118 |         let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();
 119 |         writeln!(temp_file, "Just a title").unwrap();
 120 |         writeln!(temp_file, "Content here").unwrap();
 121 | 
 122 |         let result = parse_text(temp_file.path()).unwrap();
 123 | 
 124 |         assert_eq!(result.title, Some("Just a title".to_string()));
 125 |     }
 126 | }
```

```rust
 184 | 
 185 | pub struct SettingsManager {
 186 |     path: PathBuf,
 187 | }
 188 | 
 189 | impl SettingsManager {
 190 |     pub fn new(app_data_dir: &Path) -> Self {
 191 |         Self {
 192 |             path: app_data_dir.join("settings.json"),
 193 |         }
 194 |     }
 195 | 
 196 |     pub fn load(&self) -> Result<AppSettings> {
 197 |         if !self.path.exists() {
 198 |             let defaults = AppSettings::default();
 199 |             self.save(&defaults)?;
 200 |             return Ok(defaults);
 201 |         }
 202 | 
 203 |         let content = fs::read_to_string(&self.path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
 204 | 
 205 |         serde_json::from_str(&content)
 206 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("parse_settings", e.to_string()))
 207 |     }
 208 | 
 209 |     pub fn save(&self, settings: &AppSettings) -> Result<()> {
 210 |         let content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(settings)
 211 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("serialize_settings", e.to_string()))?;
 212 | 
 213 |         fs::write(&self.path, content).map_err(FlashError::Io)
 214 |     }
 215 | }
 216 |
```
