 231 |         };
 232 |

 234 |         let loaded = manager.load().unwrap();
 235 |         assert_eq!(loaded.max_results, 100);
 236 |         assert!(matches!(loaded.theme, Theme::Dark));

```rust
   1 | #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
   2 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
   3 | 
   4 | #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
   5 | pub fn register_context_menu(_enable: bool) -> crate::error::Result<()> {
   6 |     // Operations on non-windows don't do anything for now
   7 |     Ok(())
   8 | }
   9 | 
  10 | #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
  11 | pub fn register_context_menu(enable: bool) -> Result<()> {
  12 |     use std::env;
  13 |     use winreg::enums::HKEY_CURRENT_USER;
  14 |     use winreg::RegKey;
  15 | 
  16 |     let hkcu = RegKey::predef(HKEY_CURRENT_USER);
  17 |     let path = r#"Software\Classes\Directory\shell\FlashSearch"#;
  18 | 
  19 |     if enable {
  20 |         let (key, _) = hkcu
  21 |             .create_subkey(path)
  22 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
  23 | 
  24 |         key.set_value("", &"Search with Flash Search")
  25 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
  26 | 
  27 |         let icon_path = env::current_exe().unwrap_or_default();
  28 |         key.set_value("Icon", &icon_path.to_str().unwrap_or_default())
  29 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
  30 | 
  31 |         let (command_key, _) = key
  32 |             .create_subkey("command")
  33 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
  34 | 
  35 |         let command = format!("\"{}\" \"%%1\"", icon_path.to_str().unwrap_or_default());
  36 |         command_key
  37 |             .set_value("", &command)
  38 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
  39 |     } else {
  40 |         let _ = hkcu.delete_subkey_all(path);
  41 |     }
  42 |     Ok(())
  43 | }
```

   1 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
   2 | use auto_launch::AutoLaunchBuilder;
   3 | use std::env;
   4 | 
   5 | pub fn set_auto_start(enable: bool) -> Result<()> {
   6 |     let app_path = env::current_exe().map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
   7 |     let app_name = "com.flashsearch";
   8 | 
   9 |     let auto = AutoLaunchBuilder::new()
  10 |         .set_app_name(app_name)
  11 |         .set_app_path(app_path.to_str().unwrap_or_default())
  12 |         .set_macos_launch_mode(auto_launch::MacOSLaunchMode::LaunchAgent)
  13 |         .build()
  14 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("auto_start", e.to_string()))?;
  15 | 
  16 |     if enable {
  17 |         auto.enable()
  18 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("auto_start_enable", e.to_string()))?;
  19 |     } else if auto.is_enabled().unwrap_or(false) {
  20 |         auto.disable()
  21 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("auto_start_disable", e.to_string()))?;
  22 |     }
  23 | 
  24 |     Ok(())
  25 | }
```

   3 | use tray_icon::menu::{Menu, MenuItem, PredefinedMenuItem};
   4 | use tray_icon::{Icon, TrayIcon, TrayIconBuilder};
   5 | 
   6 | pub fn create_tray_icon() -> Result<TrayIcon> {
   7 |     let icon_data = include_bytes!("../../assets/logo.png");
   8 |     let image = image::load_from_memory_with_format(icon_data, ImageFormat::Png)
   9 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("tray_icon", e.to_string()))?
  10 |         .into_rgba8();
  11 |     let (width, height) = image.dimensions();
  12 |     let rgba = image.into_raw();
  13 |     let icon = Icon::from_rgba(rgba, width, height)
  14 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("tray_icon", e.to_string()))?;
  15 | 
  16 |     let tray_menu = Menu::new();
  17 |     let show_i = MenuItem::with_id("show", "Show Flash Search", true, None);
  18 |     let quit_i = MenuItem::with_id("quit", "Quit", true, None);
  19 | 
  20 |     let _ = tray_menu.append(&show_i);
  21 |     let _ = tray_menu.append(&PredefinedMenuItem::separator());
  22 |     let _ = tray_menu.append(&quit_i);
  23 | 
  24 |     let tray_icon = TrayIconBuilder::new()
  25 |         .with_id("com.flashsearch")
  26 |         .with_menu(Box::new(tray_menu))
  27 |         .with_tooltip("Flash Search")
  28 |         .with_icon(icon)
  29 |         .build()
  30 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("tray_icon", e.to_string()))?;
  31 | 
  32 |     Ok(tray_icon)
  33 | }
```

### File: `src\watcher.rs`

- Size: 11114 bytes
- Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134175067855413381 }

```rust
   1 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
   2 | use crate::indexer::IndexManager;
   3 | use crate::metadata::MetadataDb;
   4 | use crate::parsers::parse_file;
   5 | use blake3;
   6 | use notify::{Event, EventKind, RecommendedWatcher, RecursiveMode, Watcher};
   7 | use std::collections::HashMap;
   8 | use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
   9 | use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
  10 | use std::time::Duration;
  11 | use tracing::{error, info, warn};
  12 | 
  13 | #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
  14 | enum WatcherAction {
  15 |     Index,
  16 |     Remove,
  17 | }
  18 | 
  19 | /// Manages active file system watching with debouncing
  20 | pub struct WatcherManager {
  21 |     watcher: Option<RecommendedWatcher>,
  22 |     _indexer: Arc<IndexManager>,
  23 |     _metadata_db: Arc<MetadataDb>,
  24 |     _runtime_handle: tokio::runtime::Handle,
  25 |     // Buffer for pending events: Map<Path, Action>
  26 |     // WARNING: Do not hold this Mutex across .await points to avoid deadlocks.
  27 |     event_buffer: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<PathBuf, WatcherAction>>>,
  28 | }
  29 | 
  30 | impl WatcherManager {
  31 |     pub fn new(indexer: Arc<IndexManager>, metadata_db: Arc<MetadataDb>) -> Self {
  32 |         let buffer: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<PathBuf, WatcherAction>>> =
  33 |             Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new()));
  34 |         let buffer_for_task = buffer.clone();
  35 |         let indexer_for_task = indexer.clone();
  36 |         let metadata_db_for_task = metadata_db.clone();
  37 |         let runtime_handle = tokio::runtime::Handle::current();
  38 | 
  39 |         // Spawn background processor for debounced events
  40 |         runtime_handle.spawn(async move {
  41 |             loop {
  42 |                 tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(1000)).await; // Check every 1s
  43 | 
  44 |                 let events = {
  45 |                     let mut guard = match buffer_for_task.lock() {
  46 |                         Ok(g) => g,
  47 |                         Err(e) => {
  48 |                             error!("Watcher lock poisoned: {}", e);
  49 |                             continue;
  50 |                         }
  51 |                     };
  52 |                     if guard.is_empty() {
  53 |                         continue;
  54 |                     }
  55 |                     // Take all events
  56 |                     let events: HashMap<PathBuf, WatcherAction> = std::mem::take(&mut *guard);
  57 |                     events
  58 |                 };
  59 | 
  60 |                 let mut needs_commit = false;
  61 | 
  62 |                 // First pass: collect all paths that need to be removed
  63 |                 let remove_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = events
  64 |                     .iter()
  65 |                     .filter(|(_, action)| matches!(action, WatcherAction::Remove))
  66 |                     .map(|(path, _)| path.clone())
  67 |                     .collect();
  68 | 
  69 |                 // Second pass: collect all paths that need to be indexed
  70 |                 let index_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = events
  71 |                     .iter()
  72 |                     .filter(|(_, action)| matches!(action, WatcherAction::Index))
  73 |                     .map(|(path, _)| path.clone())
  74 |                     .collect();
  75 | 
  76 |                 // Process removes first
  77 |                 for path in remove_paths {
  78 |                     let path_str = path.to_string_lossy();
  79 |                     let _ = indexer_for_task.remove_document(&path_str);
  80 |                     match metadata_db_for_task.remove_file(&path) {
  81 |                         Ok(true) => {
  82 |                             needs_commit = true;
  83 |                             info!("Removed file (watcher): {:?}", path);
  84 |                         }
  85 |                         Err(e) => error!("Watcher error removing {:?}: {}", path, e),
  86 |                         _ => {}
  87 |                     }
  88 |                 }
  89 | 
  90 |                 // Then process indexes
  91 |                 let mut docs_to_add = Vec::new();
  92 |                 let mut meta_to_update = Vec::new();
  93 | 
  94 |                 for path in index_paths {
  95 |                     match Self::reindex_single_file(&path, &metadata_db_for_task).await {
  96 |                         Ok(Some((doc, modified, size, hash))) => {
  97 |                             meta_to_update.push((doc.path.clone(), modified, size, hash));
  98 |                             docs_to_add.push((doc, modified, size));
  99 |                         }
 100 |                         Ok(None) => {} // Skipped or does not exist
 101 |                         Err(e) => {
 102 |                             error!("Watcher error indexing {:?}: {}", path, e);
 103 |                         }
 104 |                     }
 105 |                 }
 106 | 
 107 |                 if !docs_to_add.is_empty() {
 108 |                     if let Err(e) = indexer_for_task.add_documents_batch(&docs_to_add) {
 109 |                         error!("Watcher error batch adding to index: {}", e);
 110 |                     }
 111 |                     if let Err(e) = metadata_db_for_task.batch_update_metadata(&meta_to_update) {
 112 |                         error!("Watcher error batch updating DB: {}", e);
 113 |                     } else {
 114 |                         info!("Re-indexed {} files (watcher)", docs_to_add.len());
 115 |                     }
 116 |                     needs_commit = true;
 117 |                 }
 118 | 
 119 |                 if needs_commit {
 120 |                     if let Err(e) = indexer_for_task.commit() {
 121 |                         error!("Watcher failed to commit index: {}", e);
 122 |                     } else {
 123 |                         indexer_for_task.invalidate_cache();
 124 |                         info!("Watcher committed changes");
 125 |                     }
 126 |                 }
 127 |             }
 128 |         });
 129 | 
 130 |         Self {
 131 |             watcher: None,
 132 |             _indexer: indexer,
 133 |             _metadata_db: metadata_db,
 134 |             _runtime_handle: runtime_handle,
 135 |             event_buffer: buffer,
 136 |         }
 137 |     }
 138 | 
 139 |     /// Update the list of watched directories
 140 |     pub fn update_watch_list(&mut self, dirs: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {
 141 |         self.watcher = None;
 142 | 
 143 |         if dirs.is_empty() {
 144 |             return Ok(());
 145 |         }
 146 | 
 147 |         let buffer_clone = self.event_buffer.clone();
 148 | 
 149 |         let mut watcher = notify::recommended_watcher(move |res: notify::Result<Event>| {
 150 |             if let Ok(event) = res {
 151 |                 let mut guard = match buffer_clone.lock() {
 152 |                     Ok(g) => g,
 153 |                     Err(e) => {
 154 |                         error!("Watcher lock poisoned: {}", e);
 155 |                         return;
 156 |                     }
 157 |                 };
 158 | 
 159 |                 match event.kind {
 160 |                     EventKind::Modify(_) | EventKind::Create(_) | EventKind::Remove(_) => {
 161 |                         for path in &event.paths {
 162 |                             match event.kind {
 163 |                                 EventKind::Remove(_) => {
 164 |                                     guard.insert(path.clone(), WatcherAction::Remove);
 165 |                                 }
 166 |                                 _ => {
 167 |                                     // Don't check if file exists here - let reindex_single_file handle missing files
 168 |                                     guard.insert(path.clone(), WatcherAction::Index);
 169 |                                 }
 170 |                             }
 171 |                         }
 172 |                     }
 173 |                     _ => {}
 174 |                 }
 175 |             }
 176 |         })
 177 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))?;
 178 | 
 179 |         for dir in dirs {
 180 |             let path = Path::new(&dir);
 181 |             if path.exists() {
 182 |                 watcher
 183 |                     .watch(path, RecursiveMode::Recursive)
 184 |                     .map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))?;
 185 |             }
 186 |         }
 187 | 
 188 |         self.watcher = Some(watcher);
 189 |         Ok(())
 190 |     }
 191 | 
 192 |     // Returns parsed document data if file needs re-indexing
 193 |     async fn reindex_single_file(
 194 |         path: &Path,
 195 |         metadata_db: &Arc<MetadataDb>,
 196 |     ) -> Result<Option<(crate::parsers::ParsedDocument, u64, u64, [u8; 32])>> {
 197 |         if !path.exists() {
 198 |             return Ok(None);
 199 |         }
 200 | 
 201 |         let metadata = match std::fs::metadata(path) {
 202 |             Ok(m) => m,
 203 |             Err(_) => return Ok(None), // Ignore if cannot read metadata
 204 |         };
 205 | 
 206 |         let modified = metadata
 207 |             .modified()
 208 |             .unwrap_or(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
 209 |             .duration_since(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
 210 |             .unwrap_or_default()
 211 |             .as_secs();
 212 |         let size = metadata.len();
 213 | 
 214 |         // Skip check? If watcher said it changed, it probably did.
 215 |         // But checking db saves re-hashing if it was a false alarm.
 216 |         if !metadata_db
 217 |             .needs_reindex(path, modified, size)
 218 |             .unwrap_or(true)
 219 |         {
 220 |             return Ok(None);
 221 |         }
 222 | 
 223 |         let parsed = match parse_file(path) {
 224 |             Ok(p) => p,
 225 |             Err(e) => {
 226 |                 warn!("Failed to parse file {:?}: {}", path, e);
 227 |                 return Ok(None);
 228 |             }
 229 |         };
 230 | 
 231 |         let content_hash: [u8; 32] = blake3::hash(parsed.content.as_bytes()).into();
 232 | 
 233 |         Ok(Some((parsed, modified, size, content_hash)))
 234 |     }
 235 | }
 236 | 
 237 | #[cfg(test)]
 238 | mod tests {
 239 |     use super::*;
 240 |     use crate::indexer::IndexManager;
 241 |     use crate::metadata::MetadataDb;
 242 |     use std::fs;
 243 |     use std::sync::Arc;
 244 |     use tempfile::tempdir;
 245 | 
 246 |     #[tokio::test]
 247 |     async fn test_watcher_manager_creation() {
 248 |         let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
 249 |         let indexer = Arc::new(IndexManager::open(temp.path(), 256).unwrap());
 250 |         let metadata = Arc::new(MetadataDb::open(&temp.path().join("metadata.db")).unwrap());
 251 | 
 252 |         let mut watcher = WatcherManager::new(indexer, metadata);
 253 | 
 254 |         // Add a directory to watch
 255 |         let watch_dir = temp.path().join("watch_me");
 256 |         fs::create_dir(&watch_dir).unwrap();
 257 | 
 258 |         assert!(watcher
 259 |             .update_watch_list(vec![watch_dir.to_string_lossy().to_string()])
 260 |             .is_ok());
 261 |         assert!(watcher.watcher.is_some());
 262 | 
 263 |         // Empty list should remove watcher
 264 |         assert!(watcher.update_watch_list(vec![]).is_ok());
 265 |         assert!(watcher.watcher.is_none());
 266 |     }
 267 | 
 268 |     #[tokio::test]
 269 |     async fn test_reindex_single_file() {
 270 |         use std::io::Write;
 271 |         let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
 272 |         let metadata = Arc::new(MetadataDb::open(&temp.path().join("metadata.db")).unwrap());
 273 | 
 274 |         let file_path = temp.path().join("test.txt");
 275 |         let mut file = fs::File::create(&file_path).unwrap();
 276 |         writeln!(file, "Initial content").unwrap();
 277 | 
 278 |         // Should return Some on first index
 279 |         let result = WatcherManager::reindex_single_file(&file_path, &metadata).await;
 280 |         assert!(result.is_ok());
 281 |         let option = result.unwrap();
 282 |         assert!(option.is_some());
 283 |         let (doc, modified, size, _hash) = option.unwrap();
 284 |         assert_eq!(doc.content, "Initial content\n");
 285 | 
 286 |         metadata
 287 |             .update_metadata(Path::new(&doc.path), modified, size, [0; 32])
 288 |             .unwrap();
 289 | 
 290 |         // Should return None if no change
 291 |         let result = WatcherManager::reindex_single_file(&file_path, &metadata).await;
 292 |         assert!(result.is_ok());
 293 |         assert!(result.unwrap().is_none());
 294 |     }
 295 | }

   1 | use flash_search::error::Result;

   6 | 
   7 | #[tokio::test]
   8 | async fn test_end_to_end_search() -> Result<()> {
   9 |     let temp_workspace = tempdir()?;
  10 |     let index_dir = temp_workspace.path().join("index");
  11 |     let data_dir = temp_workspace.path().join("data");
  12 |     let settings_dir = temp_workspace.path().join("settings");
  13 | 
  14 |     fs::create_dir_all(&index_dir)?;

  16 |     fs::create_dir_all(&settings_dir)?;
  17 | 
  18 |     let txt_path = data_dir.join("hello.txt");
  19 |     fs::write(&txt_path, "This is a unique test string for searching.")?;
  20 | 
  21 |     let md_path = data_dir.join("notes.md");
  22 |     fs::write(
  23 |         &md_path,
  24 |         "# Notes\n\nSome markdown content with unique keyword: flashsearchintegrationtest",
  25 |     )?;
  26 | 
  27 |     let indexer = Arc::new(IndexManager::open(&index_dir, 100)?);
  28 |     let metadata_db_path = index_dir.join("metadata.redb");
  29 |     let _metadata_db = Arc::new(MetadataDb::open(&metadata_db_path)?);
  30 | 
  31 |     let txt_doc = flash_search::parsers::parse_file(&txt_path)?;
  32 |     let md_doc = flash_search::parsers::parse_file(&md_path)?;
  33 | 
  34 |     let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()
  35 |         .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
  36 |         .unwrap()
  37 |         .as_secs();
  38 | 
  39 |     indexer.add_document(&txt_doc, now, 100)?;
  40 |     indexer.add_document(&md_doc, now, 200)?;
  41 |     indexer.commit()?;
  42 | 
  43 |     std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(500));
  44 | 
  45 |     let results = indexer
  46 |         .search("unique test string", 10, None, None, None)
  47 |         .await?;
  48 |     assert_eq!(results.len(), 1);
  49 |     assert!(results[0].file_path.contains("hello.txt"));
  50 | 
  51 |     let results = indexer
  52 |         .search("flashsearchintegrationtest", 10, None, None, None)
  53 |         .await?;
  54 |     assert_eq!(results.len(), 1);
  55 |     assert!(results[0].file_path.contains("notes.md"));
  56 | 
  57 |     Ok(())
  58 | }

   1 | # Third-Party Licenses
   2 | 
   3 | Flash Search is built upon several open-source libraries. This document provides a summary of these dependencies and their respective licenses.
   4 | 
   5 | ## Core Dependencies
   6 | 
   7 | | Library | Purpose | License |
   8 | |---------|---------|---------|
   9 | | [Slint](https://slint.dev/) | GUI Framework | GPL-3.0 / Apache-2.0 |
  10 | | [Tantivy](https://github.com/quickwit-oss/tantivy) | Search Engine | MIT |
  11 | | [redb](https://github.com/cberner/redb) | Metadata Database | MIT / Apache-2.0 |
  12 | | [Tokio](https://tokio.rs/) | Async Runtime | MIT |
  13 | | [Rayon](https://github.com/rayon-rs/rayon) | Data Parallelism | MIT / Apache-2.0 |
  14 | | [quick-xml](https://github.com/tafia/quick-xml) | XML Parsing | MIT |
  15 | | [zip-rs](https://github.com/zip-rs/zip) | ZIP Extraction | MIT |
  16 | | [pdf-extract](https://github.com/jrmuizel/pdf-extract) | PDF Parsing | MIT |
  17 | | [memmap2](https://github.com/RazrFalcon/memmap2-rs) | Memory Mapping | MIT / Apache-2.0 |
  18 | | [blake3](https://github.com/BLAKE3-team/BLAKE3) | Hashing | CC0-1.0 / Apache-2.0 |
  19 | | [mimalloc](https://github.com/microsoft/mimalloc) | Memory Allocator | MIT |
  20 | 
  21 | ## Other Dependencies
  22 | 
  23 | Most other utilities used (e.g., `anyhow`, `thiserror`, `chrono`, `walkdir`, `regex`, `arboard`, `opener`, `rfd`) are licensed under either **MIT** or **Apache-2.0**.
  24 | 
  25 | The [litchi](https://github.com/DevExzh/litchi.git) crate used for document parsing is licensed under **GPL-3.0**.
  26 | 
  27 | ---
  28 | 
  29 | For full details, please refer to the individual package metadata on [crates.io](https://crates.io).
```

### File: `agents.md`

```markdown
   1 | # AGENTS.md - AI Coding Agent Instructions
   2 | 
   3 | ## Project Overview
   4 | 
   5 | **Flash Search** is an ultrafast local full-text search application built purely with Rust. This document provides comprehensive instructions for AI coding agents working on this codebase.
   6 | 
   7 | ## Architecture Overview
   8 | 
   9 | ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  10 | │                     Flash Search                             │
  11 | ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  12 | │  UI (Iced)                                                  │
  13 | │  ├── iced_ui/       - High-performance GUI components       │
  14 | │  ├── search.rs      - Search view and logic bindings        │
  15 | │  └── settings.rs    - Settings view and logic bindings      │
  16 | ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  17 | │  Rust Backend (Glue & Logic)                                │
  18 | │  ├── commands/      - Business logic handlers               │
  19 | │  └── state/         - Application state management          │
  20 | ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  21 | │  Core Engine                                                │
  22 | │  ├── indexer/       - Tantivy search engine wrapper         │
  23 | │  ├── parsers/       - File format parsers                   │
  24 | │  ├── scanner/       - File system crawler                   │
  25 | │  └── metadata/      - redb database operations              │
  26 | └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  27 | 
  28 | ## Project Structure
  29 | 
  30 | ```
  31 | flash-search/
  32 | ├── src/
  33 | │   ├── main.rs              # Application entry point
  34 | │   ├── lib.rs               # Library exports
  35 | │   ├── error.rs             # Error types
  36 | │   ├── models.rs            # Data models
  37 | │   ├── settings.rs          # Settings management
  38 | │   ├── commands/            # Business logic (AppState)
  39 | │   ├── iced_ui/             # Iced UI implementation
  40 | │   │   ├── mod.rs           # UI entry and core state
  41 | │   │   ├── search.rs        # Search screen UI
  42 | │   │   └── settings.rs      # Settings screen UI
  43 | │   ├── parsers/
  44 | │   │   ├── mod.rs           # Parser dispatch
  45 | │   │   ├── docx.rs          # DOCX parser
  46 | │   │   └── ...              # Other parsers
  47 | │   ├── indexer/
  48 | │   │   ├── mod.rs           # Indexer module
  49 | │   │   ├── schema.rs        # Tantivy schema
  50 | │   │   └── searcher.rs      # Query execution
  51 | │   └── metadata/
  52 | │       ├── mod.rs           # Metadata DB interface
  53 | │       └── db.rs            # redb definitions
  54 | ├── Cargo.toml               # Project dependencies
  55 | └── README.md
  56 | ```
  57 | 
  58 | ## Critical Implementation Guidelines
  59 | 
  60 | ### 1. Performance First
  61 | 
  62 | **This project prioritizes performance over convenience.**
  63 | 
  64 | - **Memory**: Keep RAM usage under 30MB at idle
  65 | - **Speed**: Search results must return in <50ms
  66 | - **I/O**: Use memory-mapped files for parsing
  67 | - **UI Responsiveness**: Never block the Iced event loop
  68 | 
  69 | **DON'Ts:**
  70 | - Don't perform heavy computations in Iced `update` methods (use `Command::perform` or `tokio::spawn`)
  71 | - Don't load large datasets into the UI at once (use pagination or lazy loading if needed)
  72 | 
  73 | ### 2. Error Handling
  74 | 
  75 | Use `anyhow` for error propagation and `thiserror` for custom error types:
  76 | 
  77 | ```rust
  78 | // Good
  79 | use anyhow::{Context, Result};
  80 | 
  81 | pub fn parse_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
  82 |     let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)
  83 |         .with_context(|| format!("Failed to read file: {}", path.display()))?;
  84 |     Ok(content)
  85 | }
  86 | 
  87 | // Custom errors for specific failure modes
  88 | #[derive(Error, Debug)]
  89 | pub enum ParseError {
  90 |     #[error("Unsupported file format: {0}")]
  91 |     UnsupportedFormat(String),
  92 |     #[error("File corrupted or encrypted")]
  93 |     CorruptedFile,
  94 |     #[error("IO error: {0}")]
  95 |     Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
  96 | }
  97 | ```
  98 | 
  99 | ### 3. Concurrency Model
 100 | 
 101 | ```rust
 102 | // File watching (async I/O) -> Tokio
 103 | // File parsing (CPU-bound) -> Rayon
 104 | // Search queries -> Tantivy (already parallel)
 105 | 
 106 | // Example: Hybrid approach
 107 | use rayon::prelude::*;
 108 | use tokio::task;
 109 | 
 110 | pub async fn index_directory(path: PathBuf) -> Result<()> {
 111 |     // Walk directory (async I/O)
 112 |     let files = walk_directory(&path).await?;
 113 |     
 114 |     // Parse files in parallel (CPU-bound)
 115 |     let results: Vec<_> = files
 116 |         .par_iter()
 117 |         .map(|file| parse_file(file))
 118 |         .collect();
 119 |     
 120 |     Ok(())
 121 | }
 122 | ```
 123 | 
 124 | ### 4. Parser Implementation
 125 | 
 126 | #### DOCX Parser (Critical Performance Path)
 127 | 
 128 | ```rust
 129 | use memmap2::Mmap;
 130 | use quick_xml::events::Event;
 131 | use quick_xml::Reader;
 132 | use zip::ZipArchive;
 133 | 
 134 | pub fn parse_docx(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
 135 |     // Memory map the file
 136 |     let file = std::fs::File::open(path)?;
 137 |     let mmap = unsafe { Mmap::map(&file)? };
 138 |     
 139 |     // Stream from memory
 140 |     let cursor = std::io::Cursor::new(&mmap[..]);
 141 |     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(cursor)?;
 142 |     
 143 |     // Extract document.xml
 144 |     let mut doc_xml = archive.by_name("word/document.xml")?;
 145 |     let mut xml_content = String::new();
 146 |     doc_xml.read_to_string(&mut xml_content)?;
 147 |     
 148 |     // Stream parse XML (NO DOM!)
 149 |     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(&xml_content);
 150 |     let mut buf = Vec::new();
 151 |     let mut text = String::new();
 152 |     
 153 |     loop {
 154 |         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
 155 |             Ok(Event::Start(e)) if e.name().as_ref() == b"w:t" => {
 156 |                 // Extract text content
 157 |                 if let Ok(txt) = reader.read_text(e.name()) {
 158 |                     text.push_str(&txt);
 159 |                     text.push(' ');
 160 |                 }
 161 |             }
 162 |             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
 163 |             Err(e) => return Err(e.into()),
 164 |             _ => (),
 165 |         }
 166 |         buf.clear();
 167 |     }
 168 |     
 169 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
 170 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
 171 |         content: text,
 172 |         title: extract_title(&mut archive)?,
 173 |     })
 174 | }
 175 | ```
 176 | 
 177 | ### 5. Tantivy Schema
 178 | 
 179 | ```rust
 180 | use tantivy::schema::*;
 181 | 
 182 | pub fn create_schema() -> Schema {
 183 |     let mut schema_builder = Schema::builder();
 184 |     
 185 |     // Store file path for retrieval
 186 |     schema_builder.add_text_field(
 187 |         "file_path",
 188 |         STRING | STORED
 189 |     );
 190 |     
 191 |     // Index content but don't store (retrieved from disk on demand)
 192 |     let text_options = TextOptions::default()
 193 |         .set_indexing_options(
 194 |             TextFieldIndexing::default()
 195 |                 .set_tokenizer("default")
 196 |                 .set_index_option(IndexRecordOption::WithFreqsAndPositions)
 197 |         )
 198 |         .set_stored(false);
 199 |     schema_builder.add_text_field("content", text_options);
 200 |     
 201 |     // Store title for display
 202 |     schema_builder.add_text_field(
 203 |         "title",
 204 |         TEXT | STORED
 205 |     );
 206 |     
 207 |     // Index timestamp for sorting
 208 |     schema_builder.add_date_field(
 209 |         "modified",
 210 |         FAST | INDEXED
 211 |     );
 212 |     
 213 |     schema_builder.build()
 214 | }
 215 | ```
 216 | 
 217 | ### 6. Metadata Database (redb)
 218 | 
 219 | ```rust
 220 | use redb::{Database, TableDefinition};
 221 | 
 222 | const FILES_TABLE: TableDefinition<&str, FileMetadata> = TableDefinition::new("files");
 223 | 
 224 | #[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
 225 | pub struct FileMetadata {
 226 |     pub path: String,
 227 |     pub modified: u64,      // Unix timestamp
 228 |     pub size: u64,
 229 |     pub content_hash: [u8; 32], // blake3 hash
 230 |     pub indexed_at: u64,
 231 | }
 232 | 
 233 | pub struct MetadataDb {
 234 |     db: Database,
 235 | }
 236 | 
 237 | impl MetadataDb {
 238 |     pub fn needs_reindex(&self, path: &Path, modified: u64) -> Result<bool> {
 239 |         let txn = self.db.begin_read()?;
 240 |         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE)?;
 241 |         
 242 |         match table.get(path.to_str().unwrap())? {
 243 |             Some(metadata) => {
 244 |                 let meta = metadata.value();
 245 |                 Ok(meta.modified != modified)
 246 |             }
 247 |             None => Ok(true), // File not indexed yet
 248 |         }
 249 |     }
 250 | }
 251 | ```
 252 | 
 253 | ### 7. Iced Integration (UI Glue)
 254 | 
 255 | ```rust
 256 | use iced::{Application, Command, Element};
 257 | 
 258 | pub struct SearchApp {
 259 |     state: Arc<AppState>,
 260 |     search_query: String,
 261 |     results: Vec<FileItem>,
 262 | }
 263 | 
 264 | #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
 265 | pub enum Message {
 266 |     SearchInputChanged(String),
 267 |     PerformSearch,
 268 |     SearchResultsReceived(Vec<FileItem>),
 269 | }
 270 | 
 271 | impl Application for SearchApp {
 272 |     type Message = Message;
 273 |     // ...
 274 | 
 275 |     fn update(&mut self, message: Message) -> Command<Message> {
 276 |         match message {
 277 |             Message::SearchInputChanged(query) => {
 278 |                 self.search_query = query;
 279 |                 Command::none()
 280 |             }
 281 |             Message::PerformSearch => {
 282 |                 let state = self.state.clone();
 283 |                 let query = self.search_query.clone();
 284 |                 
 285 |                 // Offload search to async command
 286 |                 Command::perform(async move {
 287 |                     let results = state.indexer.search(&query).await.unwrap_or_default();
 288 |                     results.into_iter().map(FileItem::from).collect()
 289 |                 }, Message::SearchResultsReceived)
 290 |             }
 291 |             Message::SearchResultsReceived(results) => {
 292 |                 self.results = results;
 293 |                 Command::none()
 294 |             }
 295 |         }
 296 |     }
 297 | 
 298 |     fn view(&self) -> Element<Message> {
 299 |         // ... Iced view layout construction ...
 300 |         iced::widget::text("Search View").into()
 301 |     }
 302 | }
 303 | ```
 304 | 
 305 | ### 8. Iced Component Patterns
 306 | 
 307 | Follow the standard Elm architecture when creating view components in Iced. Keep state isolated where appropriate and fire clear descriptive messages.
 308 | 
 309 | ## Common Tasks
 310 | 
 311 | ### Adding a New File Parser
 312 | 
 313 | 1. Create `src/parsers/<format>.rs`
 314 | 2. Implement `parse_<format>(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument>`
 315 | 3. Register in `src/parsers/mod.rs` dispatch function
 316 | 4. Add MIME type detection
 317 | 5. Write unit tests with sample files
 318 | 
 319 | ### Modifying the Search Schema
 320 | 
 321 | 1. Update `src/indexer/schema.rs`
 322 | 2. **WARNING**: Schema changes require reindexing all documents
 323 | 3. Consider migration strategy for existing users
 324 | 4. Bump index version in constants
 325 | 
 326 | ### Adding a New Iced Message
 327 | 
 328 | 1. Define the message variant in the relevant `Message` enum (e.g., in `src/iced_ui/mod.rs`)
 329 | 2. Handle the message in the `update()` function
 330 | 3. Return an async `Command::perform` for any IO/backend work
 331 | 4. Ensure the view triggers the matching message on user interactions
 332 | 
 333 | ## Testing Guidelines
 334 | 
 335 | ### Unit Tests (Rust)
 336 | 
 337 | ```rust
 338 | #[cfg(test)]
 339 | mod tests {
 340 |     use super::*;
 341 |     
 342 |     #[test]
 343 |     fn test_docx_parsing() {
 344 |         let doc = parse_docx(Path::new("tests/fixtures/sample.docx"))
 345 |             .expect("Should parse DOCX");
 346 |         
 347 |         assert!(doc.content.contains("Hello World"));
 348 |         assert_eq!(doc.title, Some("Sample Document".to_string()));
 349 |     }
 350 | }
 351 | ```
 352 | 
 353 | ### Integration Tests
 354 | 
 355 | - Test with large file collections (10K+ files)
 356 | - Measure memory usage with `valgrind` or `/usr/bin/time -v`
 357 | - Benchmark search latency with `cargo bench`
 358 | 
 359 | ## Build Commands
 360 | 
 361 | ```bash
 362 | # Build and Run
 363 | cargo run
 364 | 
 365 | # Build for production
 366 | cargo build --release
 367 | 
 368 | # Run Rust tests
 369 | cargo test
 370 | ```
 371 | 
 372 | ## Troubleshooting
 373 | 
 374 | ### High Memory Usage
 375 | 
 376 | - Check for memory leaks in parsers (ensure files are closed)
 377 | - Verify Tantivy index writer buffer size
 378 | - Profile with `heaptrack` or `dhat`
 379 | 
 380 | ### Slow Search
 381 | 
 382 | - Verify Tantivy reader is cached (don't recreate)
 383 | - Check if index is memory-mapped
 384 | - Profile with `perf` or `samply`
 385 | 
 386 | ### Parser Failures
 387 | 
 388 | - Check file encoding detection
 389 | - Verify zip/xml parsing handles malformed files gracefully
 390 | - Add more comprehensive error contexts
 391 | 
 392 | ## Resources
 393 | 
 394 | - [Iced Documentation](https://docs.rs/iced/latest/iced/)
 395 | - [Tantivy Documentation](https://docs.rs/tantivy/latest/tantivy/)
 396 | - [Redb Documentation](https://docs.rs/redb/latest/redb/)
 397 | - [Rayon Documentation](https://docs.rs/rayon/latest/rayon/)
 398 | 
 399 | ## Contact
 400 | 
 401 | For questions about implementation details, refer to:
 402 | - GitHub Issues: https://github.com/yourusername/flash-search/issues
 403 | - Architecture Decisions: See `docs/adr/` directory
 404 | 
 405 | ---
 406 | 
 407 | **Last Updated**: 2024
 408 | **Version**: 0.1.0
 409 | 
 410 | ## Self-Healing Rules
 411 | - On borrow checker errors: Scan stack trace, add explicit lifetimes ('a), clone owned data where safe, or refactor to Rc<RefCell<T>> / Arc<Mutex<T>> for shared state.
 412 | - Lifetime mismatches: Infer from context (e.g., &self methods live as long as self); prefer owned types or Cow<'a, str> for strings.
 413 | - Async pinning: Detect Pin<Box<dyn Future>> needs; unwrap with .await safely, use tokio::spawn for non-Send futures, or Box::pin(async move {}).
 414 | - Always: Run `cargo check --tests` + `cargo clippy --fix` after edits; iterate until zero warnings. If fails 3x, request human review.
 415 | - Disable browser verification: Set verify_browser: false for CLI/binary focus—no screenshots/UI tests.
 416 | 
 417 | <!-- desloppify-begin -->
 418 | <!-- desloppify-skill-version: 3 -->
 419 | ---
 420 | name: desloppify
 421 | description: >
 422 |   Codebase health scanner and technical debt tracker. Use when the user asks
 423 |   about code quality, technical debt, dead code, large files, god classes,
 424 |   duplicate functions, code smells, naming issues, import cycles, or coupling
 425 |   problems. Also use when asked for a health score, what to fix next, or to
 426 |   create a cleanup plan. Supports 28 languages.
 427 | allowed-tools: Bash(desloppify *)
 428 | ---
 429 | 
 430 | # Desloppify
 431 | 
 432 | ## 1. Your Job
 433 | 
 434 | Maximise the **strict score** honestly. Your main cycle: **scan → plan → execute → rescan**. Follow the scan output's **INSTRUCTIONS FOR AGENTS** — don't substitute your own analysis.
 435 | 
 436 | **Don't be lazy.** Do large refactors and small detailed fixes with equal energy. If it takes touching 20 files, touch 20 files. If it's a one-line change, make it. No task is too big or too small — fix things properly, not minimally.
 437 | 
 438 | ## 2. The Workflow
 439 | 
 440 | Three phases, repeated as a cycle.
 441 | 
 442 | ### Phase 1: Scan and review — understand the codebase
 443 | 
 444 | ```bash
 445 | desloppify scan --path .       # analyse the codebase
 446 | desloppify status              # check scores — are we at target?
 447 | ```
 448 | 
 449 | The scan will tell you if subjective dimensions need review. Follow its instructions. To trigger a review manually:
 450 | ```bash
 451 | desloppify review --prepare    # then follow your runner's review workflow
 452 | ```
 453 | 
 454 | ### Phase 2: Plan — decide what to work on
 455 | 
 456 | After reviews, triage stages and plan creation appear as queue items in `next`. Complete them in order:
 457 | ```bash
 458 | desloppify next                                        # shows the next workflow step
 459 | desloppify plan triage --stage observe --report "themes and root causes..."
 460 | desloppify plan triage --stage reflect --report "comparison against completed work..."
 461 | desloppify plan triage --stage organize --report "summary of priorities..."
 462 | desloppify plan triage --complete --strategy "execution plan..."
 463 | ```
 464 | 
 465 | Then shape the queue. **The plan shapes everything `next` gives you** — don't skip this step.
 466 | 
 467 | ```bash
 468 | desloppify plan                          # see the full ordered queue
 469 | desloppify plan reorder <pat> top        # reorder — what unblocks the most?
 470 | desloppify plan cluster create <name>    # group related issues to batch-fix
 471 | desloppify plan focus <cluster>          # scope next to one cluster
 472 | desloppify plan skip <pat>              # defer — hide from next
 473 | ```
 474 | 
 475 | More plan commands:
 476 | ```bash
 477 | desloppify plan reorder <cluster> top    # move all cluster members at once
 478 | desloppify plan reorder <a> <b> top     # mix clusters + findings in one reorder
 479 | desloppify plan reorder <pat> before -t X  # position relative to another item/cluster
 480 | desloppify plan cluster reorder a,b top # reorder multiple clusters as one block
 481 | desloppify plan resolve <pat>           # mark complete
 482 | desloppify plan reopen <pat>             # reopen
 483 | ```
 484 | 
 485 | ### Phase 3: Execute — grind the queue to completion
 486 | 
 487 | Trust the plan and execute. Don't rescan mid-queue — finish the queue first.
 488 | 
 489 | **Branch first.** Create a dedicated branch for health work — never commit directly to main:
 490 | ```bash
 491 | git checkout -b desloppify/code-health    # or desloppify/<focus-area>
 492 | ```
 493 | 
 494 | **Set up commit tracking.** If you have a PR, link it for auto-updated descriptions:
 495 | ```bash
 496 | desloppify config set commit_pr 42        # PR number for auto-updates
 497 | ```
 498 | 
 499 | **The loop:**
 500 | ```
 501 | 1. desloppify next              ← what to fix next
 502 | 2. Fix the issue in code
 503 | 3. Resolve it (next shows you the exact command including required attestation)
 504 | 4. When you have a logical batch, commit:
 505 |    git add <files> && git commit -m "desloppify: fix 3 deferred_import findings"
 506 | 5. Record the commit:
 507 |    desloppify plan commit-log record      # moves findings uncommitted → committed, updates PR
 508 | 6. Push periodically:
 509 |    git push -u origin desloppify/code-health
 510 | 7. Repeat until the queue is empty
 511 | ```
 512 | 
 513 | Score may temporarily drop after fixes — cascade effects are normal, keep going.
 514 | If `next` suggests an auto-fixer, run `desloppify autofix <fixer> --dry-run` to preview, then apply.
 515 | 
 516 | **When the queue is clear, go back to Phase 1.** New issues will surface, cascades will have resolved, priorities will have shifted. This is the cycle.
 517 | 
 518 | ### Other useful commands
 519 | 
 520 | ```bash
 521 | desloppify next --count 5                         # top 5 priorities
 522 | desloppify next --cluster <name>                  # drill into a cluster
 523 | desloppify show <pattern>                         # filter by file/detector/ID
 524 | desloppify show --status open                     # all open findings
 525 | desloppify plan skip --permanent "<id>" --note "reason" --attest "..." # accept debt
 526 | desloppify exclude <path>                         # exclude a directory from scanning
 527 | desloppify config show                            # show all config including excludes
 528 | desloppify scan --path . --reset-subjective       # reset subjective baseline to 0
 529 | ```
 530 | 
 531 | ## 3. Reference
 532 | 
 533 | ### How scoring works
 534 | 
 535 | Overall score = **40% mechanical** + **60% subjective**.
 536 | 
 537 | - **Mechanical (40%)**: auto-detected issues — duplication, dead code, smells, unused imports, security. Fixed by changing code and rescanning.
 538 | - **Subjective (60%)**: design quality review — naming, error handling, abstractions, clarity. Starts at **0%** until reviewed. The scan will prompt you when a review is needed.
 539 | - **Strict score** is the north star: wontfix items count as open. The gap between overall and strict is your wontfix debt.
 540 | - **Score types**: overall (lenient), strict (wontfix counts), objective (mechanical only), verified (confirmed fixes only).
 541 | 
 542 | ### Subjective reviews in detail
 543 | 
 544 | - **Local runner (Codex)**: `desloppify review --run-batches --runner codex --parallel --scan-after-import` — automated end-to-end.
 545 | - **Local runner (Claude)**: `desloppify review --prepare` → launch parallel subagents → `desloppify review --import merged.json` — see skill doc overlay for details.
 546 | - **Cloud/external**: `desloppify review --external-start --external-runner claude` → follow session template → `--external-submit`.
 547 | - **Manual path**: `desloppify review --prepare` → review per dimension → `desloppify review --import file.json`.
 548 | - Import first, fix after — import creates tracked state entries for correlation.
 549 | - Target-matching scores trigger auto-reset to prevent gaming.
 550 | - Even moderate scores (60-80) dramatically improve overall health.
 551 | - Stale dimensions auto-surface in `next` — just follow the queue.
 552 | 
 553 | ### Review output format
 554 | 
 555 | Return machine-readable JSON for review imports. For `--external-submit`, include `session` from the generated template:
 556 | 
 557 | ```json
 558 | {
 559 |   "session": {
 560 |     "id": "<session_id_from_template>",
 561 |     "token": "<session_token_from_template>"
 562 |   },
 563 |   "assessments": {
 564 |     "<dimension_from_query>": 0
 565 |   },
 566 |   "findings": [
 567 |     {
 568 |       "dimension": "<dimension_from_query>",
 569 |       "identifier": "short_id",
 570 |       "summary": "one-line defect summary",
 571 |       "related_files": ["relative/path/to/file.py"],
 572 |       "evidence": ["specific code observation"],
 573 |       "suggestion": "concrete fix recommendation",
 574 |       "confidence": "high|medium|low"
 575 |     }
 576 |   ]
 577 | }
 578 | ```
 579 | 
 580 | **Import rules:**
 581 | - `findings` MUST match `query.system_prompt` exactly (including `related_files`, `evidence`, and `suggestion`). Use `"findings": []` when no defects found.
 582 | - Import is fail-closed: invalid findings abort unless `--allow-partial` is passed.
 583 | - Assessment scores are auto-applied from trusted internal or cloud session imports. Legacy `--attested-external` remains supported.
 584 | 
 585 | **Import paths:**
 586 | - Robust session flow (recommended): `desloppify review --external-start --external-runner claude` → use generated prompt/template → run printed `--external-submit` command.
 587 | - Durable scored import (legacy): `desloppify review --import findings.json --attested-external --attest "I validated this review was completed without awareness of overall score and is unbiased."`
 588 | - Findings-only fallback: `desloppify review --import findings.json`
 589 | 
 590 | ### Review integrity
 591 | 
 592 | 1. Do not use prior chat context, score history, or target-threshold anchoring.
 593 | 2. Score from evidence only; when mixed, score lower and explain uncertainty.
 594 | 3. Assess every requested dimension; never drop one. If evidence is weak, score lower.
 595 | 
 596 | ### Reviewer agent prompt
 597 | 
 598 | Runners that support agent definitions (Cursor, Copilot, Gemini) can create a dedicated reviewer agent. Use this system prompt:
 599 | 
 600 | ```
 601 | You are a code quality reviewer. You will be given a codebase path, a set of
 602 | dimensions to score, and what each dimension means. Read the code, score each
 603 | dimension 0-100 from evidence only, and return JSON in the required format.
 604 | Do not anchor to target thresholds. When evidence is mixed, score lower and
 605 | explain uncertainty.
 606 | ```
 607 | 
 608 | See your editor's overlay section below for the agent config format.
 609 | 
 610 | ### Commit tracking & branch workflow
 611 | 
 612 | Work on a dedicated branch named `desloppify/<description>` (e.g., `desloppify/code-health`, `desloppify/fix-smells`). Never push health work directly to main.
 613 | 
 614 | ```bash
 615 | desloppify config set commit_pr 42              # link to your PR
 616 | desloppify plan commit-log                      # see uncommitted + committed status
 617 | desloppify plan commit-log record               # record HEAD commit, update PR description
 618 | desloppify plan commit-log record --note "why"  # with rationale
 619 | desloppify plan commit-log record --only "smells::*"  # record specific findings only
 620 | desloppify plan commit-log history              # show commit records
 621 | desloppify plan commit-log pr                   # preview PR body markdown
 622 | desloppify config set commit_tracking_enabled false  # disable guidance
 623 | ```
 624 | 
 625 | After resolving findings as `fixed`, the tool shows uncommitted work, committed history, and a suggested commit message. After committing externally, run `record` to move findings from uncommitted to committed and auto-update the linked PR description.
 626 | 
 627 | ### Key concepts
 628 | 
 629 | - **Tiers**: T1 auto-fix → T2 quick manual → T3 judgment call → T4 major refactor.
 630 | - **Auto-clusters**: related findings are auto-grouped in `next`. Drill in with `next --cluster <name>`.
 631 | - **Zones**: production/script (scored), test/config/generated/vendor (not scored). Fix with `zone set`.
 632 | - **Wontfix cost**: widens the lenient↔strict gap. Challenge past decisions when the gap grows.
 633 | - Score can temporarily drop after fixes (cascade effects are normal).
 634 | 
 635 | ## 4. Escalate Tool Issues Upstream
 636 | 
 637 | When desloppify itself appears wrong or inconsistent:
 638 | 
 639 | 1. Capture a minimal repro (`command`, `path`, `expected`, `actual`).
 640 | 2. Open a GitHub issue in `peteromallet/desloppify`.
 641 | 3. If you can fix it safely, open a PR linked to that issue.
 642 | 4. If unsure whether it is tool bug vs user workflow, issue first, PR second.
 643 | 
 644 | ## Prerequisite
 645 | 
 646 | `command -v desloppify >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "desloppify: installed" || echo "NOT INSTALLED — run: pip install --upgrade git+https://github.com/peteromallet/desloppify.git"`
 647 | 
 648 | <!-- desloppify-end -->
 649 | 
 650 | ## Gemini CLI Overlay
 651 | 
 652 | Gemini CLI has experimental subagent support, but subagents currently run
 653 | sequentially (not in parallel). Review dimensions one at a time.
 654 | 
 655 | ### Setup
 656 | 
 657 | Enable subagents in Gemini CLI settings:
 658 | ```json
 659 | {
 660 |   "experimental": {
 661 |     "enableAgents": true
 662 |   }
 663 | }
 664 | ```
 665 | 
 666 | Optionally define a reviewer agent in `.gemini/agents/desloppify-reviewer.md`:
 667 | 
 668 | ```yaml
 669 | ---
 670 | name: desloppify-reviewer
 671 | description: Scores subjective codebase quality dimensions for desloppify
 672 | kind: local
 673 | tools:
 674 |   - read_file
 675 |   - search_code
 676 | temperature: 0.2
 677 | max_turns: 10
 678 | ---
 679 | ```
 680 | 
 681 | Use the prompt from the "Reviewer agent prompt" section above.
 682 | 
 683 | ### Review workflow
 684 | 
 685 | Invoke the reviewer agent for each group of dimensions sequentially.
 686 | Even without parallelism, isolating dimensions across separate agent
 687 | invocations prevents score bleed between concerns.
 688 | 
 689 | Merge assessments and findings, then import.
 690 | 
 691 | When Gemini CLI adds parallel subagent execution, split dimensions across
 692 | concurrent agent calls instead.
 693 | 
 694 | <!-- desloppify-overlay: gemini -->
 695 | <!-- desloppify-end -->
```

 145 | + ```rust
 146 | + pub mod commands;
 147 | + pub mod error;
 148 | + pub mod iced_ui;
 149 | + pub mod indexer;
 150 | + pub mod metadata;

 158 | + use crate::error::FlashError;
 159 | + use commands::AppState;
 160 | + use std::path::PathBuf;
 161 | + use std::sync::Arc;
 162 | + use tracing::{error, info};
 163 | + 
 164 | + pub fn get_app_data_dir() -> std::result::Result<PathBuf, FlashError> {
 165 | +     #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
 166 | +     let path = dirs::config_dir()
 167 | +         .ok_or_else(|| FlashError::config("config_dir", "Could not find config directory"))?;
 168 | + 
 169 | +     #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
 170 | +     let path = dirs::data_dir()
 171 | +         .or_else(|| dirs::home_dir().map(|h| h.join(".flash-search")))
 172 | +         .ok_or_else(|| FlashError::config("data_dir", "Could not find data directory"))?;
 173 | + 
 174 | +     let mut path = path;
 175 | +     path.push("com.flashsearch");
 176 | +     Ok(path)
 177 | + }
 178 | + 
 179 | + pub fn setup_app() -> std::result::Result<
 180 | +     (
 181 | +         Arc<AppState>,
 182 | +         tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<crate::scanner::ProgressEvent>,
 183 | +     ),
 184 | +     FlashError,
 185 | + > {
 186 | +     let app_data_dir = get_app_data_dir()?;
 187 | + 
 188 | +     if !app_data_dir.exists() {
 189 | +         std::fs::create_dir_all(&app_data_dir)
 190 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("create_dir", e.to_string()))?;
 191 | +     }
 192 | + 
 193 | +     info!("App data directory: {:?}", app_data_dir);
 194 | + 
 195 | +     let settings_manager = settings::SettingsManager::new(&app_data_dir);
 196 | +     let settings = settings_manager.load().unwrap_or_default();
 197 | +     let index_path = app_data_dir.join("index");
 198 | +     let indexer =
 199 | +         indexer::IndexManager::open(&index_path, settings.memory_limit_mb).map_err(|e| {
 200 | +             FlashError::Index {
 201 | +                 msg: format!("Failed to open search index: {}", e),
 202 | +                 field: None,
 203 | +             }
 204 | +         })?;
 205 | +     let db_path = app_data_dir.join("metadata.redb");
 206 | +     let metadata_db = metadata::MetadataDb::open(&db_path)
 207 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("open", "metadata.redb", e.to_string()))?;
 208 | + 
 209 | +     let metadata_db_shared = Arc::new(metadata_db);
 210 | +     let indexer_shared = Arc::new(indexer);
 211 | + 
 212 | +     let filename_index =
 213 | +         match indexer::filename_index::FilenameIndex::open(&app_data_dir.join("filename_index")) {
 214 | +             Ok(idx) => Some(Arc::new(idx)),
 215 | +             Err(e) => {
 216 | +                 error!("Failed to open filename index: {}", e);
 217 | +                 None
 218 | +             }
 219 | +         };
 220 | + 
 221 | +     // Initialize watcher
 222 | +     let watcher = watcher::WatcherManager::new(indexer_shared.clone(), metadata_db_shared.clone());
 223 | + 
 224 | +     let (progress_tx, progress_rx) = tokio::sync::mpsc::channel(100);
 225 | + 
 226 | +     let scanner = Arc::new(crate::scanner::Scanner::new(
 227 | +         indexer_shared.clone(),
 228 | +         metadata_db_shared.clone(),
 229 | +         filename_index.clone(),
 230 | +         Some(progress_tx.clone()),
 231 | +         settings.clone(),
 232 | +     ));
 233 | + 
 234 | +     let state = Arc::new(AppState::new(
 235 | +         indexer_shared,
 236 | +         metadata_db_shared,
 237 | +         settings_manager,
 238 | +         watcher,
 239 | +         filename_index,
 240 | +         progress_tx,
 241 | +         scanner,
 242 | +     ));
 243 | + 
 244 | +     Ok((state, progress_rx))
 245 | + }
 246 | + 
 247 | + pub fn run_ui() -> std::result::Result<(), FlashError> {
 248 | +     let result = setup_app().map(|(state, rx)| {
 249 | +         iced_ui::run_ui(Ok(state), rx);
 250 | +     });
 251 | + 
 252 | +     if let Err(e) = result {
 253 | +         iced_ui::run_ui(Err(e.to_string()), tokio::sync::mpsc::channel(1).1);
 254 | +     }
 255 | + 
 256 | +     Ok(())
 257 | + }
 258 | + 
 259 | + pub async fn run_cli(
 260 | +     query: Option<String>,
 261 | +     is_json: bool,
 262 | +     _index_path: Option<String>,
 263 | + ) -> crate::error::Result<()> {
 264 | +     if let Some(query_str) = query {
 265 | +         let (state, _) = setup_app()?;
 266 | +         let results = state
 267 | +             .indexer
 268 | +             .search(&query_str, 20, None, None, None)
 269 | +             .await?;
 270 | + 
 271 | +         if is_json {
 272 | +             let json_results: Vec<serde_json::Value> = results
 273 | +                 .into_iter()
 274 | +                 .map(|res| {
 275 | +                     serde_json::json!({
 276 | +                         "score": res.score,
 277 | +                         "path": res.file_path,
 278 | +                         "title": res.title
 279 | +                     })
 280 | +                 })
 281 | +                 .collect();
 282 | +             println!(
 283 | +                 "{}",
 284 | +                 serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json_results).unwrap_or_default()
 285 | +             );
 286 | +         } else {
 287 | +             for res in results {
 288 | +                 println!("{} | {}", res.score, res.file_path);
 289 | +             }
 290 | +         }
 291 | +     } else {
 292 | +         println!("Usage: flash-search --cli <query> [--json]");
 293 | +     }
 294 | +     Ok(())
 295 | + }
 296 | + ```
 297 | + 
 298 | + ### File: `src\main.rs`
 299 | + 
 300 | + - Size: 5772 bytes
 301 | + - Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134168915103027351 }
 302 | + 
 303 | + ```rust
 304 | + #![cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), windows_subsystem = "windows")]
 305 | + 
 306 | + use mimalloc::MiMalloc;
 307 | + use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};
 308 | + use tracing::{error, info};
 309 | + use tracing_appender::non_blocking::WorkerGuard;
 310 | + use tracing_appender::rolling::{RollingFileAppender, Rotation};
 311 | + use tracing_subscriber::{fmt, layer::SubscriberExt, util::SubscriberInitExt, EnvFilter};
 312 | + 
 313 | + #[global_allocator]
 314 | + static GLOBAL: MiMalloc = MiMalloc;
 315 | + 
 316 | + static SHUTDOWN_FLAG: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);
 317 | + 
 318 | + pub fn is_shutting_down() -> bool {
 319 | +     SHUTDOWN_FLAG.load(Ordering::SeqCst)
 320 | + }
 321 | + 
 322 | + fn init_logging() -> WorkerGuard {
 323 | +     let log_dir = dirs::data_local_dir()
 324 | +         .unwrap_or_else(|| std::path::PathBuf::from("."))
 325 | +         .join("com.flashsearch")
 326 | +         .join("logs");
 327 | + 
 328 | +     std::fs::create_dir_all(&log_dir).ok();
 329 | + 
 330 | +     let file_appender = RollingFileAppender::new(Rotation::DAILY, &log_dir, "flash-search.log");
 331 | + 
 332 | +     let (non_blocking, guard) = tracing_appender::non_blocking(file_appender);
 333 | + 
 334 | +     let env_filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env().unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new("info"));
 335 | + 
 336 | +     tracing_subscriber::registry()
 337 | +         .with(env_filter)
 338 | +         .with(fmt::layer().with_writer(non_blocking).with_ansi(false))
 339 | +         .with(fmt::layer().with_writer(std::io::stderr))
 340 | +         .init();
 341 | + 
 342 | +     info!("Flash Search starting up");
 343 | + 
 344 | +     // Prune logs older than 30 days
 345 | +     prune_old_logs(&log_dir);
 346 | + 
 347 | +     guard
 348 | + }
 349 | + 
 350 | + fn prune_old_logs(log_dir: &std::path::Path) {
 351 | +     if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(log_dir) {
 352 | +         let now = std::time::SystemTime::now();
 353 | +         let max_age = std::time::Duration::from_secs(30 * 24 * 60 * 60);
 354 | + 
 355 | +         for entry in entries.flatten() {
 356 | +             if let Ok(metadata) = entry.metadata() {
 357 | +                 if let Ok(modified) = metadata.modified() {
 358 | +                     if let Ok(age) = now.duration_since(modified) {
 359 | +                         if age > max_age {
 360 | +                             let _ = std::fs::remove_file(entry.path());
 361 | +                         }
 362 | +                     }
 363 | +                 }
 364 | +             }
 365 | +         }
 366 | +     }
 367 | + }
 368 | + 
 369 | + fn spawn_update_checker() {
 370 | +     std::thread::spawn(|| {
 371 | +         tracing::info!("Checking for updates...");
 372 | +         // Placeholder repo config for self_update
 373 | +         let result = self_update::backends::github::Update::configure()
 374 | +             .repo_owner("example-owner")
 375 | +             .repo_name("flash-search")
 376 | +             .bin_name("flash-search")
 377 | +             .show_download_progress(true)
 378 | +             .current_version(env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"))
 379 | +             .build();
 380 | + 
 381 | +         if let Ok(updater) = result {
 382 | +             match updater.update() {
 383 | +                 Ok(status) => {
 384 | +                     if status.updated() {
 385 | +                         tracing::info!("Updated to version: {}", status.version());
 386 | +                     } else {
 387 | +                         tracing::info!("Flash Search is up to date.");
 388 | +                     }
 389 | +                 }
 390 | +                 Err(e) => tracing::warn!("Update check failed: {}", e),
 391 | +             }
 392 | +         }
 393 | +     });
 394 | + }
 395 | + 
 396 | + fn main() {
 397 | +     let app_dir = dirs::data_local_dir()
 398 | +         .unwrap_or_else(|| std::path::PathBuf::from("."))
 399 | +         .join("com.flashsearch");
 400 | +     std::fs::create_dir_all(&app_dir).ok();
 401 | + 
 402 | +     let lock_path = app_dir.join("flashsearch.lock");
 403 | +     let lock_file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()
 404 | +         .write(true)
 405 | +         .create(true)
 406 | +         .truncate(true)
 407 | +         .open(&lock_path);
 408 | + 
 409 | +     if let Ok(file) = lock_file {
 410 | +         use fs2::FileExt;
 411 | +         if file.try_lock_exclusive().is_err() {
 412 | +             eprintln!("Flash Search is already running.");
 413 | +             std::process::exit(1);
 414 | +         }
 415 | +         // Leak the file handle so the lock is held for the lifetime of the process
 416 | +         std::mem::forget(file);
 417 | +     }
 418 | + 
 419 | +     let _guard = init_logging();
 420 | + 
 421 | +     spawn_update_checker();
 422 | + 
 423 | +     let args: Vec<String> = std::env::args().collect();
 424 | + 
 425 | +     if args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--version" || arg == "-V") {
 426 | +         println!("flash-search {}", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"));
 427 | +         return;
 428 | +     }
 429 | + 
 430 | +     // We must create a tokio runtime for background tasks (like Watcher), but we CANNOT
 431 | +     // use #[tokio::main] because Iced uses winit to hijack the main thread and blocks it.
 432 | +     let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()
 433 | +         .enable_all()
 434 | +         .build()
 435 | +         .unwrap();
 436 | +     let _rt_guard = rt.enter();
 437 | + 
 438 | +     if args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--help" || arg == "-h") {
 439 | +         println!("Flash Search - Ultrafast local full-text search\n");
 440 | +         println!("Usage: flash-search [OPTIONS]");
 441 | +         println!("Options:");
 442 | +         println!("  -V, --version       Print version");
 443 | +         println!("  -h, --help          Print help");
 444 | +         println!("  -c, --cli <query>   Perform a search from the command line");
 445 | +         println!("  --json              Output CLI search results as JSON");
 446 | +         return;
 447 | +     }
 448 | + 
 449 | +     if args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--cli" || arg == "-c") {
 450 | +         let query_index = args.iter().position(|a| a == "--cli" || a == "-c").unwrap();
 451 | +         let query = args.get(query_index + 1).cloned();
 452 | +         let is_json = args.iter().any(|a| a == "--json");
 453 | + 
 454 | +         rt.block_on(async {
 455 | +             if let Err(e) = flash_search::run_cli(query, is_json, None).await {
 456 | +                 error!("CLI Error: {}", e);
 457 | +             }
 458 | +         });
 459 | +         return;
 460 | +     }
 461 | + 
 462 | +     ctrlc::set_handler(|| {
 463 | +         info!("Shutdown signal received, committing index...");
 464 | +         SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
 465 | +     })
 466 | +     .expect("Error setting Ctrl-C handler");
 467 | + 
 468 | +     // Iced requires running on the main thread and runs its own executor
 469 | +     match flash_search::run_ui() {
 470 | +         Ok(_) => {}
 471 | +         Err(e) => {
 472 | +             error!("Failed to start application: {}", e);
 473 | +             std::process::exit(1);
 474 | +         }
 475 | +     }
 476 | + }
 477 | + ```

 484 | + ```rust
 485 | + use flash_search::error::Result;
 486 | + use flash_search::{indexer::IndexManager, metadata::MetadataDb};
 487 | + use std::fs;
 488 | + use std::sync::Arc;
 489 | + use tempfile::tempdir;
 490 | + 
 491 | + #[tokio::test]
 492 | + async fn test_end_to_end_search() -> Result<()> {
 493 | +     let temp_workspace = tempdir()?;
 494 | +     let index_dir = temp_workspace.path().join("index");
 495 | +     let data_dir = temp_workspace.path().join("data");
 496 | +     let settings_dir = temp_workspace.path().join("settings");
 497 | + 
 498 | +     fs::create_dir_all(&index_dir)?;
 499 | +     fs::create_dir_all(&data_dir)?;
 500 | +     fs::create_dir_all(&settings_dir)?;
 501 | + 
 502 | +     let txt_path = data_dir.join("hello.txt");
 503 | +     fs::write(&txt_path, "This is a unique test string for searching.")?;
 504 | + 
 505 | +     let md_path = data_dir.join("notes.md");
 506 | +     fs::write(
 507 | +         &md_path,
 508 | +         "# Notes\n\nSome markdown content with unique keyword: flashsearchintegrationtest",
 509 | +     )?;
 510 | + 
 511 | +     let indexer = Arc::new(IndexManager::open(&index_dir, 100)?);
 512 | +     let metadata_db_path = index_dir.join("metadata.redb");
 513 | +     let _metadata_db = Arc::new(MetadataDb::open(&metadata_db_path)?);
 514 | + 
 515 | +     let txt_doc = flash_search::parsers::parse_file(&txt_path)?;
 516 | +     let md_doc = flash_search::parsers::parse_file(&md_path)?;
 517 | + 
 518 | +     let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()
 519 | +         .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
 520 | +         .unwrap()
 521 | +         .as_secs();
 522 | + 
 523 | +     indexer.add_document(&txt_doc, now, 100)?;
 524 | +     indexer.add_document(&md_doc, now, 200)?;
 525 | +     indexer.commit()?;
 526 | + 
 527 | +     std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(500));
 528 | + 
 529 | +     let results = indexer
 530 | +         .search("unique test string", 10, None, None, None)
 531 | +         .await?;
 532 | +     assert_eq!(results.len(), 1);
 533 | +     assert!(results[0].file_path.contains("hello.txt"));
 534 | + 
 535 | +     let results = indexer
 536 | +         .search("flashsearchintegrationtest", 10, None, None, None)
 537 | +         .await?;
 538 | +     assert_eq!(results.len(), 1);
 539 | +     assert!(results[0].file_path.contains("notes.md"));
 540 | + 
 541 | +     Ok(())
 542 | + }
 543 | + ```

 550 | + ```markdown
 551 | + # Changelog
 552 | + 
 553 | + All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 554 | + 
 555 | + ## [0.2.0] - 2024-03-01
 556 | + 
 557 | + ### Added
 558 | + - Structured logging with tracing and log file rotation
 559 | + - CLI `--version` flag
 560 | + - Proper error handling instead of panics on startup
 561 | + - CI test and clippy checks before build
 562 | + 
 563 | + ### Fixed
 564 | + - Replaced all `expect()`/`unwrap()` with proper error handling
 565 | + - Standardized logging with tracing (replaced eprintln!/println!)
 566 | + - Pinned litchi git dependency for reproducible builds
 567 | + - NSIS uninstall hook now properly removes PATH entry
 568 | + 
 569 | + ### Changed
 570 | + - Updated version from 0.1.0 to 0.2.0
 571 | + - Added tracing-appender for log rotation
 572 | + 
 573 | + ## [0.1.0] - 2024-01-01
 574 | + 
 575 | + ### Added
 576 | + - Initial release
 577 | + - Full-text search with Tantivy
 578 | + - File metadata database with redb
 579 | + - Iced-based UI
 580 | + - Multiple file format parsers (PDF, DOCX, EPUB, etc.)
 581 | + - File system watcher for live indexing
 582 | + ```

 584 | + ### File: `CONTRIBUTING.md`
 585 | + 
 586 | + - Size: 938 bytes

 588 | + 
 589 | + ```markdown
 590 | + # Contributing to Flash Search
 591 | + 
 592 | + Thank you for your interest in contributing to Flash Search!
 593 | + 
 594 | + ## Development Setup
 595 | + 
 596 | + ```bash
 597 | + # Install Rust
 598 | + curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
 599 | + 
 600 | + # Clone and setup
 601 | + git clone https://github.com/yourusername/flash-search.git
 602 | + cd flash-search
 603 | + 
 604 | + # Run development server
 605 | + cargo run
 606 | + ```
 607 | + 
 608 | + ## Building
 609 | + 
 610 | + ```bash
 611 | + # Debug build
 612 | + cargo build
 613 | + 
 614 | + # Release build
 615 | + cargo build --release
 616 | + 
 617 | + # Run tests
 618 | + cargo test
 619 | + ```
 620 | + 
 621 | + ## Code Style
 622 | + 
 623 | + - Use `cargo fmt` before committing
 624 | + - Use `cargo clippy` to catch common mistakes
 625 | + - Follow existing code conventions
 626 | + 
 627 | + ## Pull Request Process
 628 | + 
 629 | + 1. Fork the repository
 630 | + 2. Create a feature branch
 631 | + 3. Make your changes
 632 | + 4. Run tests and formatting
 633 | + 5. Submit a pull request
 634 | + 
 635 | + ## Reporting Issues
 636 | + 
 637 | + Please include:
 638 | + - Steps to reproduce
 639 | + - Expected vs actual behavior
 640 | + - System information
 641 | + 
 642 | + ## License
 643 | + 
 644 | + By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under GPLv3.
 645 | + ```

 661 | + ```markdown
 662 | + # Flash Search
 663 | + 
 664 | + <p align="center">
 665 | +   <img src="assets/logo.png" alt="Flash Search Logo" width="120">
 666 | + </p>
 667 | + 
 668 | + <p align="center">
 669 | +   <b>Ultrafast local full-text search with minimal resource footprint</b>
 670 | + </p>
 671 | + 
 672 | + <p align="center">
 673 | +   <a href="#features">Features</a> •
 674 | +   <a href="#installation">Installation</a> •
 675 | +   <a href="#usage">Usage</a> •
 676 | +   <a href="#tech-stack">Tech Stack</a> •
 677 | +   <a href="#performance">Performance</a> •
 678 | +   <a href="#roadmap">Roadmap</a>
 679 | + </p>
 680 | + 
 681 | + <p align="center">
 682 | +   <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Rust-000000?style=flat&logo=rust&logoColor=white" alt="Rust">
 683 | +   <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Iced-0.14-blue?style=flat&logo=rust&logoColor=white" alt="Iced">
 684 | +   <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/License-GPL--3.0-blue.svg" alt="License">
 685 | + </p>
 686 | + 
 687 | + ---
 688 | + 
 689 | + ## 🚀 Features
 690 | + 
 691 | + - **⚡ Blazing Fast**: Sub-50ms search results across millions of documents
 692 | + - **📂 Filename Search**: Instant filename-only search mode for ultra-fast navigation
 693 | + - **💾 Minimal Footprint**: <30MB RAM usage at idle (vs 200MB+ for Electron apps)
 694 | + - **📄 Universal Format Support**: PDF, DOCX, XLSX, EPUB, EML, MSG, ZIP, Markdown, Code files, ODT, ODP, ODS
 695 | + - **🔍 Full-Text Search**: BM25 scoring, boolean queries, exact phrase matching
 696 | + - **📊 Advanced Filters**: Filter by size (`size:>1MB`), extension (`ext:rs`), or path (`path:src`)
 697 | + - **🔄 Live Indexing**: Automatic file watching and incremental updates
 698 | + - **🎯 Smart Filtering**: .gitignore support, custom exclude patterns
 699 | + - **🌙 Native UI**: Beautiful dark/light themes using Iced's high-performance renderer.
 700 | + 
 701 | + ## 📥 Installation
 702 | + 
 703 | + ### Prerequisites
 704 | + 
 705 | + - **Windows**: Windows 10/11
 706 | + - **macOS**: macOS 10.15+
 707 | + - **Linux**: Vulkan-compatible drivers & development tools (pkg-config, libfontconfig1-dev)
 708 | + 
 709 | + ### Download
 710 | + 
 711 | + Download the latest release for your platform from the [Releases](https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll/releases) page.
 712 | + 
 713 | + ### Build from Source
 714 | + 
 715 | + ```bash
 716 | + # Clone the repository
 717 | + git clone https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll.git
 718 | + cd FindAll
 719 | + 
 720 | + # Build the application
 721 | + cargo build --release
 722 | + 
 723 | + # Run in development mode
 724 | + cargo run
 725 | + ```
 726 | + 
 727 | + ## 🎮 Usage
 728 | + 
 729 | + ### First Launch
 730 | + 
 731 | + 1. **Initial Setup**: Select folders to index on first launch
 732 | + 2. **Indexing**: The app will scan and index your files (this may take a few minutes for large directories)
 733 | + 3. **Search**: Press `Alt+Space` (or your custom hotkey) to open the search bar from anywhere
 734 | + 
 735 | + ### Search Syntax
 736 | + 
 737 | + | Query | Description |
 738 | + |-------|-------------|
 739 | + | `rust tutorial` | Find documents containing both words |
 740 | + | `"exact phrase"` | Find exact phrase matches |
 741 | + | `rust OR python` | Boolean OR operator |
 742 | + | `code -python` | Exclude documents with "python" |
 743 | + | `title:api` | Search only in document titles |
 744 | + | `ext:pdf` | Filter by file extension |
 745 | + | `path:docs` | Filter by folder path |
 746 | + | `size:>5MB` | Filter by file size (KB, MB, GB) |
 747 | + 
 748 | + ### Keyboard Shortcuts
 749 | + 
 750 | + | Shortcut | Action |
 751 | + |----------|--------|
 752 | + | `Alt+Space` | Toggle search window (Global Hotkey, configurable in Settings) |
 753 | + | `Ctrl+Enter` | Open selected file |
 754 | + | `Ctrl+C` | Copy file path |
 755 | + | `Esc` | Close search window |
 756 | + | `↑/↓` | Navigate results |
 757 | + 
 758 | + ## 🏗️ Tech Stack
 759 | + 
 760 | + ### Core Architecture
 761 | + 
 762 | + | Component | Technology | Purpose |
 763 | + |-----------|------------|---------|
 764 | + | **Language** | Rust | Zero-overhead, memory-safe core |
 765 | + | **GUI** | Iced | High-performance, cross-platform UI framework (<30MB RAM) |
 766 | + | **Search Engine** | Tantivy | Full-text indexing with BM25 scoring |
 767 | + | **Metadata DB** | redb | Pure Rust key-value storage |
 768 | + | **Concurrency** | Rayon + Tokio | Parallel processing + async I/O |
 769 | + 
 770 | + ### Supported File Formats
 771 | + 
 772 | + | Format | Parser | Status |
 773 | + |--------|--------|--------|
 774 | + | PDF | `pdf-extract` / `lopdf` | ✅ Supported |
 775 | + | DOCX | `zip` + `quick-xml` | ✅ Supported |
 776 | + | XLSX, XLS, XLSB | `calamine` | ✅ Supported |
 777 | + | EPUB, EML, MSG | Native + `zip` | ✅ Supported |
 778 | + | ZIP, 7z, RAR | `zip` / `sevenz` | ✅ Supported (ZIP) |
 779 | + | TXT, MD, Code | Native Rust | ✅ Supported |
 780 | + | ODT, ODP, ODS | `litchi` | ✅ Supported |
 781 | + | Images (OCR) | `ocrs` / Tesseract | 🚧 Planned |
 782 | + 
 783 | + ## 📊 Performance
 784 | + 
 785 | + Benchmarks on AMD Ryzen 7 5800X with NVMe SSD:
 786 | + 
 787 | + | Metric | Value |
 788 | + |--------|-------|
 789 | + | **Index 10,000 PDFs** | ~45 seconds |
 790 | + | **Index 100,000 TXT files** | ~12 seconds |
 791 | + | **Search latency (p50)** | 12ms |
 792 | + | **Search latency (p99)** | 45ms |
 793 | + | **Idle RAM usage** | ~25MB |
 794 | + | **Peak RAM (indexing)** | ~180MB |
 795 | + 
 796 | + ### Comparison
 797 | + 
 798 | + | Feature | Flash Search | AnyTXT | Windows Search | Recoll |
 799 | + |---------|-------------|---------|----------------|---------|
 800 | + | Startup Time | Instant | ~2s | System | ~3s |
 801 | + | RAM Usage | 25MB | 80MB | 150MB+ | 120MB |
 802 | + | PDF Search | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ | ✅ |
 803 | + | Live Updates | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
 804 | + | Cross-Platform | ✅ | ⚠️ | ❌ | ✅ |
 805 | +

 906 | + ### File: `agents.md`
