 910 | + 
 911 | + ```markdown
 912 | + # AGENTS.md - AI Coding Agent Instructions
 913 | + 
 914 | + ## Project Overview
 915 | + 
 916 | + **Flash Search** is an ultrafast local full-text search application built purely with Rust. This document provides comprehensive instructions for AI coding agents working on this codebase.
 917 | + 
 918 | + ## Architecture Overview
 919 | + 
 920 | + ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 921 | + │                     Flash Search                             │
 922 | + ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 923 | + │  UI (Iced)                                                  │
 924 | + │  ├── iced_ui/       - High-performance GUI components       │
 925 | + │  ├── search.rs      - Search view and logic bindings        │
 926 | + │  └── settings.rs    - Settings view and logic bindings      │
 927 | + ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 928 | + │  Rust Backend (Glue & Logic)                                │
 929 | + │  ├── commands/      - Business logic handlers               │
 930 | + │  └── state/         - Application state management          │
 931 | + ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 932 | + │  Core Engine                                                │
 933 | + │  ├── indexer/       - Tantivy search engine wrapper         │
 934 | + │  ├── parsers/       - File format parsers                   │
 935 | + │  ├── scanner/       - File system crawler                   │
 936 | + │  └── metadata/      - redb database operations              │
 937 | + └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
 938 | + 
 939 | + ## Project Structure
 940 | + 
 941 | + ```
 942 | + flash-search/
 943 | + ├── src/
 944 | + │   ├── main.rs              # Application entry point
 945 | + │   ├── lib.rs               # Library exports
 946 | + │   ├── error.rs             # Error types
 947 | + │   ├── models.rs            # Data models
 948 | + │   ├── settings.rs          # Settings management
 949 | + │   ├── commands/            # Business logic (AppState)
 950 | + │   ├── iced_ui/             # Iced UI implementation
 951 | + │   │   ├── mod.rs           # UI entry and core state
 952 | + │   │   ├── search.rs        # Search screen UI
 953 | + │   │   └── settings.rs      # Settings screen UI
 954 | + │   ├── parsers/
 955 | + │   │   ├── mod.rs           # Parser dispatch
 956 | + │   │   ├── docx.rs          # DOCX parser
 957 | + │   │   └── ...              # Other parsers
 958 | + │   ├── indexer/
 959 | + │   │   ├── mod.rs           # Indexer module
 960 | + │   │   ├── schema.rs        # Tantivy schema
 961 | + │   │   └── searcher.rs      # Query execution
 962 | + │   └── metadata/
 963 | + │       ├── mod.rs           # Metadata DB interface
 964 | + │       └── db.rs            # redb definitions
 965 | + ├── Cargo.toml               # Project dependencies
 966 | + └── README.md
 967 | + ```
 968 | + 
 969 | + ## Critical Implementation Guidelines
 970 | + 
 971 | + ### 1. Performance First
 972 | + 
 973 | + **This project prioritizes performance over convenience.**
 974 | + 
 975 | + - **Memory**: Keep RAM usage under 30MB at idle
 976 | + - **Speed**: Search results must return in <50ms
 977 | + - **I/O**: Use memory-mapped files for parsing
 978 | + - **UI Responsiveness**: Never block the Iced event loop
 979 | + 
 980 | + **DON'Ts:**
 981 | + - Don't perform heavy computations in Iced `update` methods (use `Command::perform` or `tokio::spawn`)
 982 | + - Don't load large datasets into the UI at once (use pagination or lazy loading if needed)
 983 | + 
 984 | + ### 2. Error Handling
 985 | + 
 986 | + Use `anyhow` for error propagation and `thiserror` for custom error types:
 987 | + 
 988 | + ```rust
 989 | + // Good
 990 | + use anyhow::{Context, Result};
 991 | + 
 992 | + pub fn parse_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
 993 | +     let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)
 994 | +         .with_context(|| format!("Failed to read file: {}", path.display()))?;
 995 | +     Ok(content)
 996 | + }
 997 | + 
 998 | + // Custom errors for specific failure modes
 999 | + #[derive(Error, Debug)]
1000 | + pub enum ParseError {
1001 | +     #[error("Unsupported file format: {0}")]
1002 | +     UnsupportedFormat(String),
1003 | +     #[error("File corrupted or encrypted")]
1004 | +     CorruptedFile,
1005 | +     #[error("IO error: {0}")]
1006 | +     Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
1007 | + }
1008 | + ```
1009 | + 
1010 | + ### 3. Concurrency Model
1011 | + 
1012 | + ```rust
1013 | + // File watching (async I/O) -> Tokio
1014 | + // File parsing (CPU-bound) -> Rayon
1015 | + // Search queries -> Tantivy (already parallel)
1016 | + 
1017 | + // Example: Hybrid approach
1018 | + use rayon::prelude::*;
1019 | + use tokio::task;
1020 | + 
1021 | + pub async fn index_directory(path: PathBuf) -> Result<()> {
1022 | +     // Walk directory (async I/O)
1023 | +     let files = walk_directory(&path).await?;
1024 | +     
1025 | +     // Parse files in parallel (CPU-bound)
1026 | +     let results: Vec<_> = files
1027 | +         .par_iter()
1028 | +         .map(|file| parse_file(file))
1029 | +         .collect();
1030 | +     
1031 | +     Ok(())
1032 | + }
1033 | + ```
1034 | + 
1035 | + ### 4. Parser Implementation
1036 | + 
1037 | + #### DOCX Parser (Critical Performance Path)
1038 | + 
1039 | + ```rust
1040 | + use memmap2::Mmap;
1041 | + use quick_xml::events::Event;
1042 | + use quick_xml::Reader;
1043 | + use zip::ZipArchive;
1044 | + 
1045 | + pub fn parse_docx(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
1046 | +     // Memory map the file
1047 | +     let file = std::fs::File::open(path)?;
1048 | +     let mmap = unsafe { Mmap::map(&file)? };
1049 | +     
1050 | +     // Stream from memory
1051 | +     let cursor = std::io::Cursor::new(&mmap[..]);
1052 | +     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(cursor)?;
1053 | +     
1054 | +     // Extract document.xml
1055 | +     let mut doc_xml = archive.by_name("word/document.xml")?;
1056 | +     let mut xml_content = String::new();
1057 | +     doc_xml.read_to_string(&mut xml_content)?;
1058 | +     
1059 | +     // Stream parse XML (NO DOM!)
1060 | +     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(&xml_content);
1061 | +     let mut buf = Vec::new();
1062 | +     let mut text = String::new();
1063 | +     
1064 | +     loop {
1065 | +         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
1066 | +             Ok(Event::Start(e)) if e.name().as_ref() == b"w:t" => {
1067 | +                 // Extract text content
1068 | +                 if let Ok(txt) = reader.read_text(e.name()) {
1069 | +                     text.push_str(&txt);
1070 | +                     text.push(' ');
1071 | +                 }
1072 | +             }
1073 | +             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
1074 | +             Err(e) => return Err(e.into()),
1075 | +             _ => (),
1076 | +         }
1077 | +         buf.clear();
1078 | +     }
1079 | +     
1080 | +     Ok(ParsedDocument {
1081 | +         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
1082 | +         content: text,
1083 | +         title: extract_title(&mut archive)?,
1084 | +     })
1085 | + }
1086 | + ```
1087 | + 
1088 | + ### 5. Tantivy Schema
1089 | + 
1090 | + ```rust
1091 | + use tantivy::schema::*;
1092 | + 
1093 | + pub fn create_schema() -> Schema {
1094 | +     let mut schema_builder = Schema::builder();
1095 | +     
1096 | +     // Store file path for retrieval
1097 | +     schema_builder.add_text_field(
1098 | +         "file_path",
1099 | +         STRING | STORED
1100 | +     );
1101 | +     
1102 | +     // Index content but don't store (retrieved from disk on demand)
1103 | +     let text_options = TextOptions::default()
1104 | +         .set_indexing_options(
1105 | +             TextFieldIndexing::default()
1106 | +                 .set_tokenizer("default")
1107 | +                 .set_index_option(IndexRecordOption::WithFreqsAndPositions)
1108 | +         )
1109 | +         .set_stored(false);
1110 | +     schema_builder.add_text_field("content", text_options);
1111 | +     
1112 | +     // Store title for display
1113 | +     schema_builder.add_text_field(
1114 | +         "title",
1115 | +         TEXT | STORED
1116 | +     );
1117 | +     
1118 | +     // Index timestamp for sorting
1119 | +     schema_builder.add_date_field(
1120 | +         "modified",
1121 | +         FAST | INDEXED
1122 | +     );
1123 | +     
1124 | +     schema_builder.build()
1125 | + }
1126 | + ```
1127 | + 
1128 | + ### 6. Metadata Database (redb)
1129 | + 
1130 | + ```rust
1131 | + use redb::{Database, TableDefinition};
1132 | + 
1133 | + const FILES_TABLE: TableDefinition<&str, FileMetadata> = TableDefinition::new("files");
1134 | + 
1135 | + #[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
1136 | + pub struct FileMetadata {
1137 | +     pub path: String,
1138 | +     pub modified: u64,      // Unix timestamp
1139 | +     pub size: u64,
1140 | +     pub content_hash: [u8; 32], // blake3 hash
1141 | +     pub indexed_at: u64,
1142 | + }
1143 | + 
1144 | + pub struct MetadataDb {
1145 | +     db: Database,
1146 | + }
1147 | + 
1148 | + impl MetadataDb {
1149 | +     pub fn needs_reindex(&self, path: &Path, modified: u64) -> Result<bool> {
1150 | +         let txn = self.db.begin_read()?;
1151 | +         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE)?;
1152 | +         
1153 | +         match table.get(path.to_str().unwrap())? {
1154 | +             Some(metadata) => {
1155 | +                 let meta = metadata.value();
1156 | +                 Ok(meta.modified != modified)
1157 | +             }
1158 | +             None => Ok(true), // File not indexed yet
1159 | +         }
1160 | +     }
1161 | + }
1162 | + ```
1163 | + 
1164 | + ### 7. Iced Integration (UI Glue)
1165 | + 
1166 | + ```rust
1167 | + use iced::{Application, Command, Element};
1168 | + 
1169 | + pub struct SearchApp {
1170 | +     state: Arc<AppState>,
1171 | +     search_query: String,
1172 | +     results: Vec<FileItem>,
1173 | + }
1174 | + 
1175 | + #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
1176 | + pub enum Message {
1177 | +     SearchInputChanged(String),
1178 | +     PerformSearch,
1179 | +     SearchResultsReceived(Vec<FileItem>),
1180 | + }
1181 | + 
1182 | + impl Application for SearchApp {
1183 | +     type Message = Message;
1184 | +     // ...
1185 | + 
1186 | +     fn update(&mut self, message: Message) -> Command<Message> {
1187 | +         match message {
1188 | +             Message::SearchInputChanged(query) => {
1189 | +                 self.search_query = query;
1190 | +                 Command::none()
1191 | +             }
1192 | +             Message::PerformSearch => {
1193 | +                 let state = self.state.clone();
1194 | +                 let query = self.search_query.clone();
1195 | +                 
1196 | +                 // Offload search to async command
1197 | +                 Command::perform(async move {
1198 | +                     let results = state.indexer.search(&query).await.unwrap_or_default();
1199 | +                     results.into_iter().map(FileItem::from).collect()
1200 | +                 }, Message::SearchResultsReceived)
1201 | +             }
1202 | +             Message::SearchResultsReceived(results) => {
1203 | +                 self.results = results;
1204 | +                 Command::none()
1205 | +             }
1206 | +         }
1207 | +     }
1208 | + 
1209 | +     fn view(&self) -> Element<Message> {
1210 | +         // ... Iced view layout construction ...
1211 | +         iced::widget::text("Search View").into()
1212 | +     }
1213 | + }
1214 | + ```
1215 | + 
1216 | + ### 8. Iced Component Patterns
1217 | + 
1218 | + Follow the standard Elm architecture when creating view components in Iced. Keep state isolated where appropriate and fire clear descriptive messages.
1219 | + 
1220 | + ## Common Tasks
1221 | + 
1222 | + ### Adding a New File Parser
1223 | + 
1224 | + 1. Create `src/parsers/<format>.rs`
1225 | + 2. Implement `parse_<format>(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument>`
1226 | + 3. Register in `src/parsers/mod.rs` dispatch function
1227 | + 4. Add MIME type detection
1228 | + 5. Write unit tests with sample files
1229 | + 
1230 | + ### Modifying the Search Schema
1231 | + 
1232 | + 1. Update `src/indexer/schema.rs`
1233 | + 2. **WARNING**: Schema changes require reindexing all documents
1234 | + 3. Consider migration strategy for existing users
1235 | + 4. Bump index version in constants
1236 | + 
1237 | + ### Adding a New Iced Message
1238 | + 
1239 | + 1. Define the message variant in the relevant `Message` enum (e.g., in `src/iced_ui/mod.rs`)
1240 | + 2. Handle the message in the `update()` function
1241 | + 3. Return an async `Command::perform` for any IO/backend work
1242 | + 4. Ensure the view triggers the matching message on user interactions
1243 | + 
1244 | + ## Testing Guidelines
1245 | + 
1246 | + ### Unit Tests (Rust)
1247 | + 
1248 | + ```rust
1249 | + #[cfg(test)]
1250 | + mod tests {
1251 | +     use super::*;
1252 | +     
1253 | +     #[test]
1254 | +     fn test_docx_parsing() {
1255 | +         let doc = parse_docx(Path::new("tests/fixtures/sample.docx"))
1256 | +             .expect("Should parse DOCX");
1257 | +         
1258 | +         assert!(doc.content.contains("Hello World"));
1259 | +         assert_eq!(doc.title, Some("Sample Document".to_string()));
1260 | +     }
1261 | + }
1262 | + ```
1263 | + 
1264 | + ### Integration Tests
1265 | + 
1266 | + - Test with large file collections (10K+ files)
1267 | + - Measure memory usage with `valgrind` or `/usr/bin/time -v`
1268 | + - Benchmark search latency with `cargo bench`
1269 | + 
1270 | + ## Build Commands
1271 | + 
1272 | + ```bash
1273 | + # Build and Run
1274 | + cargo run
1275 | + 
1276 | + # Build for production
1277 | + cargo build --release
1278 | + 
1279 | + # Run Rust tests
1280 | + cargo test
1281 | + ```
1282 | + 
1283 | + ## Troubleshooting
1284 | + 
1285 | + ### High Memory Usage
1286 | + 
1287 | + - Check for memory leaks in parsers (ensure files are closed)
1288 | + - Verify Tantivy index writer buffer size
1289 | + - Profile with `heaptrack` or `dhat`
1290 | + 
1291 | + ### Slow Search
1292 | + 
1293 | + - Verify Tantivy reader is cached (don't recreate)
1294 | + - Check if index is memory-mapped
1295 | + - Profile with `perf` or `samply`
1296 | + 
1297 | + ### Parser Failures
1298 | + 
1299 | + - Check file encoding detection
1300 | + - Verify zip/xml parsing handles malformed files gracefully
1301 | + - Add more comprehensive error contexts
1302 | + 
1303 | + ## Resources
1304 | + 
1305 | + - [Iced Documentation](https://docs.rs/iced/latest/iced/)
1306 | + - [Tantivy Documentation](https://docs.rs/tantivy/latest/tantivy/)
1307 | + - [Redb Documentation](https://docs.rs/redb/latest/redb/)
1308 | + - [Rayon Documentation](https://docs.rs/rayon/latest/rayon/)
1309 | + 
1310 | + ## Contact
1311 | + 
1312 | + For questions about implementation details, refer to:
1313 | + - GitHub Issues: https://github.com/yourusername/flash-search/issues
1314 | + - Architecture Decisions: See `docs/adr/` directory
1315 | +

2037 | + ### File: `get_iced_sig.py`
2038 | + 
2039 | + - Size: 944 bytes
2040 | + - Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134163203999939381 }
2041 | + 
2042 | + ```python
2043 | + import os
2044 | + import subprocess
2045 | + 
2046 | + def find_iced_signature():
2047 | +     cargo_home = os.environ.get('CARGO_HOME', os.path.expanduser('~/.cargo'))
2048 | +     registry_src = os.path.join(cargo_home, 'registry', 'src')
2049 | +     
2050 | +     for root, dirs, files in os.walk(registry_src):
2051 | +         if 'iced-0.14' in root or 'iced-0.13' in root:
2052 | +             if 'application.rs' in files:
2053 | +                 filepath = os.path.join(root, 'application.rs')
2054 | +                 with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
2055 | +                     content = f.read()
2056 | +                     print(f"Found {filepath}")
2057 | +                     lines = content.split('\n')
2058 | +                     for i, l in enumerate(lines):
2059 | +                         if 'pub fn application<' in l or 'pub fn application(' in l:
2060 | +                             print("\n".join(lines[i-2:i+15]))
2061 | +                             return
2062 | +     print("Could not find iced application source.")
2063 | + 
2064 | + find_iced_signature()
2065 | + ```

2199 | + import os

2201 | + 
2202 | + def find_iced_signature():
2203 | +     # Use rustc to output the docs or we can just search local crates cache
2204 | +     registry_path = os.path.expanduser('~/.cargo/registry/src')
2205 | +     # Windows cargo registry
2206 | +     cargo_home = os.environ.get('CARGO_HOME', os.path.expanduser('~/.cargo'))
2207 | +     registry_src = os.path.join(cargo_home, 'registry', 'src')
2208 | +     
2209 | +     for root, dirs, files in os.walk(registry_src):
2210 | +         if 'iced-0.14' in root or 'iced-0.13' in root:
2211 | +             if 'application.rs' in files:
2212 | +                 filepath = os.path.join(root, 'application.rs')
2213 | +                 with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
2214 | +                     content = f.read()
2215 | +                     print(f"Found {filepath}")
2216 | +                     lines = content.split('\n')
2217 | +                     for i, l in enumerate(lines):
2218 | +                         if 'pub fn application' in l:
2219 | +                             print("\n".join(lines[i:i+10]))
2220 | +                             return
2221 | +     print("Could not find iced application source.")
2222 | + 
2223 | + find_iced_signature()
2224 | + ```

2233 | + pub fn set_auto_start(enable: bool, app_name: &str, exe_path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
2234 | +     use winreg::enums::*;
2235 | +     use winreg::RegKey;
2236 | + 
2237 | +     let hkcu = RegKey::predef(HKEY_CURRENT_USER);
2238 | +     let run_key = hkcu
2239 | +         .open_subkey_with_flags(
2240 | +             r"Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run",
2241 | +             KEY_ALL_ACCESS,
2242 | +         )
2243 | +         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2244 | + 
2245 | +     if enable {
2246 | +         run_key
2247 | +             .set_value(app_name, &exe_path)
2248 | +             .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2249 | +     } else {
2250 | +         let _ = run_key.delete_value(app_name);
2251 | +     }
2252 | +     Ok(())
2253 | + }
2254 | +

2259 | +

2263 | +         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2264 | + 
2265 | +     match run_key.get_value::<String, _>(app_name) {

2268 | +     }
2269 | + }

2466 | +     query: String,
2467 | +     limit: usize,
2468 | +     state: &Arc<AppState>,
2469 | +     min_size: Option<u64>,
2470 | +     max_size: Option<u64>,
2471 | +     file_extensions: Option<Vec<String>>,
2472 | + ) -> Result<Vec<SearchResult>, String> {
2473 | +     state
2474 | +         .indexer

2486 | + pub async fn get_file_preview_internal(path: String) -> Result<String, String> {
2487 | +     let path = std::path::PathBuf::from(path);
2488 | +     match parse_file(&path) {
2489 | +         Ok(doc) => Ok(doc.content[..std::cmp::min(doc.content.len(), 10000)].to_string()),
2490 | +         Err(e) => Err(e.to_string()),
2491 | +     }
2492 | + }
2493 | + 
2494 | + pub async fn get_file_preview_highlighted_internal(
2495 | +     path: String,
2496 | +     query: String,
2497 | + ) -> Result<PreviewResult, String> {

2502 | +         Ok(doc) => Ok(PreviewResult {
2503 | +             content: doc.content[..std::cmp::min(doc.content.len(), 10000)].to_string(),
2504 | +             matched_terms,
2505 | +         }),
2506 | +         Err(e) => Err(e.to_string()),
2507 | +     }
2508 | + }

2511 | +     query: String,

2514 | + ) -> Result<Vec<FilenameSearchResult>, String> {
2515 | +     if let Some(ref filename_index) = state.filename_index {
2516 | +         filename_index
2517 | +             .search(&query, limit)
2518 | +             .map(|results| {
2519 | +                 results
2520 | +                     .into_iter()
2521 | +                     .map(|r| FilenameSearchResult {
2522 | +                         file_path: r.file_path,
2523 | +                         file_name: r.file_name,
2524 | +                     })
2525 | +                     .collect()
2526 | +             })
2527 | +             .map_err(|e| e.to_string())
2528 | +     } else {
2529 | +         Err("Filename index not initialized".to_string())
2530 | +     }
2531 | + }
2532 | + 
2533 | + pub async fn get_filename_index_stats_internal(
2534 | +     state: &Arc<AppState>,
2535 | + ) -> Result<FilenameIndexStats, String> {

2538 | +         Ok(FilenameIndexStats {
2539 | +             total_files: stats.total_files,

2541 | +         })

2580 | + }
2581 | + 
2582 | + pub fn add_recent_search_internal(query: String, state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<(), String> {
2583 | +     let mut settings = state.settings_manager.load().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2584 | + 
2585 | +     let mut recent = settings.recent_searches.unwrap_or_default();
2586 | +     recent.retain(|q| q != &query);
2587 | +     recent.insert(0, query);
2588 | +     recent.truncate(10);
2589 | + 
2590 | +     settings.recent_searches = Some(recent);
2591 | +     state
2592 | +         .settings_manager
2593 | +         .save(&settings)
2594 | +         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2595 | + 
2596 | +     Ok(())
2597 | + }
2598 | + 
2599 | + pub fn clear_recent_searches_internal(state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<(), String> {
2600 | +     let mut settings = state.settings_manager.load().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2601 | +     settings.recent_searches = Some(vec![]);
2602 | +     state
2603 | +         .settings_manager
2604 | +         .save(&settings)
2605 | +         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2606 | +     Ok(())
2607 | + }
2608 | + 
2609 | + pub fn add_search_history_internal(query: String, state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<(), String> {
2610 | +     let mut settings = state.settings_manager.load().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2611 | +     let mut history = settings.search_history.unwrap_or_default();
2612 | + 
2613 | +     let mut found = false;
2614 | +     for item in &mut history {
2615 | +         if item.query == query {
2616 | +             item.frequency += 1;
2617 | +             item.last_used = std::time::SystemTime::now()
2618 | +                 .duration_since(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
2619 | +                 .unwrap()
2620 | +                 .as_secs();
2621 | +             found = true;
2622 | +             break;
2623 | +         }
2624 | +     }
2625 | + 
2626 | +     if !found {
2627 | +         history.insert(
2628 | +             0,
2629 | +             crate::settings::SearchHistoryItem {
2630 | +                 query,
2631 | +                 frequency: 1,
2632 | +                 last_used: std::time::SystemTime::now()
2633 | +                     .duration_since(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
2634 | +                     .unwrap()
2635 | +                     .as_secs(),
2636 | +             },
2637 | +         );
2638 | +     }
2639 | + 
2640 | +     history.sort_by(|a, b| b.frequency.cmp(&a.frequency));
2641 | +     history.truncate(50);
2642 | + 
2643 | +     settings.search_history = Some(history);
2644 | +     state
2645 | +         .settings_manager
2646 | +         .save(&settings)
2647 | +         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2648 | + 
2649 | +     Ok(())
2650 | + }
2651 | + 
2652 | + pub fn get_search_history_internal(
2653 | +     limit: usize,
2654 | +     state: &Arc<AppState>,
2655 | + ) -> Result<Vec<SearchHistoryItem>, String> {
2656 | +     let settings = state.settings_manager.load().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2657 | +     let history = settings.search_history.unwrap_or_default();
2658 | + 
2659 | +     let mut sorted = history;
2660 | +     sorted.sort_by(|a, b| b.frequency.cmp(&a.frequency));
2661 | +     sorted.truncate(limit);
2662 | + 
2663 | +     Ok(sorted
2664 | +         .into_iter()
2665 | +         .map(|item| SearchHistoryItem {
2666 | +             query: item.query,
2667 | +             frequency: item.frequency,
2668 | +             last_used: item.last_used,
2669 | +         })
2670 | +         .collect())
2671 | + }
2672 | + 
2673 | + pub fn pin_file_internal(path: String, state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<(), String> {
2674 | +     let mut settings = state.settings_manager.load().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2675 | +     if !settings.pinned_files.contains(&path) {
2676 | +         settings.pinned_files.push(path);
2677 | +         state
2678 | +             .settings_manager
2679 | +             .save(&settings)
2680 | +             .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2681 | +     }
2682 | +     Ok(())
2683 | + }
2684 | + 
2685 | + pub fn unpin_file_internal(path: String, state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<(), String> {
2686 | +     let mut settings = state.settings_manager.load().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2687 | +     settings.pinned_files.retain(|p| p != &path);
2688 | +     state
2689 | +         .settings_manager
2690 | +         .save(&settings)
2691 | +         .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2692 | +     Ok(())
2693 | + }
2694 | + 
2695 | + pub fn get_pinned_files_internal(state: &Arc<AppState>) -> Result<Vec<String>, String> {
2696 | +     let settings = state.settings_manager.load().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
2697 | +     Ok(settings.pinned_files)

2797 | +     Config { key: String, cause: String },
2798 | + 
2799 | +     #[error("Archive error: {archive_type} - {operation}: {cause}")]

2804 | +     },

2818 | +     },
2819 | + }

3170 | + impl App {
3171 | +     fn new(state: Result<Arc<AppState>, String>) -> Self {
3172 | +         match state {
3173 | +             Ok(state) => {

3175 | +                 let stats = state.indexer.get_statistics().unwrap_or_default();
3176 | +                 let index_size = format!("{:.1} MB", (stats.total_size_bytes as f64) / 1_048_576.0);
3177 | +                 let is_dark = matches!(settings.theme, crate::settings::Theme::Dark);
3178 | + 
3179 | +                 let mut app = App {
3180 | +                     state: Some(state),
3181 | +                     error: None,
3182 | +                     search_error: None,
3183 | +                     search_query: String::new(),
3184 | +                     results: Vec::new(),
3185 | +                     selected_index: None,
3186 | +                     is_searching: false,
3187 | +                     settings: settings.clone(),
3188 | +                     active_tab: Tab::Search,
3189 | +                     files_indexed: stats.total_documents as i32,
3190 | +                     index_size,
3191 | +                     is_dark,
3192 | +                     search_mode: SearchMode::FullText,
3193 | +                     filter_extension: String::new(),
3194 | +                     filter_size: String::new(),
3195 | +                     preview_content: None,
3196 | +                     is_loading_preview: false,
3197 | +                     rebuild_progress: None,
3198 | +                     rebuild_status: None,
3199 | +                     progress_rx: None,
3200 | +                     tray_icon: crate::system::tray::create_tray_icon().ok(),
3201 | +                     hotkey_manager: None,
3202 | +                 };
3203 | + 
3204 | +                 if let Ok(manager) = global_hotkey::GlobalHotKeyManager::new() {
3205 | +                     if let Ok(hotkey) = settings
3206 | +                         .global_hotkey
3207 | +                         .parse::<global_hotkey::hotkey::HotKey>()
3208 | +                     {
3209 | +                         if manager.register(hotkey).is_err() {
3210 | +                             app.error = Some(format!(
3211 | +                                 "Hotkey conflict: '{}' is already registered by another application. Please choose an alternative in Settings.",
3212 | +                                 settings.global_hotkey
3213 | +                             ));
3214 | +                         }
3215 | +                     } else if !settings.global_hotkey.is_empty() {
3216 | +                         app.error = Some(format!(
3217 | +                             "Invalid hotkey format: '{}'",
3218 | +                             settings.global_hotkey
3219 | +                         ));
3220 | +                     }
3221 | +                     app.hotkey_manager = Some(manager);
3222 | +                 }
3223 | + 
3224 | +                 app
3225 | +             }
3226 | +             Err(err_msg) => App {
3227 | +                 state: None,
3228 | +                 error: Some(err_msg),
3229 | +                 search_error: None,
3230 | +                 search_query: String::new(),
3231 | +                 results: Vec::new(),
3232 | +                 selected_index: None,
3233 | +                 is_searching: false,
3234 | +                 settings: AppSettings::default(),
3235 | +                 active_tab: Tab::Search,
3236 | +                 files_indexed: 0,
3237 | +                 index_size: "0 MB".to_string(),
3238 | +                 is_dark: false,
3239 | +                 search_mode: SearchMode::FullText,
3240 | +                 filter_extension: String::new(),
3241 | +                 filter_size: String::new(),
3242 | +                 preview_content: None,
3243 | +                 is_loading_preview: false,
3244 | +                 rebuild_progress: None,
3245 | +                 rebuild_status: None,
3246 | +                 progress_rx: None,
3247 | +                 tray_icon: crate::system::tray::create_tray_icon().ok(),
3248 | +                 hotkey_manager: None,
3249 | +             },
3250 | +         }
3251 | +     }
3252 | + 
3253 | +     fn parse_size_filter(size_str: &str) -> (Option<u64>, Option<u64>) {
3254 | +         let size_str = size_str.trim();
3255 | +         if size_str.is_empty() {
3256 | +             return (None, None);
3257 | +         }
3258 | + 
3259 | +         let (op, num_str) = if let Some(stripped) = size_str.strip_prefix(">=") {
3260 | +             (">=", stripped)
3261 | +         } else if let Some(stripped) = size_str.strip_prefix("<=") {
3262 | +             ("<=", stripped)
3263 | +         } else if let Some(stripped) = size_str.strip_prefix(">") {
3264 | +             (">", stripped)
3265 | +         } else if let Some(stripped) = size_str.strip_prefix("<") {
3266 | +             ("<", stripped)
3267 | +         } else {
3268 | +             (">=", size_str)
3269 | +         };
3270 | + 
3271 | +         let num_str = num_str.trim();
3272 | +         let multiplier: u64 = if num_str.ends_with("KB") {
3273 | +             1024
3274 | +         } else if num_str.ends_with("MB") {
3275 | +             1024 * 1024
3276 | +         } else if num_str.ends_with("GB") {
3277 | +             1024 * 1024 * 1024
3278 | +         } else {
3279 | +             1
3280 | +         };
3281 | + 
3282 | +         let num: u64 = num_str
3283 | +             .trim_end_matches(|c: char| c.is_alphabetic())
3284 | +             .parse()
3285 | +             .unwrap_or(0);
3286 | +         let bytes = num * multiplier;
3287 | + 
3288 | +         match op {
3289 | +             ">" => (Some(bytes + 1), None),
3290 | +             "<" => (None, Some(bytes.saturating_sub(1))),
3291 | +             ">=" => (Some(bytes), None),
3292 | +             "<=" => (None, Some(bytes)),
3293 | +             _ => (Some(bytes), None),
3294 | +         }
3295 | +     }
3296 | + 
3297 | +     fn perform_search(&mut self) -> Task<Message> {
3298 | +         let state = match &self.state {
3299 | +             Some(s) => s.clone(),
3300 | +             None => return Task::none(),
3301 | +         };
3302 | + 
3303 | +         let query = self.search_query.clone();
3304 | +         let max_results = self.settings.max_results;
3305 | +         let mode = self.search_mode.clone();
3306 | +         let extension = if self.filter_extension.is_empty() {
3307 | +             None
3308 | +         } else {
3309 | +             Some(vec![self.filter_extension.clone()])
3310 | +         };
3311 | +         let (min_size, max_size) = Self::parse_size_filter(&self.filter_size);
3312 | + 
3313 | +         self.is_searching = true;
3314 | +         self.results.clear();
3315 | +         self.preview_content = None;
3316 | + 
3317 | +         Task::future(async move {
3318 | +             let result = match mode {
3319 | +                 SearchMode::Filename => {
3320 | +                     match search_filenames_internal(query, max_results, &state).await {
3321 | +                         Ok(results) => Message::SearchResultsReceived(
3322 | +                             results.into_iter().map(FileItem::from).collect(),
3323 | +                         ),
3324 | +                         Err(e) => Message::SearchError(e.to_string()),
3325 | +                     }
3326 | +                 }
3327 | +                 SearchMode::FullText => {
3328 | +                     match search_query_internal(
3329 | +                         query,
3330 | +                         max_results,
3331 | +                         &state,
3332 | +                         min_size,
3333 | +                         max_size,
3334 | +                         extension,
3335 | +                     )
3336 | +                     .await
3337 | +                     {
3338 | +                         Ok(results) => Message::SearchResultsReceived(
3339 | +                             results.into_iter().map(FileItem::from).collect(),
3340 | +                         ),
3341 | +                         Err(e) => Message::SearchError(e.to_string()),
3342 | +                     }
3343 | +                 }
3344 | +             };
3345 | +             result
3346 | +         })
3347 | +     }
3348 | + 
3349 | +     fn load_preview(&mut self) -> Task<Message> {
3350 | +         let idx = match self.selected_index {
3351 | +             Some(i) => i,
3352 | +             None => return Task::none(),
3353 | +         };
3354 | + 
3355 | +         let item = match self.results.get(idx) {
3356 | +             Some(i) => i.clone(),
3357 | +             None => return Task::none(),
3358 | +         };
3359 | + 
3360 | +         let path = item.path.clone();
3361 | +         let query = self.search_query.clone();
3362 | +         self.is_loading_preview = true;
3363 | + 
3364 | +         Task::future(async move {
3365 | +             let preview = match get_file_preview_highlighted_internal(path, query).await {
3366 | +                 Ok(result) => Some(result.content),
3367 | +                 Err(_) => None,
3368 | +             };
3369 | +             Message::PreviewLoaded(preview)
3370 | +         })
3371 | +     }
3372 | + 
3373 | +     fn save_settings(&self) {
3374 | +         if let Some(state) = &self.state {
3375 | +             let _ = state.settings_manager.save(&self.settings);
3376 | +         }
3377 | +     }
3378 | + }
3379 | + 
3380 | + fn update(app: &mut App, message: Message) -> Task<Message> {
3381 | +     if app.error.is_some() {
3382 | +         match message {
3383 | +             Message::DismissError => {
3384 | +                 app.error = None;
3385 | +                 Task::none()
3386 | +             }
3387 | +             Message::Quit => {
3388 | +                 std::process::exit(0);
3389 | +             }
3390 | +             _ => Task::none(),
3391 | +         }
3392 | +     } else {
3393 | +         match message {
3394 | +             Message::SearchQueryChanged(q) => {
3395 | +                 app.search_query = q;
3396 | +                 Task::none()
3397 | +             }
3398 | +             Message::SearchSubmitted => app.perform_search(),
3399 | +             Message::SearchResultsReceived(results) => {
3400 | +                 app.results = results;
3401 | +                 app.is_searching = false;
3402 | +                 app.search_error = None;
3403 | +                 if !app.results.is_empty() {
3404 | +                     app.selected_index = Some(0);
3405 | +                 }
3406 | +                 app.load_preview()
3407 | +             }
3408 | +             Message::SearchError(err) => {
3409 | +                 app.search_error = Some(err);
3410 | +                 app.is_searching = false;
3411 | +                 app.results.clear();
3412 | +                 Task::none()
3413 | +             }
3414 | +             Message::ResultSelected(idx) => {
3415 | +                 app.selected_index = Some(idx);
3416 | +                 app.load_preview()
3417 | +             }
3418 | +             Message::OpenFile(path) => {
3419 | +                 let p = PathBuf::from(&path);
3420 | +                 if p.is_absolute() && p.exists() {
3421 | +                     let _ = opener::open(p);
3422 | +                 } else {
3423 | +                     tracing::warn!("Blocked attempt to open invalid or relative path: {}", path);
3424 | +                 }
3425 | +                 Task::none()
3426 | +             }
3427 | +             Message::CopyPath(path) => {
3428 | +                 if let Ok(mut clipboard) = arboard::Clipboard::new() {
3429 | +                     let _ = clipboard.set_text(&path);
3430 | +                 }
3431 | +                 Task::none()
3432 | +             }
3433 | +             Message::TabChanged(tab) => {
3434 | +                 app.active_tab = tab;
3435 | +                 Task::none()
3436 | +             }
3437 | +             Message::RebuildIndex => {
3438 | +                 let state = match &app.state {
3439 | +                     Some(s) => s.clone(),
3440 | +                     None => return Task::none(),
3441 | +                 };
3442 | +                 let settings = app.settings.clone();
3443 | +                 app.rebuild_progress = Some(0.0);
3444 | +                 app.rebuild_status = Some("Starting rebuild...".to_string());
3445 | +                 if let Some(tray) = &app.tray_icon {
3446 | +                     let _ = tray.set_tooltip(Some("Flash Search - Rebuilding Index..."));
3447 | +                 }
3448 | +                 app.files_indexed = 0;
3449 | +                 let rx = app.progress_rx.clone();
3450 | +                 Task::batch(vec![
3451 | +                     Task::future(async move {
3452 | +                         let _ = state.indexer.clear();
3453 | +                         let _ = state.indexer.commit();
3454 | +                         let _ = state.metadata_db.clear();
3455 | +                         for dir in settings.index_dirs {
3456 | +                             let _ = state
3457 | +                                 .scanner
3458 | +                                 .scan_directory(
3459 | +                                     PathBuf::from(dir),
3460 | +                                     settings.exclude_patterns.clone(),
3461 | +                                 )
3462 | +                                 .await;
3463 | +                         }
3464 | +                         Message::IndexRebuilt
3465 | +                     }),
3466 | +                     Task::perform(
3467 | +                         async move {
3468 | +                             if let Some(r) = rx {
3469 | +                                 let mut g = r.lock().await;
3470 | +                                 g.recv().await
3471 | +                             } else {
3472 | +                                 None
3473 | +                             }
3474 | +                         },
3475 | +                         Message::PollProgressResult,
3476 | +                     ),
3477 | +                 ])
3478 | +             }
3479 | +             Message::PollProgressResult(Some(event)) => {
3480 | +                 let p = if event.total > 0 {
3481 | +                     (event.processed as f32) / (event.total as f32)
3482 | +                 } else {
3483 | +                     0.0
3484 | +                 };
3485 | +                 app.rebuild_progress = Some(p);
3486 | +                 app.rebuild_status = Some(event.status.clone());
3487 | +                 app.files_indexed = event.processed as i32;
3488 | + 
3489 | +                 let rx = app.progress_rx.clone();
3490 | +                 Task::perform(
3491 | +                     async move {
3492 | +                         if let Some(r) = rx {
3493 | +                             let mut g = r.lock().await;
3494 | +                             g.recv().await
3495 | +                         } else {
3496 | +                             None
3497 | +                         }
3498 | +                     },
3499 | +                     Message::PollProgressResult,
3500 | +                 )
3501 | +             }
3502 | +             Message::PollProgressResult(None) => Task::none(),
3503 | +             Message::RebuildProgress(_) => Task::none(), // Fallback
3504 | +             Message::IndexRebuilt => {
3505 | +                 let stats = app
3506 | +                     .state
3507 | +                     .as_ref()
3508 | +                     .map(|s| s.indexer.get_statistics().unwrap_or_default())
3509 | +                     .unwrap_or_default();
3510 | +                 app.files_indexed = stats.total_documents as i32;
3511 | +                 app.index_size = format!("{:.1} MB", (stats.total_size_bytes as f64) / 1_048_576.0);
3512 | +                 if let Some(tray) = &app.tray_icon {
3513 | +                     let _ = tray.set_tooltip(Some("Flash Search - Ready"));
3514 | +                 }
3515 | +                 app.rebuild_progress = None;
3516 | +                 app.rebuild_status = None;
3517 | +                 Task::none()
3518 | +             }
3519 | +             Message::AddFolder => Task::perform(
3520 | +                 async {
3521 | +                     rfd::AsyncFileDialog::new()
3522 | +                         .pick_folder()
3523 | +                         .await
3524 | +                         .map(|p| p.path().to_string_lossy().to_string())
3525 | +                 },
3526 | +                 Message::FolderPicked,
3527 | +             ),
3528 | +             Message::FolderPicked(Some(f)) => {
3529 | +                 if !app.settings.index_dirs.iter().any(|d| d == &f) {
3530 | +                     app.settings.index_dirs.push(f);
3531 | +                     app.save_settings();
3532 | +                 }
3533 | +                 Task::none()
3534 | +             }
3535 | +             Message::FolderPicked(None) => Task::none(),
3536 | +             Message::RemoveFolder(i) => {
3537 | +                 if i < app.settings.index_dirs.len() {
3538 | +                     app.settings.index_dirs.remove(i);
3539 | +                 }
3540 | +                 Task::none()
3541 | +             }
3542 | +             Message::SaveSettings => {
3543 | +                 app.save_settings();
3544 | +                 Task::none()
3545 | +             }
3546 | +             Message::MaxResultsChanged(val) => {
3547 | +                 if let Ok(num) = val.parse() {
3548 | +                     app.settings.max_results = num;
3549 | +                 }
3550 | +                 Task::none()
3551 | +             }
3552 | +             Message::ExcludePatternsChanged(val) => {
3553 | +                 app.settings.exclude_patterns =
3554 | +                     val.split(',').map(|s| s.trim().to_string()).collect();
3555 | +                 Task::none()
3556 | +             }
3557 | +             Message::ToggleTheme => {
3558 | +                 app.is_dark = !app.is_dark;
3559 | +                 app.settings.theme = if app.is_dark {
3560 | +                     crate::settings::Theme::Dark
3561 | +                 } else {
3562 | +                     crate::settings::Theme::Light
3563 | +                 };
3564 | +                 app.save_settings();
3565 | +                 Task::none()
3566 | +             }
3567 | +             Message::ToggleMinimizeToTray(enabled) => {
3568 | +                 app.settings.minimize_to_tray = enabled;
3569 | +                 app.save_settings();
3570 | +                 Task::none()
3571 | +             }
3572 | +             Message::ToggleAutoStart(enabled) => {
3573 | +                 app.settings.auto_start_on_boot = enabled;
3574 | +                 app.save_settings();
3575 | +                 let _ = crate::system::startup::set_auto_start(enabled);
3576 | +                 Task::none()
3577 | +             }
3578 | +             Message::ToggleContextMenu(enabled) => {
3579 | +                 app.settings.context_menu_enabled = enabled;
3580 | +                 app.save_settings();
3581 | +                 let _ = crate::system::context_menu::register_context_menu(enabled);
3582 | +                 Task::none()
3583 | +             }
3584 | +             Message::ToggleSearchMode => {
3585 | +                 app.search_mode = match app.search_mode {
3586 | +                     SearchMode::FullText => SearchMode::Filename,
3587 | +                     SearchMode::Filename => SearchMode::FullText,
3588 | +                 };
3589 | +                 if !app.search_query.is_empty() {
3590 | +                     app.perform_search()
3591 | +                 } else {
3592 | +                     Task::none()
3593 | +                 }
3594 | +             }
3595 | +             Message::FilterExtensionChanged(ext) => {
3596 | +                 app.filter_extension = ext;
3597 | +                 Task::none()
3598 | +             }
3599 | +             Message::FilterSizeChanged(size) => {
3600 | +                 app.filter_size = size;
3601 | +                 Task::none()
3602 | +             }
3603 | +             Message::PreviewRequested(idx) => {
3604 | +                 app.selected_index = Some(idx);
3605 | +                 app.load_preview()
3606 | +             }
3607 | +             Message::PreviewLoaded(content) => {
3608 | +                 app.preview_content = content;
3609 | +                 app.is_loading_preview = false;
3610 | +                 Task::none()
3611 | +             }
3612 | +             Message::MoveUp => {
3613 | +                 if let Some(current) = app.selected_index {
3614 | +                     if current > 0 {
3615 | +                         app.selected_index = Some(current - 1);
3616 | +                         return app.load_preview();
3617 | +                     }
3618 | +                 }
3619 | +                 Task::none()
3620 | +             }
3621 | +             Message::MoveDown => {
3622 | +                 if let Some(current) = app.selected_index {
3623 | +                     if !app.results.is_empty() && current < app.results.len() - 1 {
3624 | +                         app.selected_index = Some(current + 1);
3625 | +                         return app.load_preview();
3626 | +                     }
3627 | +                 }
3628 | +                 Task::none()
3629 | +             }
3630 | +             Message::DismissError => Task::none(),
3631 | +             Message::Quit => {
3632 | +                 std::process::exit(0);
3633 | +             }
3634 | +             Message::GlobalHotkeyChanged(val) => {
3635 | +                 app.settings.global_hotkey = val;
3636 | +                 Task::none()
3637 | +             }
3638 | +             Message::StartPollingProgress => Task::none(),
3639 | +             Message::PollHotkey => {
3640 | +                 if let Ok(event) = global_hotkey::GlobalHotKeyEvent::receiver().try_recv() {
3641 | +                     // Hotkey pressed, we just log it or toggle search window focus
3642 | +                     tracing::info!("Global hotkey pressed: {:?}", event);
3643 | +                     // To truly bring Iced to front, we'd need to use Iced window commands,
3644 | +                     // but since iced 0.13 removed some easy window visibility toggles without IDs,
3645 | +                     // we'll just focus the search query for now, or just let OS handle if hooked.
3646 | +                 }
3647 | +                 Task::none()
3648 | +             }
3649 | +             Message::PollTray => {
3650 | +                 if let Ok(event) = tray_icon::menu::MenuEvent::receiver().try_recv() {
3651 | +                     if event.id.0 == "quit" {
3652 | +                         std::process::exit(0);
3653 | +                     } else if event.id.0 == "show" {
3654 | +                         // TODO: Map to actual window focus task if needed
3655 | +                     }
3656 | +                 }
3657 | +                 Task::none()
3658 | +             }
3659 | +         }
3660 | +     }
3661 | + }

3663 | + #[allow(dead_code)]
3664 | + fn subscription(_app: &App) -> Subscription<Message> {
3665 | +     let subs = vec![
3666 | +         iced::time::every(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100)).map(|_| Message::PollHotkey),
3667 | +         iced::time::every(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100)).map(|_| Message::PollTray),
3668 | +     ];
3669 | + 
3670 | +     Subscription::batch(subs)
3671 | + }

3673 | + fn view(app: &App) -> Element<'_, Message> {
3674 | +     match app.active_tab {
3675 | +         Tab::Search => search::search_view(app),
3676 | +         Tab::Settings => settings::settings_view(app),
3677 | +     }
3678 | + }
3679 | + 
3680 | + #[allow(dead_code)]
3681 | + fn error_view(error: &str) -> Element<'_, Message> {
3682 | +     use iced::widget::{button, column, container, text, Space};
3683 | +     use iced::{Alignment, Length, Padding};
3684 | + 
3685 | +     container(
3686 | +         column![
3687 | +             text("An Error Occurred").size(24),
3688 | +             Space::new().height(Length::Fixed(16.0)),
3689 | +             text(error).size(14),
3690 | +             Space::new().height(Length::Fixed(24.0)),
3691 | +             button(text("Dismiss"))
3692 | +                 .on_press(Message::DismissError)
3693 | +                 .padding(Padding::new(12.0))
3694 | +         ]
3695 | +         .align_x(Alignment::Center),
3696 | +     )
3697 | +     .width(Length::Fill)
3698 | +     .height(Length::Fill)
3699 | +     .center_x(Length::Fill)
3700 | +     .center_y(Length::Fill)
3701 | +     .into()
3702 | + }
3703 | + 
3704 | + fn app_title(_app: &App) -> String {
3705 | +     String::from("Flash Search")
3706 | + }
3707 | + 
3708 | + fn app_theme(app: &App) -> iced::Theme {
3709 | +     if app.is_dark {
3710 | +         iced::Theme::Dark
3711 | +     } else {
3712 | +         iced::Theme::Light
3713 | +     }
3714 | + }
3715 | + 
3716 | + pub fn run_ui(
3717 | +     state: Result<std::sync::Arc<AppState>, String>,
3718 | +     progress_rx: mpsc::Receiver<ProgressEvent>,
3719 | + ) {
3720 | +     let progress_mutex = Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(progress_rx));
3721 | + 
3722 | +     iced::application(
3723 | +         move || {
3724 | +             let mut app = App::new(state.clone());
3725 | +             app.progress_rx = Some(progress_mutex.clone());
3726 | +             (app, Task::none())
3727 | +         },
3728 | +         update,
3729 | +         view,
3730 | +     )
3731 | +     .title(app_title)
3732 | +     .theme(app_theme)
3733 | +     .window(iced::window::Settings {
3734 | +         size: iced::Size::new(1000.0, 700.0),
3735 | +         position: iced::window::Position::Centered,
3736 | +         ..Default::default()
3737 | +     })
3738 | +     .run()
3739 | +     .unwrap();
3740 | + }

3767 | + fn load_icon(icon_name: &str) -> iced::widget::Svg<'_> {
3768 | +     let svg_data = match icon_name {
3769 | +         "search" => SEARCH_ICON_SVG,
3770 | +         "file" => FILE_ICON_SVG,
3771 | +         "settings" => SETTINGS_ICON_SVG,
3772 | +         "refresh" => REFRESH_ICON_SVG,
3773 | +         "sun" => SUN_ICON_SVG,
3774 | +         "moon" => MOON_ICON_SVG,
3775 | +         _ => SEARCH_ICON_SVG,
3776 | +     };
3777 | +     svg::Svg::new(svg::Handle::from_memory(svg_data.as_bytes().to_vec()))

3780 | + pub fn search_view(app: &App) -> Element<'_, Message> {
3781 | +     let mode_text = match app.search_mode {
3782 | +         SearchMode::FullText => "Full Text",
3783 | +         SearchMode::Filename => "Filename",
3784 | +     };
3785 | + 
3786 | +     let search_icon = container(load_icon("search")).padding(Padding::new(12.0));
3787 | + 
3788 | +     let input = TextInput::new("Search files...", &app.search_query)
3789 | +         .on_input(Message::SearchQueryChanged)
3790 | +         .on_submit(Message::SearchSubmitted)
3791 | +         .padding(Padding::new(12.0))
3792 | +         .size(16)
3793 | +         .style(theme::search_input())
3794 | +         .width(Length::Fill);
3795 | + 
3796 | +     let search_btn = button(container(load_icon("search")).padding(Padding::new(8.0)))
3797 | +         .on_press(Message::SearchSubmitted)
3798 | +         .style(theme::primary_button())
3799 | +         .padding(Padding::new(12.0));
3800 | + 
3801 | +     let search_bar = row![search_icon, input, search_btn]
3802 | +         .spacing(0)
3803 | +         .align_y(Alignment::Center)
3804 | +         .width(Length::Fill);
3805 | + 
3806 | +     let search_bar_container = container(search_bar)
3807 | +         .style(theme::input_container)
3808 | +         .width(Length::Fill);
3809 | + 
3810 | +     let error_display: Element<Message> = if let Some(ref err) = app.search_error {
3811 | +         container(
3812 | +             row![
3813 | +                 container(load_icon("settings")).style(|_theme| theme::error_container_style()),
3814 | +                 text(err).size(13).style(|_theme| theme::error_text_style()),

4287 | + pub fn primary_button() -> impl Fn(&Theme, button::Status) -> button::Style {

4304 | +             ..Default::default()

4343 | +

4599 | + impl FilenameIndex {
4600 | +     pub fn open(data_path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {

4603 | +         let entries = if data_path.exists() {
4604 | +             // Try bincode first, then fall back to legacy JSON
4605 | +             let bin_path = data_path.join(INDEX_FILENAME);

4607 | + 
4608 | +             if bin_path.exists() {
4609 | +                 match std::fs::read(&bin_path) {
4610 | +                     Ok(bytes) => {
4611 | +                         match bincode::serde::decode_from_slice(&bytes, bincode::config::standard())
4612 | +                         {
4613 | +                             Ok((entries, _)) => {
4614 | +                                 let entries: Vec<FilenameEntry> = entries;
4615 | +                                 tracing::info!(
4616 | +                                     "Loaded {} filenames from bincode index",
4617 | +                                     entries.len()
4618 | +                                 );
4619 | +                                 entries
4620 | +                             }
4621 | +                             Err(e) => {
4622 | +                                 tracing::warn!("Failed to parse bincode filename index: {}", e);
4623 | +                                 Vec::new()
4624 | +                             }
4625 | +                         }
4626 | +                     }
4627 | +                     Err(_) => Vec::new(),
4628 | +                 }
4629 | +             } else if json_path.exists() {
4630 | +                 // Migrate from legacy JSON
4631 | +                 match std::fs::read_to_string(&json_path) {
4632 | +                     Ok(content) => match serde_json::from_str::<Vec<FilenameEntry>>(&content) {
4633 | +                         Ok(entries) => {
4634 | +                             tracing::info!(
4635 | +                                 "Migrated {} filenames from legacy JSON index",
4636 | +                                 entries.len()
4637 | +                             );
4638 | +                             // Save as bincode immediately and remove JSON
4639 | +                             Self::save_to_disk_sync(&entries, &data_path);
4640 | +                             let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&json_path);
4641 | +                             entries
4642 | +                         }
4643 | +                         Err(e) => {
4644 | +                             tracing::warn!("Failed to parse legacy JSON filename index: {}", e);
4645 | +                             Vec::new()
4646 | +                         }
4647 | +                     },
4648 | +                     Err(_) => Vec::new(),
4649 | +                 }
4650 | +             } else {
4651 | +                 Vec::new()
4652 | +             }
4653 | +         } else {
4654 | +             Vec::new()
4655 | +         };

4657 | +         let fst_map = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Self::build_fst(&entries)));
4658 | + 
4659 | +         Ok(Self {

4661 | +             data_path,
4662 | +             fst_map,
4663 | +         })
4664 | +     }

4666 | +     pub fn add_file(&self, path: &str, name: &str) -> Result<()> {
4667 | +         let entry = FilenameEntry {
4668 | +             path: path.to_string(),
4669 | +             name: name.to_string(),
4670 | +         };
4671 | + 
4672 | +         let entries = self.entries.clone();
4673 | + 
4674 | +         if let Ok(mut guard) = entries.write() {
4675 | +             guard.push(entry);
4676 | + 
4677 | +             if guard.len() % 1000 == 0 {
4678 | +                 let data = guard.clone();
4679 | +                 let data_path = self.data_path.clone();
4680 | +                 std::thread::spawn(move || {
4681 | +                     Self::save_to_disk_sync(&data, &data_path);
4682 | +                 });
4683 | +             }
4684 | +         }
4685 | + 
4686 | +         Ok(())
4687 | +     }
4688 | + 
4689 | +     pub fn commit(&self) -> Result<()> {
4690 | +         let entries = self.entries.clone();
4691 | + 
4692 | +         if let Ok(guard) = entries.read() {
4693 | +             let data_path = self.data_path.clone();
4694 | +             let data: Vec<_> = guard
4695 | +                 .iter()
4696 | +                 .map(|e| FilenameEntry {
4697 | +                     path: e.path.clone(),
4698 | +                     name: e.name.clone(),
4699 | +                 })
4700 | +                 .collect();
4701 | + 
4702 | +             if let Ok(mut fst_guard) = self.fst_map.write() {
4703 | +                 *fst_guard = Self::build_fst(&data);
4704 | +             }
4705 | + 
4706 | +             std::thread::spawn(move || {
4707 | +                 Self::save_to_disk_sync(&data, &data_path);
4708 | +             });
4709 | +         }
4710 | + 
4711 | +         Ok(())
4712 | +     }
4713 | + 
4714 | +     fn build_fst(entries: &[FilenameEntry]) -> Vec<u8> {
4715 | +         let mut items: Vec<(String, u64)> = entries
4716 | +             .iter()
4717 | +             .enumerate()
4718 | +             .map(|(i, e)| (format!("{}\0{}", e.name.to_lowercase(), i), i as u64))
4719 | +             .collect();
4720 | +         items.sort_by(|a, b| a.0.cmp(&b.0));
4721 | + 
4722 | +         let mut build = fst::MapBuilder::memory();
4723 | +         for (k, v) in items {
4724 | +             let _ = build.insert(k, v);
4725 | +         }
4726 | +         build.into_inner().unwrap_or_default()
4727 | +     }
4728 | + 
4729 | +     /// Save entries to disk using bincode (P3: replaces JSON for ~10x smaller + faster)
4730 | +     fn save_to_disk_sync(entries: &[FilenameEntry], data_path: &std::path::Path) {
4731 | +         match bincode::serde::encode_to_vec(entries, bincode::config::standard()) {
4732 | +             Ok(bytes) => {
4733 | +                 let _ = std::fs::write(data_path.join(INDEX_FILENAME), bytes);
4734 | +             }
4735 | +             Err(e) => {
4736 | +                 tracing::warn!("Failed to serialize filename index: {}", e);
4737 | +             }
4738 | +         }
4739 | +     }
4740 | + 
4741 | +     pub fn search(&self, query: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<FilenameSearchResult>> {
4742 | +         let fst_bytes = self.fst_map.read().unwrap();
4743 | +         if fst_bytes.is_empty() {
4744 | +             return Ok(Vec::new());
4745 | +         }
4746 | + 
4747 | +         // FST Map
4748 | +         let map = match fst::Map::new(fst_bytes.clone()) {
4749 | +             Ok(m) => m,
4750 | +             Err(_) => return Ok(Vec::new()),
4751 | +         };
4752 | + 
4753 | +         // Fuzzy / Subsequence matching
4754 | +         let query_lower = query.to_lowercase();
4755 | +         let aut = Subsequence::new(&query_lower);
4756 | + 
4757 | +         let mut stream = map.search(aut).into_stream();
4758 | + 
4759 | +         let entries_lock = match self.entries.read() {
4760 | +             Ok(g) => g,
4761 | +             Err(_) => return Ok(Vec::new()),
4762 | +         };
4763 | + 
4764 | +         let mut results = Vec::new();
4765 | +         while let Some((_, v)) = stream.next() {
4766 | +             if results.len() >= limit {
4767 | +                 break;
4768 | +             }
4769 | +             if let Some(entry) = entries_lock.get(v as usize) {
4770 | +                 results.push(FilenameSearchResult {
4771 | +                     file_path: entry.path.clone(),
4772 | +                     file_name: entry.name.clone(),
4773 | +                 });
4774 | +             }
4775 | +         }
4776 | + 
4777 | +         Ok(results)
4778 | +     }
4779 | + 
4780 | +     pub fn clear(&self) -> Result<()> {
4781 | +         let entries = self.entries.clone();
4782 | + 
4783 | +         if let Ok(mut guard) = entries.write() {
4784 | +             guard.clear();
4785 | +             let data_path = self.data_path.clone();
4786 | +             std::thread::spawn(move || {
4787 | +                 let _ = std::fs::remove_file(data_path.join(INDEX_FILENAME));
4788 | +                 let _ = std::fs::remove_file(data_path.join(LEGACY_INDEX_FILENAME));
4789 | +             });
4790 | +         }
4791 | + 
4792 | +         Ok(())
4793 | +     }
4794 | + 
4795 | +     pub fn get_stats(&self) -> Result<FilenameIndexStats> {
4796 | +         let entries = self.entries.clone();
4797 | + 
4798 | +         let entries = match entries.read() {
4799 | +             Ok(guard) => guard,
4800 | +             Err(_) => {
4801 | +                 return Ok(FilenameIndexStats {
4802 | +                     total_files: 0,
4803 | +                     index_size_bytes: 0,
4804 | +                 })
4805 | +             }
4806 | +         };
4807 | + 
4808 | +         let size: u64 = entries
4809 | +             .iter()
4810 | +             .map(|e| e.path.len() as u64 + e.name.len() as u64 + 32)
4811 | +             .sum();
4812 | + 
4813 | +         Ok(FilenameIndexStats {
4814 | +             total_files: entries.len(),
4815 | +             index_size_bytes: size,
4816 | +         })
4817 | +     }
4818 | + 
4819 | +     pub fn rebuild_index(&self, paths: Vec<(String, String)>) -> Result<()> {
4820 | +         let entries = self.entries.clone();
4821 | +         let data_path = self.data_path.clone();
4822 | + 
4823 | +         if let Ok(mut guard) = entries.write() {
4824 | +             *guard = paths
4825 | +                 .into_iter()
4826 | +                 .map(|(path, name)| FilenameEntry { path, name })
4827 | +                 .collect();
4828 | + 
4829 | +             let data: Vec<_> = guard
4830 | +                 .iter()
4831 | +                 .map(|e| FilenameEntry {
4832 | +                     path: e.path.clone(),
4833 | +                     name: e.name.clone(),
4834 | +                 })
4835 | +                 .collect();
4836 | + 
4837 | +             if let Ok(mut fst_guard) = self.fst_map.write() {
4838 | +                 *fst_guard = Self::build_fst(&data);
4839 | +             }
4840 | + 
4841 | +             std::thread::spawn(move || {
4842 | +                 Self::save_to_disk_sync(&data, &data_path);
4843 | +             });
4844 | +         }
4845 | + 
4846 | +         Ok(())

4872 | + /// Current schema version - bump this when schema changes

4875 | + fn get_schema_version_path(index_path: &Path) -> PathBuf {

4889 | + /// Central manager for the Tantivy search index
4890 | + pub struct IndexManager {

4897 | + impl IndexManager {
4898 | +     /// Open or create index at the specified path
4899 | +     pub fn open(index_path: &Path, memory_limit_mb: u32) -> Result<Self> {
4900 | +         let schema = create_schema();
4901 | + 
4902 | +         // Ensure directory exists
4903 | +         if !index_path.exists() {
4904 | +             std::fs::create_dir_all(index_path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
4905 | +         }
4906 | + 
4907 | +         // Check schema version - if mismatch, rebuild index
4908 | +         let stored_version = read_schema_version(index_path);
4909 | +         if let Some(ref ver) = stored_version {
4910 | +             if ver != SCHEMA_VERSION {
4911 | +                 warn!(
4912 | +                     "Schema version mismatch: stored={}, current={}. Rebuilding index...",
4913 | +                     ver, SCHEMA_VERSION
4914 | +                 );
4915 | +                 std::fs::remove_dir_all(index_path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
4916 | +                 std::fs::create_dir_all(index_path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
4917 | +                 write_schema_version(index_path, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
4918 | +             }
4919 | +         } else if index_path.join("meta.json").exists() {
4920 | +             // Old index without version - rebuild
4921 | +             warn!("No schema version found. Rebuilding index...");
4922 | +             std::fs::remove_dir_all(index_path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
4923 | +             std::fs::create_dir_all(index_path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
4924 | +             write_schema_version(index_path, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
4925 | +         } else {
4926 | +             // New index - write version
4927 | +             write_schema_version(index_path, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
4928 | +         }
4929 | + 
4930 | +         // Use memory-mapped directory for efficient I/O
4931 | +         let directory = MmapDirectory::open(index_path)
4932 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to open index directory: {}", e)))?;
4933 | + 
4934 | +         let index = Index::open_or_create(directory, schema.clone())
4935 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to open or create index: {}", e)))?;
4936 | + 
4937 | +         info!(
4938 | +             "Opened index at {} with schema version {}",
4939 | +             index_path.display(),
4940 | +             SCHEMA_VERSION
4941 | +         );
4942 | + 
4943 | +         let writer = IndexWriterManager::new(&index, memory_limit_mb)?;
4944 | +         let searcher = IndexSearcher::new(&index, index_path.to_path_buf())?;
4945 | + 
4946 | +         Ok(Self {
4947 | +             index,
4948 | +             writer,
4949 | +             searcher,
4950 | +         })
4951 | +     }
4952 | + 
4953 | +     /// Add a document to the index
4954 | +     pub fn add_document(&self, doc: &ParsedDocument, modified: u64, size: u64) -> Result<()> {
4955 | +         self.writer.add_document(doc, modified, size)
4956 | +     }
4957 | + 
4958 | +     /// Add multiple documents in a single lock acquisition (much more efficient)
4959 | +     pub fn add_documents_batch(&self, docs: &[(ParsedDocument, u64, u64)]) -> Result<()> {
4960 | +         self.writer.add_documents_batch(docs)
4961 | +     }
4962 | + 
4963 | +     /// Remove a document from the index
4964 | +     pub fn remove_document(&self, path: &str) -> Result<()> {
4965 | +         self.writer.remove_document(path)
4966 | +     }
4967 | + 
4968 | +     /// Clear all documents from the index
4969 | +     pub fn clear(&self) -> Result<()> {
4970 | +         self.writer.delete_all_documents()
4971 | +     }
4972 | + 
4973 | +     /// Commit pending changes
4974 | +     pub fn commit(&self) -> Result<()> {
4975 | +         self.writer.commit()
4976 | +     }
4977 | + 
4978 | +     /// Search the index (async with caching)
4979 | +     pub async fn search(
4980 | +         &self,
4981 | +         query: &str,
4982 | +         limit: usize,
4983 | +         min_size: Option<u64>,
4984 | +         max_size: Option<u64>,

4986 | +     ) -> Result<Vec<SearchResult>> {

4989 | +             .await
4990 | +     }
4991 | +

4995 | +     }
4996 | + 
4997 | +     /// Get index statistics

5001 | + }

5010 | + use regex::Regex;
5011 | + use std::sync::OnceLock;
5012 | + 
5013 | + static OPERATOR_REGEX: OnceLock<Regex> = OnceLock::new();
5014 | + 
5015 | + /// Parsed query with operators and search terms
5016 | + #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
5017 | + pub struct ParsedQuery {
5018 | +     /// The original text query (for Tantivy)
5019 | +     pub text_query: String,
5020 | +     /// Extension filter (e.g., "pdf", "docx")
5021 | +     pub extension: Option<String>,
5022 | +     /// Path filter (search in specific path)
5023 | +     pub path_filter: Option<String>,
5024 | +     /// Title filter
5025 | +     pub title_filter: Option<String>,
5026 | +     /// Size filters
5027 | +     pub min_size: Option<u64>,
5028 | +     pub max_size: Option<u64>,
5029 | +     /// Whether fuzzy matching is enabled
5030 | +     pub fuzzy: bool,
5031 | + }
5032 | + 
5033 | + impl ParsedQuery {
5034 | +     pub fn new(query: &str) -> Self {
5035 | +         Self::parse(query)
5036 | +     }
5037 | + 
5038 | +     fn parse(input: &str) -> Self {
5039 | +         let mut extension = None;
5040 | +         let mut path_filter = None;
5041 | +         let mut title_filter = None;
5042 | +         let mut min_size = None;
5043 | +         let mut max_size = None;
5044 | +         let fuzzy = true;
5045 | + 
5046 | +         // Parse operators
5047 | +         // ext:pdf, path:docs, title:report, size:>1MB, size:<10MB, exact:"phrase"
5048 | +         let operator_regex = OPERATOR_REGEX.get_or_init(|| {
5049 | +             Regex::new(
5050 | +                 r#"(?i)(ext|path|title|size):(?:([<>]?)(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)(MB|KB|GB|B)?|"([^"]*)"|(\S+))"#,
5051 | +             )
5052 | +             .unwrap()
5053 | +         });
5054 | + 
5055 | +         let mut remaining = input.to_string();
5056 | + 
5057 | +         // Process all operators
5058 | +         for cap in operator_regex.captures_iter(input) {
5059 | +             let operator = cap
5060 | +                 .get(1)
5061 | +                 .map(|m| m.as_str().to_lowercase())
5062 | +                 .unwrap_or_default();
5063 | +             let value = cap
5064 | +                 .get(5)
5065 | +                 .map(|m| m.as_str().to_string()) // Quoted value
5066 | +                 .or_else(|| cap.get(6).map(|m| m.as_str().to_string())) // Unquoted value
5067 | +                 .unwrap_or_default();
5068 | + 
5069 | +             match operator.as_str() {
5070 | +                 "ext" => {
5071 | +                     extension = Some(value.trim_start_matches('.').to_lowercase());
5072 | +                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
5073 | +                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
5074 | +                     }
5075 | +                 }
5076 | +                 "path" => {
5077 | +                     path_filter = Some(value.to_lowercase());
5078 | +                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
5079 | +                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
5080 | +                     }
5081 | +                 }
5082 | +                 "title" => {
5083 | +                     title_filter = Some(value.to_lowercase());
5084 | +                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
5085 | +                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
5086 | +                     }
5087 | +                 }
5088 | +                 "size" => {
5089 | +                     // Handle size operators
5090 | +                     if let Some(op) = cap.get(2) {
5091 | +                         let op = op.as_str();
5092 | +                         if let Some(num_str) = cap.get(3) {
5093 | +                             if let Ok(num) = num_str.as_str().parse::<f64>() {
5094 | +                                 let multiplier = cap
5095 | +                                     .get(4)
5096 | +                                     .map(|m| match m.as_str().to_uppercase().as_str() {
5097 | +                                         "GB" => 1024 * 1024 * 1024,
5098 | +                                         "MB" => 1024 * 1024,
5099 | +                                         "KB" => 1024,
5100 | +                                         _ => 1,
5101 | +                                     })
5102 | +                                     .unwrap_or(1);
5103 | + 
5104 | +                                 let bytes = (num * multiplier as f64) as u64;
5105 | +                                 match op {
5106 | +                                     ">" => min_size = Some(bytes),
5107 | +                                     "<" => max_size = Some(bytes),
5108 | +                                     _ => {}
5109 | +                                 }
5110 | +                             }
5111 | +                         }
5112 | +                     } else if let Ok(size_val) = value.parse::<u64>() {
5113 | +                         // Exact size match (treat as minimum for practical purposes)
5114 | +                         min_size = Some(size_val);
5115 | +                         max_size = Some(size_val + 1);
5116 | +                     }
5117 | +                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
5118 | +                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
5119 | +                     }
5120 | +                 }
5121 | +                 _ => {}
5122 | +             }
5123 | +         }
5124 | + 
5125 | +         // Clean up remaining text for full-text search
5126 | +         let text_query = remaining
5127 | +             .split_whitespace()
5128 | +             .collect::<Vec<_>>()
5129 | +             .join(" ")
5130 | +             .trim()
5131 | +             .to_string();
5132 | + 
5133 | +         Self {
5134 | +             text_query: if text_query.is_empty() {
5135 | +                 "*".to_string()
5136 | +             } else {
5137 | +                 text_query
5138 | +             },
5139 | +             extension,
5140 | +             path_filter,
5141 | +             title_filter,
5142 | +             min_size,
5143 | +             max_size,
5144 | +             fuzzy,
5145 | +         }
5146 | +     }
5147 | + 
5148 | +     /// Check if a path matches the extension filter
5149 | +     pub fn matches_extension(&self, path: &str) -> bool {
5150 | +         if let Some(ref ext) = self.extension {
5151 | +             let path_lower = path.to_lowercase();
5152 | +             path_lower.ends_with(&format!(".{}", ext))
5153 | +         } else {
5154 | +             true
5155 | +         }
5156 | +     }
5157 | + 
5158 | +     /// Check if a path matches the path filter
5159 | +     pub fn matches_path(&self, path: &str) -> bool {
5160 | +         if let Some(ref filter) = self.path_filter {
5161 | +             path.to_lowercase().contains(filter)
5162 | +         } else {
5163 | +             true
5164 | +         }
5165 | +     }
5166 | + 
5167 | +     /// Check if a title matches the title filter
5168 | +     pub fn matches_title(&self, title: Option<&str>) -> bool {
5169 | +         if let Some(ref filter) = self.title_filter {
5170 | +             if let Some(t) = title {
5171 | +                 t.to_lowercase().contains(filter)
5172 | +             } else {
5173 | +                 false
5174 | +             }
5175 | +         } else {
5176 | +             true
5177 | +         }
5178 | +     }
5179 | + }
5180 | + 
5181 | + /// Extract search terms for highlighting from a query
5182 | + pub fn extract_highlight_terms(query: &str) -> Vec<String> {
5183 | +     let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
5184 | + 
5185 | +     let mut terms: Vec<String> = parsed
5186 | +         .text_query
5187 | +         .split_whitespace()
5188 | +         .filter(|t| *t != "*")
5189 | +         .map(|t| t.to_lowercase())
5190 | +         .collect();
5191 | + 
5192 | +     if terms.is_empty() && parsed.text_query == "*" {
5193 | +         terms.push("*".to_string());
5194 | +     }
5195 | + 
5196 | +     // Also add title filter terms
5197 | +     if let Some(title) = parsed.title_filter {
5198 | +         terms.push(title);
5199 | +     }
5200 | + 
5201 | +     terms
5202 | + }
5203 | +

5210 | +         let query = "ext:pdf report";
5211 | +         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
5212 | +         assert_eq!(parsed.extension, Some("pdf".to_string()));

5249 | + ```rust
5250 | + use tantivy::schema::*;
5251 | + 
5252 | + /// Create Tantivy schema optimized for file search
5253 | + pub fn create_schema() -> Schema {
5254 | +     let mut schema_builder = Schema::builder();
5255 | + 
5256 | +     // File path - stored for retrieval, indexed for exact matches
5257 | +     schema_builder.add_text_field("file_path", STRING | STORED);
5258 | + 
5259 | +     // Content - indexed for search but NOT stored (to save RAM)
5260 | +     // We retrieve content from disk on demand
5261 | +     let text_options = TextOptions::default().set_indexing_options(
5262 | +         TextFieldIndexing::default()
5263 | +             .set_tokenizer("default")
5264 | +             .set_index_option(IndexRecordOption::WithFreqsAndPositions),
5265 | +     );
5266 | +     schema_builder.add_text_field("content", text_options);
5267 | + 
5268 | +     // Title - stored for display, indexed for search
5269 | +     schema_builder.add_text_field("title", TEXT | STORED);
5270 | + 
5271 | +     // Modified timestamp - indexed for sorting
5272 | +     schema_builder.add_date_field("modified", FAST | INDEXED);
5273 | + 
5274 | +     // File size - indexed for range queries
5275 | +     schema_builder.add_u64_field("size", FAST | INDEXED);
5276 | + 
5277 | +     // File extension - indexed as keyword for fast filtering
5278 | +     schema_builder.add_text_field("extension", STRING | STORED);
5279 | + 
5280 | +     schema_builder.build()
5281 | + }
5282 | + ```

5318 | +

5350 | + impl QueryCache {
5351 | +     pub fn new() -> Self {

5356 | +                 .build(),
