5385 | + /// Manages searching the Tantivy index
5386 | + pub struct IndexSearcher {
5387 | +     reader: IndexReader,
5388 | +     query_parser: QueryParser,
5389 | +     // schema: Schema,
5390 | +     path_field: Field,
5391 | +     title_field: Field,
5392 | +     size_field: Field,
5393 | +     content_field: Field,
5394 | +     extension_field: Field,
5395 | +     cache: QueryCache,
5396 | +     index_path: std::path::PathBuf,
5397 | + }
5398 | + 
5399 | + impl IndexSearcher {
5400 | +     pub fn new(index: &Index, index_path: std::path::PathBuf) -> Result<Self> {
5401 | +         let schema = index.schema();
5402 | + 
5403 | +         // Pre-warm the reader by loading index into memory on startup
5404 | +         // This ensures first search is fast (no initial load latency)
5405 | +         let reader = index
5406 | +             .reader_builder()
5407 | +             .reload_policy(ReloadPolicy::OnCommitWithDelay)
5408 | +             .try_into()
5409 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::search("create_index_reader", e.to_string()))?;
5410 | + 
5411 | +         // Pre-warm: load the index reader
5412 | +         reader.reload().ok();
5413 | + 
5414 | +         // Get field references once to avoid repeated lookups
5415 | +         let path_field = schema
5416 | +             .get_field("file_path")
5417 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("file_path", "Field not found in schema"))?;
5418 | +         let title_field = schema
5419 | +             .get_field("title")
5420 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("title", "Field not found in schema"))?;
5421 | +         let size_field = schema
5422 | +             .get_field("size")
5423 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("size", "Field not found in schema"))?;
5424 | +         let content_field = schema
5425 | +             .get_field("content")
5426 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("content", "Field not found in schema"))?;
5427 | +         let extension_field = schema
5428 | +             .get_field("extension")
5429 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("extension", "Field not found in schema"))?;
5430 | + 
5431 | +         // Search across content, title, and file_path fields
5432 | +         let default_fields: Vec<Field> = ["content", "title", "file_path"]
5433 | +             .iter()
5434 | +             .filter_map(|field_name| schema.get_field(field_name).ok())
5435 | +             .collect();
5436 | + 
5437 | +         let query_parser = QueryParser::for_index(index, default_fields);
5438 | + 
5439 | +         Ok(Self {
5440 | +             reader,
5441 | +             query_parser,
5442 | +             path_field,
5443 | +             title_field,
5444 | +             size_field,
5445 | +             content_field,
5446 | +             extension_field,
5447 | +             cache: QueryCache::new(),
5448 | +             index_path,
5449 | +         })
5450 | +     }
5451 | + 
5452 | +     /// Search the index and return top results with optional filters
5453 | +     pub async fn search(
5454 | +         &self,
5455 | +         query: &str,
5456 | +         limit: usize,
5457 | +         min_size: Option<u64>,
5458 | +         max_size: Option<u64>,
5459 | +         file_extensions: Option<&[String]>,
5460 | +     ) -> Result<Vec<SearchResult>> {
5461 | +         use super::query_parser::{extract_highlight_terms, ParsedQuery};
5462 | + 
5463 | +         // Create cache key
5464 | +         let cache_key = CacheKey {
5465 | +             query: query.to_string(),
5466 | +             limit,
5467 | +             min_size,
5468 | +             max_size,
5469 | +             extensions: file_extensions.map(|e| e.to_vec()),
5470 | +         };
5471 | + 
5472 | +         // Check cache first
5473 | +         if let Some(cached) = self.cache.get(&cache_key) {
5474 | +             return Ok(cached);
5475 | +         }
5476 | + 
5477 | +         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
5478 | +         let highlight_terms = extract_highlight_terms(query);
5479 | + 
5480 | +         let searcher = self.reader.searcher();
5481 | + 
5482 | +         // Build the main query - use fuzzy search for better typo tolerance
5483 | +         let text_query = self.build_fuzzy_query(&parsed.text_query)?;
5484 | + 
5485 | +         // Build query with optional filters
5486 | +         let mut combine: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>)> =
5487 | +             vec![(Occur::Must, text_query)];
5488 | + 
5489 | +         // Add size filters
5490 | +         if min_size.is_some() || max_size.is_some() {
5491 | +             if let Some(min_val) = min_size {
5492 | +                 let lower = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, min_val);
5493 | +                 let upper = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, u64::MAX);
5494 | +                 let range = RangeQuery::new(Bound::Included(lower), Bound::Included(upper));
5495 | +                 combine.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(range)));
5496 | +             }
5497 | + 
5498 | +             if let Some(max_val) = max_size {
5499 | +                 let lower = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, 0);
5500 | +                 let upper = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, max_val);
5501 | +                 let range = RangeQuery::new(Bound::Included(lower), Bound::Included(upper));
5502 | +                 combine.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(range)));
5503 | +             }
5504 | +         }
5505 | + 
5506 | +         // Build file extension filter as a boolean query clause
5507 | +         if let Some(extensions) = file_extensions {
5508 | +             if !extensions.is_empty() {
5509 | +                 let extension_queries: Vec<_> = extensions
5510 | +                     .iter()
5511 | +                     .map(|ext| {
5512 | +                         let ext_lower = ext.to_lowercase();
5513 | +                         // Use TermQuery for fast extension matching (much faster than RegexQuery)
5514 | +                         let term = tantivy::Term::from_field_text(self.extension_field, &ext_lower);
5515 | +                         tantivy::query::TermQuery::new(term, IndexRecordOption::Basic)
5516 | +                     })
5517 | +                     .collect();
5518 | + 
5519 | +                 if !extension_queries.is_empty() {
5520 | +                     let extension_bool_query = tantivy::query::BooleanQuery::new(
5521 | +                         extension_queries
5522 | +                             .into_iter()
5523 | +                             .map(|q| (Occur::Should, Box::new(q) as Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>))
5524 | +                             .collect(),
5525 | +                     );
5526 | +                     combine.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(extension_bool_query)));
5527 | +                 }
5528 | +             }
5529 | +         }
5530 | + 
5531 | +         let final_query: Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query> = if combine.len() == 1 {
5532 | +             combine.remove(0).1
5533 | +         } else {
5534 | +             Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(combine))
5535 | +         };
5536 | + 
5537 | +         let top_docs = searcher
5538 | +             .search(&*final_query, &TopDocs::with_limit(limit))
5539 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::search(query, e.to_string()))?;
5540 | + 
5541 | +         let mut results = Vec::with_capacity(top_docs.len().min(limit));
5542 | + 
5543 | +         // Create snippet generator once outside the loop
5544 | +         let snippet_generator =
5545 | +             SnippetGenerator::create(&searcher, &*final_query, self.content_field)?;
5546 | + 
5547 | +         for (score, doc_address) in top_docs {
5548 | +             let retrieved_doc: TantivyDocument = searcher.doc(doc_address).map_err(|e| {
5549 | +                 FlashError::search(query, format!("Failed to retrieve document: {}", e))
5550 | +             })?;
5551 | + 
5552 | +             let file_path = retrieved_doc
5553 | +                 .get_first(self.path_field)
5554 | +                 .and_then(|f| f.as_str())
5555 | +                 .map(|s: &str| s.to_string())
5556 | +                 .unwrap_or_default();
5557 | + 
5558 | +             let title = retrieved_doc
5559 | +                 .get_first(self.title_field)
5560 | +                 .and_then(|f| f.as_str())
5561 | +                 .map(|s: &str| s.to_string());
5562 | + 
5563 | +             // Generate snippet using the pre-created generator
5564 | +             let snippet = snippet_generator.snippet_from_doc(&retrieved_doc);
5565 | +             // Get snippet as HTML then strip tags for plain text display
5566 | +             let snippet_html = snippet.to_html();
5567 | +             let snippet_text = snippet_html
5568 | +                 .replace("<b>", "")
5569 | +                 .replace("</b>", "")
5570 | +                 .replace("&lt;", "<")
5571 | +                 .replace("&gt;", ">")
5572 | +                 .replace("&amp;", "&");
5573 | + 
5574 | +             results.push(SearchResult {
5575 | +                 file_path,
5576 | +                 title,
5577 | +                 score,
5578 | +                 matched_terms: highlight_terms.clone(),
5579 | +                 snippet: Some(snippet_text),
5580 | +             });
5581 | + 
5582 | +             if results.len() >= limit {
5583 | +                 break;
5584 | +             }
5585 | +         }
5586 | + 
5587 | +         // Cache the results
5588 | +         self.cache.insert(cache_key, results.clone());
5589 | + 
5590 | +         Ok(results)
5591 | +     }
5592 | + 
5593 | +     /// Build a fuzzy query for better typo tolerance
5594 | +     fn build_fuzzy_query(&self, text_query: &str) -> Result<Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>> {
5595 | +         // Check if it's a phrase query (contains quoted strings)
5596 | +         static PHRASE_REGEX: OnceLock<regex::Regex> = OnceLock::new();
5597 | +         let phrase_regex = PHRASE_REGEX.get_or_init(|| regex::Regex::new(r#""([^"]+)""#).unwrap());
5598 | + 
5599 | +         if phrase_regex.is_match(text_query) {
5600 | +             // For phrase queries, use the query parser with phrase support
5601 | +             return Ok(Box::new(
5602 | +                 self.query_parser
5603 | +                     .parse_query(text_query)
5604 | +                     .map_err(|e| FlashError::search(text_query, e.to_string()))?,
5605 | +             ));
5606 | +         }
5607 | + 
5608 | +         // For regular queries, build a fuzzy query with OR for each term
5609 | +         let terms: Vec<&str> = text_query.split_whitespace().collect();
5610 | + 
5611 | +         if terms.is_empty() || (terms.len() == 1 && terms[0] == "*") {
5612 | +             // Match all
5613 | +             return Ok(Box::new(tantivy::query::AllQuery));
5614 | +         }
5615 | + 
5616 | +         if terms.len() == 1 {
5617 | +             // Single term - try exact first, then fuzzy
5618 | +             let term_text = terms[0];
5619 | +             let term = Term::from_field_text(self.content_field, term_text);
5620 | + 
5621 | +             // Try exact match first (higher priority)
5622 | +             let exact =
5623 | +                 tantivy::query::TermQuery::new(term, tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic);
5624 | + 
5625 | +             // Add fuzzy variant with edit distance of 2
5626 | +             let fuzzy_term = Term::from_field_text(self.content_field, term_text);
5627 | +             let fuzzy = FuzzyTermQuery::new(fuzzy_term, 2, true);
5628 | + 
5629 | +             // Combine with OR (exact first)
5630 | +             let combined = BooleanQuery::new(vec![
5631 | +                 (Occur::Should, Box::new(exact)),
5632 | +                 (Occur::Should, Box::new(fuzzy)),
5633 | +             ]);
5634 | + 
5635 | +             Ok(Box::new(combined))
5636 | +         } else {
5637 | +             // Multiple terms - build fuzzy query for each with AND logic
5638 | +             let mut subqueries: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>)> = Vec::new();
5639 | + 
5640 | +             for term_text in terms {
5641 | +                 let term = Term::from_field_text(self.content_field, term_text);
5642 | + 
5643 | +                 // Exact term query
5644 | +                 let exact = tantivy::query::TermQuery::new(
5645 | +                     term.clone(),
5646 | +                     tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,
5647 | +                 );
5648 | + 
5649 | +                 // Fuzzy variant
5650 | +                 let fuzzy = FuzzyTermQuery::new(term, 2, true);
5651 | + 
5652 | +                 // Combine exact and fuzzy for this term
5653 | +                 let term_query = BooleanQuery::new(vec![
5654 | +                     (Occur::Should, Box::new(exact)),
5655 | +                     (Occur::Should, Box::new(fuzzy)),
5656 | +                 ]);
5657 | + 
5658 | +                 subqueries.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(term_query)));
5659 | +             }
5660 | + 
5661 | +             Ok(Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(subqueries)))
5662 | +         }
5663 | +     }
5664 | + 
5665 | +     /// Invalidate the search cache (call after index updates)
5666 | +     pub fn invalidate_cache(&self) {
5667 | +         self.cache.invalidate();
5668 | +     }
5669 | + 
5670 | +     /// Get statistics about the index
5671 | +     pub fn get_statistics(&self) -> Result<IndexStatistics> {
5672 | +         let searcher = self.reader.searcher();
5673 | +         let total_docs = searcher.num_docs() as usize;
5674 | + 
5675 | +         let mut total_size = 0;
5676 | +         if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(&self.index_path) {
5677 | +             for entry in entries.flatten() {
5678 | +                 if let Ok(metadata) = entry.metadata() {
5679 | +                     if metadata.is_file() {
5680 | +                         total_size += metadata.len();
5681 | +                     }
5682 | +                 }
5683 | +             }
5684 | +         }
5685 | + 
5686 | +         Ok(IndexStatistics {
5687 | +             total_documents: total_docs,
5688 | +             total_size_bytes: total_size,
5689 | +             last_updated: Some(chrono::Local::now().format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S").to_string()),
5690 | +         })
5691 | +     }
5692 | + }
5693 | + ```

5708 | + /// Manages writing documents to the Tantivy index with batch support
5709 | + pub struct IndexWriterManager {
5710 | +     writer: Mutex<IndexWriter>,
5711 | +     #[allow(dead_code)]
5712 | +     schema: Schema,
5713 | +     path_field: Field,
5714 | +     content_field: Field,
5715 | +     title_field: Field,
5716 | +     modified_field: Field,
5717 | +     size_field: Field,
5718 | +     extension_field: Field,
5719 | + }
5720 | + 
5721 | + impl IndexWriterManager {
5722 | +     /// Calculate optimal heap size based on system resources
5723 | +     /// Returns heap size in bytes (min 32MB, max 256MB)
5724 | +     fn calculate_heap_size() -> usize {
5725 | +         // Get system memory info (using sysinfo crate would be better, but this is lightweight)
5726 | +         let available_memory = std::env::var("FLASH_SEARCH_MEMORY_MB")
5727 | +             .ok()
5728 | +             .and_then(|s| s.parse::<usize>().ok())
5729 | +             .map(|mb| mb * 1_000_000)
5730 | +             .unwrap_or_else(|| {
5731 | +                 // Default calculation: use 5% of system memory, capped at 256MB
5732 | +                 // This is a heuristic - in production, use sysinfo to get actual memory
5733 | +                 let mut sys = sysinfo::System::new_all();
5734 | +                 sys.refresh_memory();
5735 | +                 let system_memory = sys.total_memory() as usize;
5736 | + 
5737 | +                 (system_memory / 20).clamp(32_000_000, 256_000_000)
5738 | +             });
5739 | + 
5740 | +         available_memory.clamp(32_000_000, 256_000_000)
5741 | +     }
5742 | + 
5743 | +     pub fn new(index: &Index, _memory_limit_mb: u32) -> Result<Self> {
5744 | +         let schema = index.schema();
5745 | + 
5746 | +         // Configure with adaptive heap size based on system resources
5747 | +         let heap_size = Self::calculate_heap_size();
5748 | +         info!(
5749 | +             "Tantivy index writer heap size: {} MB",
5750 | +             heap_size / 1_000_000
5751 | +         );
5752 | + 
5753 | +         let writer = index
5754 | +             .writer(heap_size)
5755 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to create index writer: {}", e)))?;
5756 | + 
5757 | +         let path_field = schema
5758 | +             .get_field("file_path")
5759 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("file_path", "Field not found in schema"))?;
5760 | +         let content_field = schema
5761 | +             .get_field("content")
5762 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("content", "Field not found in schema"))?;
5763 | +         let title_field = schema
5764 | +             .get_field("title")
5765 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("title", "Field not found in schema"))?;
5766 | +         let modified_field = schema
5767 | +             .get_field("modified")
5768 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("modified", "Field not found in schema"))?;
5769 | +         let size_field = schema
5770 | +             .get_field("size")
5771 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("size", "Field not found in schema"))?;
5772 | +         let extension_field = schema
5773 | +             .get_field("extension")
5774 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("extension", "Field not found in schema"))?;
5775 | + 
5776 | +         Ok(Self {
5777 | +             writer: Mutex::new(writer),
5778 | +             schema,
5779 | +             path_field,
5780 | +             content_field,
5781 | +             title_field,
5782 | +             modified_field,
5783 | +             size_field,
5784 | +             extension_field,
5785 | +         })
5786 | +     }
5787 | + 
5788 | +     /// Add a single document to the index
5789 | +     /// Note: For better performance, use add_documents_batch for multiple docs
5790 | +     pub fn add_document(&self, doc: &ParsedDocument, modified: u64, size: u64) -> Result<()> {
5791 | +         let tantivy_doc = self.create_tantivy_document(doc, modified, size);
5792 | + 
5793 | +         let writer = self
5794 | +             .writer
5795 | +             .lock()
5796 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
5797 | + 
5798 | +         writer
5799 | +             .add_document(tantivy_doc)
5800 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to add document: {}", e)))?;
5801 | + 
5802 | +         Ok(())
5803 | +     }
5804 | + 
5805 | +     /// Add multiple documents in a single lock acquisition (much more efficient)
5806 | +     pub fn add_documents_batch(&self, docs: &[(ParsedDocument, u64, u64)]) -> Result<()> {
5807 | +         if docs.is_empty() {
5808 | +             return Ok(());
5809 | +         }
5810 | + 
5811 | +         let writer = self
5812 | +             .writer
5813 | +             .lock()
5814 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
5815 | + 
5816 | +         for (doc, modified, size) in docs {
5817 | +             let tantivy_doc = self.create_tantivy_document(doc, *modified, *size);
5818 | +             writer
5819 | +                 .add_document(tantivy_doc)
5820 | +                 .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to add document: {}", e)))?;
5821 | +         }
5822 | + 
5823 | +         Ok(())
5824 | +     }
5825 | + 
5826 | +     /// Create a Tantivy document from ParsedDocument
5827 | +     #[inline]
5828 | +     fn create_tantivy_document(
5829 | +         &self,
5830 | +         doc: &ParsedDocument,
5831 | +         modified: u64,
5832 | +         size: u64,
5833 | +     ) -> TantivyDocument {
5834 | +         let mut document = TantivyDocument::default();
5835 | + 
5836 | +         document.add_text(self.path_field, &doc.path);
5837 | +         document.add_text(self.content_field, &doc.content);
5838 | + 
5839 | +         if let Some(ref title) = doc.title {
5840 | +             document.add_text(self.title_field, title);
5841 | +         }
5842 | + 
5843 | +         let modified_date = tantivy::DateTime::from_timestamp_secs(modified as i64);
5844 | +         document.add_date(self.modified_field, modified_date);
5845 | +         document.add_u64(self.size_field, size);
5846 | + 
5847 | +         // Index file extension for fast filtering
5848 | +         if let Some(ext) = std::path::Path::new(&doc.path)
5849 | +             .extension()
5850 | +             .and_then(|e| e.to_str())
5851 | +         {
5852 | +             document.add_text(self.extension_field, ext.to_lowercase());
5853 | +         }
5854 | + 
5855 | +         document
5856 | +     }
5857 | + 
5858 | +     /// Remove a document from the index
5859 | +     pub fn remove_document(&self, path: &str) -> Result<()> {
5860 | +         let writer = self
5861 | +             .writer
5862 | +             .lock()
5863 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
5864 | + 
5865 | +         let term = tantivy::Term::from_field_text(self.path_field, path);
5866 | +         writer.delete_term(term);
5867 | + 
5868 | +         Ok(())
5869 | +     }
5870 | + 
5871 | +     /// Delete all documents from the index
5872 | +     pub fn delete_all_documents(&self) -> Result<()> {
5873 | +         let writer = self
5874 | +             .writer
5875 | +             .lock()
5876 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
5877 | + 
5878 | +         writer
5879 | +             .delete_all_documents()
5880 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to delete all documents: {}", e)))?;
5881 | + 
5882 | +         Ok(())
5883 | +     }
5884 | + 
5885 | +     /// Commit pending changes to disk
5886 | +     pub fn commit(&self) -> Result<()> {
5887 | +         let mut writer = self
5888 | +             .writer
5889 | +             .lock()
5890 | +             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
5891 | + 
5892 | +         writer
5893 | +             .commit()
5894 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to commit index: {}", e)))?;
5895 | + 
5896 | +         Ok(())
5897 | +     }
5898 | + }

5958 | + /// Manages file metadata database using redb
5959 | + /// Implements connection pooling pattern for redb (even though it's embedded)
5960 | + /// to ensure proper resource management and monitoring
5961 | + pub struct MetadataDb {
5962 | +     db: Arc<Database>,
5963 | +     metrics: Arc<ConnectionMetrics>,
5964 | + }
5965 | + 
5966 | + impl MetadataDb {
5967 | +     /// Open or create the metadata database
5968 | +     pub fn open(db_path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
5969 | +         let db = Arc::new(Database::create(db_path).map_err(|e| {
5970 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
5971 | +         })?);
5972 | + 
5973 | +         // Create table if it doesn't exist
5974 | +         let txn = db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
5975 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
5976 | +         })?;
5977 | +         {
5978 | +             let _table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
5979 | +                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
5980 | +             })?;
5981 | +         }
5982 | +         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
5983 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
5984 | +         })?;
5985 | + 
5986 | +         Ok(Self {
5987 | +             db,
5988 | +             metrics: Arc::new(ConnectionMetrics::default()),
5989 | +         })
5990 | +     }
5991 | + 
5992 | +     /// Clone with shared state (for multi-threaded access)
5993 | +     pub fn clone_for_thread(&self) -> Self {
5994 | +         Self {
5995 | +             db: Arc::clone(&self.db),
5996 | +             metrics: Arc::clone(&self.metrics),
5997 | +         }
5998 | +     }
5999 | + 
6000 | +     /// Get current metrics snapshot
6001 | +     pub fn get_metrics(&self) -> ConnectionMetricsSnapshot {
6002 | +         ConnectionMetricsSnapshot {
6003 | +             read_operations: self.metrics.read_operations.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
6004 | +             write_operations: self.metrics.write_operations.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
6005 | +             bytes_read: self.metrics.bytes_read.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
6006 | +             bytes_written: self.metrics.bytes_written.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
6007 | +         }
6008 | +     }
6009 | + 
6010 | +     /// Check if file needs reindexing based on modification time and hash
6011 | +     pub fn needs_reindex(&self, path: &Path, modified: u64, size: u64) -> Result<bool> {
6012 | +         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
6013 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6014 | +         })?;
6015 | + 
6016 | +         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
6017 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6018 | +         })?;
6019 | + 
6020 | +         let path_str = path.to_str().unwrap_or("");
6021 | + 
6022 | +         let result = match table
6023 | +             .get(path_str)
6024 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string()))?
6025 | +         {
6026 | +             Some(metadata) => {
6027 | +                 let bytes = metadata.value();
6028 | +                 self.metrics.read_operations.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
6029 | +                 self.metrics
6030 | +                     .bytes_read
6031 | +                     .fetch_add(bytes.len() as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
6032 | +                 // Zero-copy read and validate
6033 | +                 if let Ok(meta) =
6034 | +                     rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
6035 | +                 {
6036 | +                     meta.modified != modified || meta.size != size
6037 | +                 } else {
6038 | +                     true // Reindex if validation fails
6039 | +                 }
6040 | +             }
6041 | +             None => true, // File not indexed yet
6042 | +         };
6043 | + 
6044 | +         Ok(result)
6045 | +     }

6058 | +

6065 | +                 path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
6066 | +                 modified,
6067 | +                 size,
6068 | +                 content_hash,
6069 | +                 indexed_at: SystemTime::now()
6070 | +                     .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
6071 | +                     .unwrap_or_default()
6072 | +                     .as_secs(),
6073 | +             };
6074 | + 
6075 | +             let bytes = rkyv::to_bytes::<rkyv::rancor::Error>(&metadata).map_err(|e| {
6076 | +                 FlashError::database(
6077 | +                     "database_operation",
6078 | +                     "files_table",
6079 | +                     format!("Serialization error: {}", e),
6080 | +                 )
6081 | +             })?;
6082 | + 
6083 | +             table
6084 | +                 .insert(path.to_str().unwrap_or(""), bytes.as_slice())
6085 | +                 .map_err(|e| {
6086 | +                     FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6087 | +                 })?;
6088 | +         }
6089 | + 
6090 | +         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
6091 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6092 | +         })?;
6093 | + 
6094 | +         Ok(())
6095 | +     }
6096 | + 
6097 | +     /// Remove a file from the metadata database
6098 | +     pub fn remove_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<bool> {
6099 | +         let txn = self.db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
6100 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6101 | +         })?;
6102 | + 
6103 | +         let existed = {
6104 | +             let mut table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
6105 | +                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6106 | +             })?;
6107 | + 
6108 | +             let path_str = path.to_str().unwrap_or("");
6109 | +             let removed = table.remove(path_str).map_err(|e| {
6110 | +                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6111 | +             })?;
6112 | +             removed.is_some()
6113 | +         };
6114 | + 
6115 | +         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
6116 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6117 | +         })?;
6118 | + 
6119 | +         Ok(existed)
6120 | +     }
6121 | + 
6122 | +     /// Clear all metadata (nuke the table)
6123 | +     pub fn clear(&self) -> Result<()> {
6124 | +         let txn = self.db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
6125 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6126 | +         })?;
6127 | + 
6128 | +         {
6129 | +             // Deleting the table is the fastest way to clear it
6130 | +             txn.delete_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
6131 | +                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6132 | +             })?;
6133 | +             // We must recreate it in the same transaction or next open?
6134 | +             // Actually, open_table in next usage will recreate it if we create it here?
6135 | +             // Safer to just open it again to ensure it exists empty.
6136 | +             let _ = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
6137 | +                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6138 | +             })?;
6139 | +         }
6140 | + 
6141 | +         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
6142 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6143 | +         })?;
6144 | + 
6145 | +         Ok(())
6146 | +     }
6147 | + 
6148 | +     /// Get metadata for a specific file
6149 | +     pub fn get_metadata(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<Option<FileMetadata>> {
6150 | +         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
6151 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6152 | +         })?;
6153 | + 
6154 | +         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
6155 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6156 | +         })?;
6157 | + 
6158 | +         let result = match table
6159 | +             .get(path.to_str().unwrap_or(""))
6160 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string()))?
6161 | +         {
6162 | +             Some(metadata) => {
6163 | +                 let bytes = metadata.value();
6164 | +                 if let Ok(meta) =
6165 | +                     rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
6166 | +                 {
6167 | +                     rkyv::deserialize::<FileMetadata, rkyv::rancor::Error>(meta).ok()
6168 | +                 } else {
6169 | +                     None
6170 | +                 }
6171 | +             }
6172 | +             None => None,
6173 | +         };
6174 | + 
6175 | +         Ok(result)
6176 | +     }
6177 | + 
6178 | +     /// Batch update metadata for multiple files (much more efficient)
6179 | +     /// Updates all files in a single transaction to minimize I/O overhead
6180 | +     pub fn batch_update_metadata(
6181 | +         &self,
6182 | +         entries: &[(String, u64, u64, [u8; 32])], // (path, modified, size, hash)
6183 | +     ) -> Result<usize> {
6184 | +         if entries.is_empty() {
6185 | +             return Ok(0);
6186 | +         }
6187 | + 
6188 | +         let txn = self.db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
6189 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6190 | +         })?;
6191 | + 
6192 | +         let indexed_at = SystemTime::now()
6193 | +             .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
6194 | +             .unwrap_or_default()
6195 | +             .as_secs();
6196 | + 
6197 | +         let mut total_bytes_written = 0u64;
6198 | + 
6199 | +         {
6200 | +             let mut table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
6201 | +                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6202 | +             })?;
6203 | + 
6204 | +             for (path, modified, size, content_hash) in entries {
6205 | +                 let metadata = FileMetadata {
6206 | +                     path: path.clone(),
6207 | +                     modified: *modified,
6208 | +                     size: *size,
6209 | +                     content_hash: *content_hash,
6210 | +                     indexed_at,
6211 | +                 };
6212 | + 
6213 | +                 let bytes = rkyv::to_bytes::<rkyv::rancor::Error>(&metadata).map_err(|e| {
6214 | +                     FlashError::database(
6215 | +                         "database_operation",
6216 | +                         "files_table",
6217 | +                         format!("Serialization error: {}", e),
6218 | +                     )
6219 | +                 })?;
6220 | + 
6221 | +                 total_bytes_written += bytes.len() as u64;
6222 | +                 total_bytes_written += path.len() as u64;
6223 | + 
6224 | +                 table.insert(path.as_str(), bytes.as_slice()).map_err(|e| {
6225 | +                     FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6226 | +                 })?;
6227 | +             }
6228 | +         }
6229 | + 
6230 | +         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
6231 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6232 | +         })?;
6233 | + 
6234 | +         // Update metrics
6235 | +         self.metrics
6236 | +             .write_operations
6237 | +             .fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
6238 | +         self.metrics
6239 | +             .bytes_written
6240 | +             .fetch_add(total_bytes_written, Ordering::Relaxed);
6241 | + 
6242 | +         Ok(entries.len())
6243 | +     }
6244 | + 
6245 | +     /// Batch check which files need reindexing
6246 | +     /// Returns a vector of booleans indicating if each file needs reindexing
6247 | +     pub fn batch_needs_reindex(
6248 | +         &self,
6249 | +         entries: &[(String, u64, u64)], // (path, modified, size)
6250 | +     ) -> Result<Vec<bool>> {
6251 | +         if entries.is_empty() {
6252 | +             return Ok(vec![]);
6253 | +         }
6254 | + 
6255 | +         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
6256 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6257 | +         })?;
6258 | + 
6259 | +         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
6260 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6261 | +         })?;
6262 | + 
6263 | +         let results: Vec<bool> = entries
6264 | +             .iter()
6265 | +             .map(|(path, modified, size)| {
6266 | +                 match table.get(path.as_str()) {
6267 | +                     Ok(Some(metadata)) => {
6268 | +                         let bytes = metadata.value();
6269 | +                         if let Ok(meta) =
6270 | +                             rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
6271 | +                         {
6272 | +                             meta.modified != *modified || meta.size != *size
6273 | +                         } else {
6274 | +                             true
6275 | +                         }
6276 | +                     }
6277 | +                     Ok(None) => true, // File not indexed yet
6278 | +                     Err(_) => true,   // Error reading, safer to reindex
6279 | +                 }
6280 | +             })
6281 | +             .collect();
6282 | + 
6283 | +         Ok(results)
6284 | +     }
6285 | + 
6286 | +     /// Get recently modified files sorted by modification time
6287 | +     /// Note: This loads all files into memory. For large datasets, consider using a separate index.
6288 | +     pub fn get_recent_files(&self, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<RecentFileEntry>> {
6289 | +         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
6290 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6291 | +         })?;
6292 | + 
6293 | +         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
6294 | +             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
6295 | +         })?;
6296 | + 
6297 | +         let mut files: Vec<(String, u64, u64)> = table
6298 | +             .iter()
6299 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string()))?
6300 | +             .filter_map(|entry| {
6301 | +                 entry.ok().map(|(k, v)| {
6302 | +                     let bytes = v.value();
6303 | +                     if let Ok(meta) =
6304 | +                         rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
6305 | +                     {
6306 | +                         (
6307 | +                             k.value().to_string(),
6308 | +                             meta.modified.to_native(),
6309 | +                             meta.size.to_native(),
6310 | +                         )
6311 | +                     } else {
6312 | +                         (k.value().to_string(), 0, 0)
6313 | +                     }
6314 | +                 })
6315 | +             })
6316 | +             .collect();
6317 | + 
6318 | +         // Use select_nth_unstable for O(n) partial sort instead of O(n log n) full sort
6319 | +         if files.len() > limit {
6320 | +             // use std::cmp::Ordering;
6321 | +             files.select_nth_unstable_by(limit, |a, b| b.1.cmp(&a.1)); // Reverse order for max-heap behavior
6322 | +             files.truncate(limit);
6323 | +         } else {
6324 | +             files.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.cmp(&a.1));
6325 | +         }
6326 | + 
6327 | +         // Convert to the expected format (without titles for now, can be enhanced)
6328 | +         Ok(files
6329 | +             .into_iter()
6330 | +             .map(|(path, modified, size)| (path, None, modified, size))
6331 | +             .collect())
6332 | +     }
6333 | + }
6334 | + ```

6417 | + use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};

6424 | + pub fn parse_docx(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
6425 | +     let doc = Document::open(path)
6426 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open document: {}", e)))?;
6427 | + 
6428 | +     let text = doc
6429 | +         .text()
6430 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to extract text: {}", e)))?;
6431 | + 
6432 | +     let content = if text.len() > MAX_TEXT_LENGTH {
6433 | +         text[..MAX_TEXT_LENGTH].to_string()
6434 | +     } else {
6435 | +         text
6436 | +     };
6437 | + 
6438 | +     let title = path.file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string());
6439 | + 
6440 | +     Ok(ParsedDocument {
6441 | +         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
6442 | +         content,
6443 | +         title,
6444 | +     })
6445 | + }

6457 | +

6460 | + 
6461 | + ```rust
6462 | + use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
6463 | + use crate::parsers::memory_map;
6464 | + use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
6465 | + use quick_xml::events::Event;
6466 | + use quick_xml::Reader;
6467 | + use std::io::Read;
6468 | + use std::path::Path;
6469 | + use tracing::warn;
6470 | + use zip::ZipArchive;
6471 | + 
6472 | + const MAX_HTML_SIZE: usize = 50 * 1024 * 1024;
6473 | + const MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_SIZE: usize = 200 * 1024 * 1024;
6474 | + 
6475 | + pub fn parse_epub(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
6476 | +     let bytes = memory_map::read_file(path)?;
6477 | + 
6478 | +     let cursor = std::io::Cursor::new(bytes);
6479 | +     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(cursor)
6480 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to read EPUB archive: {}", e)))?;
6481 | + 
6482 | +     let mut combined_text = String::with_capacity(1024 * 1024);
6483 | +     let mut total_extracted_size: usize = 0;
6484 | + 
6485 | +     let file_names: Vec<String> = archive.file_names().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect();
6486 | + 
6487 | +     for name in file_names {
6488 | +         if name.ends_with(".xhtml") || name.ends_with(".html") {
6489 | +             match archive.by_name(&name) {
6490 | +                 Ok(mut inner_file) => {
6491 | +                     if inner_file.size() > MAX_HTML_SIZE as u64 {
6492 | +                         warn!(
6493 | +                             "Skipping large HTML file {} in EPUB ({} bytes)",
6494 | +                             name,
6495 | +                             inner_file.size()
6496 | +                         );
6497 | +                         continue;
6498 | +                     }
6499 | + 
6500 | +                     let mut content = String::with_capacity(inner_file.size() as usize);
6501 | + 
6502 | +                     match inner_file.read_to_string(&mut content) {
6503 | +                         Ok(_) => {
6504 | +                             if total_extracted_size + content.len() > MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_SIZE {
6505 | +                                 warn!(
6506 | +                                     "EPUB {} has exceeded maximum text size limit",
6507 | +                                     path.display()
6508 | +                                 );
6509 | +                                 break;
6510 | +                             }
6511 | + 
6512 | +                             extract_text_from_html(&content, &mut combined_text);
6513 | +                             total_extracted_size += content.len();
6514 | +                         }
6515 | +                         Err(e) => {
6516 | +                             warn!("Failed to read {} from EPUB: {}", name, e);
6517 | +                         }
6518 | +                     }
6519 | +                 }
6520 | +                 Err(e) => {
6521 | +                     warn!("Failed to access {} in EPUB: {}", name, e);
6522 | +                 }
6523 | +             }
6524 | +         }
6525 | +     }
6526 | + 
6527 | +     drop(archive);
6528 | + 
6529 | +     Ok(ParsedDocument {
6530 | +         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
6531 | +         content: combined_text.trim().to_string(),
6532 | +         title: extract_epub_title(path).ok(),
6533 | +     })
6534 | + }
6535 | + 
6536 | + fn extract_text_from_html(html: &str, output: &mut String) {
6537 | +     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(html);
6538 | +     reader.config_mut().trim_text(true);
6539 | + 
6540 | +     let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(1024);
6541 | + 
6542 | +     loop {
6543 | +         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
6544 | +             Ok(Event::Text(e)) => {
6545 | +                 if let Ok(decoded) = e.decode() {
6546 | +                     let text = match quick_xml::escape::unescape(&decoded) {
6547 | +                         Ok(unescaped) => unescaped.into_owned(),
6548 | +                         Err(_) => decoded.into_owned(),
6549 | +                     };
6550 | +                     output.push_str(&text);
6551 | +                     output.push(' ');
6552 | +                 }
6553 | +             }
6554 | +             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
6555 | +             Err(e) => {
6556 | +                 warn!("HTML parsing error: {}", e);
6557 | +                 break;
6558 | +             }
6559 | +             _ => {}
6560 | +         }
6561 | +         buf.clear();
6562 | +     }
6563 | + }
6564 | + 
6565 | + fn extract_epub_title(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
6566 | +     let bytes = memory_map::read_file(path)?;
6567 | +     let cursor = std::io::Cursor::new(bytes);
6568 | +     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(cursor)
6569 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to read EPUB: {}", e)))?;
6570 | + 
6571 | +     let op_path = if let Ok(mut container_xml) = archive.by_name("META-INF/container.xml") {
6572 | +         let mut content = String::new();
6573 | +         if container_xml.read_to_string(&mut content).is_ok() {
6574 | +             extract_opf_path(&content)
6575 | +         } else {
6576 | +             None
6577 | +         }
6578 | +     } else {
6579 | +         None
6580 | +     };
6581 | + 
6582 | +     if let Some(opf_path) = op_path {
6583 | +         if let Ok(mut opf_file) = archive.by_name(&opf_path) {
6584 | +             let mut opf_content = String::new();
6585 | +             if opf_file.read_to_string(&mut opf_content).is_ok() {
6586 | +                 if let Some(title) = extract_title_from_opf(&opf_content) {
6587 | +                     return Ok(title);
6588 | +                 }
6589 | +             }
6590 | +         }
6591 | +     }
6592 | + 
6593 | +     path.file_stem()
6594 | +         .map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string())
6595 | +         .ok_or_else(|| FlashError::parse(path, "Could not extract title"))
6596 | + }
6597 | + 
6598 | + fn extract_opf_path(container_xml: &str) -> Option<String> {
6599 | +     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(container_xml);
6600 | +     let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(512);
6601 | + 
6602 | +     loop {
6603 | +         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
6604 | +             Ok(Event::Empty(e)) | Ok(Event::Start(e)) => {
6605 | +                 if e.name().as_ref() == b"rootfile" {
6606 | +                     for attr in e.attributes().flatten() {
6607 | +                         if attr.key.as_ref() == b"full-path" {
6608 | +                             if let Ok(path) = std::str::from_utf8(&attr.value) {
6609 | +                                 return Some(path.to_string());
6610 | +                             }
6611 | +                         }
6612 | +                     }
6613 | +                 }
6614 | +             }
6615 | +             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
6616 | +             _ => {}
6617 | +         }
6618 | +         buf.clear();
6619 | +     }
6620 | + 
6621 | +     Some("OEBPS/content.opf".to_string())
6622 | + }
6623 | + 
6624 | + fn extract_title_from_opf(opf_content: &str) -> Option<String> {
6625 | +     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(opf_content);
6626 | +     let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(1024);
6627 | +     let mut in_title = false;
6628 | + 
6629 | +     loop {
6630 | +         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
6631 | +             Ok(Event::Start(e)) => {
6632 | +                 if e.name().as_ref() == b"dc:title" {
6633 | +                     in_title = true;
6634 | +                 }
6635 | +             }
6636 | +             Ok(Event::Text(e)) => {
6637 | +                 if in_title {
6638 | +                     if let Ok(decoded) = e.decode() {
6639 | +                         let text = match quick_xml::escape::unescape(&decoded) {
6640 | +                             Ok(unescaped) => unescaped.into_owned(),
6641 | +                             Err(_) => decoded.into_owned(),
6642 | +                         };
6643 | +                         let title = text;
6644 | +                         if !title.trim().is_empty() {
6645 | +                             return Some(title);
6646 | +                         }
6647 | +                     }
6648 | +                 }
6649 | +             }
6650 | +             Ok(Event::End(e)) => {
6651 | +                 if e.name().as_ref() == b"dc:title" {
6652 | +                     in_title = false;
6653 | +                 }
6654 | +             }
6655 | +             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
6656 | +             _ => {}
6657 | +         }
6658 | +         buf.clear();
6659 | +     }
6660 | + 
6661 | +     None
6662 | + }
6663 | + ```

6678 | + 
6679 | + pub fn parse_excel(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {

6700 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open XLSX: {}", e)))?;
6701 | +

6703 | + }

6714 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open XLS: {}", e)))?;
6715 | + 
6716 | +     extract_excel_content(path, &mut workbook)
6717 | + }
6718 | + 
6719 | + fn extract_excel_content<P, RS>(path: &Path, workbook: &mut P) -> Result<ParsedDocument>
6720 | + where
6721 | +     RS: std::io::Read + std::io::Seek,
6722 | +     P: Reader<RS>,
6723 | + {
6724 | +     let mut combined_text = String::with_capacity(1024 * 1024);
6725 | +     let mut total_cells = 0usize;
6726 | +     let sheet_names = workbook.sheet_names().to_vec();
6727 | + 
6728 | +     for sheet_name in &sheet_names {
6729 | +         combined_text.push_str("Sheet: ");
6730 | +         combined_text.push_str(sheet_name);
6731 | +         combined_text.push('\n');
6732 | + 
6733 | +         if let Ok(range) = workbook.worksheet_range(sheet_name) {
6734 | +             for row in range.rows() {
6735 | +                 for cell in row {
6736 | +                     total_cells += 1;
6737 | + 
6738 | +                     if total_cells > MAX_CELLS_PER_SHEET {
6739 | +                         break;
6740 | +                     }
6741 | + 
6742 | +                     let cell_text = format_cell_value(cell);
6743 | +                     if !cell_text.is_empty() {
6744 | +                         combined_text.push_str(&cell_text);
6745 | +                         combined_text.push(' ');
6746 | +                     }
6747 | +                 }
6748 | +                 combined_text.push('\n');
6749 | + 
6750 | +                 if combined_text.len() > MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_LENGTH {
6751 | +                     break;
6752 | +                 }
6753 | +             }
6754 | +         }
6755 | +         combined_text.push('\n');
6756 | +     }
6757 | + 
6758 | +     let title = path.file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string());
6759 | + 
6760 | +     Ok(ParsedDocument {
6761 | +         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
6762 | +         content: combined_text.trim().to_string(),
6763 | +         title,
6764 | +     })
6765 | + }
6766 | + 
6767 | + fn format_cell_value(cell: &calamine::Data) -> String {
6768 | +     match cell {
6769 | +         calamine::Data::Empty => String::new(),
6770 | +         calamine::Data::String(s) => s.clone(),
6771 | +         calamine::Data::Float(f) => f.to_string(),
6772 | +         calamine::Data::Int(i) => i.to_string(),
6773 | +         calamine::Data::Bool(b) => b.to_string(),
6774 | +         calamine::Data::DateTime(dt) => dt.to_string(),
6775 | +         calamine::Data::Error(e) => format!("#ERROR: {:?}", e),
6776 | +         _ => String::new(),
6777 | +     }
6778 | + }
6779 | + 
6780 | + #[cfg(test)]
6781 | + mod tests {
6782 | +     use super::*;
6783 | + 
6784 | +     #[test]
6785 | +     fn test_format_cell_value() {
6786 | +         assert_eq!(format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Empty), "");
6787 | +         assert_eq!(
6788 | +             format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::String("test".to_string())),
6789 | +             "test"
6790 | +         );
6791 | +         assert_eq!(
6792 | +             format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Float(std::f64::consts::PI)),
6793 | +             "3.141592653589793"
6794 | +         );
6795 | +         assert_eq!(format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Int(42)), "42");
6796 | +         assert_eq!(format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Bool(true)), "true");
6797 | +     }
6798 | +

6806 | +         fs::write(&path, b"not a valid excel file").unwrap();
6807 | + 
6808 | +         let result = parse_excel(&path);
6809 | +         assert!(result.is_err());
6810 | +     }

6861 | +         let line_lower = line.to_lowercase();
6862 | +

6864 | +             title = line[8..].trim().to_string();
6865 | +         } else if line_lower.starts_with("from:")
6866 | +             || line_lower.starts_with("to:")
6867 | +             || line_lower.starts_with("date:")
6868 | +         {
6869 | +             text.push_str(line);
6870 | +             text.push(' ');
6871 | +         } else if line_lower.is_empty() {

6873 | +         } else if in_body {

6876 | +         }

6897 | +         } else if in_string && current.len() > 3 {

6992 | + pub fn parse_zip_content(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
6993 | +     use zip::ZipArchive;
6994 | + 
6995 | +     // Use File::open instead of reading entire file into memory (P5)
6996 | +     let file = std::fs::File::open(path)
6997 | +         .map_err(|e: std::io::Error| FlashError::parse("open_zip", e.to_string()))?;
6998 | +     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(std::io::BufReader::new(file))
6999 | +         .map_err(|e: zip::result::ZipError| FlashError::parse("open_archive", e.to_string()))?;
7000 | + 
7001 | +     let mut all_text = String::new();
7002 | +     let max_size = 10 * 1024 * 1024; // 10MB limit
7003 | + 
7004 | +     for i in 0..archive.len() {
7005 | +         if let Ok(file) = archive.by_index(i) {
7006 | +             if !file.is_dir() {
7007 | +                 let name = file.name().to_lowercase();
7008 | + 
7009 | +                 if let Some(ext) = name.rsplit('.').next() {
7010 | +                     if TEXT_EXTENSIONS.contains(ext) {
7011 | +                         // Limit read size per file and total
7012 | +                         let mut content = String::new();
7013 | +                         // Put a cap on individual file read to avoid safe-bomb/DoS
7014 | +                         let mut take = file.take(1024 * 1024); // 1MB per file
7015 | +                         if take.read_to_string(&mut content).is_ok() {
7016 | +                             all_text.push_str(&content);
7017 | +                             all_text.push_str("\n\n");
7018 | +                         }
7019 | +                     }
7020 | +                 }
7021 | +             }
7022 | +         }
7023 | +         if all_text.len() >= max_size {
7024 | +             break;
7025 | +         }
7026 | +     }
7027 | + 
7028 | +     if all_text.is_empty() {
7029 | +         // Just return metadata if no text extracted? Or error?
7030 | +         // Original returned error, but maybe return empty doc is better?
7031 | +         // Let's stick to original behavior or just return empty content.
7032 | +         // Returning error might be filtered out as failure.
7033 | +         // Let's return empty content if valid zip but no text.
7034 | +         // But if it was empty because no text files found, that's fine.
7035 | +         // The original error "unsupported_format" is a bit misleading if it IS a zip.
7036 | +         // Let's return valid doc with empty content.
7037 | +     }
7038 | + 
7039 | +     Ok(ParsedDocument {
7040 | +         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
7041 | +         content: all_text,
7042 | +         title: None,
7043 | +     })
7044 | + }
7045 | +

7091 | + ### File: `src\parsers\memory_map.rs`
7092 | + 
7093 | + - Size: 2073 bytes
7094 | + - Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134168914525145568 }
7095 | + 
7096 | + ```rust
7097 | + use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
7098 | + use memmap2::Mmap;
7099 | + use std::fs::File;
7100 | + use std::io::Read;
7101 | + use std::path::Path;
7102 | + 
7103 | + const MMAP_THRESHOLD: u64 = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB
7104 | + const MAX_FILE_SIZE: u64 = 100 * 1024 * 1024; // 100MB
7105 | + 
7106 | + pub fn read_file(path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
7107 | +     let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
7108 | + 
7109 | +     let file_size = metadata.len();
7110 | + 
7111 | +     if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE {
7112 | +         return Err(FlashError::parse(
7113 | +             path,
7114 | +             format!(
7115 | +                 "File too large: {} bytes (max: {})",
7116 | +                 file_size, MAX_FILE_SIZE
7117 | +             ),
7118 | +         ));
7119 | +     }
7120 | + 
7121 | +     if file_size > MMAP_THRESHOLD {
7122 | +         read_with_mmap(path)
7123 | +     } else {
7124 | +         read_with_buffer(path)
7125 | +     }
7126 | + }
7127 | + 
7128 | + pub fn read_file_as_string(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
7129 | +     let bytes = read_file(path)?;
7130 | +     String::from_utf8(bytes).map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Invalid UTF-8: {}", e)))
7131 | + }
7132 | + 
7133 | + fn read_with_buffer(path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
7134 | +     let mut file = File::open(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
7135 | +     let mut bytes = Vec::new();
7136 | +     file.read_to_end(&mut bytes).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
7137 | +     Ok(bytes)
7138 | + }
7139 | + 
7140 | + fn read_with_mmap(path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
7141 | +     let file = File::open(path)
7142 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open file: {}", e)))?;
7143 | + 
7144 | +     // SAFETY: Memory mapping a file is unsafe because another process could modify it
7145 | +     // while we are reading, potentially violating Rust's memory safety guarantees.
7146 | +     // For a local desktop search engine, this risk is acceptable and typically results
7147 | +     // in a process crash rather than an exploitable vulnerability.
7148 | +     let mmap = unsafe { Mmap::map(&file).map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))? };
7149 | + 
7150 | +     Ok(mmap.to_vec())
7151 | + }
7152 | + 
7153 | + pub fn is_mmap_applicable(path: &Path) -> bool {
7154 | +     std::fs::metadata(path)
7155 | +         .map(|m| m.len() > MMAP_THRESHOLD)
7156 | +         .unwrap_or(false)
7157 | + }
7158 | + 
7159 | + pub fn get_file_size(path: &Path) -> Result<u64> {
7160 | +     std::fs::metadata(path)
7161 | +         .map(|m| m.len())
7162 | +         .map_err(FlashError::Io)
7163 | + }
7164 | + ```

7310 | + static OTHER_EXTENSIONS: phf::Map<&'static str, ParserType> = phf_map! {
7311 | +     "epub" => ParserType::Epub,
7312 | +     "pdf" => ParserType::Pdf,
7313 | +     "xlsx" => ParserType::Excel,
7314 | +     "xls" => ParserType::Excel,
7315 | +     "xlsb" => ParserType::Excel,
7316 | +     "rtf" => ParserType::Rtf,
7317 | +     "eml" => ParserType::Eml,
7318 | +     "msg" => ParserType::Msg,
7319 | +     "chm" => ParserType::Chm,
7320 | +     "azw" => ParserType::Azw,
7321 | +     "azw3" => ParserType::Azw,
7322 | +     "mobi" => ParserType::Azw,
7323 | +     "zip" => ParserType::Zip,
7324 | +     "7z" => ParserType::SevenZ,
7325 | +     "rar" => ParserType::Rar,
7326 | +     "legacy_office" => ParserType::LegacyOffice,
7327 | + };
7328 | + 
7329 | + #[derive(Clone, Copy)]
7330 | + enum ParserType {
7331 | +     Docx,
7332 | +     Pptx,
7333 | +     Odf,
7334 | +     Epub,
7335 | +     Pdf,
7336 | +     Excel,
7337 | +     Rtf,
7338 | +     Eml,
7339 | +     Msg,
7340 | +     Chm,
7341 | +     Azw,
7342 | +     Zip,
7343 | +     SevenZ,
7344 | +     Rar,
7345 | +     LegacyOffice,
7346 | +     Text,
7347 | + }
7348 | + 
7349 | + impl ParserType {
7350 | +     fn from_extension(ext: &str) -> Option<Self> {
7351 | +         let ext_lower = ext.to_lowercase();
7352 | +         if DOCX_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
7353 | +             Some(ParserType::Docx)
7354 | +         } else if PPTX_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
7355 | +             Some(ParserType::Pptx)
7356 | +         } else if ODF_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
7357 | +             Some(ParserType::Odf)
7358 | +         } else if TEXT_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
7359 | +             Some(ParserType::Text)
7360 | +         } else if LEGACY_OFFICE_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
7361 | +             Some(ParserType::LegacyOffice)
7362 | +         } else {
7363 | +             OTHER_EXTENSIONS.get(&ext_lower).copied()
7364 | +         }
7365 | +     }
7366 | + }
7367 | + 
7368 | + /// Parse file without allocating - uses byte comparison
7369 | + #[inline]
7370 | + #[allow(dead_code)]
7371 | + fn extension_matches(ext: &OsStr, target: &str) -> bool {
7372 | +     if let Some(ext_bytes) = ext.to_str().map(|s| s.as_bytes()) {
7373 | +         if ext_bytes.len() != target.len() {
7374 | +             return false;
7375 | +         }
7376 | +         ext_bytes
7377 | +             .iter()
7378 | +             .zip(target.bytes())
7379 | +             .all(|(a, b)| a.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&b))
7380 | +     } else {
7381 | +         false
7382 | +     }
7383 | + }
7384 | + 
7385 | + /// Detect file type and route to appropriate parser
7386 | + /// Uses phf for O(1) static lookup
7387 | + pub fn parse_file(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
7388 | +     let extension = path.extension().unwrap_or_default();
7389 | + 
7390 | +     if let Some(ext_str) = extension.to_str() {
7391 | +         let ext_lower = ext_str.to_lowercase();
7392 | +         match ParserType::from_extension(&ext_lower) {
7393 | +             Some(ParserType::Docx) => return docx::parse_docx(path),
7394 | +             Some(ParserType::Pptx) => return pptx::parse_pptx(path),
7395 | +             Some(ParserType::Odf) => return odf::parse_odf(path),
7396 | +             Some(ParserType::Epub) => return epub::parse_epub(path),
7397 | +             Some(ParserType::Pdf) => return pdf::parse_pdf(path),
7398 | +             Some(ParserType::Excel) => return excel::parse_excel(path),
7399 | +             Some(ParserType::Rtf) => return extended::parse_rtf(path),
7400 | +             Some(ParserType::Eml) => return extended::parse_eml(path),
7401 | +             Some(ParserType::Msg) => return extended::parse_msg(path),
7402 | +             Some(ParserType::Chm) => return extended::parse_chm(path),
7403 | +             Some(ParserType::Azw) => return extended::parse_azw(path),
7404 | +             Some(ParserType::Zip) => return extended::parse_zip_content(path),
7405 | +             Some(ParserType::SevenZ) => return extended::parse_7z_content(path),
7406 | +             Some(ParserType::Rar) => return extended::parse_rar_content(path),
7407 | +             Some(ParserType::Text) => return text::parse_text(path),
7408 | +             Some(ParserType::LegacyOffice) => return extended::parse_legacy_office(path),
7409 | +             None => {}
7410 | +         }
7411 | +     }
7412 | + 
7413 | +     let ext_str = extension.to_string_lossy().to_string();
7414 | +     Err(FlashError::unsupported_format(ext_str.clone(), ext_str))
7415 | + }
7416 | +

7425 | +         assert!(extension_matches(OsStr::new("DOCX"), "docx"));
7426 | +         assert!(extension_matches(OsStr::new("Docx"), "docx"));
7427 | +         assert!(!extension_matches(OsStr::new("pdf"), "docx"));

7437 | +             ParserType::from_extension("TXT"),

7446 | +             Some(ParserType::Text)
7447 | +         ));
7448 | +         assert!(matches!(

7450 | +             Some(ParserType::Docx)

7454 | + 
7455 | +     #[test]
7456 | +     fn test_parse_file_txt() {
7457 | +         let path = PathBuf::from("tests/fixtures/sample.txt");
7458 | +         if path.exists() {
7459 | +             let result = super::parse_file(&path);
7460 | +             assert!(result.is_ok());
7461 | +         }
7462 | +     }

7472 | + use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
7473 | + use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
7474 | + use litchi::odf::Document;
7475 | + use std::path::Path;
7476 | + 
7477 | + const MAX_TEXT_LENGTH: usize = 100 * 1024 * 1024;
7478 | + 
7479 | + pub fn parse_odf(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
7480 | +     let doc = Document::open(path).map_err(|e| {
7481 | +         FlashError::parse(
7482 | +             path,
7483 | +             format!("Failed to open ODF document {}: {}", path.display(), e),
7484 | +         )
7485 | +     })?;
7486 | + 
7487 | +     let text = doc.text().map_err(|e| {
7488 | +         FlashError::parse(
7489 | +             path,
7490 | +             format!("Failed to extract text from {}: {}", path.display(), e),
7491 | +         )
7492 | +     })?;
7493 | + 
7494 | +     let content = if text.len() > MAX_TEXT_LENGTH {
7495 | +         text[..MAX_TEXT_LENGTH].to_string()
7496 | +     } else {
7497 | +         text
7498 | +     };
7499 | + 
7500 | +     let title = path.file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string());
7501 | + 
7502 | +     Ok(ParsedDocument {
7503 | +         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
7504 | +         content,
7505 | +         title,
7506 | +     })
7507 | + }
7508 | +

7527 | + ### File: `src\parsers\pdf.rs`
7528 | + 
7529 | + - Size: 3634 bytes
7530 | + - Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134168558668456113 }
7531 | + 
7532 | + ```rust
7533 | + use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
7534 | + use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
7535 | + use pdfium_render::prelude::*;
7536 | + use std::path::Path;
7537 | + use tracing::warn;
7538 | + 
7539 | + /// Maximum PDF content size to prevent memory issues
7540 | + const MAX_PDF_SIZE: u64 = 500 * 1024 * 1024; // 500MB limit
7541 | + 
7542 | + /// Parse PDF file using pdf-extract crate
7543 | + /// Implements proper resource management and error handling
7544 | + pub fn parse_pdf(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
7545 | +     // Check file size first to prevent OOM
7546 | +     let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path)
7547 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to read PDF metadata: {}", e)))?;
7548 | + 
7549 | +     if metadata.len() > MAX_PDF_SIZE {
7550 | +         return Err(FlashError::parse(
7551 | +             path,
7552 | +             format!(
7553 | +                 "PDF file too large: {} bytes (max: {})",
7554 | +                 metadata.len(),
7555 | +                 MAX_PDF_SIZE
7556 | +             ),
7557 | +         ));
7558 | +     }
7559 | + 
7560 | +     let content = match std::panic::catch_unwind(|| -> std::result::Result<String, String> {
7561 | +         let bind = Pdfium::bind_to_library(Pdfium::pdfium_platform_library_name_at_path("./"))
7562 | +             .or_else(|_| Pdfium::bind_to_system_library());
7563 | + 
7564 | +         let pdfium = match bind {
7565 | +             Ok(b) => Pdfium::new(b),
7566 | +             Err(e) => return Err(format!("Failed to bind: {}", e)),
7567 | +         };
7568 | + 
7569 | +         let document = match pdfium.load_pdf_from_file(path, None) {
7570 | +             Ok(doc) => doc,
7571 | +             Err(e) => return Err(format!("Failed to load: {}", e)),
7572 | +         };
7573 | + 
7574 | +         let mut text = String::new();
7575 | +         for page in document.pages().iter() {
7576 | +             if let Ok(page_text) = page.text() {
7577 | +                 text.push_str(&page_text.all());
7578 | +                 text.push('\n');
7579 | +             }
7580 | +         }
7581 | +         Ok(text)
7582 | +     }) {
7583 | +         Ok(Ok(text)) => text,
7584 | +         Ok(Err(e)) => {
7585 | +             warn!("Failed to extract PDF text from {}: {}", path.display(), e);
7586 | +             String::new()
7587 | +         }
7588 | +         Err(_) => {
7589 | +             warn!(
7590 | +                 "PDF extraction panicked for {}. File may be corrupted or encrypted.",
7591 | +                 path.display()
7592 | +             );
7593 | +             String::new()
7594 | +         }
7595 | +     };
7596 | + 
7597 | +     // Try to extract title from first line or filename
7598 | +     let title = extract_title_from_content(&content)
7599 | +         .or_else(|| path.file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string()));
7600 | + 
7601 | +     Ok(ParsedDocument {
7602 | +         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
7603 | +         content,
7604 | +         title,
7605 | +     })
7606 | + }
7607 | + 
7608 | + /// Extract title from first non-empty line of content
7609 | + /// Filters out lines that are too long (likely not titles)
7610 | + fn extract_title_from_content(content: &str) -> Option<String> {
7611 | +     content
7612 | +         .lines()
7613 | +         .find(|line| {
7614 | +             let trimmed = line.trim();
7615 | +             !trimmed.is_empty() && trimmed.len() < 200
7616 | +         })
7617 | +         .map(|line| {
7618 | +             let trimmed = line.trim();
7619 | +             // Clean up common PDF artifacts
7620 | +             trimmed
7621 | +                 .replace("\u{0000}", "") // Remove null bytes
7622 | +                 .replace("\r", "") // Remove carriage returns
7623 | +         })
7624 | + }
7625 | + 
7626 | + #[cfg(test)]
7627 | + mod tests {
7628 | +     use super::*;
7629 | + 
7630 | +     #[test]
7631 | +     fn test_extract_title_from_content() {
7632 | +         let content = "\n\n  Document Title  \n\nBody content here\n";
7633 | +         assert_eq!(
7634 | +             extract_title_from_content(content),
7635 | +             Some("Document Title".to_string())
7636 | +         );
7637 | +     }
7638 | + 
7639 | +     #[test]
7640 | +     fn test_extract_title_empty() {
7641 | +         let content = "   \n\n   \n";
7642 | +         assert_eq!(extract_title_from_content(content), None);
7643 | +     }
7644 | + 
7645 | +     #[test]
7646 | +     fn test_extract_title_too_long() {
7647 | +         let content = "a".repeat(300);
7648 | +         assert_eq!(extract_title_from_content(&content), None);
7649 | +     }
7650 | + }
7651 | + ```

7666 | + pub fn parse_pptx(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
7667 | +     let pres = Presentation::open(path).map_err(|e| {
7668 | +         FlashError::parse(
7669 | +             path,
7670 | +             format!("Failed to open presentation {}: {}", path.display(), e),
7671 | +         )
7672 | +     })?;
7673 | + 
7674 | +     let text = pres.text().map_err(|e| {
7675 | +         FlashError::parse(
7676 | +             path,
7677 | +             format!("Failed to extract text from {}: {}", path.display(), e),
7678 | +         )
7679 | +     })?;
7680 | + 
7681 | +     let content = if text.len() > MAX_TEXT_LENGTH {
7682 | +         text[..MAX_TEXT_LENGTH].to_string()
7683 | +     } else {
7684 | +         text
7685 | +     };
7686 | + 
7687 | +     let slide_count = pres.slide_count().unwrap_or(0);
7688 | +     let title = path
7689 | +         .file_stem()
7690 | +         .map(|s| format!("{} ({} slides)", s.to_string_lossy(), slide_count));
7691 | + 
7692 | +     Ok(ParsedDocument {
7693 | +         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
7694 | +         content,
7695 | +         title,
7696 | +     })
7697 | + }

7717 | + ### File: `src\parsers\text.rs`
7718 | + 
7719 | + - Size: 4276 bytes
7720 | + - Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134168914525225653 }
7721 | + 
7722 | + ```rust
7723 | + use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
7724 | + use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
7725 | + use memmap2::Mmap;
7726 | + use std::fs::File;
7727 | + use std::io::{BufReader, Read};
7728 | + use std::path::Path;
7729 | + 
7730 | + /// Threshold for using memory mapping (files larger than this will be memory-mapped)
7731 | + const MMAP_THRESHOLD: u64 = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB
7732 | + /// Maximum file size to parse (prevent DOS)
7733 | + const MAX_FILE_SIZE: u64 = 100 * 1024 * 1024; // 100MB
7734 | + 
7735 | + /// Parse plain text files (TXT, MD, code files)
7736 | + /// Uses memory mapping for large files to reduce memory usage
7737 | + pub fn parse_text(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
7738 | +     let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
7739 | + 
7740 | +     let file_size = metadata.len();
7741 | + 
7742 | +     // Security: skip extremely large files
7743 | +     if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE {
7744 | +         return Err(FlashError::parse(
7745 | +             path,
7746 | +             format!(
7747 | +                 "File too large: {} bytes (max: {})",
7748 | +                 file_size, MAX_FILE_SIZE
7749 | +             ),
7750 | +         ));
7751 | +     }
7752 | + 
7753 | +     // Choose parsing strategy based on file size
7754 | +     let content = if file_size > MMAP_THRESHOLD {
7755 | +         parse_with_mmap(path)?
7756 | +     } else {
7757 | +         parse_with_buffer(path)?
7758 | +     };
7759 | + 
7760 | +     // Extract title from first non-empty line
7761 | +     let title = extract_title(&content);
7762 | + 
7763 | +     Ok(ParsedDocument {
7764 | +         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
7765 | +         content,
7766 | +         title,
7767 | +     })
7768 | + }
7769 | + 
7770 | + /// Parse small files using buffered reader (faster for small files)
7771 | + fn parse_with_buffer(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
7772 | +     let file = File::open(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
7773 | + 
7774 | +     let mut content = String::new();
7775 | +     BufReader::new(file)
7776 | +         .read_to_string(&mut content)
7777 | +         .map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
7778 | + 
7779 | +     Ok(content)
7780 | + }
7781 | + 
7782 | + /// Parse large files using memory mapping (zero-copy, better for large files)
7783 | + fn parse_with_mmap(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
7784 | +     let file = File::open(path)
7785 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open file: {}", e)))?;
7786 | + 
7787 | +     // SAFETY: Memory mapping a file is unsafe because another process could modify it
7788 | +     // while we are reading, potentially violating Rust's memory safety guarantees.
7789 | +     // We only read the data and accept this risk for performance reasons.
7790 | +     let mmap = unsafe { Mmap::map(&file).map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))? };
7791 | + 
7792 | +     // Use from_utf8_lossy to handle potential invalid UTF-8 sequences without erroring out
7793 | +     // and to avoid an explicit intermediate Vec allocation (mmap.to_vec()).
7794 | +     // This returns Cow<str>, into_owned() converts to String.
7795 | +     Ok(String::from_utf8_lossy(&mmap).into_owned())
7796 | + }
7797 | + 
7798 | + /// Extract title from first non-empty line
7799 | + fn extract_title(content: &str) -> Option<String> {
7800 | +     content
7801 | +         .lines()
7802 | +         .find(|line| !line.trim().is_empty())
7803 | +         .map(|line| {
7804 | +             // Remove markdown heading markers if present
7805 | +             let trimmed = line.trim();
7806 | +             if let Some(stripped) = trimmed.strip_prefix("# ") {
7807 | +                 stripped.to_string()
7808 | +             } else if let Some(stripped) = trimmed.strip_prefix("## ") {
7809 | +                 stripped.to_string()
7810 | +             } else if let Some(stripped) = trimmed.strip_prefix("### ") {
7811 | +                 stripped.to_string()
7812 | +             } else {
7813 | +                 trimmed.to_string()
7814 | +             }
7815 | +         })
7816 | +         .filter(|line| line.len() < 200) // Sanity check: don't use entire paragraphs
7817 | + }

7819 | + #[cfg(test)]
7820 | + mod tests {
7821 | +     use super::*;
7822 | +     use std::io::Write;
7823 | +     use tempfile::NamedTempFile;
7824 | + 
7825 | +     #[test]
7826 | +     fn test_parse_text_file() {
7827 | +         let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();
7828 | +         writeln!(temp_file, "# My Title").unwrap();
7829 | +         writeln!(temp_file, "Some content here").unwrap();
7830 | + 
7831 | +         let result = parse_text(temp_file.path()).unwrap();
7832 | + 
7833 | +         assert!(result.content.contains("My Title"));
7834 | +         assert!(result.content.contains("Some content"));
7835 | +         assert_eq!(result.title, Some("My Title".to_string()));
7836 | +     }
7837 | + 
7838 | +     #[test]
7839 | +     fn test_parse_text_no_heading() {
7840 | +         let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();
7841 | +         writeln!(temp_file, "Just a title").unwrap();
7842 | +         writeln!(temp_file, "Content here").unwrap();
7843 | + 
7844 | +         let result = parse_text(temp_file.path()).unwrap();
7845 | + 
7846 | +         assert_eq!(result.title, Some("Just a title".to_string()));
7847 | +     }
7848 | + }

7913 | +         progress_tx: Option<mpsc::Sender<ProgressEvent>>,

7917 | +             indexer,
