7925 | +     #[instrument(skip(self, exclude_patterns), fields(root = %root.display()))]
7926 | +     pub async fn scan_directory(&self, root: PathBuf, exclude_patterns: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {
7927 | +         info!("Starting directory scan for {}", root.display());
7928 | + 
7929 | +         // P3/P4: Run blocking WalkBuilder in a separate thread to avoid blocking Tokio runtime
7930 | +         // and allow pipelined consumption.
7931 | +         let (path_tx, path_rx): (
7932 | +             std::sync::mpsc::Sender<PathBuf>,
7933 | +             std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<PathBuf>,
7934 | +         ) = std::sync::mpsc::channel();
7935 | +         let total = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
7936 | +         let root_clone = root.clone();
7937 | +         let tx_clone = self.progress_tx.clone();
7938 | +         let total_clone = total.clone();
7939 | + 
7940 | +         let walker_handle = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
7941 | +             let mut builder = WalkBuilder::new(&root_clone);
7942 | +             // ... (keep logic same) ...
7943 | + 
7944 | +             // Add overrides
7945 | +             let mut override_builder = ignore::overrides::OverrideBuilder::new(&root_clone);
7946 | +             for pattern in &exclude_patterns {
7947 | +                 let ignore_pattern = format!("!{}", pattern);
7948 | +                 if let Err(e) = override_builder.add(&ignore_pattern) {
7949 | +                     warn!("Invalid exclude pattern '{}': {}", pattern, e);
7950 | +                 }
7951 | +             }
7952 | +             if let Ok(overrides) = override_builder.build() {
7953 | +                 builder.overrides(overrides);
7954 | +             }
7955 | + 
7956 | +             builder.follow_links(true).standard_filters(false);
7957 | + 
7958 | +             // --- Stage 1: Filename Indexing ---
7959 | +             info!("Stage 1: Filename Indexing");
7960 | +             let builder = builder.build_parallel();
7961 | +             builder.run(|| {
7962 | +                 let path_tx = path_tx.clone();
7963 | +                 let tx = tx_clone.clone();
7964 | +                 let total = total_clone.clone();
7965 | +                 Box::new(move |entry| {
7966 | +                     if let Ok(entry) = entry {
7967 | +                         if entry.file_type().map(|ft| ft.is_file()).unwrap_or(false) {
7968 | +                             let path = entry.path().to_path_buf();
7969 | +                             let _ = path_tx.send(path);
7970 | +                             let count = total.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
7971 | + 
7972 | +                             // Send progress update periodically
7973 | +                             if count % 100 == 0 {
7974 | +                                 if let Some(tx) = &tx {
7975 | +                                     let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
7976 | +                                         ptype: ProgressType::Filename,
7977 | +                                         current_file: entry
7978 | +                                             .file_name()
7979 | +                                             .to_string_lossy()
7980 | +                                             .to_string(),
7981 | +                                         current_folder: "".to_string(),
7982 | +                                         processed: count,
7983 | +                                         total: 0, // Unknown
7984 | +                                         status: "Scanning filenames...".to_string(),
7985 | +                                         eta_seconds: 0,
7986 | +                                         files_per_second: 0.0,
7987 | +                                     });
7988 | +                                 }
7989 | +                             }
7990 | +                         }
7991 | +                     }
7992 | +                     ignore::WalkState::Continue
7993 | +                 })
7994 | +             });
7995 | + 
7996 | +             // Final update for stage 1
7997 | +             let final_count = total_clone.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
7998 | +             if let Some(tx) = &tx_clone {
7999 | +                 let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
8000 | +                     ptype: ProgressType::Filename,
8001 | +                     current_file: "".to_string(),
8002 | +                     current_folder: "".to_string(),
8003 | +                     processed: final_count,
8004 | +                     total: final_count,
8005 | +                     status: "Filename scan complete".to_string(),
8006 | +                     eta_seconds: 0,
8007 | +                     files_per_second: 0.0,
8008 | +                 });
8009 | +             }
8010 | +         });
8011 | + 
8012 | +         // --- Stage 2: Content Indexing (Batched) ---
8013 | +         // Parse files in parallel via Rayon, collect results via mpsc,
8014 | +         // then batch-write to the index and metadata DB.
8015 | +         let (task_tx, task_rx) = std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel::<IndexTask>(BATCH_SIZE * 8);
8016 | +         let metadata_db_for_parser = self.metadata_db.clone();
8017 | +         let metadata_db_for_writer = self.metadata_db.clone();
8018 | +         let indexer_clone = self.indexer.clone();
8019 | +         let filename_index_clone = self.filename_index.clone();
8020 | +         let progress_tx_clone = self.progress_tx.clone();
8021 | +         let total_files = total.clone();
8022 | + 
8023 | +         // Configure Rayon thread pool based on settings limit
8024 | +         let threads = self.settings.indexing_threads as usize;
8025 | +         let pool = rayon::ThreadPoolBuilder::new()
8026 | +             .num_threads(threads)
8027 | +             .build()
8028 | +             .unwrap_or_else(|_| rayon::ThreadPoolBuilder::new().build().unwrap());
8029 | +         let file_size_limit_mb = self.settings.index_file_size_limit_mb;
8030 | + 
8031 | +         // --- Stage 2a: Parallel parsing (Rayon) → sends IndexTask into channel ---
8032 | +         let parser_handle = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
8033 | +             info!("Stage 2a: Parallel Parsing ({} threads)", threads);
8034 | + 
8035 | +             pool.install(|| {
8036 | +                 let mut chunk = Vec::with_capacity(200);
8037 | +                 for path in path_rx.into_iter() {
8038 | +                     chunk.push(path);
8039 | +                     if chunk.len() >= 200 {
8040 | +                         Scanner::process_chunk(
8041 | +                             &chunk,
8042 | +                             &metadata_db_for_parser,
8043 | +                             file_size_limit_mb,
8044 | +                             &task_tx,
8045 | +                         );
8046 | +                         chunk.clear();
8047 | +                     }
8048 | +                 }
8049 | +                 if !chunk.is_empty() {
8050 | +                     Scanner::process_chunk(
8051 | +                         &chunk,
8052 | +                         &metadata_db_for_parser,
8053 | +                         file_size_limit_mb,
8054 | +                         &task_tx,
8055 | +                     );
8056 | +                 }
8057 | +             });
8058 | +             // task_tx is dropped here, closing the channel
8059 | +         });
8060 | + 
8061 | +         // --- Stage 2b: Sequential batch writer (single thread) ---
8062 | +         let writer_handle = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
8063 | +             info!("Stage 2b: Batch Writing");
8064 | +             let start = Instant::now();
8065 | +             let mut doc_batch: Vec<(crate::parsers::ParsedDocument, u64, u64)> =
8066 | +                 Vec::with_capacity(BATCH_SIZE);
8067 | +             let mut meta_batch: Vec<(String, u64, u64, [u8; 32])> = Vec::with_capacity(BATCH_SIZE);
8068 | +             let mut processed: usize = 0;
8069 | + 
8070 | +             for task in task_rx.iter() {
8071 | +                 // Add to filename index
8072 | +                 if let Some(f_index) = &filename_index_clone {
8073 | +                     let path = std::path::Path::new(&task.doc.path);
8074 | +                     if let Some(name) = path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {
8075 | +                         let _ = f_index.add_file(&task.doc.path, name);
8076 | +                     }
8077 | +                 }
8078 | + 
8079 | +                 // Clone path before moving doc
8080 | +                 let doc_path = task.doc.path.clone();
8081 | +                 doc_batch.push((task.doc, task.modified, task.size));
8082 | +                 meta_batch.push((doc_path, task.modified, task.size, task.content_hash));
8083 | +                 processed += 1;
8084 | + 
8085 | +                 // Flush batch when full
8086 | +                 if doc_batch.len() >= BATCH_SIZE {
8087 | +                     let _ = indexer_clone.add_documents_batch(&doc_batch);
8088 | +                     let _ = indexer_clone.commit();
8089 | +                     indexer_clone.invalidate_cache();
8090 | +                     let _ = metadata_db_for_writer.batch_update_metadata(&meta_batch);
8091 | +                     doc_batch.clear();
8092 | +                     meta_batch.clear();
8093 | +                 }
8094 | + 
8095 | +                 // Progress update
8096 | +                 if processed % 10 == 0 {
8097 | +                     let current_total = total_files.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
8098 | +                     let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
8099 | +                     let rate = if elapsed > 0.0 {
8100 | +                         processed as f64 / elapsed
8101 | +                     } else {
8102 | +                         0.0
8103 | +                     };
8104 | +                     if let Some(tx) = &progress_tx_clone {
8105 | +                         let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
8106 | +                             ptype: ProgressType::Content,
8107 | +                             current_file: "".to_string(),
8108 | +                             current_folder: "".to_string(),
8109 | +                             processed,
8110 | +                             total: current_total,
8111 | +                             status: "Indexing contents...".to_string(),
8112 | +                             eta_seconds: if rate > 0.0 {
8113 | +                                 (current_total.saturating_sub(processed) as f64 / rate) as u64
8114 | +                             } else {
8115 | +                                 0
8116 | +                             },
8117 | +                             files_per_second: rate,
8118 | +                         });
8119 | +                     }
8120 | +                 }
8121 | +             }
8122 | + 
8123 | +             // Flush remaining items (B1: always commit at end)
8124 | +             if !doc_batch.is_empty() {
8125 | +                 let _ = indexer_clone.add_documents_batch(&doc_batch);
8126 | +                 let _ = indexer_clone.commit();
8127 | +                 indexer_clone.invalidate_cache();
8128 | +                 let _ = metadata_db_for_writer.batch_update_metadata(&meta_batch);
8129 | +             }
8130 | + 
8131 | +             // Final progress
8132 | +             if let Some(tx) = &progress_tx_clone {
8133 | +                 let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
8134 | +                     ptype: ProgressType::Content,
8135 | +                     current_file: "".to_string(),
8136 | +                     current_folder: "".to_string(),
8137 | +                     processed,
8138 | +                     total: processed,
8139 | +                     status: "All files indexed".to_string(),
8140 | +                     eta_seconds: 0,
8141 | +                     files_per_second: 0.0,
8142 | +                 });
8143 | +             }
8144 | + 
8145 | +             info!(
8146 | +                 "Indexed {} files in {:.2}s",
8147 | +                 processed,
8148 | +                 start.elapsed().as_secs_f64()
8149 | +             );
8150 | +         });
8151 | + 
8152 | +         // Wait for all stages to complete
8153 | +         walker_handle
8154 | +             .await
8155 | +             .map_err(|e| crate::error::FlashError::index(format!("Walk task failed: {}", e)))?;
8156 | +         parser_handle
8157 | +             .await
8158 | +             .map_err(|e| crate::error::FlashError::index(format!("Parse task failed: {}", e)))?;
8159 | +         writer_handle
8160 | +             .await
8161 | +             .map_err(|e| crate::error::FlashError::index(format!("Write task failed: {}", e)))?;
8162 | + 
8163 | +         // Commit filename index to disk
8164 | +         if let Some(f_index) = &self.filename_index {
8165 | +             let _ = f_index.commit();
8166 | +         }
8167 | + 
8168 | +         Ok(())
8169 | +     }
8170 | + 
8171 | +     fn process_chunk(
8172 | +         chunk: &[PathBuf],
8173 | +         metadata_db: &Arc<MetadataDb>,
8174 | +         file_size_limit_mb: u32,
8175 | +         task_tx: &std::sync::mpsc::SyncSender<IndexTask>,
8176 | +     ) {
8177 | +         let limit_bytes = (file_size_limit_mb as u64) * 1024 * 1024;
8178 | +         let mut batch_entries = Vec::with_capacity(chunk.len());
8179 | +         let mut valid_paths = Vec::with_capacity(chunk.len());
8180 | + 
8181 | +         for path in chunk {
8182 | +             let metadata = match std::fs::metadata(path) {
8183 | +                 Ok(m) => m,
8184 | +                 Err(_) => continue,
8185 | +             };
8186 | + 
8187 | +             let size = metadata.len();
8188 | +             if size > limit_bytes {
8189 | +                 warn!(
8190 | +                     "Skipping large file: {} ({} bytes > limit of {} bytes)",
8191 | +                     path.display(),
8192 | +                     size,
8193 | +                     limit_bytes
8194 | +                 );
8195 | +                 continue;
8196 | +             }
8197 | + 
8198 | +             let modified = metadata
8199 | +                 .modified()
8200 | +                 .unwrap_or(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
8201 | +                 .duration_since(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
8202 | +                 .unwrap_or_default()
8203 | +                 .as_secs();
8204 | + 
8205 | +             batch_entries.push((path.to_string_lossy().to_string(), modified, size));
8206 | +             valid_paths.push((path.clone(), modified, size));
8207 | +         }
8208 | + 
8209 | +         if batch_entries.is_empty() {
8210 | +             return;
8211 | +         }
8212 | + 
8213 | +         let needs_reindex = metadata_db
8214 | +             .batch_needs_reindex(&batch_entries)
8215 | +             .unwrap_or_else(|_| vec![true; batch_entries.len()]);
8216 | + 
8217 | +         let paths_to_parse: Vec<_> = valid_paths
8218 | +             .into_iter()
8219 | +             .enumerate()
8220 | +             .filter(|(i, _)| needs_reindex[*i])
8221 | +             .map(|(_, data)| data)
8222 | +             .collect();
8223 | + 
8224 | +         paths_to_parse
8225 | +             .into_par_iter()
8226 | +             .for_each(|(path, modified, size)| {
8227 | +                 if let Ok(parsed) = parse_file(&path) {
8228 | +                     let content_hash = blake3::hash(parsed.content.as_bytes()).into();
8229 | +                     let _ = task_tx.send(IndexTask {
8230 | +                         doc: parsed,
8231 | +                         modified,
8232 | +                         size,
8233 | +                         content_hash,
8234 | +                     });
8235 | +                 } else {
8236 | +                     warn!("Failed to parse file {:?}", path);
8237 | +                 }
8238 | +             });
8239 | +     }
8240 | + }
8241 | + ```

8334 | + impl std::fmt::Display for Theme {
8335 | +     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {

8337 | +             Theme::Auto => write!(f, "auto"),
8338 | +             Theme::Light => write!(f, "light"),
8339 | +             Theme::Dark => write!(f, "dark"),
8340 | +         }
8341 | +     }
8342 | + }

8380 | +             // Indexing

8384 | +                 "node_modules/".to_string(),

8389 | +                 "Thumbs.db".to_string(),
8390 | +                 ".DS_Store".to_string(),
8391 | +             ],
8392 | +             exclude_folders: vec![

8394 | +                 "System Volume Information".to_string(),
8395 | +             ],
8396 | +             auto_index_on_startup: true,
8397 | +             index_file_size_limit_mb: 100,
8398 | + 
8399 | +             // Search
8400 | +             max_results: 50,
8401 | +             search_history_enabled: true,
8402 | +             fuzzy_matching: true,
8403 | +             case_sensitive: false,
8404 | +             default_filters: DefaultFilters::default(),
8405 | +             recent_searches: Some(vec![]),
8406 | +             search_history: Some(vec![]),
8407 | +             filename_index_enabled: true,
8408 | + 
8409 | +             // Appearance
8410 | +             theme: Theme::default(),
8411 | +             font_size: FontSize::default(),
8412 | +             show_file_extensions: true,
8413 | +             results_per_page: 50,
8414 | + 
8415 | +             // Behavior
8416 | +             minimize_to_tray: true,
8417 | +             auto_start_on_boot: false,
8418 | +             double_click_action: DoubleClickAction::default(),
8419 | +             show_preview_panel: true,
8420 | +             context_menu_enabled: false,
8421 | +             global_hotkey: default_global_hotkey(),
8422 | + 
8423 | +             // Performance
8424 | +             indexing_threads: 4,
8425 | +             memory_limit_mb: 512,
8426 | + 
8427 | +             // Pinned files
8428 | +             pinned_files: vec![],
8429 | +         }
8430 | +     }
8431 | + }

8433 | + pub struct SettingsManager {
8434 | +     path: PathBuf,
8435 | + }
8436 | + 
8437 | + impl SettingsManager {
8438 | +     pub fn new(app_data_dir: &Path) -> Self {
8439 | +         Self {
8440 | +             path: app_data_dir.join("settings.json"),
8441 | +         }
8442 | +     }
8443 | + 
8444 | +     pub fn load(&self) -> Result<AppSettings> {
8445 | +         if !self.path.exists() {
8446 | +             let defaults = AppSettings::default();
8447 | +             self.save(&defaults)?;
8448 | +             return Ok(defaults);
8449 | +         }
8450 | + 
8451 | +         let content = fs::read_to_string(&self.path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
8452 | + 
8453 | +         serde_json::from_str(&content)
8454 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("parse_settings", e.to_string()))
8455 | +     }
8456 | + 
8457 | +     pub fn save(&self, settings: &AppSettings) -> Result<()> {
8458 | +         let content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(settings)
8459 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("serialize_settings", e.to_string()))?;
8460 | + 
8461 | +         fs::write(&self.path, content).map_err(FlashError::Io)
8462 | +     }
8463 | + }
8464 | + 
8465 | + #[cfg(test)]
8466 | + mod tests {
8467 | +     use super::*;
8468 | +     use tempfile::tempdir;
8469 | + 
8470 | +     #[test]
8471 | +     fn test_settings_save_load() {
8472 | +         let temp_dir = tempdir().unwrap();
8473 | +         let manager = SettingsManager::new(temp_dir.path());
8474 | + 
8475 | +         let settings = AppSettings {
8476 | +             max_results: 100,
8477 | +             theme: Theme::Dark,
8478 | +             ..Default::default()
8479 | +         };
8480 | + 
8481 | +         manager.save(&settings).unwrap();
8482 | +         let loaded = manager.load().unwrap();
8483 | +         assert_eq!(loaded.max_results, 100);
8484 | +         assert!(matches!(loaded.theme, Theme::Dark));
8485 | +     }
8486 | +     #[allow(dead_code)]
8487 | +     fn test_settings_unknown_fields() {
8488 | +         let temp_dir = tempdir().unwrap();
8489 | +         let manager = SettingsManager::new(temp_dir.path());
8490 | + 
8491 | +         // Create a JSON string with an unknown field "future_feature_flag"
8492 | +         let json_with_unknown = r#"{
8493 | +             "max_results": 150,
8494 | +             "theme": "dark",
8495 | +             "future_feature_flag": true,
8496 | +             "index_dirs": [],
8497 | +             "exclude_patterns": [],
8498 | +             "exclude_folders": [],
8499 | +             "auto_index_on_startup": true,
8500 | +             "index_file_size_limit_mb": 100,
8501 | +             "search_history_enabled": true,
8502 | +             "fuzzy_matching": true,
8503 | +             "case_sensitive": false,
8504 | +             "default_filters": {
8505 | +                 "file_types": [],
8506 | +                 "min_size_mb": null,
8507 | +                 "max_size_mb": null,
8508 | +                 "modified_within_days": null
8509 | +             },
8510 | +             "recent_searches": null,
8511 | +             "search_history": null,
8512 | +             "filename_index_enabled": true,
8513 | +             "font_size": "medium",
8514 | +             "show_file_extensions": true,
8515 | +             "results_per_page": 50,
8516 | +             "minimize_to_tray": true,
8517 | +             "auto_start_on_boot": false,
8518 | +             "double_click_action": "open_file",
8519 | +             "show_preview_panel": true,
8520 | +             "context_menu_enabled": false,
8521 | +             "global_hotkey": "Alt+Space",
8522 | +             "indexing_threads": 4,
8523 | +             "memory_limit_mb": 512,
8524 | +             "pinned_files": []
8525 | +         }"#;
8526 | + 
8527 | +         fs::write(manager.path.clone(), json_with_unknown).unwrap();
8528 | + 
8529 | +         let loaded = manager.load().expect("Should tolerate unknown fields");
8530 | +         assert_eq!(loaded.max_results, 150);
8531 | +         assert!(matches!(loaded.theme, Theme::Dark));
8532 | +     }
8533 | + }
8534 | + ```

8542 | + #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
8543 | + use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
8544 | + 
8545 | + #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
8546 | + pub fn register_context_menu(_enable: bool) -> crate::error::Result<()> {
8547 | +     // Operations on non-windows don't do anything for now
8548 | +     Ok(())
8549 | + }
8550 | + 
8551 | + #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
8552 | + pub fn register_context_menu(enable: bool) -> Result<()> {
8553 | +     use std::env;
8554 | +     use winreg::enums::*;
8555 | +     use winreg::RegKey;
8556 | + 
8557 | +     let hkcu = RegKey::predef(HKEY_CURRENT_USER);
8558 | +     let path = r#"Software\Classes\Directory\shell\FlashSearch"#;
8559 | + 
8560 | +     if enable {
8561 | +         let (key, _) = hkcu
8562 | +             .create_subkey(path)
8563 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
8564 | + 
8565 | +         key.set_value("", &"Search with Flash Search")
8566 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
8567 | + 
8568 | +         let icon_path = env::current_exe().unwrap_or_default();
8569 | +         key.set_value("Icon", &icon_path.to_str().unwrap_or_default())
8570 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
8571 | + 
8572 | +         let (command_key, _) = key
8573 | +             .create_subkey("command")
8574 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
8575 | + 
8576 | +         let command = format!("\"{}\" \"%%1\"", icon_path.to_str().unwrap_or_default());
8577 | +         command_key
8578 | +             .set_value("", &command)
8579 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
8580 | +     } else {
8581 | +         let _ = hkcu.delete_subkey_all(path);
8582 | +     }
8583 | +     Ok(())
8584 | + }
8585 | + ```

8607 | + 
8608 | + pub fn set_auto_start(enable: bool) -> Result<()> {
8609 | +     let app_path = env::current_exe().map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
8610 | +     let app_name = "com.flashsearch";
8611 | + 
8612 | +     let auto = AutoLaunchBuilder::new()
8613 | +         .set_app_name(app_name)
8614 | +         .set_app_path(app_path.to_str().unwrap_or_default())
8615 | +         .set_macos_launch_mode(auto_launch::MacOSLaunchMode::LaunchAgent)
8616 | +         .build()
8617 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("auto_start", e.to_string()))?;
8618 | + 
8619 | +     if enable {
8620 | +         auto.enable()
8621 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("auto_start_enable", e.to_string()))?;
8622 | +     } else if auto.is_enabled().unwrap_or(false) {
8623 | +         auto.disable()
8624 | +             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("auto_start_disable", e.to_string()))?;
8625 | +     }
8626 | + 
8627 | +     Ok(())
8628 | + }

8637 | + use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};

8641 | + 
8642 | + pub fn create_tray_icon() -> Result<TrayIcon> {
8643 | +     let icon_data = include_bytes!("../../assets/logo.png");
8644 | +     let image = image::load_from_memory_with_format(icon_data, ImageFormat::Png)
8645 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("tray_icon", e.to_string()))?
8646 | +         .into_rgba8();
8647 | +     let (width, height) = image.dimensions();
8648 | +     let rgba = image.into_raw();
8649 | +     let icon = Icon::from_rgba(rgba, width, height)
8650 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("tray_icon", e.to_string()))?;
8651 | + 
8652 | +     let tray_menu = Menu::new();
8653 | +     let show_i = MenuItem::with_id("show", "Show Flash Search", true, None);
8654 | +     let quit_i = MenuItem::with_id("quit", "Quit", true, None);
8655 | + 
8656 | +     let _ = tray_menu.append(&show_i);
8657 | +     let _ = tray_menu.append(&PredefinedMenuItem::separator());
8658 | +     let _ = tray_menu.append(&quit_i);
8659 | + 
8660 | +     let tray_icon = TrayIconBuilder::new()
8661 | +         .with_id("com.flashsearch")
8662 | +         .with_menu(Box::new(tray_menu))
8663 | +         .with_tooltip("Flash Search")
8664 | +         .with_icon(icon)
8665 | +         .build()
8666 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("tray_icon", e.to_string()))?;
8667 | + 
8668 | +     Ok(tray_icon)
8669 | + }
8670 | + ```

8672 | + ### File: `src\watcher.rs`
8673 | + 
8674 | + - Size: 9723 bytes
8675 | + - Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134168942588200663 }
8676 | + 
8677 | + ```rust
8678 | + use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
8679 | + use crate::indexer::IndexManager;
8680 | + use crate::metadata::MetadataDb;
8681 | + use crate::parsers::parse_file;
8682 | + use blake3;
8683 | + use notify::{Event, EventKind, RecommendedWatcher, RecursiveMode, Watcher};
8684 | + use std::collections::HashMap;
8685 | + use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
8686 | + use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
8687 | + use std::time::Duration;
8688 | + use tracing::{error, info, warn};
8689 | + 
8690 | + #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
8691 | + enum WatcherAction {
8692 | +     Index,
8693 | +     Remove,
8694 | + }
8695 | + 
8696 | + /// Manages active file system watching with debouncing
8697 | + pub struct WatcherManager {
8698 | +     watcher: Option<RecommendedWatcher>,
8699 | +     _indexer: Arc<IndexManager>,
8700 | +     _metadata_db: Arc<MetadataDb>,
8701 | +     _runtime_handle: tokio::runtime::Handle,
8702 | +     // Buffer for pending events: Map<Path, Action>
8703 | +     // WARNING: Do not hold this Mutex across .await points to avoid deadlocks.
8704 | +     event_buffer: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<PathBuf, WatcherAction>>>,
8705 | + }
8706 | + 
8707 | + impl WatcherManager {
8708 | +     pub fn new(indexer: Arc<IndexManager>, metadata_db: Arc<MetadataDb>) -> Self {
8709 | +         let buffer: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<PathBuf, WatcherAction>>> =
8710 | +             Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new()));
8711 | +         let buffer_for_task = buffer.clone();
8712 | +         let indexer_for_task = indexer.clone();
8713 | +         let metadata_db_for_task = metadata_db.clone();
8714 | +         let runtime_handle = tokio::runtime::Handle::current();
8715 | + 
8716 | +         // Spawn background processor for debounced events
8717 | +         runtime_handle.spawn(async move {
8718 | +             loop {
8719 | +                 tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(1000)).await; // Check every 1s
8720 | + 
8721 | +                 let events = {
8722 | +                     let mut guard = match buffer_for_task.lock() {
8723 | +                         Ok(g) => g,
8724 | +                         Err(e) => {
8725 | +                             error!("Watcher lock poisoned: {}", e);
8726 | +                             continue;
8727 | +                         }
8728 | +                     };
8729 | +                     if guard.is_empty() {
8730 | +                         continue;
8731 | +                     }
8732 | +                     // Take all events
8733 | +                     let events: HashMap<PathBuf, WatcherAction> = std::mem::take(&mut *guard);
8734 | +                     events
8735 | +                 };
8736 | + 
8737 | +                 let mut needs_commit = false;
8738 | + 
8739 | +                 // First pass: collect all paths that need to be removed
8740 | +                 let remove_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = events
8741 | +                     .iter()
8742 | +                     .filter(|(_, action)| matches!(action, WatcherAction::Remove))
8743 | +                     .map(|(path, _)| path.clone())
8744 | +                     .collect();
8745 | + 
8746 | +                 // Second pass: collect all paths that need to be indexed
8747 | +                 let index_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = events
8748 | +                     .iter()
8749 | +                     .filter(|(_, action)| matches!(action, WatcherAction::Index))
8750 | +                     .map(|(path, _)| path.clone())
8751 | +                     .collect();
8752 | + 
8753 | +                 // Process removes first
8754 | +                 for path in remove_paths {
8755 | +                     let path_str = path.to_string_lossy();
8756 | +                     let _ = indexer_for_task.remove_document(&path_str);
8757 | +                     match metadata_db_for_task.remove_file(&path) {
8758 | +                         Ok(true) => {
8759 | +                             needs_commit = true;
8760 | +                             info!("Removed file (watcher): {:?}", path);
8761 | +                         }
8762 | +                         Err(e) => error!("Watcher error removing {:?}: {}", path, e),
8763 | +                         _ => {}
8764 | +                     }
8765 | +                 }
8766 | + 
8767 | +                 // Then process indexes
8768 | +                 let mut docs_to_add = Vec::new();
8769 | +                 let mut meta_to_update = Vec::new();
8770 | + 
8771 | +                 for path in index_paths {
8772 | +                     match Self::reindex_single_file(&path, &metadata_db_for_task).await {
8773 | +                         Ok(Some((doc, modified, size, hash))) => {
8774 | +                             meta_to_update.push((doc.path.clone(), modified, size, hash));
8775 | +                             docs_to_add.push((doc, modified, size));
8776 | +                         }
8777 | +                         Ok(None) => {} // Skipped or does not exist
8778 | +                         Err(e) => {
8779 | +                             error!("Watcher error indexing {:?}: {}", path, e);
8780 | +                         }
8781 | +                     }
8782 | +                 }
8783 | + 
8784 | +                 if !docs_to_add.is_empty() {
8785 | +                     if let Err(e) = indexer_for_task.add_documents_batch(&docs_to_add) {
8786 | +                         error!("Watcher error batch adding to index: {}", e);
8787 | +                     }
8788 | +                     if let Err(e) = metadata_db_for_task.batch_update_metadata(&meta_to_update) {
8789 | +                         error!("Watcher error batch updating DB: {}", e);
8790 | +                     } else {
8791 | +                         info!("Re-indexed {} files (watcher)", docs_to_add.len());
8792 | +                     }
8793 | +                     needs_commit = true;
8794 | +                 }
8795 | + 
8796 | +                 if needs_commit {
8797 | +                     if let Err(e) = indexer_for_task.commit() {
8798 | +                         error!("Watcher failed to commit index: {}", e);
8799 | +                     } else {
8800 | +                         indexer_for_task.invalidate_cache();
8801 | +                         info!("Watcher committed changes");
8802 | +                     }
8803 | +                 }
8804 | +             }
8805 | +         });
8806 | + 
8807 | +         Self {
8808 | +             watcher: None,
8809 | +             _indexer: indexer,
8810 | +             _metadata_db: metadata_db,
8811 | +             _runtime_handle: runtime_handle,
8812 | +             event_buffer: buffer,
8813 | +         }
8814 | +     }
8815 | + 
8816 | +     /// Update the list of watched directories
8817 | +     pub fn update_watch_list(&mut self, dirs: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {
8818 | +         self.watcher = None;
8819 | + 
8820 | +         if dirs.is_empty() {
8821 | +             return Ok(());
8822 | +         }
8823 | + 
8824 | +         let buffer_clone = self.event_buffer.clone();
8825 | + 
8826 | +         let mut watcher = notify::recommended_watcher(move |res: notify::Result<Event>| {
8827 | +             if let Ok(event) = res {
8828 | +                 let mut guard = match buffer_clone.lock() {
8829 | +                     Ok(g) => g,
8830 | +                     Err(e) => {
8831 | +                         error!("Watcher lock poisoned: {}", e);
8832 | +                         return;
8833 | +                     }
8834 | +                 };
8835 | + 
8836 | +                 match event.kind {
8837 | +                     EventKind::Modify(_) | EventKind::Create(_) | EventKind::Remove(_) => {
8838 | +                         for path in &event.paths {
8839 | +                             match event.kind {
8840 | +                                 EventKind::Remove(_) => {
8841 | +                                     guard.insert(path.clone(), WatcherAction::Remove);
8842 | +                                 }
8843 | +                                 _ => {
8844 | +                                     if path.is_file() {
8845 | +                                         guard.insert(path.clone(), WatcherAction::Index);
8846 | +                                     }
8847 | +                                 }
8848 | +                             }
8849 | +                         }
8850 | +                     }
8851 | +                     _ => {}
8852 | +                 }
8853 | +             }
8854 | +         })
8855 | +         .map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))?;
8856 | + 
8857 | +         for dir in dirs {
8858 | +             let path = Path::new(&dir);
8859 | +             if path.exists() {
8860 | +                 watcher
8861 | +                     .watch(path, RecursiveMode::Recursive)
8862 | +                     .map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))?;
8863 | +             }
8864 | +         }
8865 | + 
8866 | +         self.watcher = Some(watcher);
8867 | +         Ok(())
8868 | +     }
8869 | + 
8870 | +     // Returns parsed document data if file needs re-indexing
8871 | +     async fn reindex_single_file(
8872 | +         path: &Path,
8873 | +         metadata_db: &Arc<MetadataDb>,
8874 | +     ) -> Result<Option<(crate::parsers::ParsedDocument, u64, u64, [u8; 32])>> {
8875 | +         if !path.exists() {
8876 | +             return Ok(None);
8877 | +         }
8878 | + 
8879 | +         let metadata = match std::fs::metadata(path) {
8880 | +             Ok(m) => m,
8881 | +             Err(_) => return Ok(None), // Ignore if cannot read metadata
8882 | +         };
8883 | + 
8884 | +         let modified = metadata
8885 | +             .modified()
8886 | +             .unwrap_or(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
8887 | +             .duration_since(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
8888 | +             .unwrap_or_default()
8889 | +             .as_secs();
8890 | +         let size = metadata.len();
8891 | + 
8892 | +         // Skip check? If watcher said it changed, it probably did.
8893 | +         // But checking db saves re-hashing if it was a false alarm.
8894 | +         if !metadata_db
8895 | +             .needs_reindex(path, modified, size)
8896 | +             .unwrap_or(true)
8897 | +         {
8898 | +             return Ok(None);
8899 | +         }
8900 | + 
8901 | +         let parsed = match parse_file(path) {
8902 | +             Ok(p) => p,
8903 | +             Err(e) => {
8904 | +                 warn!("Failed to parse file {:?}: {}", path, e);
8905 | +                 return Ok(None);
8906 | +             }
8907 | +         };
8908 | + 
8909 | +         let content_hash: [u8; 32] = blake3::hash(parsed.content.as_bytes()).into();
8910 | + 
8911 | +         Ok(Some((parsed, modified, size, content_hash)))
8912 | +     }
8913 | + }
8914 | + 
8915 | + #[cfg(test)]
8916 | + mod tests {
8917 | +     use super::*;
8918 | +     use crate::indexer::IndexManager;
8919 | +     use crate::metadata::MetadataDb;
8920 | +     use std::fs;
8921 | +     use std::sync::Arc;
8922 | +     use tempfile::tempdir;
8923 | + 
8924 | +     #[tokio::test]
8925 | +     async fn test_watcher_manager_creation() {
8926 | +         let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
8927 | +         let indexer = Arc::new(IndexManager::open(temp.path(), 256).unwrap());
8928 | +         let metadata = Arc::new(MetadataDb::open(&temp.path().join("metadata.db")).unwrap());
8929 | + 
8930 | +         let mut watcher = WatcherManager::new(indexer, metadata);
8931 | + 
8932 | +         // Add a directory to watch
8933 | +         let watch_dir = temp.path().join("watch_me");
8934 | +         fs::create_dir(&watch_dir).unwrap();
8935 | + 
8936 | +         assert!(watcher
8937 | +             .update_watch_list(vec![watch_dir.to_string_lossy().to_string()])
8938 | +             .is_ok());
8939 | +         assert!(watcher.watcher.is_some());
8940 | + 
8941 | +         // Empty list should remove watcher
8942 | +         assert!(watcher.update_watch_list(vec![]).is_ok());
8943 | +         assert!(watcher.watcher.is_none());
8944 | +     }
8945 | + }
8946 | + ```

9222 | ### Diff: `reorder.py`
9223 | 
9224 | _Status: Added_
9225 | 
9226 | ```diff
9227 | + import sys
9228 | + import re
9229 | + 
9230 | + def main():
9231 | +     if len(sys.argv) < 2:
9232 | +         return
9233 | +     
9234 | +     filepath = sys.argv[1]
9235 | +     with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
9236 | +         content = f.read()
9237 | + 
9238 | +     parts = content.split('## File Contents\n\n')
9239 | +     if len(parts) < 2:
9240 | +         print("Could not find '## File Contents'")
9241 | +         with open('context.md', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
9242 | +             f.write(content)
9243 | +         return
9244 | + 
9245 | +     header = parts[0] + '## File Contents\n\n'
9246 | +     body = parts[1]
9247 | + 
9248 | +     blocks = re.split(r'(### File: `[^`]+`)', body)
9249 | + 
9250 | +     if len(blocks) < 3:
9251 | +         with open('context.md', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
9252 | +             f.write(content)
9253 | +         return
9254 | + 
9255 | +     parsed_blocks = []
9256 | +     for i in range(1, len(blocks), 2):
9257 | +         heading = blocks[i]
9258 | +         content_block = blocks[i+1]
9259 | +         
9260 | +         m = re.search(r'### File: `([^`]+)`', heading)
9261 | +         fname = m.group(1).replace('\\', '/') if m else ""
9262 | +         
9263 | +         parsed_blocks.append((fname, heading + content_block))
9264 | + 
9265 | +     def get_priority(fname):
9266 | +         if fname == 'src/lib.rs': return 0
9267 | +         if fname == 'src/main.rs': return 1
9268 | +         if fname == 'Cargo.toml': return 2
9269 | +         if fname.startswith('tests/'): return 3
9270 | +         if fname.startswith('benches/'): return 4
9271 | +         if fname.startswith('examples/'): return 5
9272 | +         return 6
9273 | + 
9274 | +     parsed_blocks.sort(key=lambda x: (get_priority(x[0]), x[0]))
9275 | + 
9276 | +     final_content = header + "".join([b[1] for b in parsed_blocks])
9277 | +     with open('context.md', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
9278 | +         f.write(final_content)
9279 | +     print("Reorganized successfully")
9280 | + 
9281 | + if __name__ == "__main__":
9282 | +     main()
9283 | ```

9684 | pub fn get_app_data_dir() -> std::result::Result<PathBuf, FlashError> {
9685 |     #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
9686 |     let path = dirs::config_dir()
9687 |         .ok_or_else(|| FlashError::config("config_dir", "Could not find config directory"))?;
9688 | 
9689 |     #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
9690 |     let path = dirs::data_dir()
9691 |         .or_else(|| dirs::home_dir().map(|h| h.join(".flash-search")))
9692 |         .ok_or_else(|| FlashError::config("data_dir", "Could not find data directory"))?;
9693 | 
9694 |     let mut path = path;
9695 |     path.push("com.flashsearch");
9696 |     Ok(path)
9697 | }
9698 | 
9699 | pub fn setup_app() -> std::result::Result<
9700 |     (
9701 |         Arc<AppState>,
9702 |         tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<crate::scanner::ProgressEvent>,
9703 |     ),
9704 |     FlashError,
9705 | > {
9706 |     let app_data_dir = get_app_data_dir()?;
9707 | 
9708 |     if !app_data_dir.exists() {
9709 |         std::fs::create_dir_all(&app_data_dir)
9710 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("create_dir", e.to_string()))?;
9711 |     }
9712 | 
9713 |     info!("App data directory: {:?}", app_data_dir);
9714 | 
9715 |     let settings_manager = settings::SettingsManager::new(&app_data_dir);
9716 |     let settings = settings_manager.load().unwrap_or_default();
9717 |     let index_path = app_data_dir.join("index");
9718 |     let indexer =
9719 |         indexer::IndexManager::open(&index_path, settings.memory_limit_mb).map_err(|e| {
9720 |             FlashError::Index {
9721 |                 msg: format!("Failed to open search index: {}", e),
9722 |                 field: None,
9723 |             }
9724 |         })?;
9725 |     let db_path = app_data_dir.join("metadata.redb");
9726 |     let metadata_db = metadata::MetadataDb::open(&db_path)
9727 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("open", "metadata.redb", e.to_string()))?;
9728 | 
9729 |     let metadata_db_shared = Arc::new(metadata_db);
9730 |     let indexer_shared = Arc::new(indexer);
9731 | 
9732 |     let filename_index =
9733 |         match indexer::filename_index::FilenameIndex::open(&app_data_dir.join("filename_index")) {
9734 |             Ok(idx) => Some(Arc::new(idx)),
9735 |             Err(e) => {
9736 |                 error!("Failed to open filename index: {}", e);
9737 |                 None
9738 |             }
9739 |         };
9740 | 
9741 |     // Initialize watcher
9742 |     let watcher = watcher::WatcherManager::new(indexer_shared.clone(), metadata_db_shared.clone());
9743 | 
9744 |     let (progress_tx, progress_rx) = tokio::sync::mpsc::channel(100);
9745 | 
9746 |     let scanner = Arc::new(crate::scanner::Scanner::new(
9747 |         indexer_shared.clone(),
9748 |         metadata_db_shared.clone(),
9749 |         filename_index.clone(),
9750 |         Some(progress_tx.clone()),
9751 |         settings.clone(),
9752 |     ));
9753 | 
9754 |     let state = Arc::new(AppState::new(
9755 |         indexer_shared,
9756 |         metadata_db_shared,
9757 |         settings_manager,
9758 |         watcher,
9759 |         filename_index,
9760 |         progress_tx,
9761 |         scanner,
9762 |     ));
9763 | 
9764 |     Ok((state, progress_rx))
9765 | }
9766 | 
9767 | pub fn run_ui() -> std::result::Result<(), FlashError> {
9768 |     let result = setup_app().map(|(state, rx)| {
9769 |         iced_ui::run_ui(Ok(state), rx);
9770 |     });
9771 | 
9772 |     if let Err(e) = result {
9773 |         iced_ui::run_ui(Err(e.to_string()), tokio::sync::mpsc::channel(1).1);
9774 |     }
9775 | 
9776 |     Ok(())
9777 | }
9778 | 
9779 | pub async fn run_cli(
9780 |     query: Option<String>,
9781 |     is_json: bool,
9782 |     _index_path: Option<String>,
9783 | ) -> crate::error::Result<()> {
9784 |     if let Some(query_str) = query {
9785 |         let (state, _) = setup_app()?;
9786 |         let results = state
9787 |             .indexer
9788 |             .search(&query_str, 20, None, None, None)
9789 |             .await?;
9790 | 
9791 |         if is_json {
9792 |             let json_results: Vec<serde_json::Value> = results
9793 |                 .into_iter()
9794 |                 .map(|res| {
9795 |                     serde_json::json!({
9796 |                         "score": res.score,
9797 |                         "path": res.file_path,
9798 |                         "title": res.title
9799 |                     })
9800 |                 })
9801 |                 .collect();
9802 |             println!(
9803 |                 "{}",
9804 |                 serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json_results).unwrap_or_default()
9805 |             );
9806 |         } else {
9807 |             for res in results {
9808 |                 println!("{} | {}", res.score, res.file_path);
9809 |             }
9810 |         }
9811 |     } else {
9812 |         println!("Usage: flash-search --cli <query> [--json]");
9813 |     }
9814 |     Ok(())
9815 | }
9816 | ```

9822 | 
9823 | ```rust
9824 | #![cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), windows_subsystem = "windows")]
9825 | 
9826 | use mimalloc::MiMalloc;
9827 | use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};
9828 | use tracing::{error, info};
9829 | use tracing_appender::non_blocking::WorkerGuard;
9830 | use tracing_appender::rolling::{RollingFileAppender, Rotation};
9831 | use tracing_subscriber::{fmt, layer::SubscriberExt, util::SubscriberInitExt, EnvFilter};
9832 | 
9833 | #[global_allocator]
9834 | static GLOBAL: MiMalloc = MiMalloc;
9835 | 
9836 | static SHUTDOWN_FLAG: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);
9837 | 
9838 | pub fn is_shutting_down() -> bool {
9839 |     SHUTDOWN_FLAG.load(Ordering::SeqCst)
9840 | }
9841 | 
9842 | fn init_logging() -> WorkerGuard {
9843 |     let log_dir = dirs::data_local_dir()
9844 |         .unwrap_or_else(|| std::path::PathBuf::from("."))
9845 |         .join("com.flashsearch")
9846 |         .join("logs");
9847 | 
9848 |     std::fs::create_dir_all(&log_dir).ok();
9849 | 
9850 |     let file_appender = RollingFileAppender::new(Rotation::DAILY, &log_dir, "flash-search.log");
9851 | 
9852 |     let (non_blocking, guard) = tracing_appender::non_blocking(file_appender);
9853 | 
9854 |     let env_filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env().unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new("info"));
9855 | 
9856 |     tracing_subscriber::registry()
9857 |         .with(env_filter)
9858 |         .with(fmt::layer().with_writer(non_blocking).with_ansi(false))
9859 |         .with(fmt::layer().with_writer(std::io::stderr))
9860 |         .init();
9861 | 
9862 |     info!("Flash Search starting up");
9863 | 
9864 |     // Prune logs older than 30 days
9865 |     prune_old_logs(&log_dir);
9866 | 
9867 |     guard
9868 | }
9869 | 
9870 | fn prune_old_logs(log_dir: &std::path::Path) {
9871 |     if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(log_dir) {
9872 |         let now = std::time::SystemTime::now();
9873 |         let max_age = std::time::Duration::from_secs(30 * 24 * 60 * 60);
9874 | 
9875 |         for entry in entries.flatten() {
9876 |             if let Ok(metadata) = entry.metadata() {
9877 |                 if let Ok(modified) = metadata.modified() {
9878 |                     if let Ok(age) = now.duration_since(modified) {
9879 |                         if age > max_age {
9880 |                             let _ = std::fs::remove_file(entry.path());
9881 |                         }
9882 |                     }
9883 |                 }
9884 |             }
9885 |         }
9886 |     }
9887 | }
9888 | 
9889 | fn spawn_update_checker() {
9890 |     std::thread::spawn(|| {
9891 |         tracing::info!("Checking for updates...");
9892 |         // Placeholder repo config for self_update
9893 |         let result = self_update::backends::github::Update::configure()
9894 |             .repo_owner("example-owner")
9895 |             .repo_name("flash-search")
9896 |             .bin_name("flash-search")
9897 |             .show_download_progress(true)
9898 |             .current_version(env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"))
9899 |             .build();
9900 | 
9901 |         if let Ok(updater) = result {
9902 |             match updater.update() {
9903 |                 Ok(status) => {
9904 |                     if status.updated() {
9905 |                         tracing::info!("Updated to version: {}", status.version());
9906 |                     } else {
9907 |                         tracing::info!("Flash Search is up to date.");
9908 |                     }
9909 |                 }
9910 |                 Err(e) => tracing::warn!("Update check failed: {}", e),
9911 |             }
9912 |         }
9913 |     });
9914 | }
9915 | 
9916 | fn main() {
9917 |     let app_dir = dirs::data_local_dir()
9918 |         .unwrap_or_else(|| std::path::PathBuf::from("."))
9919 |         .join("com.flashsearch");
9920 |     std::fs::create_dir_all(&app_dir).ok();
9921 | 
9922 |     let lock_path = app_dir.join("flashsearch.lock");
9923 |     let lock_file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()
9924 |         .write(true)
9925 |         .create(true)
9926 |         .truncate(true)
9927 |         .open(&lock_path);
9928 | 
9929 |     if let Ok(file) = lock_file {
9930 |         use fs2::FileExt;
9931 |         if file.try_lock_exclusive().is_err() {
9932 |             eprintln!("Flash Search is already running.");
9933 |             std::process::exit(1);
9934 |         }
9935 |         // Leak the file handle so the lock is held for the lifetime of the process
9936 |         std::mem::forget(file);
9937 |     }
9938 | 
9939 |     let _guard = init_logging();
9940 | 
9941 |     spawn_update_checker();
9942 | 
9943 |     let args: Vec<String> = std::env::args().collect();
9944 | 
9945 |     if args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--version" || arg == "-V") {
9946 |         println!("flash-search {}", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"));
9947 |         return;
9948 |     }
9949 | 
9950 |     // We must create a tokio runtime for background tasks (like Watcher), but we CANNOT
9951 |     // use #[tokio::main] because Iced uses winit to hijack the main thread and blocks it.
9952 |     let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()
9953 |         .enable_all()
9954 |         .build()
9955 |         .unwrap();
9956 |     let _rt_guard = rt.enter();
9957 | 
9958 |     if args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--help" || arg == "-h") {
9959 |         println!("Flash Search - Ultrafast local full-text search\n");
9960 |         println!("Usage: flash-search [OPTIONS]");
9961 |         println!("Options:");
9962 |         println!("  -V, --version       Print version");
9963 |         println!("  -h, --help          Print help");
9964 |         println!("  -c, --cli <query>   Perform a search from the command line");
9965 |         println!("  --json              Output CLI search results as JSON");
9966 |         return;
9967 |     }
9968 | 
9969 |     if args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--cli" || arg == "-c") {
9970 |         let query_index = args.iter().position(|a| a == "--cli" || a == "-c").unwrap();
9971 |         let query = args.get(query_index + 1).cloned();
9972 |         let is_json = args.iter().any(|a| a == "--json");
9973 | 
9974 |         rt.block_on(async {
9975 |             if let Err(e) = flash_search::run_cli(query, is_json, None).await {
9976 |                 error!("CLI Error: {}", e);
9977 |             }
9978 |         });
9979 |         return;
9980 |     }
9981 | 
9982 |     ctrlc::set_handler(|| {
9983 |         info!("Shutdown signal received, committing index...");
9984 |         SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
9985 |     })
9986 |     .expect("Error setting Ctrl-C handler");
9987 | 
9988 |     // Iced requires running on the main thread and runs its own executor
9989 |     match flash_search::run_ui() {
9990 |         Ok(_) => {}
9991 |         Err(e) => {
9992 |             error!("Failed to start application: {}", e);
9993 |             std::process::exit(1);
9994 |         }
9995 |     }
9996 | }
9997 | ```

10011 | #[tokio::test]
10012 | async fn test_end_to_end_search() -> Result<()> {
10013 |     let temp_workspace = tempdir()?;
10014 |     let index_dir = temp_workspace.path().join("index");

10027 |         &md_path,
10028 |         "# Notes\n\nSome markdown content with unique keyword: flashsearchintegrationtest",
10029 |     )?;

10036 |     let md_doc = flash_search::parsers::parse_file(&md_path)?;
10037 |

10039 |         .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
10040 |         .unwrap()
10041 |         .as_secs();
10042 | 
10043 |     indexer.add_document(&txt_doc, now, 100)?;
10044 |     indexer.add_document(&md_doc, now, 200)?;
10045 |     indexer.commit()?;
10046 | 
10047 |     std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(500));

10051 |         .await?;

10096 | # Changelog
10097 | 
10098 | All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
10099 | 
10100 | ## [0.2.0] - 2024-03-01
10101 | 
10102 | ### Added
10103 | - Structured logging with tracing and log file rotation
10104 | - CLI `--version` flag
10105 | - Proper error handling instead of panics on startup
10106 | - CI test and clippy checks before build
10107 | 
10108 | ### Fixed
10109 | - Replaced all `expect()`/`unwrap()` with proper error handling
10110 | - Standardized logging with tracing (replaced eprintln!/println!)
10111 | - Pinned litchi git dependency for reproducible builds
10112 | - NSIS uninstall hook now properly removes PATH entry
10113 | 
10114 | ### Changed
10115 | - Updated version from 0.1.0 to 0.2.0
10116 | - Added tracing-appender for log rotation
10117 | 
10118 | ## [0.1.0] - 2024-01-01
10119 | 
10120 | ### Added
10121 | - Initial release
10122 | - Full-text search with Tantivy
10123 | - File metadata database with redb
10124 | - Iced-based UI
10125 | - Multiple file format parsers (PDF, DOCX, EPUB, etc.)
10126 | - File system watcher for live indexing

10134 | ```markdown
10135 | # Contributing to Flash Search
10136 | 
10137 | Thank you for your interest in contributing to Flash Search!
10138 | 
10139 | ## Development Setup
10140 | 
10141 | ```bash
10142 | # Install Rust
10143 | curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
10144 | 
10145 | # Clone and setup
10146 | git clone https://github.com/yourusername/flash-search.git
10147 | cd flash-search
10148 | 
10149 | # Run development server
10150 | cargo run
10151 | ```
10152 | 
10153 | ## Building
10154 | 
10155 | ```bash
10156 | # Debug build
10157 | cargo build
10158 | 
10159 | # Release build
10160 | cargo build --release
10161 | 
10162 | # Run tests
10163 | cargo test
10164 | ```
10165 | 
10166 | ## Code Style
10167 | 
10168 | - Use `cargo fmt` before committing
10169 | - Use `cargo clippy` to catch common mistakes
10170 | - Follow existing code conventions
10171 | 
10172 | ## Pull Request Process
10173 | 
10174 | 1. Fork the repository
10175 | 2. Create a feature branch
10176 | 3. Make your changes
10177 | 4. Run tests and formatting
10178 | 5. Submit a pull request
10179 | 
10180 | ## Reporting Issues
10181 | 
10182 | Please include:
10183 | - Steps to reproduce
10184 | - Expected vs actual behavior
10185 | - System information
10186 | 
10187 | ## License
10188 | 
10189 | By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under GPLv3.
10190 | ```

10201 | ### File: `README.md`

10206 | ```markdown
10207 | # Flash Search
10208 | 
10209 | <p align="center">
10210 |   <img src="assets/logo.png" alt="Flash Search Logo" width="120">
10211 | </p>
10212 | 
10213 | <p align="center">
10214 |   <b>Ultrafast local full-text search with minimal resource footprint</b>
10215 | </p>
10216 | 
10217 | <p align="center">
10218 |   <a href="#features">Features</a> •
10219 |   <a href="#installation">Installation</a> •
10220 |   <a href="#usage">Usage</a> •
10221 |   <a href="#tech-stack">Tech Stack</a> •
10222 |   <a href="#performance">Performance</a> •
10223 |   <a href="#roadmap">Roadmap</a>
10224 | </p>
10225 | 
10226 | <p align="center">
10227 |   <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Rust-000000?style=flat&logo=rust&logoColor=white" alt="Rust">
10228 |   <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Iced-0.14-blue?style=flat&logo=rust&logoColor=white" alt="Iced">
10229 |   <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/License-GPL--3.0-blue.svg" alt="License">
10230 | </p>
10231 | 
10232 | ---
10233 | 
10234 | ## 🚀 Features
10235 | 
10236 | - **⚡ Blazing Fast**: Sub-50ms search results across millions of documents
10237 | - **📂 Filename Search**: Instant filename-only search mode for ultra-fast navigation
10238 | - **💾 Minimal Footprint**: <30MB RAM usage at idle (vs 200MB+ for Electron apps)
10239 | - **📄 Universal Format Support**: PDF, DOCX, XLSX, EPUB, EML, MSG, ZIP, Markdown, Code files, ODT, ODP, ODS
10240 | - **🔍 Full-Text Search**: BM25 scoring, boolean queries, exact phrase matching
10241 | - **📊 Advanced Filters**: Filter by size (`size:>1MB`), extension (`ext:rs`), or path (`path:src`)
10242 | - **🔄 Live Indexing**: Automatic file watching and incremental updates
10243 | - **🎯 Smart Filtering**: .gitignore support, custom exclude patterns
10244 | - **🌙 Native UI**: Beautiful dark/light themes using Iced's high-performance renderer.
10245 | 
10246 | ## 📥 Installation
10247 | 
10248 | ### Prerequisites
10249 | 
10250 | - **Windows**: Windows 10/11
10251 | - **macOS**: macOS 10.15+
10252 | - **Linux**: Vulkan-compatible drivers & development tools (pkg-config, libfontconfig1-dev)
10253 | 
10254 | ### Download
10255 | 
10256 | Download the latest release for your platform from the [Releases](https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll/releases) page.
10257 | 
10258 | ### Build from Source
10259 | 
10260 | ```bash
10261 | # Clone the repository
10262 | git clone https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll.git
10263 | cd FindAll
10264 | 
10265 | # Build the application
10266 | cargo build --release
10267 | 
10268 | # Run in development mode
10269 | cargo run
10270 | ```
10271 | 
10272 | ## 🎮 Usage
10273 | 
10274 | ### First Launch
10275 | 
10276 | 1. **Initial Setup**: Select folders to index on first launch
10277 | 2. **Indexing**: The app will scan and index your files (this may take a few minutes for large directories)
10278 | 3. **Search**: Press `Alt+Space` (or your custom hotkey) to open the search bar from anywhere
10279 | 
10280 | ### Search Syntax
10281 | 
10282 | | Query | Description |
10283 | |-------|-------------|
10284 | | `rust tutorial` | Find documents containing both words |
10285 | | `"exact phrase"` | Find exact phrase matches |
10286 | | `rust OR python` | Boolean OR operator |
10287 | | `code -python` | Exclude documents with "python" |
10288 | | `title:api` | Search only in document titles |
10289 | | `ext:pdf` | Filter by file extension |
10290 | | `path:docs` | Filter by folder path |
10291 | | `size:>5MB` | Filter by file size (KB, MB, GB) |
10292 | 
10293 | ### Keyboard Shortcuts
10294 | 
10295 | | Shortcut | Action |
10296 | |----------|--------|
10297 | | `Alt+Space` | Toggle search window (Global Hotkey, configurable in Settings) |
10298 | | `Ctrl+Enter` | Open selected file |
10299 | | `Ctrl+C` | Copy file path |
10300 | | `Esc` | Close search window |
10301 | | `↑/↓` | Navigate results |
10302 | 
10303 | ## 🏗️ Tech Stack
10304 | 
10305 | ### Core Architecture
10306 | 
10307 | | Component | Technology | Purpose |
10308 | |-----------|------------|---------|
10309 | | **Language** | Rust | Zero-overhead, memory-safe core |
10310 | | **GUI** | Iced | High-performance, cross-platform UI framework (<30MB RAM) |
10311 | | **Search Engine** | Tantivy | Full-text indexing with BM25 scoring |
10312 | | **Metadata DB** | redb | Pure Rust key-value storage |
10313 | | **Concurrency** | Rayon + Tokio | Parallel processing + async I/O |
10314 | 
10315 | ### Supported File Formats
10316 | 
10317 | | Format | Parser | Status |
10318 | |--------|--------|--------|
10319 | | PDF | `pdf-extract` / `lopdf` | ✅ Supported |
10320 | | DOCX | `zip` + `quick-xml` | ✅ Supported |
10321 | | XLSX, XLS, XLSB | `calamine` | ✅ Supported |
10322 | | EPUB, EML, MSG | Native + `zip` | ✅ Supported |
10323 | | ZIP, 7z, RAR | `zip` / `sevenz` | ✅ Supported (ZIP) |
10324 | | TXT, MD, Code | Native Rust | ✅ Supported |
10325 | | ODT, ODP, ODS | `litchi` | ✅ Supported |
10326 | | Images (OCR) | `ocrs` / Tesseract | 🚧 Planned |
10327 | 
10328 | ## 📊 Performance
10329 | 
10330 | Benchmarks on AMD Ryzen 7 5800X with NVMe SSD:
10331 | 
10332 | | Metric | Value |
10333 | |--------|-------|
10334 | | **Index 10,000 PDFs** | ~45 seconds |
10335 | | **Index 100,000 TXT files** | ~12 seconds |
10336 | | **Search latency (p50)** | 12ms |
10337 | | **Search latency (p99)** | 45ms |
10338 | | **Idle RAM usage** | ~25MB |
10339 | | **Peak RAM (indexing)** | ~180MB |
10340 | 
10341 | ### Comparison
10342 | 
10343 | | Feature | Flash Search | AnyTXT | Windows Search | Recoll |
10344 | |---------|-------------|---------|----------------|---------|
10345 | | Startup Time | Instant | ~2s | System | ~3s |
10346 | | RAM Usage | 25MB | 80MB | 150MB+ | 120MB |
10347 | | PDF Search | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ | ✅ |
10348 | | Live Updates | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
10349 | | Cross-Platform | ✅ | ⚠️ | ❌ | ✅ |
10350 | 
10351 | ## 🗺️ Roadmap
10352 | 
10353 | ### Phase 1: Core (Completed ✅)
10354 | - [x] Project setup with Iced
10355 | - [x] Basic file parsers (PDF, DOCX, TXT)
10356 | - [x] Tantivy integration
10357 | - [x] Parallel file scanning
10358 | 
10359 | ### Phase 2: Polish (Completed ✅)
10360 | - [x] Advanced search filters (date, size, type)
10361 | - [x] Search result preview panel
10362 | - [x] Export search results (CSV, JSON)
10363 | - [x] Fast filename-only search & indexing
10364 | - [x] Enhanced indexing telemetry
10365 | 
10366 | ### Phase 3: Advanced Features (In Progress 🚧)
10367 | - [x] Search history and favorites (Pinned files)
10368 | - [ ] Natural language queries
10369 | - [ ] Plugin system for custom parsers
10370 | - [ ] OCR support for images and scanned PDFs
10371 | - [ ] Cloud sync for index
10372 | 
10373 | ### Phase 4: Enterprise (Future 🔮)
10374 | - [ ] Network drive support
10375 | - [ ] Multi-user indexing
10376 | - [ ] Web interface
10377 | - [ ] API for integrations
10378 | 
10379 | ## 🤝 Contributing
10380 | 
10381 | We welcome contributions! Please see our [Contributing Guide](CONTRIBUTING.md) for details.
10382 | 
10383 | ### Development Setup
10384 | 
10385 | ```bash
10386 | # Install Rust
10387 | curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
10388 | 
10389 | # Clone and setup
10390 | git clone https://github.com/SV-stark/FindAll.git
10391 | cd FindAll
10392 | 
10393 | # Run development server
10394 | cargo run
10395 | ```
10396 | 
10397 | ## 📄 License
10398 | 
10399 | This project is licensed under the GNU General Public License v3 - see the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.
10400 | 
10401 | ## 🙏 Acknowledgments
10402 | 
10403 | - [Tantivy](https://github.com/quickwit-oss/tantivy) - The blazing-fast search engine library
10404 | - [Iced](https://iced.rs/) - The cross-platform UI framework for Rust
10405 | - [Redb](https://github.com/cberner/redb) - The pure Rust embedded database

10464 | 
10465 | - Size: 13712 bytes

10467 | 
10468 | ```markdown
10469 | # AGENTS.md - AI Coding Agent Instructions
10470 | 
10471 | ## Project Overview
10472 | 
10473 | **Flash Search** is an ultrafast local full-text search application built purely with Rust. This document provides comprehensive instructions for AI coding agents working on this codebase.
10474 | 
10475 | ## Architecture Overview
10476 | 
10477 | ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
10478 | │                     Flash Search                             │
10479 | ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
10480 | │  UI (Iced)                                                  │
10481 | │  ├── iced_ui/       - High-performance GUI components       │
10482 | │  ├── search.rs      - Search view and logic bindings        │
10483 | │  └── settings.rs    - Settings view and logic bindings      │
10484 | ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
10485 | │  Rust Backend (Glue & Logic)                                │
10486 | │  ├── commands/      - Business logic handlers               │
10487 | │  └── state/         - Application state management          │
10488 | ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
10489 | │  Core Engine                                                │
10490 | │  ├── indexer/       - Tantivy search engine wrapper         │
10491 | │  ├── parsers/       - File format parsers                   │
10492 | │  ├── scanner/       - File system crawler                   │
10493 | │  └── metadata/      - redb database operations              │
10494 | └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
10495 | 
10496 | ## Project Structure
10497 | 
10498 | ```
10499 | flash-search/
10500 | ├── src/
10501 | │   ├── main.rs              # Application entry point
10502 | │   ├── lib.rs               # Library exports
10503 | │   ├── error.rs             # Error types
10504 | │   ├── models.rs            # Data models
10505 | │   ├── settings.rs          # Settings management
10506 | │   ├── commands/            # Business logic (AppState)
10507 | │   ├── iced_ui/             # Iced UI implementation
10508 | │   │   ├── mod.rs           # UI entry and core state
10509 | │   │   ├── search.rs        # Search screen UI
10510 | │   │   └── settings.rs      # Settings screen UI
10511 | │   ├── parsers/
10512 | │   │   ├── mod.rs           # Parser dispatch
10513 | │   │   ├── docx.rs          # DOCX parser
10514 | │   │   └── ...              # Other parsers
10515 | │   ├── indexer/
10516 | │   │   ├── mod.rs           # Indexer module
10517 | │   │   ├── schema.rs        # Tantivy schema
10518 | │   │   └── searcher.rs      # Query execution
10519 | │   └── metadata/
10520 | │       ├── mod.rs           # Metadata DB interface
10521 | │       └── db.rs            # redb definitions
10522 | ├── Cargo.toml               # Project dependencies
10523 | └── README.md
10524 | ```
10525 | 
10526 | ## Critical Implementation Guidelines
10527 | 
10528 | ### 1. Performance First
10529 | 
10530 | **This project prioritizes performance over convenience.**
10531 | 
10532 | - **Memory**: Keep RAM usage under 30MB at idle
10533 | - **Speed**: Search results must return in <50ms
10534 | - **I/O**: Use memory-mapped files for parsing
10535 | - **UI Responsiveness**: Never block the Iced event loop
10536 | 
10537 | **DON'Ts:**
10538 | - Don't perform heavy computations in Iced `update` methods (use `Command::perform` or `tokio::spawn`)
10539 | - Don't load large datasets into the UI at once (use pagination or lazy loading if needed)
10540 | 
10541 | ### 2. Error Handling
10542 | 
10543 | Use `anyhow` for error propagation and `thiserror` for custom error types:
10544 | 
10545 | ```rust
10546 | // Good
10547 | use anyhow::{Context, Result};
10548 | 
10549 | pub fn parse_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
10550 |     let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)
10551 |         .with_context(|| format!("Failed to read file: {}", path.display()))?;
10552 |     Ok(content)
10553 | }
10554 | 
10555 | // Custom errors for specific failure modes
10556 | #[derive(Error, Debug)]
10557 | pub enum ParseError {
10558 |     #[error("Unsupported file format: {0}")]
10559 |     UnsupportedFormat(String),
10560 |     #[error("File corrupted or encrypted")]
10561 |     CorruptedFile,
10562 |     #[error("IO error: {0}")]
10563 |     Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
10564 | }
10565 | ```
10566 | 
10567 | ### 3. Concurrency Model
10568 | 
10569 | ```rust
10570 | // File watching (async I/O) -> Tokio
10571 | // File parsing (CPU-bound) -> Rayon
10572 | // Search queries -> Tantivy (already parallel)
10573 | 
10574 | // Example: Hybrid approach
10575 | use rayon::prelude::*;
10576 | use tokio::task;
10577 | 
10578 | pub async fn index_directory(path: PathBuf) -> Result<()> {
10579 |     // Walk directory (async I/O)
10580 |     let files = walk_directory(&path).await?;
10581 |     
10582 |     // Parse files in parallel (CPU-bound)
10583 |     let results: Vec<_> = files
10584 |         .par_iter()
10585 |         .map(|file| parse_file(file))
10586 |         .collect();
10587 |     
10588 |     Ok(())
10589 | }
10590 | ```
10591 | 
10592 | ### 4. Parser Implementation
10593 | 
10594 | #### DOCX Parser (Critical Performance Path)
10595 | 
10596 | ```rust
10597 | use memmap2::Mmap;
10598 | use quick_xml::events::Event;
10599 | use quick_xml::Reader;
10600 | use zip::ZipArchive;
10601 | 
10602 | pub fn parse_docx(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
10603 |     // Memory map the file
10604 |     let file = std::fs::File::open(path)?;
10605 |     let mmap = unsafe { Mmap::map(&file)? };
10606 |     
10607 |     // Stream from memory
10608 |     let cursor = std::io::Cursor::new(&mmap[..]);
10609 |     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(cursor)?;
10610 |     
10611 |     // Extract document.xml
10612 |     let mut doc_xml = archive.by_name("word/document.xml")?;
10613 |     let mut xml_content = String::new();
10614 |     doc_xml.read_to_string(&mut xml_content)?;
10615 |     
10616 |     // Stream parse XML (NO DOM!)
10617 |     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(&xml_content);
10618 |     let mut buf = Vec::new();
10619 |     let mut text = String::new();
10620 |     
10621 |     loop {
10622 |         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
10623 |             Ok(Event::Start(e)) if e.name().as_ref() == b"w:t" => {
10624 |                 // Extract text content
10625 |                 if let Ok(txt) = reader.read_text(e.name()) {
10626 |                     text.push_str(&txt);
10627 |                     text.push(' ');
10628 |                 }
10629 |             }
10630 |             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
10631 |             Err(e) => return Err(e.into()),
10632 |             _ => (),
10633 |         }
10634 |         buf.clear();
10635 |     }
10636 |     
10637 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
10638 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
10639 |         content: text,
10640 |         title: extract_title(&mut archive)?,
10641 |     })
10642 | }
10643 | ```
10644 | 
10645 | ### 5. Tantivy Schema
10646 | 
10647 | ```rust
10648 | use tantivy::schema::*;
10649 | 
10650 | pub fn create_schema() -> Schema {
10651 |     let mut schema_builder = Schema::builder();
10652 |     
10653 |     // Store file path for retrieval
10654 |     schema_builder.add_text_field(
10655 |         "file_path",
10656 |         STRING | STORED
10657 |     );
10658 |     
10659 |     // Index content but don't store (retrieved from disk on demand)
10660 |     let text_options = TextOptions::default()
10661 |         .set_indexing_options(
10662 |             TextFieldIndexing::default()
10663 |                 .set_tokenizer("default")
10664 |                 .set_index_option(IndexRecordOption::WithFreqsAndPositions)
10665 |         )
10666 |         .set_stored(false);
10667 |     schema_builder.add_text_field("content", text_options);
10668 |     
10669 |     // Store title for display
10670 |     schema_builder.add_text_field(
10671 |         "title",
10672 |         TEXT | STORED
10673 |     );
10674 |     
10675 |     // Index timestamp for sorting
10676 |     schema_builder.add_date_field(
10677 |         "modified",
10678 |         FAST | INDEXED
10679 |     );
10680 |     
10681 |     schema_builder.build()
10682 | }
10683 | ```
10684 | 
10685 | ### 6. Metadata Database (redb)
10686 | 
10687 | ```rust
10688 | use redb::{Database, TableDefinition};
10689 | 
10690 | const FILES_TABLE: TableDefinition<&str, FileMetadata> = TableDefinition::new("files");
10691 | 
10692 | #[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
10693 | pub struct FileMetadata {
10694 |     pub path: String,
10695 |     pub modified: u64,      // Unix timestamp
10696 |     pub size: u64,
10697 |     pub content_hash: [u8; 32], // blake3 hash
10698 |     pub indexed_at: u64,
10699 | }
10700 | 
10701 | pub struct MetadataDb {
10702 |     db: Database,
10703 | }
10704 | 
10705 | impl MetadataDb {
10706 |     pub fn needs_reindex(&self, path: &Path, modified: u64) -> Result<bool> {
10707 |         let txn = self.db.begin_read()?;
10708 |         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE)?;
10709 |         
10710 |         match table.get(path.to_str().unwrap())? {
10711 |             Some(metadata) => {
10712 |                 let meta = metadata.value();
10713 |                 Ok(meta.modified != modified)
10714 |             }
10715 |             None => Ok(true), // File not indexed yet
10716 |         }
10717 |     }
10718 | }
10719 | ```
10720 | 
10721 | ### 7. Iced Integration (UI Glue)
10722 | 
10723 | ```rust
10724 | use iced::{Application, Command, Element};
10725 | 
10726 | pub struct SearchApp {
10727 |     state: Arc<AppState>,
10728 |     search_query: String,
10729 |     results: Vec<FileItem>,
10730 | }
10731 | 
10732 | #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
10733 | pub enum Message {
10734 |     SearchInputChanged(String),
10735 |     PerformSearch,
10736 |     SearchResultsReceived(Vec<FileItem>),
10737 | }
10738 | 
10739 | impl Application for SearchApp {
10740 |     type Message = Message;
10741 |     // ...
10742 | 
10743 |     fn update(&mut self, message: Message) -> Command<Message> {
10744 |         match message {
10745 |             Message::SearchInputChanged(query) => {
10746 |                 self.search_query = query;
10747 |                 Command::none()
10748 |             }
10749 |             Message::PerformSearch => {
10750 |                 let state = self.state.clone();
10751 |                 let query = self.search_query.clone();
10752 |                 
10753 |                 // Offload search to async command
10754 |                 Command::perform(async move {
10755 |                     let results = state.indexer.search(&query).await.unwrap_or_default();
10756 |                     results.into_iter().map(FileItem::from).collect()
10757 |                 }, Message::SearchResultsReceived)
10758 |             }
10759 |             Message::SearchResultsReceived(results) => {
10760 |                 self.results = results;
10761 |                 Command::none()
10762 |             }
10763 |         }
10764 |     }
10765 | 
10766 |     fn view(&self) -> Element<Message> {
10767 |         // ... Iced view layout construction ...
10768 |         iced::widget::text("Search View").into()
10769 |     }
10770 | }
10771 | ```
10772 | 
10773 | ### 8. Iced Component Patterns
10774 | 
10775 | Follow the standard Elm architecture when creating view components in Iced. Keep state isolated where appropriate and fire clear descriptive messages.
10776 | 
10777 | ## Common Tasks
10778 | 
10779 | ### Adding a New File Parser
10780 | 
10781 | 1. Create `src/parsers/<format>.rs`
10782 | 2. Implement `parse_<format>(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument>`
10783 | 3. Register in `src/parsers/mod.rs` dispatch function
10784 | 4. Add MIME type detection
10785 | 5. Write unit tests with sample files
10786 | 
10787 | ### Modifying the Search Schema
10788 | 
10789 | 1. Update `src/indexer/schema.rs`
10790 | 2. **WARNING**: Schema changes require reindexing all documents
10791 | 3. Consider migration strategy for existing users
10792 | 4. Bump index version in constants
10793 | 
10794 | ### Adding a New Iced Message
10795 | 
10796 | 1. Define the message variant in the relevant `Message` enum (e.g., in `src/iced_ui/mod.rs`)
10797 | 2. Handle the message in the `update()` function
10798 | 3. Return an async `Command::perform` for any IO/backend work
10799 | 4. Ensure the view triggers the matching message on user interactions
10800 | 
10801 | ## Testing Guidelines
10802 | 
10803 | ### Unit Tests (Rust)
10804 | 
10805 | ```rust
10806 | #[cfg(test)]
10807 | mod tests {
10808 |     use super::*;
10809 |     
10810 |     #[test]
10811 |     fn test_docx_parsing() {
10812 |         let doc = parse_docx(Path::new("tests/fixtures/sample.docx"))
10813 |             .expect("Should parse DOCX");
10814 |         
10815 |         assert!(doc.content.contains("Hello World"));
10816 |         assert_eq!(doc.title, Some("Sample Document".to_string()));
10817 |     }
10818 | }
10819 | ```
10820 | 
10821 | ### Integration Tests
10822 | 
10823 | - Test with large file collections (10K+ files)
10824 | - Measure memory usage with `valgrind` or `/usr/bin/time -v`
10825 | - Benchmark search latency with `cargo bench`
10826 | 
10827 | ## Build Commands
10828 | 
10829 | ```bash
10830 | # Build and Run
10831 | cargo run
10832 | 
10833 | # Build for production
10834 | cargo build --release
10835 | 
10836 | # Run Rust tests
10837 | cargo test
10838 | ```
10839 | 
10840 | ## Troubleshooting
10841 | 
10842 | ### High Memory Usage
10843 | 
10844 | - Check for memory leaks in parsers (ensure files are closed)
10845 | - Verify Tantivy index writer buffer size
10846 | - Profile with `heaptrack` or `dhat`
10847 | 
10848 | ### Slow Search
10849 | 
10850 | - Verify Tantivy reader is cached (don't recreate)
10851 | - Check if index is memory-mapped
10852 | - Profile with `perf` or `samply`
10853 | 
10854 | ### Parser Failures
10855 | 
10856 | - Check file encoding detection
10857 | - Verify zip/xml parsing handles malformed files gracefully
10858 | - Add more comprehensive error contexts
10859 |

10861 |

10863 | - [Tantivy Documentation](https://docs.rs/tantivy/latest/tantivy/)
10864 | - [Redb Documentation](https://docs.rs/redb/latest/redb/)
10865 | - [Rayon Documentation](https://docs.rs/rayon/latest/rayon/)
10866 |

10869 | For questions about implementation details, refer to:

10871 | - Architecture Decisions: See `docs/adr/` directory
10872 | 
10873 | ---
10874 | 
10875 | **Last Updated**: 2024
10876 | **Version**: 0.1.0
10877 | 
10878 | ## Self-Healing Rules
10879 | - On borrow checker errors: Scan stack trace, add explicit lifetimes ('a), clone owned data where safe, or refactor to Rc<RefCell<T>> / Arc<Mutex<T>> for shared state.
10880 | - Lifetime mismatches: Infer from context (e.g., &self methods live as long as self); prefer owned types or Cow<'a, str> for strings.
10881 | - Async pinning: Detect Pin<Box<dyn Future>> needs; unwrap with .await safely, use tokio::spawn for non-Send futures, or Box::pin(async move {}).
10882 | - Always: Run `cargo check --tests` + `cargo clippy --fix` after edits; iterate until zero warnings. If fails 3x, request human review.
10883 | - Disable browser verification: Set verify_browser: false for CLI/binary focus—no screenshots/UI tests.
10884 | ```

11680 | import os
