14696 | ```rust
14697 | use regex::Regex;
14698 | use std::sync::OnceLock;
14699 | 
14700 | static OPERATOR_REGEX: OnceLock<Regex> = OnceLock::new();
14701 | 
14702 | /// Parsed query with operators and search terms
14703 | #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
14704 | pub struct ParsedQuery {
14705 |     /// The original text query (for Tantivy)
14706 |     pub text_query: String,
14707 |     /// Extension filter (e.g., "pdf", "docx")
14708 |     pub extension: Option<String>,
14709 |     /// Path filter (search in specific path)
14710 |     pub path_filter: Option<String>,
14711 |     /// Title filter
14712 |     pub title_filter: Option<String>,
14713 |     /// Size filters
14714 |     pub min_size: Option<u64>,
14715 |     pub max_size: Option<u64>,
14716 |     /// Whether fuzzy matching is enabled
14717 |     pub fuzzy: bool,
14718 | }
14719 | 
14720 | impl ParsedQuery {
14721 |     pub fn new(query: &str) -> Self {
14722 |         Self::parse(query)
14723 |     }
14724 | 
14725 |     fn parse(input: &str) -> Self {
14726 |         let mut extension = None;
14727 |         let mut path_filter = None;
14728 |         let mut title_filter = None;
14729 |         let mut min_size = None;
14730 |         let mut max_size = None;
14731 |         let fuzzy = true;
14732 | 
14733 |         // Parse operators
14734 |         // ext:pdf, path:docs, title:report, size:>1MB, size:<10MB, exact:"phrase"
14735 |         let operator_regex = OPERATOR_REGEX.get_or_init(|| {
14736 |             Regex::new(
14737 |                 r#"(?i)(ext|path|title|size):(?:([<>]?)(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)(MB|KB|GB|B)?|"([^"]*)"|(\S+))"#,
14738 |             )
14739 |             .unwrap()
14740 |         });
14741 | 
14742 |         let mut remaining = input.to_string();
14743 | 
14744 |         // Process all operators
14745 |         for cap in operator_regex.captures_iter(input) {
14746 |             let operator = cap
14747 |                 .get(1)
14748 |                 .map(|m| m.as_str().to_lowercase())
14749 |                 .unwrap_or_default();
14750 |             let value = cap
14751 |                 .get(5)
14752 |                 .map(|m| m.as_str().to_string()) // Quoted value
14753 |                 .or_else(|| cap.get(6).map(|m| m.as_str().to_string())) // Unquoted value
14754 |                 .unwrap_or_default();
14755 | 
14756 |             match operator.as_str() {
14757 |                 "ext" => {
14758 |                     extension = Some(value.trim_start_matches('.').to_lowercase());
14759 |                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
14760 |                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
14761 |                     }
14762 |                 }
14763 |                 "path" => {
14764 |                     path_filter = Some(value.to_lowercase());
14765 |                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
14766 |                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
14767 |                     }
14768 |                 }
14769 |                 "title" => {
14770 |                     title_filter = Some(value.to_lowercase());
14771 |                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
14772 |                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
14773 |                     }
14774 |                 }
14775 |                 "size" => {
14776 |                     // Handle size operators
14777 |                     if let Some(op) = cap.get(2) {
14778 |                         let op = op.as_str();
14779 |                         if let Some(num_str) = cap.get(3) {
14780 |                             if let Ok(num) = num_str.as_str().parse::<f64>() {
14781 |                                 let multiplier = cap
14782 |                                     .get(4)
14783 |                                     .map(|m| match m.as_str().to_uppercase().as_str() {
14784 |                                         "GB" => 1024 * 1024 * 1024,
14785 |                                         "MB" => 1024 * 1024,
14786 |                                         "KB" => 1024,
14787 |                                         _ => 1,
14788 |                                     })
14789 |                                     .unwrap_or(1);
14790 | 
14791 |                                 let bytes = (num * multiplier as f64) as u64;
14792 |                                 match op {
14793 |                                     ">" => min_size = Some(bytes),
14794 |                                     "<" => max_size = Some(bytes),
14795 |                                     _ => {}
14796 |                                 }
14797 |                             }
14798 |                         }
14799 |                     } else if let Ok(size_val) = value.parse::<u64>() {
14800 |                         // Exact size match (treat as minimum for practical purposes)
14801 |                         min_size = Some(size_val);
14802 |                         max_size = Some(size_val + 1);
14803 |                     }
14804 |                     if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
14805 |                         remaining = remaining.replace(m.as_str(), "");
14806 |                     }
14807 |                 }
14808 |                 _ => {}
14809 |             }
14810 |         }
14811 | 
14812 |         // Clean up remaining text for full-text search
14813 |         let text_query = remaining
14814 |             .split_whitespace()
14815 |             .collect::<Vec<_>>()
14816 |             .join(" ")
14817 |             .trim()
14818 |             .to_string();
14819 | 
14820 |         Self {
14821 |             text_query: if text_query.is_empty() {
14822 |                 "*".to_string()
14823 |             } else {
14824 |                 text_query
14825 |             },
14826 |             extension,
14827 |             path_filter,
14828 |             title_filter,
14829 |             min_size,
14830 |             max_size,
14831 |             fuzzy,
14832 |         }
14833 |     }
14834 | 
14835 |     /// Check if a path matches the extension filter
14836 |     pub fn matches_extension(&self, path: &str) -> bool {
14837 |         if let Some(ref ext) = self.extension {
14838 |             let path_lower = path.to_lowercase();
14839 |             path_lower.ends_with(&format!(".{}", ext))
14840 |         } else {
14841 |             true
14842 |         }
14843 |     }
14844 | 
14845 |     /// Check if a path matches the path filter
14846 |     pub fn matches_path(&self, path: &str) -> bool {
14847 |         if let Some(ref filter) = self.path_filter {
14848 |             path.to_lowercase().contains(filter)
14849 |         } else {
14850 |             true
14851 |         }
14852 |     }
14853 | 
14854 |     /// Check if a title matches the title filter
14855 |     pub fn matches_title(&self, title: Option<&str>) -> bool {
14856 |         if let Some(ref filter) = self.title_filter {
14857 |             if let Some(t) = title {
14858 |                 t.to_lowercase().contains(filter)
14859 |             } else {
14860 |                 false
14861 |             }
14862 |         } else {
14863 |             true
14864 |         }
14865 |     }
14866 | }
14867 | 
14868 | /// Extract search terms for highlighting from a query
14869 | pub fn extract_highlight_terms(query: &str) -> Vec<String> {
14870 |     let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
14871 | 
14872 |     let mut terms: Vec<String> = parsed
14873 |         .text_query
14874 |         .split_whitespace()
14875 |         .filter(|t| *t != "*")
14876 |         .map(|t| t.to_lowercase())
14877 |         .collect();
14878 | 
14879 |     if terms.is_empty() && parsed.text_query == "*" {
14880 |         terms.push("*".to_string());
14881 |     }
14882 | 
14883 |     // Also add title filter terms
14884 |     if let Some(title) = parsed.title_filter {
14885 |         terms.push(title);
14886 |     }
14887 | 
14888 |     terms
14889 | }
14890 | 
14891 | #[cfg(test)]
14892 | mod tests {

14894 |

14897 |         let query = "ext:pdf report";
14898 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
14899 |         assert_eq!(parsed.extension, Some("pdf".to_string()));
14900 |         assert_eq!(parsed.text_query, "report");

14902 | 
14903 |     #[test]

14910 |

14914 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);

14918 | 
14919 |     #[test]
14920 |     fn test_multiple_operators() {
14921 |         let query = "ext:pdf path:reports annual size:<10MB";
14922 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
14923 |         assert_eq!(parsed.extension, Some("pdf".to_string()));
14924 |         assert_eq!(parsed.path_filter, Some("reports".to_string()));
14925 |         assert_eq!(parsed.max_size, Some(10485760));
14926 |         assert_eq!(parsed.text_query, "annual");
14927 |     }
14928 | }

14931 | ### File: `src\indexer\schema.rs`
14932 |

14935 | 
14936 | ```rust
14937 | use tantivy::schema::*;
14938 | 
14939 | /// Create Tantivy schema optimized for file search
14940 | pub fn create_schema() -> Schema {
14941 |     let mut schema_builder = Schema::builder();
14942 | 
14943 |     // File path - stored for retrieval, indexed for exact matches
14944 |     schema_builder.add_text_field("file_path", STRING | STORED);
14945 | 
14946 |     // Content - indexed for search but NOT stored (to save RAM)
14947 |     // We retrieve content from disk on demand
14948 |     let text_options = TextOptions::default().set_indexing_options(
14949 |         TextFieldIndexing::default()
14950 |             .set_tokenizer("default")
14951 |             .set_index_option(IndexRecordOption::WithFreqsAndPositions),
14952 |     );
14953 |     schema_builder.add_text_field("content", text_options);
14954 | 
14955 |     // Title - stored for display, indexed for search
14956 |     schema_builder.add_text_field("title", TEXT | STORED);
14957 | 
14958 |     // Modified timestamp - indexed for sorting
14959 |     schema_builder.add_date_field("modified", FAST | INDEXED);
14960 | 
14961 |     // File size - indexed for range queries
14962 |     schema_builder.add_u64_field("size", FAST | INDEXED);
14963 | 
14964 |     // File extension - indexed as keyword for fast filtering
14965 |     schema_builder.add_text_field("extension", STRING | STORED);
14966 | 
14967 |     schema_builder.build()
14968 | }
14969 | ```

14989 | 
14990 | /// Maximum number of cached query results
14991 | const MAX_CACHE_SIZE: usize = 100;
14992 | /// Cache TTL in seconds
14993 | const CACHE_TTL_SECS: u64 = 30;
14994 | 
14995 | #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone)]
14996 | pub struct SearchResult {

15005 |

15008 | pub struct IndexStatistics {

15013 | 
15014 | /// Cached search result with timestamp

15016 | struct CachedResult {

15018 |     #[allow(dead_code)]

15020 | }
15021 | 
15022 | /// Cache key for search queries
15023 | #[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq)]
15024 | pub(crate) struct CacheKey {
15025 |     query: String,
15026 |     limit: usize,
15027 |     min_size: Option<u64>,
15028 |     max_size: Option<u64>,
15029 |     extensions: Option<Vec<String>>,
15030 | }
15031 | 
15032 | /// LRU-style query result cache using moka + ahash
15033 | pub struct QueryCache {
15034 |     cache: Cache<CacheKey, CachedResult>,
15035 | }
15036 | 
15037 | impl QueryCache {
15038 |     pub fn new() -> Self {
15039 |         Self {
15040 |             cache: Cache::builder()
15041 |                 .max_capacity(MAX_CACHE_SIZE as u64)
15042 |                 .time_to_live(Duration::from_secs(CACHE_TTL_SECS))
15043 |                 .build(),
15044 |         }
15045 |     }
15046 | 
15047 |     pub(crate) fn get(&self, key: &CacheKey) -> Option<Vec<SearchResult>> {
15048 |         self.cache.get(key).map(|cached| cached.results)
15049 |     }
15050 | 
15051 |     pub(crate) fn insert(&self, key: CacheKey, results: Vec<SearchResult>) {
15052 |         self.cache.insert(
15053 |             key,
15054 |             CachedResult {
15055 |                 results,
15056 |                 timestamp: Instant::now(),
15057 |             },
15058 |         );
15059 |     }
15060 | 
15061 |     pub fn invalidate(&self) {
15062 |         self.cache.invalidate_all();
15063 |     }
15064 | }

15068 |         Self::new()
15069 |     }
15070 | }
15071 | 
15072 | /// Manages searching the Tantivy index
15073 | pub struct IndexSearcher {
15074 |     reader: IndexReader,
15075 |     query_parser: QueryParser,
15076 |     // schema: Schema,
15077 |     path_field: Field,
15078 |     title_field: Field,
15079 |     size_field: Field,
15080 |     content_field: Field,
15081 |     extension_field: Field,
15082 |     cache: QueryCache,
15083 |     index_path: std::path::PathBuf,
15084 | }
15085 | 
15086 | impl IndexSearcher {
15087 |     pub fn new(index: &Index, index_path: std::path::PathBuf) -> Result<Self> {
15088 |         let schema = index.schema();
15089 | 
15090 |         // Pre-warm the reader by loading index into memory on startup
15091 |         // This ensures first search is fast (no initial load latency)
15092 |         let reader = index
15093 |             .reader_builder()
15094 |             .reload_policy(ReloadPolicy::OnCommitWithDelay)
15095 |             .try_into()
15096 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::search("create_index_reader", e.to_string()))?;
15097 | 
15098 |         // Pre-warm: load the index reader
15099 |         reader.reload().ok();
15100 | 
15101 |         // Get field references once to avoid repeated lookups
15102 |         let path_field = schema
15103 |             .get_field("file_path")
15104 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("file_path", "Field not found in schema"))?;
15105 |         let title_field = schema
15106 |             .get_field("title")
15107 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("title", "Field not found in schema"))?;
15108 |         let size_field = schema
15109 |             .get_field("size")
15110 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("size", "Field not found in schema"))?;
15111 |         let content_field = schema
15112 |             .get_field("content")
15113 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("content", "Field not found in schema"))?;
15114 |         let extension_field = schema
15115 |             .get_field("extension")
15116 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("extension", "Field not found in schema"))?;
15117 | 
15118 |         // Search across content, title, and file_path fields
15119 |         let default_fields: Vec<Field> = ["content", "title", "file_path"]
15120 |             .iter()
15121 |             .filter_map(|field_name| schema.get_field(field_name).ok())
15122 |             .collect();
15123 | 
15124 |         let query_parser = QueryParser::for_index(index, default_fields);
15125 | 
15126 |         Ok(Self {
15127 |             reader,
15128 |             query_parser,
15129 |             path_field,
15130 |             title_field,
15131 |             size_field,
15132 |             content_field,
15133 |             extension_field,
15134 |             cache: QueryCache::new(),
15135 |             index_path,
15136 |         })
15137 |     }
15138 | 
15139 |     /// Search the index and return top results with optional filters
15140 |     pub async fn search(
15141 |         &self,
15142 |         query: &str,
15143 |         limit: usize,
15144 |         min_size: Option<u64>,
15145 |         max_size: Option<u64>,
15146 |         file_extensions: Option<&[String]>,
15147 |     ) -> Result<Vec<SearchResult>> {
15148 |         use super::query_parser::{extract_highlight_terms, ParsedQuery};
15149 | 
15150 |         // Create cache key
15151 |         let cache_key = CacheKey {
15152 |             query: query.to_string(),
15153 |             limit,
15154 |             min_size,
15155 |             max_size,
15156 |             extensions: file_extensions.map(|e| e.to_vec()),
15157 |         };
15158 | 
15159 |         // Check cache first
15160 |         if let Some(cached) = self.cache.get(&cache_key) {
15161 |             return Ok(cached);
15162 |         }
15163 | 
15164 |         let parsed = ParsedQuery::new(query);
15165 |         let highlight_terms = extract_highlight_terms(query);
15166 | 
15167 |         let searcher = self.reader.searcher();
15168 | 
15169 |         // Build the main query - use fuzzy search for better typo tolerance
15170 |         let text_query = self.build_fuzzy_query(&parsed.text_query)?;
15171 | 
15172 |         // Build query with optional filters
15173 |         let mut combine: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>)> =
15174 |             vec![(Occur::Must, text_query)];
15175 | 
15176 |         // Add size filters
15177 |         if min_size.is_some() || max_size.is_some() {
15178 |             if let Some(min_val) = min_size {
15179 |                 let lower = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, min_val);
15180 |                 let upper = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, u64::MAX);
15181 |                 let range = RangeQuery::new(Bound::Included(lower), Bound::Included(upper));
15182 |                 combine.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(range)));
15183 |             }
15184 | 
15185 |             if let Some(max_val) = max_size {
15186 |                 let lower = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, 0);
15187 |                 let upper = Term::from_field_u64(self.size_field, max_val);
15188 |                 let range = RangeQuery::new(Bound::Included(lower), Bound::Included(upper));
15189 |                 combine.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(range)));
15190 |             }
15191 |         }
15192 | 
15193 |         // Build file extension filter as a boolean query clause
15194 |         if let Some(extensions) = file_extensions {
15195 |             if !extensions.is_empty() {
15196 |                 let extension_queries: Vec<_> = extensions
15197 |                     .iter()
15198 |                     .map(|ext| {
15199 |                         let ext_lower = ext.to_lowercase();
15200 |                         // Use TermQuery for fast extension matching (much faster than RegexQuery)
15201 |                         let term = tantivy::Term::from_field_text(self.extension_field, &ext_lower);
15202 |                         tantivy::query::TermQuery::new(term, IndexRecordOption::Basic)
15203 |                     })
15204 |                     .collect();
15205 | 
15206 |                 if !extension_queries.is_empty() {
15207 |                     let extension_bool_query = tantivy::query::BooleanQuery::new(
15208 |                         extension_queries
15209 |                             .into_iter()
15210 |                             .map(|q| (Occur::Should, Box::new(q) as Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>))
15211 |                             .collect(),
15212 |                     );
15213 |                     combine.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(extension_bool_query)));
15214 |                 }
15215 |             }
15216 |         }
15217 | 
15218 |         let final_query: Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query> = if combine.len() == 1 {
15219 |             combine.remove(0).1
15220 |         } else {
15221 |             Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(combine))
15222 |         };
15223 | 
15224 |         let top_docs = searcher
15225 |             .search(&*final_query, &TopDocs::with_limit(limit))
15226 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::search(query, e.to_string()))?;
15227 | 
15228 |         let mut results = Vec::with_capacity(top_docs.len().min(limit));
15229 | 
15230 |         // Create snippet generator once outside the loop
15231 |         let snippet_generator =
15232 |             SnippetGenerator::create(&searcher, &*final_query, self.content_field)?;
15233 | 
15234 |         for (score, doc_address) in top_docs {
15235 |             let retrieved_doc: TantivyDocument = searcher.doc(doc_address).map_err(|e| {
15236 |                 FlashError::search(query, format!("Failed to retrieve document: {}", e))
15237 |             })?;
15238 | 
15239 |             let file_path = retrieved_doc
15240 |                 .get_first(self.path_field)
15241 |                 .and_then(|f| f.as_str())
15242 |                 .map(|s: &str| s.to_string())
15243 |                 .unwrap_or_default();
15244 | 
15245 |             let title = retrieved_doc
15246 |                 .get_first(self.title_field)
15247 |                 .and_then(|f| f.as_str())
15248 |                 .map(|s: &str| s.to_string());
15249 | 
15250 |             // Generate snippet using the pre-created generator
15251 |             let snippet = snippet_generator.snippet_from_doc(&retrieved_doc);
15252 |             // Get snippet as HTML then strip tags for plain text display
15253 |             let snippet_html = snippet.to_html();
15254 |             let snippet_text = snippet_html
15255 |                 .replace("<b>", "")
15256 |                 .replace("</b>", "")
15257 |                 .replace("&lt;", "<")
15258 |                 .replace("&gt;", ">")
15259 |                 .replace("&amp;", "&");
15260 | 
15261 |             results.push(SearchResult {
15262 |                 file_path,
15263 |                 title,
15264 |                 score,
15265 |                 matched_terms: highlight_terms.clone(),
15266 |                 snippet: Some(snippet_text),
15267 |             });
15268 | 
15269 |             if results.len() >= limit {
15270 |                 break;
15271 |             }
15272 |         }
15273 | 
15274 |         // Cache the results
15275 |         self.cache.insert(cache_key, results.clone());
15276 | 
15277 |         Ok(results)
15278 |     }
15279 | 
15280 |     /// Build a fuzzy query for better typo tolerance
15281 |     fn build_fuzzy_query(&self, text_query: &str) -> Result<Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>> {
15282 |         // Check if it's a phrase query (contains quoted strings)
15283 |         static PHRASE_REGEX: OnceLock<regex::Regex> = OnceLock::new();
15284 |         let phrase_regex = PHRASE_REGEX.get_or_init(|| regex::Regex::new(r#""([^"]+)""#).unwrap());
15285 | 
15286 |         if phrase_regex.is_match(text_query) {
15287 |             // For phrase queries, use the query parser with phrase support
15288 |             return Ok(Box::new(
15289 |                 self.query_parser
15290 |                     .parse_query(text_query)
15291 |                     .map_err(|e| FlashError::search(text_query, e.to_string()))?,
15292 |             ));
15293 |         }
15294 | 
15295 |         // For regular queries, build a fuzzy query with OR for each term
15296 |         let terms: Vec<&str> = text_query.split_whitespace().collect();
15297 | 
15298 |         if terms.is_empty() || (terms.len() == 1 && terms[0] == "*") {
15299 |             // Match all
15300 |             return Ok(Box::new(tantivy::query::AllQuery));
15301 |         }
15302 | 
15303 |         if terms.len() == 1 {
15304 |             // Single term - try exact first, then fuzzy
15305 |             let term_text = terms[0];
15306 |             let term = Term::from_field_text(self.content_field, term_text);
15307 | 
15308 |             // Try exact match first (higher priority)
15309 |             let exact =
15310 |                 tantivy::query::TermQuery::new(term, tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic);
15311 | 
15312 |             // Add fuzzy variant with edit distance of 2
15313 |             let fuzzy_term = Term::from_field_text(self.content_field, term_text);
15314 |             let fuzzy = FuzzyTermQuery::new(fuzzy_term, 2, true);
15315 | 
15316 |             // Combine with OR (exact first)
15317 |             let combined = BooleanQuery::new(vec![
15318 |                 (Occur::Should, Box::new(exact)),
15319 |                 (Occur::Should, Box::new(fuzzy)),
15320 |             ]);
15321 | 
15322 |             Ok(Box::new(combined))
15323 |         } else {
15324 |             // Multiple terms - build fuzzy query for each with AND logic
15325 |             let mut subqueries: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query>)> = Vec::new();
15326 | 
15327 |             for term_text in terms {
15328 |                 let term = Term::from_field_text(self.content_field, term_text);
15329 | 
15330 |                 // Exact term query
15331 |                 let exact = tantivy::query::TermQuery::new(
15332 |                     term.clone(),
15333 |                     tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,
15334 |                 );
15335 | 
15336 |                 // Fuzzy variant
15337 |                 let fuzzy = FuzzyTermQuery::new(term, 2, true);
15338 | 
15339 |                 // Combine exact and fuzzy for this term
15340 |                 let term_query = BooleanQuery::new(vec![
15341 |                     (Occur::Should, Box::new(exact)),
15342 |                     (Occur::Should, Box::new(fuzzy)),
15343 |                 ]);
15344 | 
15345 |                 subqueries.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(term_query)));
15346 |             }
15347 | 
15348 |             Ok(Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(subqueries)))
15349 |         }
15350 |     }
15351 | 
15352 |     /// Invalidate the search cache (call after index updates)
15353 |     pub fn invalidate_cache(&self) {
15354 |         self.cache.invalidate();
15355 |     }
15356 | 
15357 |     /// Get statistics about the index
15358 |     pub fn get_statistics(&self) -> Result<IndexStatistics> {
15359 |         let searcher = self.reader.searcher();
15360 |         let total_docs = searcher.num_docs() as usize;
15361 | 
15362 |         let mut total_size = 0;
15363 |         if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(&self.index_path) {
15364 |             for entry in entries.flatten() {
15365 |                 if let Ok(metadata) = entry.metadata() {
15366 |                     if metadata.is_file() {
15367 |                         total_size += metadata.len();
15368 |                     }
15369 |                 }
15370 |             }
15371 |         }
15372 | 
15373 |         Ok(IndexStatistics {
15374 |             total_documents: total_docs,
15375 |             total_size_bytes: total_size,
15376 |             last_updated: Some(chrono::Local::now().format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S").to_string()),
15377 |         })
15378 |     }
15379 | }
15380 | ```

15387 | ```rust
15388 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
15389 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
15390 | use std::sync::Mutex;
15391 | use tantivy::schema::{Field, Schema};
15392 | use tantivy::{Index, IndexWriter, TantivyDocument};
15393 | use tracing::info;
15394 | 
15395 | /// Manages writing documents to the Tantivy index with batch support
15396 | pub struct IndexWriterManager {
15397 |     writer: Mutex<IndexWriter>,
15398 |     #[allow(dead_code)]
15399 |     schema: Schema,
15400 |     path_field: Field,
15401 |     content_field: Field,
15402 |     title_field: Field,
15403 |     modified_field: Field,
15404 |     size_field: Field,
15405 |     extension_field: Field,
15406 | }
15407 | 
15408 | impl IndexWriterManager {
15409 |     /// Calculate optimal heap size based on system resources
15410 |     /// Returns heap size in bytes (min 32MB, max 256MB)
15411 |     fn calculate_heap_size() -> usize {
15412 |         // Get system memory info (using sysinfo crate would be better, but this is lightweight)
15413 |         let available_memory = std::env::var("FLASH_SEARCH_MEMORY_MB")
15414 |             .ok()
15415 |             .and_then(|s| s.parse::<usize>().ok())
15416 |             .map(|mb| mb * 1_000_000)
15417 |             .unwrap_or_else(|| {
15418 |                 // Default calculation: use 5% of system memory, capped at 256MB
15419 |                 // This is a heuristic - in production, use sysinfo to get actual memory
15420 |                 let mut sys = sysinfo::System::new_all();
15421 |                 sys.refresh_memory();
15422 |                 let system_memory = sys.total_memory() as usize;
15423 | 
15424 |                 (system_memory / 20).clamp(32_000_000, 256_000_000)
15425 |             });
15426 | 
15427 |         available_memory.clamp(32_000_000, 256_000_000)
15428 |     }
15429 | 
15430 |     pub fn new(index: &Index, _memory_limit_mb: u32) -> Result<Self> {
15431 |         let schema = index.schema();
15432 | 
15433 |         // Configure with adaptive heap size based on system resources
15434 |         let heap_size = Self::calculate_heap_size();
15435 |         info!(
15436 |             "Tantivy index writer heap size: {} MB",
15437 |             heap_size / 1_000_000
15438 |         );
15439 | 
15440 |         let writer = index
15441 |             .writer(heap_size)
15442 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to create index writer: {}", e)))?;
15443 | 
15444 |         let path_field = schema
15445 |             .get_field("file_path")
15446 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("file_path", "Field not found in schema"))?;
15447 |         let content_field = schema
15448 |             .get_field("content")
15449 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("content", "Field not found in schema"))?;
15450 |         let title_field = schema
15451 |             .get_field("title")
15452 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("title", "Field not found in schema"))?;
15453 |         let modified_field = schema
15454 |             .get_field("modified")
15455 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("modified", "Field not found in schema"))?;
15456 |         let size_field = schema
15457 |             .get_field("size")
15458 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("size", "Field not found in schema"))?;
15459 |         let extension_field = schema
15460 |             .get_field("extension")
15461 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::index_field("extension", "Field not found in schema"))?;
15462 | 
15463 |         Ok(Self {
15464 |             writer: Mutex::new(writer),
15465 |             schema,
15466 |             path_field,
15467 |             content_field,
15468 |             title_field,
15469 |             modified_field,
15470 |             size_field,
15471 |             extension_field,
15472 |         })
15473 |     }
15474 | 
15475 |     /// Add a single document to the index
15476 |     /// Note: For better performance, use add_documents_batch for multiple docs
15477 |     pub fn add_document(&self, doc: &ParsedDocument, modified: u64, size: u64) -> Result<()> {
15478 |         let tantivy_doc = self.create_tantivy_document(doc, modified, size);
15479 | 
15480 |         let writer = self
15481 |             .writer
15482 |             .lock()
15483 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
15484 | 
15485 |         writer
15486 |             .add_document(tantivy_doc)
15487 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to add document: {}", e)))?;
15488 | 
15489 |         Ok(())
15490 |     }
15491 | 
15492 |     /// Add multiple documents in a single lock acquisition (much more efficient)
15493 |     pub fn add_documents_batch(&self, docs: &[(ParsedDocument, u64, u64)]) -> Result<()> {
15494 |         if docs.is_empty() {
15495 |             return Ok(());
15496 |         }
15497 | 
15498 |         let writer = self
15499 |             .writer
15500 |             .lock()
15501 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
15502 | 
15503 |         for (doc, modified, size) in docs {
15504 |             let tantivy_doc = self.create_tantivy_document(doc, *modified, *size);
15505 |             writer
15506 |                 .add_document(tantivy_doc)
15507 |                 .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to add document: {}", e)))?;
15508 |         }
15509 | 
15510 |         Ok(())
15511 |     }
15512 | 
15513 |     /// Create a Tantivy document from ParsedDocument
15514 |     #[inline]
15515 |     fn create_tantivy_document(
15516 |         &self,
15517 |         doc: &ParsedDocument,
15518 |         modified: u64,
15519 |         size: u64,
15520 |     ) -> TantivyDocument {
15521 |         let mut document = TantivyDocument::default();
15522 | 
15523 |         document.add_text(self.path_field, &doc.path);
15524 |         document.add_text(self.content_field, &doc.content);
15525 | 
15526 |         if let Some(ref title) = doc.title {
15527 |             document.add_text(self.title_field, title);
15528 |         }
15529 | 
15530 |         let modified_date = tantivy::DateTime::from_timestamp_secs(modified as i64);
15531 |         document.add_date(self.modified_field, modified_date);
15532 |         document.add_u64(self.size_field, size);
15533 | 
15534 |         // Index file extension for fast filtering
15535 |         if let Some(ext) = std::path::Path::new(&doc.path)
15536 |             .extension()
15537 |             .and_then(|e| e.to_str())
15538 |         {
15539 |             document.add_text(self.extension_field, ext.to_lowercase());
15540 |         }
15541 | 
15542 |         document
15543 |     }
15544 | 
15545 |     /// Remove a document from the index
15546 |     pub fn remove_document(&self, path: &str) -> Result<()> {
15547 |         let writer = self
15548 |             .writer
15549 |             .lock()
15550 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
15551 | 
15552 |         let term = tantivy::Term::from_field_text(self.path_field, path);
15553 |         writer.delete_term(term);
15554 | 
15555 |         Ok(())
15556 |     }
15557 | 
15558 |     /// Delete all documents from the index
15559 |     pub fn delete_all_documents(&self) -> Result<()> {
15560 |         let writer = self
15561 |             .writer
15562 |             .lock()
15563 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
15564 | 
15565 |         writer
15566 |             .delete_all_documents()
15567 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to delete all documents: {}", e)))?;
15568 | 
15569 |         Ok(())
15570 |     }
15571 | 
15572 |     /// Commit pending changes to disk
15573 |     pub fn commit(&self) -> Result<()> {
15574 |         let mut writer = self
15575 |             .writer
15576 |             .lock()
15577 |             .map_err(|_| FlashError::poisoned_lock("IndexWriter"))?;
15578 | 
15579 |         writer
15580 |             .commit()
15581 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::index(format!("Failed to commit index: {}", e)))?;
15582 | 
15583 |         Ok(())
15584 |     }
15585 | }
15586 | ```

15645 | /// Manages file metadata database using redb
15646 | /// Implements connection pooling pattern for redb (even though it's embedded)
15647 | /// to ensure proper resource management and monitoring
15648 | pub struct MetadataDb {
15649 |     db: Arc<Database>,
15650 |     metrics: Arc<ConnectionMetrics>,
15651 | }
15652 | 
15653 | impl MetadataDb {
15654 |     /// Open or create the metadata database
15655 |     pub fn open(db_path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
15656 |         let db = Arc::new(Database::create(db_path).map_err(|e| {
15657 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15658 |         })?);
15659 | 
15660 |         // Create table if it doesn't exist
15661 |         let txn = db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
15662 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15663 |         })?;
15664 |         {
15665 |             let _table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
15666 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15667 |             })?;
15668 |         }
15669 |         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
15670 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15671 |         })?;
15672 | 
15673 |         Ok(Self {
15674 |             db,
15675 |             metrics: Arc::new(ConnectionMetrics::default()),
15676 |         })
15677 |     }
15678 | 
15679 |     /// Clone with shared state (for multi-threaded access)
15680 |     pub fn clone_for_thread(&self) -> Self {
15681 |         Self {
15682 |             db: Arc::clone(&self.db),
15683 |             metrics: Arc::clone(&self.metrics),
15684 |         }
15685 |     }
15686 | 
15687 |     /// Get current metrics snapshot
15688 |     pub fn get_metrics(&self) -> ConnectionMetricsSnapshot {
15689 |         ConnectionMetricsSnapshot {
15690 |             read_operations: self.metrics.read_operations.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
15691 |             write_operations: self.metrics.write_operations.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
15692 |             bytes_read: self.metrics.bytes_read.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
15693 |             bytes_written: self.metrics.bytes_written.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
15694 |         }
15695 |     }
15696 | 
15697 |     /// Check if file needs reindexing based on modification time and hash
15698 |     pub fn needs_reindex(&self, path: &Path, modified: u64, size: u64) -> Result<bool> {
15699 |         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
15700 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15701 |         })?;
15702 | 
15703 |         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
15704 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15705 |         })?;
15706 | 
15707 |         let path_str = path.to_str().unwrap_or("");
15708 | 
15709 |         let result = match table
15710 |             .get(path_str)
15711 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string()))?
15712 |         {
15713 |             Some(metadata) => {
15714 |                 let bytes = metadata.value();
15715 |                 self.metrics.read_operations.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
15716 |                 self.metrics
15717 |                     .bytes_read
15718 |                     .fetch_add(bytes.len() as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
15719 |                 // Zero-copy read and validate
15720 |                 if let Ok(meta) =
15721 |                     rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
15722 |                 {
15723 |                     meta.modified != modified || meta.size != size
15724 |                 } else {
15725 |                     true // Reindex if validation fails
15726 |                 }
15727 |             }
15728 |             None => true, // File not indexed yet
15729 |         };
15730 | 
15731 |         Ok(result)
15732 |     }
15733 | 
15734 |     /// Update file metadata after indexing
15735 |     pub fn update_metadata(
15736 |         &self,
15737 |         path: &Path,
15738 |         modified: u64,
15739 |         size: u64,
15740 |         content_hash: [u8; 32],
15741 |     ) -> Result<()> {
15742 |         let txn = self.db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
15743 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15744 |         })?;
15745 | 
15746 |         {
15747 |             let mut table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
15748 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15749 |             })?;
15750 | 
15751 |             let metadata = FileMetadata {
15752 |                 path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
15753 |                 modified,
15754 |                 size,
15755 |                 content_hash,
15756 |                 indexed_at: SystemTime::now()
15757 |                     .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
15758 |                     .unwrap_or_default()
15759 |                     .as_secs(),
15760 |             };
15761 | 
15762 |             let bytes = rkyv::to_bytes::<rkyv::rancor::Error>(&metadata).map_err(|e| {
15763 |                 FlashError::database(
15764 |                     "database_operation",
15765 |                     "files_table",
15766 |                     format!("Serialization error: {}", e),
15767 |                 )
15768 |             })?;
15769 | 
15770 |             table
15771 |                 .insert(path.to_str().unwrap_or(""), bytes.as_slice())
15772 |                 .map_err(|e| {
15773 |                     FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15774 |                 })?;
15775 |         }
15776 | 
15777 |         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
15778 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15779 |         })?;
15780 | 
15781 |         Ok(())
15782 |     }
15783 | 
15784 |     /// Remove a file from the metadata database
15785 |     pub fn remove_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<bool> {
15786 |         let txn = self.db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
15787 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15788 |         })?;
15789 | 
15790 |         let existed = {
15791 |             let mut table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
15792 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15793 |             })?;
15794 | 
15795 |             let path_str = path.to_str().unwrap_or("");
15796 |             let removed = table.remove(path_str).map_err(|e| {
15797 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15798 |             })?;
15799 |             removed.is_some()
15800 |         };
15801 | 
15802 |         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
15803 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15804 |         })?;
15805 | 
15806 |         Ok(existed)
15807 |     }
15808 | 
15809 |     /// Clear all metadata (nuke the table)
15810 |     pub fn clear(&self) -> Result<()> {
15811 |         let txn = self.db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
15812 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15813 |         })?;
15814 | 
15815 |         {
15816 |             // Deleting the table is the fastest way to clear it
15817 |             txn.delete_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
15818 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15819 |             })?;
15820 |             // We must recreate it in the same transaction or next open?
15821 |             // Actually, open_table in next usage will recreate it if we create it here?
15822 |             // Safer to just open it again to ensure it exists empty.
15823 |             let _ = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
15824 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15825 |             })?;
15826 |         }
15827 | 
15828 |         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
15829 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15830 |         })?;
15831 | 
15832 |         Ok(())
15833 |     }
15834 | 
15835 |     /// Get metadata for a specific file
15836 |     pub fn get_metadata(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<Option<FileMetadata>> {
15837 |         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
15838 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15839 |         })?;
15840 | 
15841 |         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
15842 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15843 |         })?;
15844 | 
15845 |         let result = match table
15846 |             .get(path.to_str().unwrap_or(""))
15847 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string()))?
15848 |         {
15849 |             Some(metadata) => {
15850 |                 let bytes = metadata.value();
15851 |                 if let Ok(meta) =
15852 |                     rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
15853 |                 {
15854 |                     rkyv::deserialize::<FileMetadata, rkyv::rancor::Error>(meta).ok()
15855 |                 } else {
15856 |                     None
15857 |                 }
15858 |             }
15859 |             None => None,
15860 |         };
15861 | 
15862 |         Ok(result)
15863 |     }
15864 | 
15865 |     /// Batch update metadata for multiple files (much more efficient)
15866 |     /// Updates all files in a single transaction to minimize I/O overhead
15867 |     pub fn batch_update_metadata(
15868 |         &self,
15869 |         entries: &[(String, u64, u64, [u8; 32])], // (path, modified, size, hash)
15870 |     ) -> Result<usize> {
15871 |         if entries.is_empty() {
15872 |             return Ok(0);
15873 |         }
15874 | 
15875 |         let txn = self.db.begin_write().map_err(|e| {
15876 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15877 |         })?;
15878 | 
15879 |         let indexed_at = SystemTime::now()
15880 |             .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
15881 |             .unwrap_or_default()
15882 |             .as_secs();
15883 | 
15884 |         let mut total_bytes_written = 0u64;
15885 | 
15886 |         {
15887 |             let mut table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
15888 |                 FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15889 |             })?;
15890 | 
15891 |             for (path, modified, size, content_hash) in entries {
15892 |                 let metadata = FileMetadata {
15893 |                     path: path.clone(),
15894 |                     modified: *modified,
15895 |                     size: *size,
15896 |                     content_hash: *content_hash,
15897 |                     indexed_at,
15898 |                 };
15899 | 
15900 |                 let bytes = rkyv::to_bytes::<rkyv::rancor::Error>(&metadata).map_err(|e| {
15901 |                     FlashError::database(
15902 |                         "database_operation",
15903 |                         "files_table",
15904 |                         format!("Serialization error: {}", e),
15905 |                     )
15906 |                 })?;
15907 | 
15908 |                 total_bytes_written += bytes.len() as u64;
15909 |                 total_bytes_written += path.len() as u64;
15910 | 
15911 |                 table.insert(path.as_str(), bytes.as_slice()).map_err(|e| {
15912 |                     FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15913 |                 })?;
15914 |             }
15915 |         }
15916 | 
15917 |         txn.commit().map_err(|e| {
15918 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15919 |         })?;
15920 | 
15921 |         // Update metrics
15922 |         self.metrics
15923 |             .write_operations
15924 |             .fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
15925 |         self.metrics
15926 |             .bytes_written
15927 |             .fetch_add(total_bytes_written, Ordering::Relaxed);
15928 | 
15929 |         Ok(entries.len())
15930 |     }
15931 | 
15932 |     /// Batch check which files need reindexing
15933 |     /// Returns a vector of booleans indicating if each file needs reindexing
15934 |     pub fn batch_needs_reindex(
15935 |         &self,
15936 |         entries: &[(String, u64, u64)], // (path, modified, size)
15937 |     ) -> Result<Vec<bool>> {
15938 |         if entries.is_empty() {
15939 |             return Ok(vec![]);
15940 |         }
15941 | 
15942 |         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
15943 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15944 |         })?;
15945 | 
15946 |         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
15947 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15948 |         })?;
15949 | 
15950 |         let results: Vec<bool> = entries
15951 |             .iter()
15952 |             .map(|(path, modified, size)| {
15953 |                 match table.get(path.as_str()) {
15954 |                     Ok(Some(metadata)) => {
15955 |                         let bytes = metadata.value();
15956 |                         if let Ok(meta) =
15957 |                             rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
15958 |                         {
15959 |                             meta.modified != *modified || meta.size != *size
15960 |                         } else {
15961 |                             true
15962 |                         }
15963 |                     }
15964 |                     Ok(None) => true, // File not indexed yet
15965 |                     Err(_) => true,   // Error reading, safer to reindex
15966 |                 }
15967 |             })
15968 |             .collect();
15969 | 
15970 |         Ok(results)
15971 |     }
15972 | 
15973 |     /// Get recently modified files sorted by modification time
15974 |     /// Note: This loads all files into memory. For large datasets, consider using a separate index.
15975 |     pub fn get_recent_files(&self, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<RecentFileEntry>> {
15976 |         let txn = self.db.begin_read().map_err(|e| {
15977 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15978 |         })?;
15979 | 
15980 |         let table = txn.open_table(FILES_TABLE).map_err(|e| {
15981 |             FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string())
15982 |         })?;
15983 | 
15984 |         let mut files: Vec<(String, u64, u64)> = table
15985 |             .iter()
15986 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::database("database_operation", "files_table", e.to_string()))?
15987 |             .filter_map(|entry| {
15988 |                 entry.ok().map(|(k, v)| {
15989 |                     let bytes = v.value();
15990 |                     if let Ok(meta) =
15991 |                         rkyv::access::<rkyv::Archived<FileMetadata>, rkyv::rancor::Error>(bytes)
15992 |                     {
15993 |                         (
15994 |                             k.value().to_string(),
15995 |                             meta.modified.to_native(),
15996 |                             meta.size.to_native(),
15997 |                         )
15998 |                     } else {
15999 |                         (k.value().to_string(), 0, 0)
16000 |                     }
16001 |                 })
16002 |             })
16003 |             .collect();
16004 | 
16005 |         // Use select_nth_unstable for O(n) partial sort instead of O(n log n) full sort
16006 |         if files.len() > limit {
16007 |             // use std::cmp::Ordering;
16008 |             files.select_nth_unstable_by(limit, |a, b| b.1.cmp(&a.1)); // Reverse order for max-heap behavior
16009 |             files.truncate(limit);
16010 |         } else {
16011 |             files.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.cmp(&a.1));
16012 |         }
16013 | 
16014 |         // Convert to the expected format (without titles for now, can be enhanced)
16015 |         Ok(files
16016 |             .into_iter()
16017 |             .map(|(path, modified, size)| (path, None, modified, size))
16018 |             .collect())
16019 |     }
16020 | }
16021 | ```

16104 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
16105 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
16106 | use litchi::Document;
16107 | use std::path::Path;

16109 | const MAX_TEXT_LENGTH: usize = 100 * 1024 * 1024;
16110 | 
16111 | pub fn parse_docx(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
16112 |     let doc = Document::open(path)
16113 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open document: {}", e)))?;
16114 | 
16115 |     let text = doc
16116 |         .text()
16117 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to extract text: {}", e)))?;
16118 | 
16119 |     let content = if text.len() > MAX_TEXT_LENGTH {
16120 |         text[..MAX_TEXT_LENGTH].to_string()
16121 |     } else {
16122 |         text
16123 |     };
16124 | 
16125 |     let title = path.file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string());
16126 | 
16127 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
16128 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
16129 |         content,
16130 |         title,
16131 |     })
16132 | }
16133 |

16148 | ```rust
16149 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
16150 | use crate::parsers::memory_map;
16151 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
16152 | use quick_xml::events::Event;
16153 | use quick_xml::Reader;
16154 | use std::io::Read;
16155 | use std::path::Path;
16156 | use tracing::warn;
16157 | use zip::ZipArchive;
16158 | 
16159 | const MAX_HTML_SIZE: usize = 50 * 1024 * 1024;
16160 | const MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_SIZE: usize = 200 * 1024 * 1024;
16161 | 
16162 | pub fn parse_epub(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
16163 |     let bytes = memory_map::read_file(path)?;
16164 | 
16165 |     let cursor = std::io::Cursor::new(bytes);
16166 |     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(cursor)
16167 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to read EPUB archive: {}", e)))?;
16168 | 
16169 |     let mut combined_text = String::with_capacity(1024 * 1024);
16170 |     let mut total_extracted_size: usize = 0;
16171 | 
16172 |     let file_names: Vec<String> = archive.file_names().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect();
16173 | 
16174 |     for name in file_names {
16175 |         if name.ends_with(".xhtml") || name.ends_with(".html") {
16176 |             match archive.by_name(&name) {
16177 |                 Ok(mut inner_file) => {
16178 |                     if inner_file.size() > MAX_HTML_SIZE as u64 {
16179 |                         warn!(
16180 |                             "Skipping large HTML file {} in EPUB ({} bytes)",
16181 |                             name,
16182 |                             inner_file.size()
16183 |                         );
16184 |                         continue;
16185 |                     }
16186 | 
16187 |                     let mut content = String::with_capacity(inner_file.size() as usize);
16188 | 
16189 |                     match inner_file.read_to_string(&mut content) {
16190 |                         Ok(_) => {
16191 |                             if total_extracted_size + content.len() > MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_SIZE {
16192 |                                 warn!(
16193 |                                     "EPUB {} has exceeded maximum text size limit",
16194 |                                     path.display()
16195 |                                 );
16196 |                                 break;
16197 |                             }
16198 | 
16199 |                             extract_text_from_html(&content, &mut combined_text);
16200 |                             total_extracted_size += content.len();
16201 |                         }
16202 |                         Err(e) => {
16203 |                             warn!("Failed to read {} from EPUB: {}", name, e);
16204 |                         }
16205 |                     }
16206 |                 }
16207 |                 Err(e) => {
16208 |                     warn!("Failed to access {} in EPUB: {}", name, e);
16209 |                 }
16210 |             }
16211 |         }
16212 |     }
16213 | 
16214 |     drop(archive);
16215 | 
16216 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
16217 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
16218 |         content: combined_text.trim().to_string(),
16219 |         title: extract_epub_title(path).ok(),
16220 |     })
16221 | }
16222 | 
16223 | fn extract_text_from_html(html: &str, output: &mut String) {
16224 |     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(html);
16225 |     reader.config_mut().trim_text(true);
16226 | 
16227 |     let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(1024);
16228 | 
16229 |     loop {
16230 |         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
16231 |             Ok(Event::Text(e)) => {
16232 |                 if let Ok(decoded) = e.decode() {
16233 |                     let text = match quick_xml::escape::unescape(&decoded) {
16234 |                         Ok(unescaped) => unescaped.into_owned(),
16235 |                         Err(_) => decoded.into_owned(),
16236 |                     };
16237 |                     output.push_str(&text);
16238 |                     output.push(' ');
16239 |                 }
16240 |             }
16241 |             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
16242 |             Err(e) => {
16243 |                 warn!("HTML parsing error: {}", e);
16244 |                 break;
16245 |             }
16246 |             _ => {}
16247 |         }
16248 |         buf.clear();
16249 |     }
16250 | }
16251 | 
16252 | fn extract_epub_title(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
16253 |     let bytes = memory_map::read_file(path)?;
16254 |     let cursor = std::io::Cursor::new(bytes);
16255 |     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(cursor)
16256 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to read EPUB: {}", e)))?;
16257 | 
16258 |     let op_path = if let Ok(mut container_xml) = archive.by_name("META-INF/container.xml") {
16259 |         let mut content = String::new();
16260 |         if container_xml.read_to_string(&mut content).is_ok() {
16261 |             extract_opf_path(&content)
16262 |         } else {
16263 |             None
16264 |         }
16265 |     } else {
16266 |         None
16267 |     };
16268 | 
16269 |     if let Some(opf_path) = op_path {
16270 |         if let Ok(mut opf_file) = archive.by_name(&opf_path) {
16271 |             let mut opf_content = String::new();
16272 |             if opf_file.read_to_string(&mut opf_content).is_ok() {
16273 |                 if let Some(title) = extract_title_from_opf(&opf_content) {
16274 |                     return Ok(title);
16275 |                 }
16276 |             }
16277 |         }
16278 |     }
16279 | 
16280 |     path.file_stem()
16281 |         .map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string())
16282 |         .ok_or_else(|| FlashError::parse(path, "Could not extract title"))
16283 | }
16284 | 
16285 | fn extract_opf_path(container_xml: &str) -> Option<String> {
16286 |     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(container_xml);
16287 |     let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(512);
16288 | 
16289 |     loop {
16290 |         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
16291 |             Ok(Event::Empty(e)) | Ok(Event::Start(e)) => {
16292 |                 if e.name().as_ref() == b"rootfile" {
16293 |                     for attr in e.attributes().flatten() {
16294 |                         if attr.key.as_ref() == b"full-path" {
16295 |                             if let Ok(path) = std::str::from_utf8(&attr.value) {
16296 |                                 return Some(path.to_string());
16297 |                             }
16298 |                         }
16299 |                     }
16300 |                 }
16301 |             }
16302 |             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
16303 |             _ => {}
16304 |         }
16305 |         buf.clear();
16306 |     }
16307 | 
16308 |     Some("OEBPS/content.opf".to_string())
16309 | }
16310 | 
16311 | fn extract_title_from_opf(opf_content: &str) -> Option<String> {
16312 |     let mut reader = Reader::from_str(opf_content);
16313 |     let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(1024);
16314 |     let mut in_title = false;
16315 | 
16316 |     loop {
16317 |         match reader.read_event_into(&mut buf) {
16318 |             Ok(Event::Start(e)) => {
16319 |                 if e.name().as_ref() == b"dc:title" {
16320 |                     in_title = true;
16321 |                 }
16322 |             }
16323 |             Ok(Event::Text(e)) => {
16324 |                 if in_title {
16325 |                     if let Ok(decoded) = e.decode() {
16326 |                         let text = match quick_xml::escape::unescape(&decoded) {
16327 |                             Ok(unescaped) => unescaped.into_owned(),
16328 |                             Err(_) => decoded.into_owned(),
16329 |                         };
16330 |                         let title = text;
16331 |                         if !title.trim().is_empty() {
16332 |                             return Some(title);
16333 |                         }
16334 |                     }
16335 |                 }
16336 |             }
16337 |             Ok(Event::End(e)) => {
16338 |                 if e.name().as_ref() == b"dc:title" {
16339 |                     in_title = false;
16340 |                 }
16341 |             }
16342 |             Ok(Event::Eof) => break,
16343 |             _ => {}
16344 |         }
16345 |         buf.clear();
16346 |     }
16347 | 
16348 |     None
16349 | }
16350 | ```

16357 | ```rust
16358 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
16359 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
16360 | use calamine::{open_workbook, Reader, Xls, Xlsb, Xlsx};
16361 | use std::path::Path;
16362 | 
16363 | const MAX_CELLS_PER_SHEET: usize = 1_000_000;
16364 | const MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_LENGTH: usize = 50_000_000;
16365 | 
16366 | pub fn parse_excel(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
16367 |     if let Ok(result) = parse_xlsx(path) {
16368 |         return Ok(result);
16369 |     }
16370 | 
16371 |     if let Ok(result) = parse_xlsb(path) {
16372 |         return Ok(result);
16373 |     }
16374 | 
16375 |     if let Ok(result) = parse_xls(path) {
16376 |         return Ok(result);
16377 |     }
16378 | 
16379 |     Err(FlashError::parse(
16380 |         path,
16381 |         format!("Failed to parse Excel file: {}", path.display()),
16382 |     ))
16383 | }
16384 | 
16385 | fn parse_xlsx(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
16386 |     let mut workbook: Xlsx<_> = open_workbook(path)
16387 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open XLSX: {}", e)))?;
16388 | 
16389 |     extract_excel_content(path, &mut workbook)
16390 | }
16391 | 
16392 | fn parse_xlsb(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
16393 |     let mut workbook: Xlsb<_> = open_workbook(path)
16394 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open XLSB: {}", e)))?;
16395 | 
16396 |     extract_excel_content(path, &mut workbook)
16397 | }
16398 | 
16399 | fn parse_xls(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
16400 |     let mut workbook: Xls<_> = open_workbook(path)
16401 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open XLS: {}", e)))?;
16402 | 
16403 |     extract_excel_content(path, &mut workbook)
16404 | }
16405 | 
16406 | fn extract_excel_content<P, RS>(path: &Path, workbook: &mut P) -> Result<ParsedDocument>
16407 | where
16408 |     RS: std::io::Read + std::io::Seek,
16409 |     P: Reader<RS>,
16410 | {
16411 |     let mut combined_text = String::with_capacity(1024 * 1024);
16412 |     let mut total_cells = 0usize;
16413 |     let sheet_names = workbook.sheet_names().to_vec();
16414 | 
16415 |     for sheet_name in &sheet_names {
16416 |         combined_text.push_str("Sheet: ");
16417 |         combined_text.push_str(sheet_name);
16418 |         combined_text.push('\n');
16419 | 
16420 |         if let Ok(range) = workbook.worksheet_range(sheet_name) {
16421 |             for row in range.rows() {
16422 |                 for cell in row {
16423 |                     total_cells += 1;
16424 | 
16425 |                     if total_cells > MAX_CELLS_PER_SHEET {
16426 |                         break;
16427 |                     }
16428 | 
16429 |                     let cell_text = format_cell_value(cell);
16430 |                     if !cell_text.is_empty() {
16431 |                         combined_text.push_str(&cell_text);
16432 |                         combined_text.push(' ');
16433 |                     }
16434 |                 }
16435 |                 combined_text.push('\n');
16436 | 
16437 |                 if combined_text.len() > MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_LENGTH {
16438 |                     break;
16439 |                 }
16440 |             }
16441 |         }
16442 |         combined_text.push('\n');
16443 |     }
16444 | 
16445 |     let title = path.file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string());
16446 | 
16447 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
16448 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
16449 |         content: combined_text.trim().to_string(),
16450 |         title,
16451 |     })
16452 | }
16453 | 
16454 | fn format_cell_value(cell: &calamine::Data) -> String {
16455 |     match cell {
16456 |         calamine::Data::Empty => String::new(),
16457 |         calamine::Data::String(s) => s.clone(),
16458 |         calamine::Data::Float(f) => f.to_string(),
16459 |         calamine::Data::Int(i) => i.to_string(),
16460 |         calamine::Data::Bool(b) => b.to_string(),
16461 |         calamine::Data::DateTime(dt) => dt.to_string(),
16462 |         calamine::Data::Error(e) => format!("#ERROR: {:?}", e),
16463 |         _ => String::new(),
16464 |     }
16465 | }
16466 | 
16467 | #[cfg(test)]
16468 | mod tests {
16469 |     use super::*;
16470 | 
16471 |     #[test]
16472 |     fn test_format_cell_value() {
16473 |         assert_eq!(format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Empty), "");
16474 |         assert_eq!(
16475 |             format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::String("test".to_string())),
16476 |             "test"
16477 |         );
16478 |         assert_eq!(
16479 |             format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Float(std::f64::consts::PI)),
16480 |             "3.141592653589793"
16481 |         );
16482 |         assert_eq!(format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Int(42)), "42");
16483 |         assert_eq!(format_cell_value(&calamine::Data::Bool(true)), "true");
16484 |     }
16485 | 
16486 |     #[test]
16487 |     fn test_parse_excel_corrupted() {
16488 |         use std::fs;
16489 |         use tempfile::tempdir;
16490 | 
16491 |         let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
16492 |         let path = temp.path().join("corrupted.xlsx");
16493 |         fs::write(&path, b"not a valid excel file").unwrap();
16494 | 
16495 |         let result = parse_excel(&path);
16496 |         assert!(result.is_err());
16497 |     }
16498 | }
16499 | ```

16519 | pub fn parse_rtf(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
16520 |     // Litchi integration is currently disabled due to crate API incompatibility
16521 |     /*

16526 |             title: None,
16527 |         }),
16528 |         Err(_) => {}
16529 |     }
16530 |     */
16531 |

16533 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
16534 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),

16536 |         title: None,

16540 | pub fn parse_eml(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
16541 |     let content = memory_map::read_file_as_string(path)?;
16542 | 
16543 |     let mut title = String::new();
16544 |     let mut text = String::new();
16545 |     let mut in_body = false;
16546 | 
16547 |     for line in content.lines() {
16548 |         let line_lower = line.to_lowercase();
16549 | 
16550 |         if line_lower.starts_with("subject:") {
16551 |             title = line[8..].trim().to_string();
16552 |         } else if line_lower.starts_with("from:")
16553 |             || line_lower.starts_with("to:")
16554 |             || line_lower.starts_with("date:")
16555 |         {
16556 |             text.push_str(line);
16557 |             text.push(' ');
16558 |         } else if line_lower.is_empty() {
16559 |             in_body = true;
16560 |         } else if in_body {
16561 |             text.push_str(line);
16562 |             text.push(' ');
16563 |         }
16564 |     }
16565 | 
16566 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
16567 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
16568 |         content: text.trim().to_string(),
16569 |         title: if title.is_empty() { None } else { Some(title) },
16570 |     })
16571 | }
16572 | 
16573 | pub fn parse_msg(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
16574 |     let content = memory_map::read_file(path)?;
16575 | 
16576 |     let mut text = String::new();
16577 |     let mut in_string = false;
16578 |     let mut current = String::new();
16579 | 
16580 |     for byte in content {
16581 |         if byte.is_ascii_graphic() || byte == b' ' || byte == b'\n' {
16582 |             current.push(byte as char);
16583 |             in_string = true;
16584 |         } else if in_string && current.len() > 3 {
16585 |             text.push_str(&current);
16586 |             text.push(' ');
16587 |             current.clear();
16588 |             in_string = false;
16589 |         } else {
16590 |             current.clear();
16591 |             in_string = false;
16592 |         }
16593 |     }
16594 | 
16595 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
16596 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
16597 |         content: text
16598 |             .split_whitespace()
16599 |             .take(5000)
16600 |             .collect::<Vec<_>>()
16601 |             .join(" "),
16602 |         title: None,
16603 |     })
16604 | }
16605 |

16614 |             current.push(byte);
16615 |         } else if current.len() > 4 {
16616 |             if let Ok(s) = String::from_utf8(current.clone()) {
16617 |                 if s.chars().all(|c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c.is_whitespace()) {
16618 |                     text.push_str(&s);

16620 |                 }
16621 |             }
16622 |             current.clear();
16623 |         } else {
16624 |             current.clear();

16639 | pub fn parse_azw(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
16640 |     let content = memory_map::read_file(path)?;
16641 | 
16642 |     let mut text = String::new();
16643 |     let mut current = Vec::new();
16644 | 
16645 |     if content.len() > 68 && (&content[0..4] == b"TPZ" || &content[60..68] == b"BOOKMOBI") {
16646 |         // Kindle format detected
16647 |     }
16648 | 
16649 |     for byte in content {
16650 |         if byte.is_ascii_graphic() || byte == b' ' || byte == b'\n' {
16651 |             current.push(byte);
16652 |         } else if current.len() > 5 {
16653 |             if let Ok(s) = String::from_utf8(current.clone()) {
16654 |                 if s.len() > 5
16655 |                     && s.chars()
16656 |                         .all(|c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c.is_whitespace() || c == '.' || c == ',')
16657 |                 {
16658 |                     text.push_str(&s);
16659 |                     text.push(' ');
16660 |                 }
16661 |             }
16662 |             current.clear();
16663 |         } else {
16664 |             current.clear();
16665 |         }
16666 |     }
16667 | 
16668 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
16669 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
16670 |         content: text
16671 |             .split_whitespace()
16672 |             .take(10000)
16673 |             .collect::<Vec<_>>()
16674 |             .join(" "),
16675 |         title: None,
16676 |     })
16677 | }

16679 | pub fn parse_zip_content(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
16680 |     use zip::ZipArchive;
16681 | 
16682 |     // Use File::open instead of reading entire file into memory (P5)
16683 |     let file = std::fs::File::open(path)
16684 |         .map_err(|e: std::io::Error| FlashError::parse("open_zip", e.to_string()))?;
16685 |     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(std::io::BufReader::new(file))
16686 |         .map_err(|e: zip::result::ZipError| FlashError::parse("open_archive", e.to_string()))?;
16687 | 
16688 |     let mut all_text = String::new();
16689 |     let max_size = 10 * 1024 * 1024; // 10MB limit
16690 | 
16691 |     for i in 0..archive.len() {
16692 |         if let Ok(file) = archive.by_index(i) {
16693 |             if !file.is_dir() {
16694 |                 let name = file.name().to_lowercase();
16695 | 
16696 |                 if let Some(ext) = name.rsplit('.').next() {
16697 |                     if TEXT_EXTENSIONS.contains(ext) {
16698 |                         // Limit read size per file and total
16699 |                         let mut content = String::new();
16700 |                         // Put a cap on individual file read to avoid safe-bomb/DoS
16701 |                         let mut take = file.take(1024 * 1024); // 1MB per file
16702 |                         if take.read_to_string(&mut content).is_ok() {
16703 |                             all_text.push_str(&content);
16704 |                             all_text.push_str("\n\n");
16705 |                         }
16706 |                     }
16707 |                 }
16708 |             }
16709 |         }
16710 |         if all_text.len() >= max_size {
16711 |             break;
16712 |         }
16713 |     }
16714 | 
16715 |     if all_text.is_empty() {
16716 |         // Just return metadata if no text extracted? Or error?
16717 |         // Original returned error, but maybe return empty doc is better?
16718 |         // Let's stick to original behavior or just return empty content.
16719 |         // Returning error might be filtered out as failure.
16720 |         // Let's return empty content if valid zip but no text.
16721 |         // But if it was empty because no text files found, that's fine.
16722 |         // The original error "unsupported_format" is a bit misleading if it IS a zip.
16723 |         // Let's return valid doc with empty content.
16724 |     }
16725 | 
16726 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
16727 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
16728 |         content: all_text,
16729 |         title: None,
16730 |     })
16731 | }
16732 |

16734 |     let metadata = memory_map::get_file_size(path)?;
16735 | 
16736 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
16737 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
16738 |         content: format!("7z archive: {} bytes", metadata),
16739 |         title: None,

16778 | ### File: `src\parsers\memory_map.rs`
16779 |

16782 | 
16783 | ```rust
16784 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
16785 | use memmap2::Mmap;
16786 | use std::fs::File;
16787 | use std::io::Read;
16788 | use std::path::Path;
16789 | 
16790 | const MMAP_THRESHOLD: u64 = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB
16791 | const MAX_FILE_SIZE: u64 = 100 * 1024 * 1024; // 100MB
16792 | 
16793 | pub fn read_file(path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
16794 |     let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
16795 | 
16796 |     let file_size = metadata.len();
16797 | 
16798 |     if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE {
16799 |         return Err(FlashError::parse(
16800 |             path,
16801 |             format!(
16802 |                 "File too large: {} bytes (max: {})",
16803 |                 file_size, MAX_FILE_SIZE
16804 |             ),
16805 |         ));
16806 |     }
16807 | 
16808 |     if file_size > MMAP_THRESHOLD {
16809 |         read_with_mmap(path)
16810 |     } else {
16811 |         read_with_buffer(path)
16812 |     }
16813 | }
16814 | 
16815 | pub fn read_file_as_string(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
16816 |     let bytes = read_file(path)?;
16817 |     String::from_utf8(bytes).map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Invalid UTF-8: {}", e)))
16818 | }
16819 | 
16820 | fn read_with_buffer(path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
16821 |     let mut file = File::open(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
16822 |     let mut bytes = Vec::new();
16823 |     file.read_to_end(&mut bytes).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
16824 |     Ok(bytes)
16825 | }
16826 | 
16827 | fn read_with_mmap(path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
16828 |     let file = File::open(path)
16829 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open file: {}", e)))?;
16830 | 
16831 |     // SAFETY: Memory mapping a file is unsafe because another process could modify it
16832 |     // while we are reading, potentially violating Rust's memory safety guarantees.
16833 |     // For a local desktop search engine, this risk is acceptable and typically results
16834 |     // in a process crash rather than an exploitable vulnerability.
16835 |     let mmap = unsafe { Mmap::map(&file).map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))? };
16836 | 
16837 |     Ok(mmap.to_vec())
16838 | }
16839 | 
16840 | pub fn is_mmap_applicable(path: &Path) -> bool {
16841 |     std::fs::metadata(path)
16842 |         .map(|m| m.len() > MMAP_THRESHOLD)
16843 |         .unwrap_or(false)
16844 | }
16845 | 
16846 | pub fn get_file_size(path: &Path) -> Result<u64> {
16847 |     std::fs::metadata(path)
16848 |         .map(|m| m.len())
16849 |         .map_err(FlashError::Io)
16850 | }
16851 | ```
