16988 |     "zsh" => (),
16989 |     "csh" => (),
16990 |     "awk" => (),
16991 |     "sed" => (),
16992 |     "vim" => (),
16993 |     "vimrc" => (),
16994 |     "gitconfig" => (),
16995 | };
16996 | 
16997 | static OTHER_EXTENSIONS: phf::Map<&'static str, ParserType> = phf_map! {
16998 |     "epub" => ParserType::Epub,
16999 |     "pdf" => ParserType::Pdf,
17000 |     "xlsx" => ParserType::Excel,
17001 |     "xls" => ParserType::Excel,
17002 |     "xlsb" => ParserType::Excel,
17003 |     "rtf" => ParserType::Rtf,
17004 |     "eml" => ParserType::Eml,
17005 |     "msg" => ParserType::Msg,
17006 |     "chm" => ParserType::Chm,
17007 |     "azw" => ParserType::Azw,
17008 |     "azw3" => ParserType::Azw,
17009 |     "mobi" => ParserType::Azw,
17010 |     "zip" => ParserType::Zip,
17011 |     "7z" => ParserType::SevenZ,
17012 |     "rar" => ParserType::Rar,
17013 |     "legacy_office" => ParserType::LegacyOffice,
17014 | };
17015 | 
17016 | #[derive(Clone, Copy)]
17017 | enum ParserType {
17018 |     Docx,
17019 |     Pptx,
17020 |     Odf,
17021 |     Epub,
17022 |     Pdf,
17023 |     Excel,
17024 |     Rtf,
17025 |     Eml,
17026 |     Msg,
17027 |     Chm,
17028 |     Azw,
17029 |     Zip,
17030 |     SevenZ,
17031 |     Rar,
17032 |     LegacyOffice,
17033 |     Text,
17034 | }
17035 | 
17036 | impl ParserType {
17037 |     fn from_extension(ext: &str) -> Option<Self> {
17038 |         let ext_lower = ext.to_lowercase();
17039 |         if DOCX_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
17040 |             Some(ParserType::Docx)
17041 |         } else if PPTX_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
17042 |             Some(ParserType::Pptx)
17043 |         } else if ODF_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
17044 |             Some(ParserType::Odf)
17045 |         } else if TEXT_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
17046 |             Some(ParserType::Text)
17047 |         } else if LEGACY_OFFICE_EXTENSIONS.contains_key(&ext_lower) {
17048 |             Some(ParserType::LegacyOffice)
17049 |         } else {
17050 |             OTHER_EXTENSIONS.get(&ext_lower).copied()
17051 |         }
17052 |     }
17053 | }
17054 | 
17055 | /// Parse file without allocating - uses byte comparison
17056 | #[inline]
17057 | #[allow(dead_code)]
17058 | fn extension_matches(ext: &OsStr, target: &str) -> bool {
17059 |     if let Some(ext_bytes) = ext.to_str().map(|s| s.as_bytes()) {
17060 |         if ext_bytes.len() != target.len() {
17061 |             return false;
17062 |         }
17063 |         ext_bytes
17064 |             .iter()
17065 |             .zip(target.bytes())
17066 |             .all(|(a, b)| a.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&b))
17067 |     } else {
17068 |         false
17069 |     }
17070 | }
17071 | 
17072 | /// Detect file type and route to appropriate parser
17073 | /// Uses phf for O(1) static lookup
17074 | pub fn parse_file(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
17075 |     let extension = path.extension().unwrap_or_default();
17076 | 
17077 |     if let Some(ext_str) = extension.to_str() {
17078 |         let ext_lower = ext_str.to_lowercase();
17079 |         match ParserType::from_extension(&ext_lower) {
17080 |             Some(ParserType::Docx) => return docx::parse_docx(path),
17081 |             Some(ParserType::Pptx) => return pptx::parse_pptx(path),
17082 |             Some(ParserType::Odf) => return odf::parse_odf(path),
17083 |             Some(ParserType::Epub) => return epub::parse_epub(path),
17084 |             Some(ParserType::Pdf) => return pdf::parse_pdf(path),
17085 |             Some(ParserType::Excel) => return excel::parse_excel(path),
17086 |             Some(ParserType::Rtf) => return extended::parse_rtf(path),
17087 |             Some(ParserType::Eml) => return extended::parse_eml(path),
17088 |             Some(ParserType::Msg) => return extended::parse_msg(path),
17089 |             Some(ParserType::Chm) => return extended::parse_chm(path),
17090 |             Some(ParserType::Azw) => return extended::parse_azw(path),
17091 |             Some(ParserType::Zip) => return extended::parse_zip_content(path),
17092 |             Some(ParserType::SevenZ) => return extended::parse_7z_content(path),
17093 |             Some(ParserType::Rar) => return extended::parse_rar_content(path),
17094 |             Some(ParserType::Text) => return text::parse_text(path),
17095 |             Some(ParserType::LegacyOffice) => return extended::parse_legacy_office(path),
17096 |             None => {}
17097 |         }
17098 |     }
17099 | 
17100 |     let ext_str = extension.to_string_lossy().to_string();
17101 |     Err(FlashError::unsupported_format(ext_str.clone(), ext_str))
17102 | }
17103 | 
17104 | #[cfg(test)]
17105 | mod tests {

17108 | 
17109 |     #[test]
17110 |     fn test_extension_matches() {
17111 |         assert!(extension_matches(OsStr::new("docx"), "docx"));
17112 |         assert!(extension_matches(OsStr::new("DOCX"), "docx"));
17113 |         assert!(extension_matches(OsStr::new("Docx"), "docx"));
17114 |         assert!(!extension_matches(OsStr::new("pdf"), "docx"));
17115 |     }
17116 | 
17117 |     #[test]

17122 |         ));
17123 |         assert!(matches!(
17124 |             ParserType::from_extension("TXT"),
17125 |             Some(ParserType::Text)
17126 |         ));
17127 |         assert!(matches!(
17128 |             ParserType::from_extension("rs"),
17129 |             Some(ParserType::Text)
17130 |         ));
17131 |         assert!(matches!(
17132 |             ParserType::from_extension("js"),
17133 |             Some(ParserType::Text)
17134 |         ));
17135 |         assert!(matches!(
17136 |             ParserType::from_extension("docx"),
17137 |             Some(ParserType::Docx)
17138 |         ));
17139 |         assert!(ParserType::from_extension("exe").is_none());

17145 |         if path.exists() {

17158 | ```rust
17159 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
17160 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
17161 | use litchi::odf::Document;
17162 | use std::path::Path;
17163 | 
17164 | const MAX_TEXT_LENGTH: usize = 100 * 1024 * 1024;
17165 | 
17166 | pub fn parse_odf(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
17167 |     let doc = Document::open(path).map_err(|e| {
17168 |         FlashError::parse(
17169 |             path,
17170 |             format!("Failed to open ODF document {}: {}", path.display(), e),
17171 |         )
17172 |     })?;
17173 | 
17174 |     let text = doc.text().map_err(|e| {
17175 |         FlashError::parse(
17176 |             path,
17177 |             format!("Failed to extract text from {}: {}", path.display(), e),
17178 |         )
17179 |     })?;
17180 | 
17181 |     let content = if text.len() > MAX_TEXT_LENGTH {
17182 |         text[..MAX_TEXT_LENGTH].to_string()
17183 |     } else {
17184 |         text
17185 |     };
17186 | 
17187 |     let title = path.file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string());
17188 | 
17189 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
17190 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
17191 |         content,
17192 |         title,
17193 |     })
17194 | }
17195 | 
17196 | #[cfg(test)]
17197 | mod tests {
17198 |     use super::*;
17199 |     use std::fs;
17200 |     use tempfile::tempdir;
17201 | 
17202 |     #[test]
17203 |     fn test_odf_parsing_corrupted() {
17204 |         let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
17205 |         let path = temp.path().join("corrupted.odt");
17206 |         fs::write(&path, b"not a valid odf").unwrap();
17207 | 
17208 |         let result = parse_odf(&path);
17209 |         assert!(result.is_err());
17210 |     }
17211 | }
17212 | ```

17220 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
17221 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
17222 | use pdfium_render::prelude::*;
17223 | use std::path::Path;
17224 | use tracing::warn;
17225 | 
17226 | /// Maximum PDF content size to prevent memory issues
17227 | const MAX_PDF_SIZE: u64 = 500 * 1024 * 1024; // 500MB limit
17228 | 
17229 | /// Parse PDF file using pdf-extract crate
17230 | /// Implements proper resource management and error handling
17231 | pub fn parse_pdf(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
17232 |     // Check file size first to prevent OOM
17233 |     let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path)
17234 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to read PDF metadata: {}", e)))?;
17235 | 
17236 |     if metadata.len() > MAX_PDF_SIZE {
17237 |         return Err(FlashError::parse(
17238 |             path,
17239 |             format!(
17240 |                 "PDF file too large: {} bytes (max: {})",
17241 |                 metadata.len(),
17242 |                 MAX_PDF_SIZE
17243 |             ),
17244 |         ));
17245 |     }
17246 | 
17247 |     let content = match std::panic::catch_unwind(|| -> std::result::Result<String, String> {
17248 |         let bind = Pdfium::bind_to_library(Pdfium::pdfium_platform_library_name_at_path("./"))
17249 |             .or_else(|_| Pdfium::bind_to_system_library());
17250 | 
17251 |         let pdfium = match bind {
17252 |             Ok(b) => Pdfium::new(b),
17253 |             Err(e) => return Err(format!("Failed to bind: {}", e)),
17254 |         };
17255 | 
17256 |         let document = match pdfium.load_pdf_from_file(path, None) {
17257 |             Ok(doc) => doc,
17258 |             Err(e) => return Err(format!("Failed to load: {}", e)),
17259 |         };
17260 | 
17261 |         let mut text = String::new();
17262 |         for page in document.pages().iter() {
17263 |             if let Ok(page_text) = page.text() {
17264 |                 text.push_str(&page_text.all());
17265 |                 text.push('\n');
17266 |             }
17267 |         }
17268 |         Ok(text)
17269 |     }) {
17270 |         Ok(Ok(text)) => text,
17271 |         Ok(Err(e)) => {
17272 |             warn!("Failed to extract PDF text from {}: {}", path.display(), e);
17273 |             String::new()
17274 |         }
17275 |         Err(_) => {
17276 |             warn!(
17277 |                 "PDF extraction panicked for {}. File may be corrupted or encrypted.",
17278 |                 path.display()
17279 |             );
17280 |             String::new()
17281 |         }
17282 |     };
17283 | 
17284 |     // Try to extract title from first line or filename
17285 |     let title = extract_title_from_content(&content)
17286 |         .or_else(|| path.file_stem().map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_string()));
17287 | 
17288 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
17289 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
17290 |         content,
17291 |         title,
17292 |     })
17293 | }
17294 | 
17295 | /// Extract title from first non-empty line of content
17296 | /// Filters out lines that are too long (likely not titles)
17297 | fn extract_title_from_content(content: &str) -> Option<String> {
17298 |     content
17299 |         .lines()
17300 |         .find(|line| {
17301 |             let trimmed = line.trim();
17302 |             !trimmed.is_empty() && trimmed.len() < 200
17303 |         })
17304 |         .map(|line| {
17305 |             let trimmed = line.trim();
17306 |             // Clean up common PDF artifacts
17307 |             trimmed
17308 |                 .replace("\u{0000}", "") // Remove null bytes
17309 |                 .replace("\r", "") // Remove carriage returns
17310 |         })
17311 | }
17312 | 
17313 | #[cfg(test)]

17316 |

17318 |     fn test_extract_title_from_content() {
17319 |         let content = "\n\n  Document Title  \n\nBody content here\n";
17320 |         assert_eq!(
17321 |             extract_title_from_content(content),

17334 |         let content = "a".repeat(300);
17335 |         assert_eq!(extract_title_from_content(&content), None);
17336 |     }

17350 | 
17351 | const MAX_TEXT_LENGTH: usize = 50_000_000;
17352 | 
17353 | pub fn parse_pptx(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
17354 |     let pres = Presentation::open(path).map_err(|e| {
17355 |         FlashError::parse(
17356 |             path,
17357 |             format!("Failed to open presentation {}: {}", path.display(), e),
17358 |         )
17359 |     })?;
17360 | 
17361 |     let text = pres.text().map_err(|e| {
17362 |         FlashError::parse(
17363 |             path,
17364 |             format!("Failed to extract text from {}: {}", path.display(), e),
17365 |         )
17366 |     })?;
17367 | 
17368 |     let content = if text.len() > MAX_TEXT_LENGTH {
17369 |         text[..MAX_TEXT_LENGTH].to_string()
17370 |     } else {
17371 |         text
17372 |     };
17373 | 
17374 |     let slide_count = pres.slide_count().unwrap_or(0);
17375 |     let title = path
17376 |         .file_stem()
17377 |         .map(|s| format!("{} ({} slides)", s.to_string_lossy(), slide_count));
17378 | 
17379 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
17380 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
17381 |         content,
17382 |         title,
17383 |     })
17384 | }

17409 | ```rust
17410 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
17411 | use crate::parsers::ParsedDocument;
17412 | use memmap2::Mmap;
17413 | use std::fs::File;
17414 | use std::io::{BufReader, Read};
17415 | use std::path::Path;
17416 | 
17417 | /// Threshold for using memory mapping (files larger than this will be memory-mapped)
17418 | const MMAP_THRESHOLD: u64 = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB
17419 | /// Maximum file size to parse (prevent DOS)
17420 | const MAX_FILE_SIZE: u64 = 100 * 1024 * 1024; // 100MB
17421 | 
17422 | /// Parse plain text files (TXT, MD, code files)
17423 | /// Uses memory mapping for large files to reduce memory usage
17424 | pub fn parse_text(path: &Path) -> Result<ParsedDocument> {
17425 |     let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
17426 | 
17427 |     let file_size = metadata.len();
17428 | 
17429 |     // Security: skip extremely large files
17430 |     if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE {
17431 |         return Err(FlashError::parse(
17432 |             path,
17433 |             format!(
17434 |                 "File too large: {} bytes (max: {})",
17435 |                 file_size, MAX_FILE_SIZE
17436 |             ),
17437 |         ));
17438 |     }
17439 | 
17440 |     // Choose parsing strategy based on file size
17441 |     let content = if file_size > MMAP_THRESHOLD {
17442 |         parse_with_mmap(path)?
17443 |     } else {
17444 |         parse_with_buffer(path)?
17445 |     };
17446 | 
17447 |     // Extract title from first non-empty line
17448 |     let title = extract_title(&content);
17449 | 
17450 |     Ok(ParsedDocument {
17451 |         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
17452 |         content,
17453 |         title,
17454 |     })
17455 | }
17456 | 
17457 | /// Parse small files using buffered reader (faster for small files)
17458 | fn parse_with_buffer(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
17459 |     let file = File::open(path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
17460 | 
17461 |     let mut content = String::new();
17462 |     BufReader::new(file)
17463 |         .read_to_string(&mut content)
17464 |         .map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
17465 | 
17466 |     Ok(content)
17467 | }
17468 | 
17469 | /// Parse large files using memory mapping (zero-copy, better for large files)
17470 | fn parse_with_mmap(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
17471 |     let file = File::open(path)
17472 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::parse(path, format!("Failed to open file: {}", e)))?;
17473 | 
17474 |     // SAFETY: Memory mapping a file is unsafe because another process could modify it
17475 |     // while we are reading, potentially violating Rust's memory safety guarantees.
17476 |     // We only read the data and accept this risk for performance reasons.
17477 |     let mmap = unsafe { Mmap::map(&file).map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))? };
17478 | 
17479 |     // Use from_utf8_lossy to handle potential invalid UTF-8 sequences without erroring out
17480 |     // and to avoid an explicit intermediate Vec allocation (mmap.to_vec()).
17481 |     // This returns Cow<str>, into_owned() converts to String.
17482 |     Ok(String::from_utf8_lossy(&mmap).into_owned())
17483 | }
17484 | 
17485 | /// Extract title from first non-empty line
17486 | fn extract_title(content: &str) -> Option<String> {
17487 |     content
17488 |         .lines()
17489 |         .find(|line| !line.trim().is_empty())
17490 |         .map(|line| {
17491 |             // Remove markdown heading markers if present
17492 |             let trimmed = line.trim();
17493 |             if let Some(stripped) = trimmed.strip_prefix("# ") {
17494 |                 stripped.to_string()
17495 |             } else if let Some(stripped) = trimmed.strip_prefix("## ") {
17496 |                 stripped.to_string()
17497 |             } else if let Some(stripped) = trimmed.strip_prefix("### ") {
17498 |                 stripped.to_string()
17499 |             } else {
17500 |                 trimmed.to_string()
17501 |             }
17502 |         })
17503 |         .filter(|line| line.len() < 200) // Sanity check: don't use entire paragraphs
17504 | }
17505 |

17511 |

17513 |     fn test_parse_text_file() {
17514 |         let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();
17515 |         writeln!(temp_file, "# My Title").unwrap();
17516 |         writeln!(temp_file, "Some content here").unwrap();

17602 |     ) -> Self {
17603 |         Self {
17604 |             indexer,
17605 |             metadata_db,
17606 |             filename_index,
17607 |             progress_tx,
17608 |             settings,
17609 |         }
17610 |     }
17611 | 
17612 |     #[instrument(skip(self, exclude_patterns), fields(root = %root.display()))]
17613 |     pub async fn scan_directory(&self, root: PathBuf, exclude_patterns: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {
17614 |         info!("Starting directory scan for {}", root.display());
17615 | 
17616 |         // P3/P4: Run blocking WalkBuilder in a separate thread to avoid blocking Tokio runtime
17617 |         // and allow pipelined consumption.
17618 |         let (path_tx, path_rx): (
17619 |             std::sync::mpsc::Sender<PathBuf>,
17620 |             std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<PathBuf>,
17621 |         ) = std::sync::mpsc::channel();
17622 |         let total = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
17623 |         let root_clone = root.clone();
17624 |         let tx_clone = self.progress_tx.clone();
17625 |         let total_clone = total.clone();
17626 | 
17627 |         let walker_handle = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
17628 |             let mut builder = WalkBuilder::new(&root_clone);
17629 |             // ... (keep logic same) ...
17630 | 
17631 |             // Add overrides
17632 |             let mut override_builder = ignore::overrides::OverrideBuilder::new(&root_clone);
17633 |             for pattern in &exclude_patterns {
17634 |                 let ignore_pattern = format!("!{}", pattern);
17635 |                 if let Err(e) = override_builder.add(&ignore_pattern) {
17636 |                     warn!("Invalid exclude pattern '{}': {}", pattern, e);
17637 |                 }
17638 |             }
17639 |             if let Ok(overrides) = override_builder.build() {
17640 |                 builder.overrides(overrides);
17641 |             }
17642 | 
17643 |             builder.follow_links(true).standard_filters(false);
17644 | 
17645 |             // --- Stage 1: Filename Indexing ---
17646 |             info!("Stage 1: Filename Indexing");
17647 |             let builder = builder.build_parallel();
17648 |             builder.run(|| {
17649 |                 let path_tx = path_tx.clone();
17650 |                 let tx = tx_clone.clone();
17651 |                 let total = total_clone.clone();
17652 |                 Box::new(move |entry| {
17653 |                     if let Ok(entry) = entry {
17654 |                         if entry.file_type().map(|ft| ft.is_file()).unwrap_or(false) {
17655 |                             let path = entry.path().to_path_buf();
17656 |                             let _ = path_tx.send(path);
17657 |                             let count = total.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
17658 | 
17659 |                             // Send progress update periodically
17660 |                             if count % 100 == 0 {
17661 |                                 if let Some(tx) = &tx {
17662 |                                     let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
17663 |                                         ptype: ProgressType::Filename,
17664 |                                         current_file: entry
17665 |                                             .file_name()
17666 |                                             .to_string_lossy()
17667 |                                             .to_string(),
17668 |                                         current_folder: "".to_string(),
17669 |                                         processed: count,
17670 |                                         total: 0, // Unknown
17671 |                                         status: "Scanning filenames...".to_string(),
17672 |                                         eta_seconds: 0,
17673 |                                         files_per_second: 0.0,
17674 |                                     });
17675 |                                 }
17676 |                             }
17677 |                         }
17678 |                     }
17679 |                     ignore::WalkState::Continue
17680 |                 })
17681 |             });
17682 | 
17683 |             // Final update for stage 1
17684 |             let final_count = total_clone.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
17685 |             if let Some(tx) = &tx_clone {
17686 |                 let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
17687 |                     ptype: ProgressType::Filename,
17688 |                     current_file: "".to_string(),
17689 |                     current_folder: "".to_string(),
17690 |                     processed: final_count,
17691 |                     total: final_count,
17692 |                     status: "Filename scan complete".to_string(),
17693 |                     eta_seconds: 0,
17694 |                     files_per_second: 0.0,
17695 |                 });
17696 |             }
17697 |         });
17698 | 
17699 |         // --- Stage 2: Content Indexing (Batched) ---
17700 |         // Parse files in parallel via Rayon, collect results via mpsc,
17701 |         // then batch-write to the index and metadata DB.
17702 |         let (task_tx, task_rx) = std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel::<IndexTask>(BATCH_SIZE * 8);
17703 |         let metadata_db_for_parser = self.metadata_db.clone();
17704 |         let metadata_db_for_writer = self.metadata_db.clone();
17705 |         let indexer_clone = self.indexer.clone();
17706 |         let filename_index_clone = self.filename_index.clone();
17707 |         let progress_tx_clone = self.progress_tx.clone();
17708 |         let total_files = total.clone();
17709 | 
17710 |         // Configure Rayon thread pool based on settings limit
17711 |         let threads = self.settings.indexing_threads as usize;
17712 |         let pool = rayon::ThreadPoolBuilder::new()
17713 |             .num_threads(threads)
17714 |             .build()
17715 |             .unwrap_or_else(|_| rayon::ThreadPoolBuilder::new().build().unwrap());
17716 |         let file_size_limit_mb = self.settings.index_file_size_limit_mb;
17717 | 
17718 |         // --- Stage 2a: Parallel parsing (Rayon) → sends IndexTask into channel ---
17719 |         let parser_handle = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
17720 |             info!("Stage 2a: Parallel Parsing ({} threads)", threads);
17721 | 
17722 |             pool.install(|| {
17723 |                 let mut chunk = Vec::with_capacity(200);
17724 |                 for path in path_rx.into_iter() {
17725 |                     chunk.push(path);
17726 |                     if chunk.len() >= 200 {
17727 |                         Scanner::process_chunk(
17728 |                             &chunk,
17729 |                             &metadata_db_for_parser,
17730 |                             file_size_limit_mb,
17731 |                             &task_tx,
17732 |                         );
17733 |                         chunk.clear();
17734 |                     }
17735 |                 }
17736 |                 if !chunk.is_empty() {
17737 |                     Scanner::process_chunk(
17738 |                         &chunk,
17739 |                         &metadata_db_for_parser,
17740 |                         file_size_limit_mb,
17741 |                         &task_tx,
17742 |                     );
17743 |                 }
17744 |             });
17745 |             // task_tx is dropped here, closing the channel
17746 |         });
17747 | 
17748 |         // --- Stage 2b: Sequential batch writer (single thread) ---
17749 |         let writer_handle = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
17750 |             info!("Stage 2b: Batch Writing");
17751 |             let start = Instant::now();
17752 |             let mut doc_batch: Vec<(crate::parsers::ParsedDocument, u64, u64)> =
17753 |                 Vec::with_capacity(BATCH_SIZE);
17754 |             let mut meta_batch: Vec<(String, u64, u64, [u8; 32])> = Vec::with_capacity(BATCH_SIZE);
17755 |             let mut processed: usize = 0;
17756 | 
17757 |             for task in task_rx.iter() {
17758 |                 // Add to filename index
17759 |                 if let Some(f_index) = &filename_index_clone {
17760 |                     let path = std::path::Path::new(&task.doc.path);
17761 |                     if let Some(name) = path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {
17762 |                         let _ = f_index.add_file(&task.doc.path, name);
17763 |                     }
17764 |                 }
17765 | 
17766 |                 // Clone path before moving doc
17767 |                 let doc_path = task.doc.path.clone();
17768 |                 doc_batch.push((task.doc, task.modified, task.size));
17769 |                 meta_batch.push((doc_path, task.modified, task.size, task.content_hash));
17770 |                 processed += 1;
17771 | 
17772 |                 // Flush batch when full
17773 |                 if doc_batch.len() >= BATCH_SIZE {
17774 |                     let _ = indexer_clone.add_documents_batch(&doc_batch);
17775 |                     let _ = indexer_clone.commit();
17776 |                     indexer_clone.invalidate_cache();
17777 |                     let _ = metadata_db_for_writer.batch_update_metadata(&meta_batch);
17778 |                     doc_batch.clear();
17779 |                     meta_batch.clear();
17780 |                 }
17781 | 
17782 |                 // Progress update
17783 |                 if processed % 10 == 0 {
17784 |                     let current_total = total_files.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
17785 |                     let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
17786 |                     let rate = if elapsed > 0.0 {
17787 |                         processed as f64 / elapsed
17788 |                     } else {
17789 |                         0.0
17790 |                     };
17791 |                     if let Some(tx) = &progress_tx_clone {
17792 |                         let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
17793 |                             ptype: ProgressType::Content,
17794 |                             current_file: "".to_string(),
17795 |                             current_folder: "".to_string(),
17796 |                             processed,
17797 |                             total: current_total,
17798 |                             status: "Indexing contents...".to_string(),
17799 |                             eta_seconds: if rate > 0.0 {
17800 |                                 (current_total.saturating_sub(processed) as f64 / rate) as u64
17801 |                             } else {
17802 |                                 0
17803 |                             },
17804 |                             files_per_second: rate,
17805 |                         });
17806 |                     }
17807 |                 }
17808 |             }
17809 | 
17810 |             // Flush remaining items (B1: always commit at end)
17811 |             if !doc_batch.is_empty() {
17812 |                 let _ = indexer_clone.add_documents_batch(&doc_batch);
17813 |                 let _ = indexer_clone.commit();
17814 |                 indexer_clone.invalidate_cache();
17815 |                 let _ = metadata_db_for_writer.batch_update_metadata(&meta_batch);
17816 |             }
17817 | 
17818 |             // Final progress
17819 |             if let Some(tx) = &progress_tx_clone {
17820 |                 let _ = tx.try_send(ProgressEvent {
17821 |                     ptype: ProgressType::Content,
17822 |                     current_file: "".to_string(),
17823 |                     current_folder: "".to_string(),
17824 |                     processed,
17825 |                     total: processed,
17826 |                     status: "All files indexed".to_string(),
17827 |                     eta_seconds: 0,
17828 |                     files_per_second: 0.0,
17829 |                 });
17830 |             }
17831 | 
17832 |             info!(
17833 |                 "Indexed {} files in {:.2}s",
17834 |                 processed,
17835 |                 start.elapsed().as_secs_f64()
17836 |             );
17837 |         });
17838 | 
17839 |         // Wait for all stages to complete
17840 |         walker_handle
17841 |             .await
17842 |             .map_err(|e| crate::error::FlashError::index(format!("Walk task failed: {}", e)))?;
17843 |         parser_handle
17844 |             .await
17845 |             .map_err(|e| crate::error::FlashError::index(format!("Parse task failed: {}", e)))?;
17846 |         writer_handle
17847 |             .await
17848 |             .map_err(|e| crate::error::FlashError::index(format!("Write task failed: {}", e)))?;
17849 | 
17850 |         // Commit filename index to disk
17851 |         if let Some(f_index) = &self.filename_index {
17852 |             let _ = f_index.commit();
17853 |         }
17854 | 
17855 |         Ok(())
17856 |     }
17857 | 
17858 |     fn process_chunk(
17859 |         chunk: &[PathBuf],
17860 |         metadata_db: &Arc<MetadataDb>,
17861 |         file_size_limit_mb: u32,
17862 |         task_tx: &std::sync::mpsc::SyncSender<IndexTask>,
17863 |     ) {
17864 |         let limit_bytes = (file_size_limit_mb as u64) * 1024 * 1024;
17865 |         let mut batch_entries = Vec::with_capacity(chunk.len());
17866 |         let mut valid_paths = Vec::with_capacity(chunk.len());
17867 | 
17868 |         for path in chunk {
17869 |             let metadata = match std::fs::metadata(path) {
17870 |                 Ok(m) => m,
17871 |                 Err(_) => continue,
17872 |             };
17873 | 
17874 |             let size = metadata.len();
17875 |             if size > limit_bytes {
17876 |                 warn!(
17877 |                     "Skipping large file: {} ({} bytes > limit of {} bytes)",
17878 |                     path.display(),
17879 |                     size,
17880 |                     limit_bytes
17881 |                 );
17882 |                 continue;
17883 |             }
17884 | 
17885 |             let modified = metadata
17886 |                 .modified()
17887 |                 .unwrap_or(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
17888 |                 .duration_since(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
17889 |                 .unwrap_or_default()
17890 |                 .as_secs();
17891 | 
17892 |             batch_entries.push((path.to_string_lossy().to_string(), modified, size));
17893 |             valid_paths.push((path.clone(), modified, size));
17894 |         }
17895 | 
17896 |         if batch_entries.is_empty() {
17897 |             return;
17898 |         }
17899 | 
17900 |         let needs_reindex = metadata_db
17901 |             .batch_needs_reindex(&batch_entries)
17902 |             .unwrap_or_else(|_| vec![true; batch_entries.len()]);
17903 | 
17904 |         let paths_to_parse: Vec<_> = valid_paths
17905 |             .into_iter()
17906 |             .enumerate()
17907 |             .filter(|(i, _)| needs_reindex[*i])
17908 |             .map(|(_, data)| data)
17909 |             .collect();
17910 | 
17911 |         paths_to_parse
17912 |             .into_par_iter()
17913 |             .for_each(|(path, modified, size)| {
17914 |                 if let Ok(parsed) = parse_file(&path) {
17915 |                     let content_hash = blake3::hash(parsed.content.as_bytes()).into();
17916 |                     let _ = task_tx.send(IndexTask {
17917 |                         doc: parsed,
17918 |                         modified,
17919 |                         size,
17920 |                         content_hash,
17921 |                     });
17922 |                 } else {
17923 |                     warn!("Failed to parse file {:?}", path);
17924 |                 }
17925 |             });
17926 |     }
17927 | }
17928 | ```

18120 | pub struct SettingsManager {
18121 |     path: PathBuf,
18122 | }
18123 | 
18124 | impl SettingsManager {
18125 |     pub fn new(app_data_dir: &Path) -> Self {
18126 |         Self {
18127 |             path: app_data_dir.join("settings.json"),
18128 |         }
18129 |     }
18130 | 
18131 |     pub fn load(&self) -> Result<AppSettings> {
18132 |         if !self.path.exists() {
18133 |             let defaults = AppSettings::default();
18134 |             self.save(&defaults)?;
18135 |             return Ok(defaults);
18136 |         }
18137 | 
18138 |         let content = fs::read_to_string(&self.path).map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
18139 | 
18140 |         serde_json::from_str(&content)
18141 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("parse_settings", e.to_string()))
18142 |     }
18143 | 
18144 |     pub fn save(&self, settings: &AppSettings) -> Result<()> {
18145 |         let content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(settings)
18146 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("serialize_settings", e.to_string()))?;
18147 | 
18148 |         fs::write(&self.path, content).map_err(FlashError::Io)
18149 |     }
18150 | }

18157 |     #[test]
18158 |     fn test_settings_save_load() {
18159 |         let temp_dir = tempdir().unwrap();
18160 |         let manager = SettingsManager::new(temp_dir.path());
18161 | 
18162 |         let settings = AppSettings {
18163 |             max_results: 100,
18164 |             theme: Theme::Dark,
18165 |             ..Default::default()
18166 |         };
18167 | 
18168 |         manager.save(&settings).unwrap();
18169 |         let loaded = manager.load().unwrap();
18170 |         assert_eq!(loaded.max_results, 100);
18171 |         assert!(matches!(loaded.theme, Theme::Dark));
18172 |     }

18206 |             "show_preview_panel": true,
18207 |             "context_menu_enabled": false,
18208 |             "global_hotkey": "Alt+Space",
18209 |             "indexing_threads": 4,
18210 |             "memory_limit_mb": 512,
18211 |             "pinned_files": []
18212 |         }"#;
18213 | 
18214 |         fs::write(manager.path.clone(), json_with_unknown).unwrap();
18215 | 
18216 |         let loaded = manager.load().expect("Should tolerate unknown fields");
18217 |         assert_eq!(loaded.max_results, 150);
18218 |         assert!(matches!(loaded.theme, Theme::Dark));
18219 |     }
18220 | }
18221 | ```

18232 | #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]

18237 | 
18238 | #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
18239 | pub fn register_context_menu(enable: bool) -> Result<()> {
18240 |     use std::env;
18241 |     use winreg::enums::*;
18242 |     use winreg::RegKey;
18243 | 
18244 |     let hkcu = RegKey::predef(HKEY_CURRENT_USER);
18245 |     let path = r#"Software\Classes\Directory\shell\FlashSearch"#;
18246 | 
18247 |     if enable {
18248 |         let (key, _) = hkcu
18249 |             .create_subkey(path)
18250 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
18251 | 
18252 |         key.set_value("", &"Search with Flash Search")
18253 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
18254 | 
18255 |         let icon_path = env::current_exe().unwrap_or_default();
18256 |         key.set_value("Icon", &icon_path.to_str().unwrap_or_default())
18257 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
18258 | 
18259 |         let (command_key, _) = key
18260 |             .create_subkey("command")
18261 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
18262 | 
18263 |         let command = format!("\"{}\" \"%%1\"", icon_path.to_str().unwrap_or_default());
18264 |         command_key
18265 |             .set_value("", &command)
18266 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("context_menu", e.to_string()))?;
18267 |     } else {
18268 |         let _ = hkcu.delete_subkey_all(path);
18269 |     }
18270 |     Ok(())
18271 | }
18272 | ```

18294 | 
18295 | pub fn set_auto_start(enable: bool) -> Result<()> {
18296 |     let app_path = env::current_exe().map_err(FlashError::Io)?;
18297 |     let app_name = "com.flashsearch";
18298 | 
18299 |     let auto = AutoLaunchBuilder::new()
18300 |         .set_app_name(app_name)
18301 |         .set_app_path(app_path.to_str().unwrap_or_default())
18302 |         .set_macos_launch_mode(auto_launch::MacOSLaunchMode::LaunchAgent)
18303 |         .build()
18304 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("auto_start", e.to_string()))?;
18305 | 
18306 |     if enable {
18307 |         auto.enable()
18308 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("auto_start_enable", e.to_string()))?;
18309 |     } else if auto.is_enabled().unwrap_or(false) {
18310 |         auto.disable()
18311 |             .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("auto_start_disable", e.to_string()))?;
18312 |     }
18313 | 
18314 |     Ok(())
18315 | }
18316 | ```

18328 | 
18329 | pub fn create_tray_icon() -> Result<TrayIcon> {
18330 |     let icon_data = include_bytes!("../../assets/logo.png");
18331 |     let image = image::load_from_memory_with_format(icon_data, ImageFormat::Png)
18332 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("tray_icon", e.to_string()))?
18333 |         .into_rgba8();
18334 |     let (width, height) = image.dimensions();
18335 |     let rgba = image.into_raw();
18336 |     let icon = Icon::from_rgba(rgba, width, height)
18337 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("tray_icon", e.to_string()))?;
18338 | 
18339 |     let tray_menu = Menu::new();
18340 |     let show_i = MenuItem::with_id("show", "Show Flash Search", true, None);
18341 |     let quit_i = MenuItem::with_id("quit", "Quit", true, None);
18342 | 
18343 |     let _ = tray_menu.append(&show_i);
18344 |     let _ = tray_menu.append(&PredefinedMenuItem::separator());
18345 |     let _ = tray_menu.append(&quit_i);
18346 | 
18347 |     let tray_icon = TrayIconBuilder::new()
18348 |         .with_id("com.flashsearch")
18349 |         .with_menu(Box::new(tray_menu))
18350 |         .with_tooltip("Flash Search")
18351 |         .with_icon(icon)
18352 |         .build()
18353 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::config("tray_icon", e.to_string()))?;
18354 | 
18355 |     Ok(tray_icon)
18356 | }
18357 | ```

18359 | ### File: `src\watcher.rs`
18360 | 
18361 | - Size: 9723 bytes
18362 | - Modified: SystemTime { intervals: 134168942588200663 }
18363 | 
18364 | ```rust
18365 | use crate::error::{FlashError, Result};
18366 | use crate::indexer::IndexManager;
18367 | use crate::metadata::MetadataDb;
18368 | use crate::parsers::parse_file;
18369 | use blake3;
18370 | use notify::{Event, EventKind, RecommendedWatcher, RecursiveMode, Watcher};
18371 | use std::collections::HashMap;
18372 | use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
18373 | use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
18374 | use std::time::Duration;
18375 | use tracing::{error, info, warn};
18376 | 
18377 | #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
18378 | enum WatcherAction {
18379 |     Index,
18380 |     Remove,
18381 | }
18382 | 
18383 | /// Manages active file system watching with debouncing
18384 | pub struct WatcherManager {
18385 |     watcher: Option<RecommendedWatcher>,
18386 |     _indexer: Arc<IndexManager>,
18387 |     _metadata_db: Arc<MetadataDb>,
18388 |     _runtime_handle: tokio::runtime::Handle,
18389 |     // Buffer for pending events: Map<Path, Action>
18390 |     // WARNING: Do not hold this Mutex across .await points to avoid deadlocks.
18391 |     event_buffer: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<PathBuf, WatcherAction>>>,
18392 | }
18393 | 
18394 | impl WatcherManager {
18395 |     pub fn new(indexer: Arc<IndexManager>, metadata_db: Arc<MetadataDb>) -> Self {
18396 |         let buffer: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<PathBuf, WatcherAction>>> =
18397 |             Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new()));
18398 |         let buffer_for_task = buffer.clone();
18399 |         let indexer_for_task = indexer.clone();
18400 |         let metadata_db_for_task = metadata_db.clone();
18401 |         let runtime_handle = tokio::runtime::Handle::current();
18402 | 
18403 |         // Spawn background processor for debounced events
18404 |         runtime_handle.spawn(async move {
18405 |             loop {
18406 |                 tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(1000)).await; // Check every 1s
18407 | 
18408 |                 let events = {
18409 |                     let mut guard = match buffer_for_task.lock() {
18410 |                         Ok(g) => g,
18411 |                         Err(e) => {
18412 |                             error!("Watcher lock poisoned: {}", e);
18413 |                             continue;
18414 |                         }
18415 |                     };
18416 |                     if guard.is_empty() {
18417 |                         continue;
18418 |                     }
18419 |                     // Take all events
18420 |                     let events: HashMap<PathBuf, WatcherAction> = std::mem::take(&mut *guard);
18421 |                     events
18422 |                 };
18423 | 
18424 |                 let mut needs_commit = false;
18425 | 
18426 |                 // First pass: collect all paths that need to be removed
18427 |                 let remove_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = events
18428 |                     .iter()
18429 |                     .filter(|(_, action)| matches!(action, WatcherAction::Remove))
18430 |                     .map(|(path, _)| path.clone())
18431 |                     .collect();
18432 | 
18433 |                 // Second pass: collect all paths that need to be indexed
18434 |                 let index_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = events
18435 |                     .iter()
18436 |                     .filter(|(_, action)| matches!(action, WatcherAction::Index))
18437 |                     .map(|(path, _)| path.clone())
18438 |                     .collect();
18439 | 
18440 |                 // Process removes first
18441 |                 for path in remove_paths {
18442 |                     let path_str = path.to_string_lossy();
18443 |                     let _ = indexer_for_task.remove_document(&path_str);
18444 |                     match metadata_db_for_task.remove_file(&path) {
18445 |                         Ok(true) => {
18446 |                             needs_commit = true;
18447 |                             info!("Removed file (watcher): {:?}", path);
18448 |                         }
18449 |                         Err(e) => error!("Watcher error removing {:?}: {}", path, e),
18450 |                         _ => {}
18451 |                     }
18452 |                 }
18453 | 
18454 |                 // Then process indexes
18455 |                 let mut docs_to_add = Vec::new();
18456 |                 let mut meta_to_update = Vec::new();
18457 | 
18458 |                 for path in index_paths {
18459 |                     match Self::reindex_single_file(&path, &metadata_db_for_task).await {
18460 |                         Ok(Some((doc, modified, size, hash))) => {
18461 |                             meta_to_update.push((doc.path.clone(), modified, size, hash));
18462 |                             docs_to_add.push((doc, modified, size));
18463 |                         }
18464 |                         Ok(None) => {} // Skipped or does not exist
18465 |                         Err(e) => {
18466 |                             error!("Watcher error indexing {:?}: {}", path, e);
18467 |                         }
18468 |                     }
18469 |                 }
18470 | 
18471 |                 if !docs_to_add.is_empty() {
18472 |                     if let Err(e) = indexer_for_task.add_documents_batch(&docs_to_add) {
18473 |                         error!("Watcher error batch adding to index: {}", e);
18474 |                     }
18475 |                     if let Err(e) = metadata_db_for_task.batch_update_metadata(&meta_to_update) {
18476 |                         error!("Watcher error batch updating DB: {}", e);
18477 |                     } else {
18478 |                         info!("Re-indexed {} files (watcher)", docs_to_add.len());
18479 |                     }
18480 |                     needs_commit = true;
18481 |                 }
18482 | 
18483 |                 if needs_commit {
18484 |                     if let Err(e) = indexer_for_task.commit() {
18485 |                         error!("Watcher failed to commit index: {}", e);
18486 |                     } else {
18487 |                         indexer_for_task.invalidate_cache();
18488 |                         info!("Watcher committed changes");
18489 |                     }
18490 |                 }
18491 |             }
18492 |         });
18493 | 
18494 |         Self {
18495 |             watcher: None,
18496 |             _indexer: indexer,
18497 |             _metadata_db: metadata_db,
18498 |             _runtime_handle: runtime_handle,
18499 |             event_buffer: buffer,
18500 |         }
18501 |     }
18502 | 
18503 |     /// Update the list of watched directories
18504 |     pub fn update_watch_list(&mut self, dirs: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {
18505 |         self.watcher = None;
18506 | 
18507 |         if dirs.is_empty() {
18508 |             return Ok(());
18509 |         }
18510 | 
18511 |         let buffer_clone = self.event_buffer.clone();
18512 | 
18513 |         let mut watcher = notify::recommended_watcher(move |res: notify::Result<Event>| {
18514 |             if let Ok(event) = res {
18515 |                 let mut guard = match buffer_clone.lock() {
18516 |                     Ok(g) => g,
18517 |                     Err(e) => {
18518 |                         error!("Watcher lock poisoned: {}", e);
18519 |                         return;
18520 |                     }
18521 |                 };
18522 | 
18523 |                 match event.kind {
18524 |                     EventKind::Modify(_) | EventKind::Create(_) | EventKind::Remove(_) => {
18525 |                         for path in &event.paths {
18526 |                             match event.kind {
18527 |                                 EventKind::Remove(_) => {
18528 |                                     guard.insert(path.clone(), WatcherAction::Remove);
18529 |                                 }
18530 |                                 _ => {
18531 |                                     if path.is_file() {
18532 |                                         guard.insert(path.clone(), WatcherAction::Index);
18533 |                                     }
18534 |                                 }
18535 |                             }
18536 |                         }
18537 |                     }
18538 |                     _ => {}
18539 |                 }
18540 |             }
18541 |         })
18542 |         .map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))?;
18543 | 
18544 |         for dir in dirs {
18545 |             let path = Path::new(&dir);
18546 |             if path.exists() {
18547 |                 watcher
18548 |                     .watch(path, RecursiveMode::Recursive)
18549 |                     .map_err(|e| FlashError::Io(std::io::Error::other(e)))?;
18550 |             }
18551 |         }
18552 | 
18553 |         self.watcher = Some(watcher);
18554 |         Ok(())
18555 |     }
18556 | 
18557 |     // Returns parsed document data if file needs re-indexing
18558 |     async fn reindex_single_file(
18559 |         path: &Path,
18560 |         metadata_db: &Arc<MetadataDb>,
18561 |     ) -> Result<Option<(crate::parsers::ParsedDocument, u64, u64, [u8; 32])>> {
18562 |         if !path.exists() {
18563 |             return Ok(None);
18564 |         }
18565 | 
18566 |         let metadata = match std::fs::metadata(path) {
18567 |             Ok(m) => m,
18568 |             Err(_) => return Ok(None), // Ignore if cannot read metadata
18569 |         };
18570 | 
18571 |         let modified = metadata
18572 |             .modified()
18573 |             .unwrap_or(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
18574 |             .duration_since(std::time::SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
18575 |             .unwrap_or_default()
18576 |             .as_secs();
18577 |         let size = metadata.len();
18578 | 
18579 |         // Skip check? If watcher said it changed, it probably did.
18580 |         // But checking db saves re-hashing if it was a false alarm.
18581 |         if !metadata_db
18582 |             .needs_reindex(path, modified, size)
18583 |             .unwrap_or(true)
18584 |         {
18585 |             return Ok(None);
18586 |         }
18587 | 
18588 |         let parsed = match parse_file(path) {
18589 |             Ok(p) => p,
18590 |             Err(e) => {
18591 |                 warn!("Failed to parse file {:?}: {}", path, e);
18592 |                 return Ok(None);
18593 |             }
18594 |         };
18595 | 
18596 |         let content_hash: [u8; 32] = blake3::hash(parsed.content.as_bytes()).into();
18597 | 
18598 |         Ok(Some((parsed, modified, size, content_hash)))
18599 |     }
18600 | }
18601 | 
18602 | #[cfg(test)]
18603 | mod tests {
18604 |     use super::*;
18605 |     use crate::indexer::IndexManager;
18606 |     use crate::metadata::MetadataDb;
18607 |     use std::fs;
18608 |     use std::sync::Arc;
18609 |     use tempfile::tempdir;
18610 | 
18611 |     #[tokio::test]
18612 |     async fn test_watcher_manager_creation() {
18613 |         let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
18614 |         let indexer = Arc::new(IndexManager::open(temp.path(), 256).unwrap());
18615 |         let metadata = Arc::new(MetadataDb::open(&temp.path().join("metadata.db")).unwrap());
18616 | 
18617 |         let mut watcher = WatcherManager::new(indexer, metadata);
18618 | 
18619 |         // Add a directory to watch
18620 |         let watch_dir = temp.path().join("watch_me");
18621 |         fs::create_dir(&watch_dir).unwrap();
18622 | 
18623 |         assert!(watcher
18624 |             .update_watch_list(vec![watch_dir.to_string_lossy().to_string()])
18625 |             .is_ok());
18626 |         assert!(watcher.watcher.is_some());
18627 | 
18628 |         // Empty list should remove watcher
18629 |         assert!(watcher.update_watch_list(vec![]).is_ok());
18630 |         assert!(watcher.watcher.is_none());
18631 |     }
18632 | }
18633 | ```
18634 | 
```
